42\u2192struct MissingListenerConfigStats {\n    43\u2192  ALL_MISSING_LISTENER_CONFIG_STATS(GENERATE_COUNTER_STRUCT)\n    44\u2192};\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192class ListenerMessageUtil {\n    47\u2192public:\n    48\u2192  /**\n    49\u2192   * @return true if listener message lhs and rhs have the same socket options.\n    50\u2192   */\n    51\u2192  static bool socketOptionsEqual(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& lhs,\n    52\u2192                                 const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& rhs);\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  /**\n    55\u2192   * @return true if listener message lhs and rhs are the same if ignoring filter_chains field.\n    56\u2192   */\n    57\u2192  static bool filterChainOnlyChange(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& lhs,\n    58\u2192                                    const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& rhs);\n    59\u2192};\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192class ListenerManagerImpl;\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192class ListenSocketFactoryImpl : public Network::ListenSocketFactory,\n    64\u2192                                protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n    65\u2192public:\n    66\u2192  static absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenSocketFactoryImpl>>\n    67\u2192  create(ListenerComponentFactory& factory, Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address,\n    68\u2192         Network::Socket::Type socket_type, const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n    69\u2192         const std::string& listener_name, uint32_t tcp_backlog_size,\n    70\u2192         ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type,\n    71\u2192         const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t num_sockets);\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192  // Network::ListenSocketFactory\n    74\u2192  Network::Socket::Type socketType() const override { return socket_type_; }\n    75\u2192  const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& localAddress() const override {\n    76\u2192    return local_address_;\n    77\u2192  }\n    78\u2192  Network::SocketSharedPtr getListenSocket(uint32_t worker_index) override;\n    79\u2192  Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr clone() const override {\n    80\u2192    return absl::WrapUnique(new ListenSocketFactoryImpl(*this));\n    81\u2192  }\n    82\u2192  void closeAllSockets() override {\n    83\u2192    for (auto& socket : sockets_) {\n    84\u2192      socket->close();\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192  }\n    87\u2192  absl::Status doFinalPreWorkerInit() override;\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192private:\n    90\u2192  ListenSocketFactoryImpl(ListenerComponentFactory& factory,\n    91\u2192                          Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address,\n    92\u2192                          Network::Socket::Type socket_type,\n    93\u2192                          const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n    94\u2192                          const std::string& listener_name, uint32_t tcp_backlog_size,\n    95\u2192                          ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type,\n    96\u2192                          const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options,\n    97\u2192                          uint32_t num_sockets, absl::Status& creation_status);\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  ListenSocketFactoryImpl(const ListenSocketFactoryImpl& factory_to_clone);\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr>\n   102\u2192  createListenSocketAndApplyOptions(ListenerComponentFactory& factory,\n   103\u2192                                    Network::Socket::Type socket_type, uint32_t worker_index);\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192  ListenerComponentFactory& factory_;\n   106\u2192  // Initially, its port number might be 0. Once a socket is created, its port\n   107\u2192  // will be set to the binding port.\n   108\u2192  Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr local_address_;\n   109\u2192  const Network::Socket::Type socket_type_;\n   110\u2192  const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr options_;\n   111\u2192  const std::string listener_name_;\n   112\u2192  const uint32_t tcp_backlog_size_;\n   113\u2192  ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type_;\n   114\u2192  const Network::SocketCreationOptions socket_creation_options_;\n   115\u2192  // One socket for each worker, pre-created before the workers fetch the sockets. There are\n   116\u2192  // 3 different cases:\n   117\u2192  // 1) All are null when doing config validation.\n   118\u2192  // 2) A single socket has been duplicated for each worker (no reuse_port).\n   119\u2192  // 3) A unique socket for each worker (reuse_port).\n   120\u2192  //\n   121\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): If a listener does not bind, it still has a socket. This is confusing\n   122\u2192  // and not needed and can be cleaned up.\n   123\u2192  std::vector<Network::SocketSharedPtr> sockets_;\n   124\u2192};\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192// TODO(mattklein123): Consider getting rid of pre-worker start and post-worker start code by\n   127\u2192//                     initializing all listeners after workers are started.\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192class ListenerImpl;\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192/**\n   132\u2192 * The common functionality shared by PerListenerFilterFactoryContexts and\n   133\u2192 * PerFilterChainFactoryFactoryContexts.\n   134\u2192 */\n   135\u2192class ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl final : public Server::FactoryContextImplBase,\n   136\u2192                                             public Network::DrainDecision {\n   137\u2192public:\n   138\u2192  ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\n   139\u2192                                 ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\n   140\u2192                                 const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   141\u2192                                 Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192  Init::Manager& initManager() override;\n   144\u2192  Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  // DrainDecision\n   147\u2192  bool drainClose(Network::DrainDirection scope) const override {\n   148\u2192    return drain_manager_->drainClose(scope) || server_.drainManager().drainClose(scope);\n   149\u2192  }\n   150\u2192  Common::CallbackHandlePtr addOnDrainCloseCb(Network::DrainDirection,\n   151\u2192                                              DrainCloseCb) const override {\n   152\u2192    IS_ENVOY_BUG(\"Unexpected function call\");\n   153\u2192    return nullptr;\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192  Server::DrainManager& drainManager();\n   156\u2192  friend class ListenerImpl;\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192private:\n   159\u2192  const Server::DrainManagerPtr drain_manager_;\n   160\u2192};\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192// TODO(lambdai): Strip the interface since ListenerFactoryContext only need to support\n   163\u2192// ListenerFilterChain creation. e.g, Is listenerMetaData() required? Is it required only at\n   164\u2192// listener update or during the lifetime of listener?\n   165\u2192class PerListenerFactoryContextImpl : public Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext {\n   166\u2192public:\n   167\u2192  PerListenerFactoryContextImpl(Envoy::Server::Instance& server,\n   168\u2192                                ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,\n   169\u2192                                const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config_message,\n   170\u2192                                ListenerImpl& listener_impl, DrainManagerPtr drain_manager);\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  PerListenerFactoryContextImpl(\n   173\u2192      std::shared_ptr<ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl> listener_factory_context_base,\n   174\u2192      ListenerImpl& listener_impl)\n   175\u2192      : listener_factory_context_base_(listener_factory_context_base),\n   176\u2192        listener_impl_(listener_impl) {}\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192  // FactoryContext\n   179\u2192  Network::DrainDecision& drainDecision() override;\n   180\u2192  Init::Manager& initManager() override;\n   181\u2192  Stats::Scope& scope() override;\n   182\u2192  const Network::ListenerInfo& listenerInfo() const override;\n   183\u2192  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& messageValidationVisitor() const override;\n   184\u2192  Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& serverFactoryContext() const override;\n   185\u2192  Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContext& getTransportSocketFactoryContext() const override;\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192  Stats::Scope& listenerScope() override;\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl& parentFactoryContext() { return *listener_factory_context_base_; }\n   190\u2192  friend class ListenerImpl;\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192private:\n   193\u2192  std::shared_ptr<ListenerFactoryContextBaseImpl> listener_factory_context_base_;\n   194\u2192  ListenerImpl& listener_impl_;\n   195\u2192};\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192/**\n   198\u2192 * Maps proto config to runtime config for a listener with a network filter chain.\n   199\u2192 */\n   200\u2192class ListenerImpl final : public Network::ListenerConfig,\n   201\u2192                           public Network::FilterChainFactory,\n   202\u2192                           Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n   203\u2192public:\n   204\u2192  /**\n   205\u2192   * Create a new listener.\n   206\u2192   * @param config supplies the configuration proto.\n   207\u2192   * @param version_info supplies the xDS version of the listener.\n   208\u2192   * @param parent supplies the owning manager.\n   209\u2192   * @param name supplies the listener name.\n   210\u2192   * @param added_via_api supplies whether the listener can be updated or removed.\n   211\u2192   * @param workers_started supplies whether the listener is being added before or after workers\n   212\u2192   *        have been started. This controls various behavior related to init management.\n   213\u2192   * @param hash supplies the hash to use for duplicate checking.\n   214\u2192   * @param concurrency is the number of listeners instances to be created.\n   215\u2192   */\n   216\u2192  static absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>>\n   217\u2192  create(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\n   218\u2192         ListenerManagerImpl& parent, const std::string& name, bool added_via_api,\n   219\u2192         bool workers_started, uint64_t hash);\n   220\u2192  ~ListenerImpl() override;\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  // TODO(lambdai): Explore using the same ListenerImpl object to execute in place filter chain\n   223\u2192  // update.\n   224\u2192  /**\n   225\u2192   * Execute in place filter chain update. The filter chain update is less expensive than full\n   226\u2192   * listener update because connections may not need to be drained.\n   227\u2192   */\n   228\u2192  absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<ListenerImpl>>\n   229\u2192  newListenerWithFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   230\u2192                             bool workers_started, uint64_t hash);\n   231\u2192  /**\n   232\u2192   * Determine if in place filter chain update could be executed at this moment.\n   233\u2192   */\n   234\u2192  bool supportUpdateFilterChain(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& new_config,\n   235\u2192                                bool worker_started);\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  /**\n   238\u2192   * Run the callback on each filter chain that exists in this listener but not in the passed\n   239\u2192   * listener config.\n   240\u2192   */\n   241\u2192  void diffFilterChain(const ListenerImpl& another_listener,\n   242\u2192                       std::function<void(Network::DrainableFilterChain&)> callback);\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  /**\n   245\u2192   * Helper functions to determine whether a listener is blocked for update or remove.\n   246\u2192   */\n   247\u2192  bool blockUpdate(uint64_t new_hash) { return new_hash == hash_ || !added_via_api_; }\n   248\u2192  bool blockRemove() { return !added_via_api_; }\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192  const std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>& addresses() const {\n   251\u2192    return addresses_;\n   252\u2192  }\n   253\u2192  const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config() const { return config_; }\n   254\u2192  const std::vector<Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr>& getSocketFactories() const {\n   255\u2192    return socket_factories_;\n   256\u2192  }\n   257\u2192  void debugLog(const std::string& message);\n   258\u2192  void initialize();\n   259\u2192  DrainManager& localDrainManager() const {\n   260\u2192    return listener_factory_context_->listener_factory_context_base_->drainManager();\n   261\u2192  }\n   262\u2192  absl::Status addSocketFactory(Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr&& socket_factory);\n   263\u2192  void setSocketAndOptions(const Network::SocketSharedPtr& socket);\n   264\u2192  const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& listenSocketOptions(uint32_t address_index) {\n   265\u2192    ASSERT(listen_socket_options_list_.size() > address_index);\n   266\u2192    return listen_socket_options_list_[address_index];\n   267\u2192  }\n   268\u2192  const std::string& versionInfo() const { return version_info_; }\n   269\u2192  bool reusePort() const { return reuse_port_; }\n   270\u2192  static bool getReusePortOrDefault(Server::Instance& server,\n   271\u2192                                    const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   272\u2192                                    Network::Socket::Type socket_type);\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192  // Check whether a new listener can share sockets with this listener.\n   275\u2192  bool hasCompatibleAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const;\n   276\u2192  // Check whether a new listener has duplicated listening address this listener.\n   277\u2192  bool hasDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerImpl& other) const;\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192  // Network::ListenerConfig\n   280\u2192  Network::FilterChainManager& filterChainManager() override { return *filter_chain_manager_; }\n   281\u2192  Network::FilterChainFactory& filterChainFactory() override { return *this; }\n   282\u2192  std::vector<Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr>& listenSocketFactories() override {\n   283\u2192    return socket_factories_;\n   284\u2192  }\n   285\u2192  bool bindToPort() const override { return bind_to_port_; }\n   286\u2192  bool mptcpEnabled() { return mptcp_enabled_; }\n   287\u2192  bool handOffRestoredDestinationConnections() const override {\n   288\u2192    return hand_off_restored_destination_connections_;\n   289\u2192  }\n   290\u2192  uint32_t perConnectionBufferLimitBytes() const override {\n   291\u2192    return per_connection_buffer_limit_bytes_;\n   292\u2192  }\n   293\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds listenerFiltersTimeout() const override {\n   294\u2192    return listener_filters_timeout_;\n   295\u2192  }\n   296\u2192  bool continueOnListenerFiltersTimeout() const override {\n   297\u2192    return continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_;\n   298\u2192  }\n   299\u2192  Stats::Scope& listenerScope() override { return listener_factory_context_->listenerScope(); }\n   300\u2192  uint64_t listenerTag() const override { return listener_tag_; }\n   301\u2192  const std::string& name() const override { return name_; }\n   302\u2192  Network::UdpListenerConfigOptRef udpListenerConfig() override {\n   303\u2192    return udp_listener_config_ != nullptr ? *udp_listener_config_\n   304\u2192                                           : Network::UdpListenerConfigOptRef();\n   305\u2192  }\n   306\u2192  Network::InternalListenerConfigOptRef internalListenerConfig() override {\n   307\u2192    return internal_listener_config_ != nullptr ? *internal_listener_config_\n   308\u2192                                                : Network::InternalListenerConfigOptRef();\n   309\u2192  }\n   310\u2192  Network::ConnectionBalancer&\n   311\u2192  connectionBalancer(const Network::Address::Instance& address) override {\n   312\u2192    auto balancer = connection_balancers_.find(address.asString());\n   313\u2192    ASSERT(balancer != connection_balancers_.end());\n   314\u2192    return *balancer->second;\n   315\u2192  }\n   316\u2192  ResourceLimit& openConnections() override { return *open_connections_; }\n   317\u2192  const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& accessLogs() const override { return access_logs_; }\n   318\u2192  uint32_t tcpBacklogSize() const override { return tcp_backlog_size_; }\n   319\u2192  uint32_t maxConnectionsToAcceptPerSocketEvent() const override {\n   320\u2192    return max_connections_to_accept_per_socket_event_;\n   321\u2192  }\n   322\u2192  Init::Manager& initManager() override;\n   323\u2192  bool ignoreGlobalConnLimit() const override { return ignore_global_conn_limit_; }\n   324\u2192  bool shouldBypassOverloadManager() const override { return bypass_overload_manager_; }\n   325\u2192  const Network::ListenerInfoConstSharedPtr& listenerInfo() const override {\n   326\u2192    ASSERT(listener_factory_context_ != nullptr);\n   327\u2192    return listener_factory_context_->listener_factory_context_base_->listener_info_;\n   328\u2192  }\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  void ensureSocketOptions(Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options) {\n   331\u2192    if (options == nullptr) {\n   332\u2192      options = std::make_shared<std::vector<Network::Socket::OptionConstSharedPtr>>();\n   333\u2192    }\n   334\u2192  }\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192  absl::Status cloneSocketFactoryFrom(const ListenerImpl& other);\n   337\u2192  void closeAllSockets();\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192  Network::Socket::Type socketType() const { return socket_type_; }\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192  // Network::FilterChainFactory\n   342\u2192  bool createNetworkFilterChain(Network::Connection& connection,\n   343\u2192                                const Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList& factories) override;\n   344\u2192  bool createListenerFilterChain(Network::ListenerFilterManager& manager) override;\n   345\u2192  void createUdpListenerFilterChain(Network::UdpListenerFilterManager& udp_listener,\n   346\u2192                                    Network::UdpReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n   347\u2192  bool createQuicListenerFilterChain(Network::QuicListenerFilterManager& manager) override;\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192  SystemTime last_updated_;\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192private:\n   352\u2192  ListenerImpl(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\n   353\u2192               ListenerManagerImpl& parent, const std::string& name, bool added_via_api,\n   354\u2192               bool workers_started, uint64_t hash, absl::Status& creation_status);\n   355\u2192  struct UdpListenerConfigImpl : public Network::UdpListenerConfig {\n   356\u2192    UdpListenerConfigImpl(const envoy::config::listener::v3::UdpListenerConfig config)\n   357\u2192        : config_(config) {}\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192    // Network::UdpListenerConfig\n   360\u2192    Network::ActiveUdpListenerFactory& listenerFactory() override { return *listener_factory_; }\n   361\u2192    Network::UdpPacketWriterFactory& packetWriterFactory() override { return *writer_factory_; }\n   362\u2192    Network::UdpListenerWorkerRouter&\n   363\u2192    listenerWorkerRouter(const Network::Address::Instance& address) override {\n   364\u2192      auto iter = listener_worker_routers_.find(address.asString());\n   365\u2192      ASSERT(iter != listener_worker_routers_.end());\n   366\u2192      return *iter->second;\n   367\u2192    }\n   368\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::UdpListenerConfig& config() override { return config_; }\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192    const envoy::config::listener::v3::UdpListenerConfig config_;\n   371\u2192    Network::ActiveUdpListenerFactoryPtr listener_factory_;\n   372\u2192    Network::UdpPacketWriterFactoryPtr writer_factory_;\n   373\u2192    absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, Network::UdpListenerWorkerRouterPtr> listener_worker_routers_;\n   374\u2192  };\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192  class InternalListenerConfigImpl : public Network::InternalListenerConfig {\n   377\u2192  public:\n   378\u2192    InternalListenerConfigImpl(Network::InternalListenerRegistry& internal_listener_registry)\n   379\u2192        : internal_listener_registry_(internal_listener_registry) {}\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192    Network::InternalListenerRegistry& internalListenerRegistry() override {\n   382\u2192      return internal_listener_registry_;\n   383\u2192    }\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192  private:\n   386\u2192    Network::InternalListenerRegistry& internal_listener_registry_;\n   387\u2192  };\n   388\u2192\n   389\u2192  /**\n   390\u2192   * Create a new listener from an existing listener and the new config message if the in place\n   391\u2192   * filter chain update is decided. Should be called only by newListenerWithFilterChain().\n   392\u2192   */\n   393\u2192  ListenerImpl(ListenerImpl& origin, const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   394\u2192               const std::string& version_info, ListenerManagerImpl& parent,\n   395\u2192               const std::string& name, bool added_via_api, bool workers_started, uint64_t hash,\n   396\u2192               absl::Status& creation_status);\n   397\u2192  // Helpers for constructor.\n   398\u2192  void buildAccessLog(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   399\u2192  absl::Status buildInternalListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   400\u2192  absl::Status validateConfig();\n   401\u2192  bool buildUdpListenerWorkerRouter(const Network::Address::Instance& address,\n   402\u2192                                    uint32_t concurrency);\n   403\u2192  absl::Status buildUdpListenerFactory(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   404\u2192                                       uint32_t concurrency);\n   405\u2192  void buildListenSocketOptions(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   406\u2192                                std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<\n   407\u2192                                    envoy::config::core::v3::SocketOption>>>& address_opts_list);\n   408\u2192  absl::Status createListenerFilterFactories(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   409\u2192  absl::Status validateFilterChains(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   410\u2192  absl::Status buildFilterChains(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   411\u2192  absl::Status buildConnectionBalancer(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n   412\u2192                                       const Network::Address::Instance& address);\n   413\u2192  void buildSocketOptions(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   414\u2192  void buildOriginalDstListenerFilter(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   415\u2192  void buildProxyProtocolListenerFilter(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config);\n   416\u2192  absl::Status checkIpv4CompatAddress(const Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& address,\n   417\u2192                                      const envoy::config::core::v3::Address& proto_address);\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192  void addListenSocketOptions(Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n   420\u2192                              const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& append_options) {\n   421\u2192    ensureSocketOptions(options);\n   422\u2192    Network::Socket::appendOptions(options, append_options);\n   423\u2192  }\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192  ListenerManagerImpl& parent_;\n   426\u2192  std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr> addresses_;\n   427\u2192  const Network::Socket::Type socket_type_;\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192  std::vector<Network::ListenSocketFactoryPtr> socket_factories_;\n   430\u2192  const bool bind_to_port_;\n   431\u2192  const bool mptcp_enabled_;\n   432\u2192  const bool hand_off_restored_destination_connections_;\n   433\u2192  const uint32_t per_connection_buffer_limit_bytes_;\n   434\u2192  const uint64_t listener_tag_;\n   435\u2192  const std::string name_;\n   436\u2192  const bool added_via_api_;\n   437\u2192  const bool workers_started_;\n   438\u2192  const uint64_t hash_;\n   439\u2192  const uint32_t tcp_backlog_size_;\n   440\u2192  const uint32_t max_connections_to_accept_per_socket_event_;\n   441\u2192  ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor_;\n   442\u2192  const bool ignore_global_conn_limit_;\n   443\u2192  const bool bypass_overload_manager_;\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192  // A target is added to Server's InitManager if workers_started_ is false.\n   446\u2192  Init::TargetImpl listener_init_target_;\n   447\u2192  // This init manager is populated with targets from the filter chain factories, namely\n   448\u2192  // RdsRouteConfigSubscription::init_target_, so the listener can wait for route configs.\n   449\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Init::Manager> dynamic_init_manager_;\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192  Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList listener_filter_factories_;\n   452\u2192  std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb> udp_listener_filter_factories_;\n   453\u2192  Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList quic_listener_filter_factories_;\n   454\u2192  AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector access_logs_;\n   455\u2192  const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener config_;\n   456\u2192  const std::string version_info_;\n   457\u2192  // Using std::vector instead of hash map for supporting multiple zero port addresses.\n   458\u2192  std::vector<Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr> listen_socket_options_list_;\n   459\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds listener_filters_timeout_;\n   460\u2192  const bool continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_;\n   461\u2192  std::shared_ptr<UdpListenerConfigImpl> udp_listener_config_;\n   462\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Network::InternalListenerConfig> internal_listener_config_;\n   463\u2192  // The key is the address string, the value is the address specific connection balancer.\n   464\u2192  // TODO (soulxu): Add hash support for address, then needn't a string address as key anymore.\n   465\u2192  absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, Network::ConnectionBalancerSharedPtr> connection_balancers_;\n   466\u2192 469\u2192\n   470\u2192  // Per-listener connection limits are only specified via runtime.\n   471\u2192  //\n   472\u2192  // TODO (tonya11en): Move this functionality into the overload manager.\n   473\u2192  const std::string cx_limit_runtime_key_;\n   474\u2192  std::shared_ptr<BasicResourceLimitImpl> open_connections_;\n   475\u2192\n   476\u2192  // This init watcher, if workers_started_ is false, notifies the \"parent\" listener manager when\n   477\u2192  // listener initialization is complete.\n   478\u2192  // Important: local_init_watcher_ must be the last field in the class to avoid unexpected\n   479\u2192  // watcher callback during the destroy of ListenerImpl.\n   480\u2192  Init::WatcherImpl local_init_watcher_;\n   481\u2192 485\u2192  MissingListenerConfigStats missing_listener_config_stats_;\n   486\u2192\n   487\u2192  // to access ListenerManagerImpl::factory_.\n   488\u2192  friend class ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder;\n   489\u2192};\n   490\u2192\n   491\u2192} // namespace Server\n   492\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   493\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you stream_info_.get(); }\n    86\u2192  bool connected() const { return connected_; }\n    87\u2192  bool isEndFilterIteration() const { return iter_ == accept_filters_.end(); }\n    88\u2192\n It's used to get the correct listener\n    96\u2192  // when rebalancing. Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/listener_manager/active_stream_listener_base.h"
}
Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <atomic>\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     5\u2192#include <list>\n     6\u2192#include <memory>\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/common/time.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/event/dispatcher.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection_handler.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/network/listener.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"envoy/stats/timespan.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/stream_info/stream_info.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192#include \"source/common/common/linked_object.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"source/common/listener_manager/active_tcp_socket.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"source/server/active_listener_base.h\"\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    21\u2192namespace Server {\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// The base class of the stream listener. It owns listener filter handling of active sockets.\n    24\u2192// After the active socket passes all the listener filters, a server connection is created. The\n    25\u2192// derived listener must override ``newActiveConnection`` to take the ownership of that server\n    26\u2192// connection.\n    27\u2192// TODO(lambdai): Refactor the listener filter test cases to adopt this class.\n    28\u2192class ActiveStreamListenerBase : public ActiveListenerImplBase,\n    29\u2192                                 protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::conn_handler> {\n    30\u2192public:\n    31\u2192  ActiveStreamListenerBase(Network::ConnectionHandler& parent, Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\n    32\u2192                           Network::ListenerPtr&& listener, Network::ListenerConfig& config);\n    33\u2192  static void emitLogs(Network::ListenerConfig& config, StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info);\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() { return dispatcher_; }\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192  /**\n    38\u2192   * Schedule to remove and destroy the active connections which are not tracked by listener\n    39\u2192   * config. Caution: The connection are not destroyed yet when function returns.\n    40\u2192   */\n    41\u2192  void onFilterChainDraining(\n    42\u2192      const std::list<const Network::FilterChain*>& draining_filter_chains) override {\n    43\u2192    // Need to recover the original deleting state.\n    44\u2192    const bool was_deleting = is_deleting_;\n    45\u2192    is_deleting_ = true;\n    46\u2192    for (const auto* filter_chain : draining_filter_chains) {\n    47\u2192      removeFilterChain(filter_chain);\n    48\u2192    }\n    49\u2192    is_deleting_ = was_deleting;\n    50\u2192  }\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192  virtual void incNumConnections() PURE;\n    53\u2192  virtual void decNumConnections() PURE;\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192  /**\n    56\u2192   * Create a new connection from a socket accepted by the listener.\n    57\u2192   */\n    58\u2192  void newConnection(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n    59\u2192                     std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo> stream_info);\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192  /**\n    62\u2192   * Remove the socket from this listener. Should be called when the socket passes the listener\n    63\u2192   * filter.\n    64\u2192   * @return std::unique_ptr<ActiveTcpSocket> the exact same socket in the parameter but in the\n    65\u2192   * state that not owned by the listener.\n    66\u2192   */\n    67\u2192  std::unique_ptr<ActiveTcpSocket> removeSocket(ActiveTcpSocket&& socket) {\n    68\u2192    return socket.removeFromList(sockets_);\n    69\u2192  }\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192  /**\n    72\u2192   * @return const std::list<std::unique_ptr<ActiveTcpSocket>>& the sockets going through the\n    73\u2192   * listener filters.\n    74\u2192   */\n    75\u2192  const std::list<std::unique_ptr<ActiveTcpSocket>>& sockets() const { return sockets_; }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  /**\n    78\u2192   * Schedule removal and destruction of all active connections owned by a filter chain.\n    79\u2192   */\n    80\u2192  virtual void removeFilterChain(const Network::FilterChain* filter_chain) PURE;\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192  virtual Network::BalancedConnectionHandlerOptRef\n    83\u2192  getBalancedHandlerByAddress(const Network::Address::Instance& address) PURE;\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  void onSocketAccepted(std::unique_ptr<ActiveTcpSocket> active_socket) {\n    86\u2192    // Create and run the filters\n    87\u2192    if (config_->filterChainFactory().createListenerFilterChain(*active_socket)) {\n    88\u2192      active_socket->startFilterChain();\n    89\u2192    } else {\n    90\u2192      // If create listener filter chain failed, it means the listener is missing\n    91\u2192      // config due to the ECDS. Then close the connection directly.\n    92\u2192      active_socket->socket().close();\n    93\u2192      ASSERT(active_socket->isEndFilterIteration());\n    94\u2192    }\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    // Move active_socket to the sockets_ list if filter iteration needs to continue later.\n    97\u2192    // Otherwise we let active_socket be destructed when it goes out of scope.\n    98\u2192    if (!active_socket->isEndFilterIteration()) {\n    99\u2192      active_socket->startTimer();\n   100\u2192      LinkedList::moveIntoListBack(std::move(active_socket), sockets_);\n   101\u2192    } else {\n   102\u2192      if (!active_socket->connected()) {\n   103\u2192        // If active_socket is about to be destructed, emit logs if a connection is not created.\n   104\u2192        if (active_socket->streamInfo() != nullptr) {\n   105\u2192          emitLogs(*config_, *active_socket->streamInfo());\n   106\u2192        } else {\n   107\u2192          // If the active_socket is not connected, this socket is not promoted to active\n   108\u2192          // connection. Thus the stream_info_ is owned by this active socket.\n   109\u2192          ENVOY_BUG(active_socket->streamInfo() != nullptr,\n   110\u2192                    \"the unconnected active socket must have stream info.\");\n   111\u2192        }\n   112\u2192      }\n   113\u2192    }\n   114\u2192  }\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192  // Below members are open to access by ActiveTcpSocket.\n   117\u2192  Network::ConnectionHandler& parent_;\n   118\u2192  const std::chrono::milliseconds listener_filters_timeout_;\n   119\u2192  const bool continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_;\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192protected:\n   122\u2192  /**\n   123\u2192   * Create the active connection from server connection. This active listener owns the created\n   124\u2192   * active connection.\n   125\u2192   *\n   126\u2192   * @param filter_chain The network filter chain linking to the connection.\n   127\u2192   * @param server_conn_ptr The server connection.\n   128\u2192   * @param stream_info The stream info of the active connection.\n   129\u2192   */\n   130\u2192  virtual void newActiveConnection(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n   131\u2192                                   Network::ServerConnectionPtr server_conn_ptr,\n   132\u2192                                   std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo> stream_info) PURE;\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  std::list<std::unique_ptr<ActiveTcpSocket>> sockets_;\n   135\u2192  Network::ListenerPtr listener_;\n   136\u2192  // True if the follow up connection deletion is raised by the connection collection deletion is\n   137\u2192  // performing. Otherwise, the collection should be deleted when the last connection in the\n   138\u2192  // collection is removed. This state is maintained in base class because this state is independent\n   139\u2192  // from concrete connection type.\n   140\u2192  bool is_deleting_{false};\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192private:\n   143\u2192  Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher_;\n   144\u2192};\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192struct ActiveTcpConnection;\n   147\u2192class OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase;\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192/**\n   150\u2192 * Wrapper for a group of active connections which are attached to the same filter chain context.\n   151\u2192 */\n   152\u2192class ActiveConnections : public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n   153\u2192public:\n   154\u2192  ActiveConnections(OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase& listener,\n   155\u2192                    const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain);\n   156\u2192  ~ActiveConnections() override;\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192  // listener filter chain pair is the owner of the connections\n   159\u2192  OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase& listener_;\n   160\u2192  const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain_;\n   161\u2192  // Owned connections.\n   162\u2192  std::list<std::unique_ptr<ActiveTcpConnection>> connections_;\n   163\u2192};\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192/**\n   166\u2192 * Wrapper for an active TCP connection owned by this handler.\n   167\u2192 */\n   168\u2192struct ActiveTcpConnection : LinkedObject<ActiveTcpConnection>,\n   169\u2192                             public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n   170\u2192                             public Network::ConnectionCallbacks,\n   171\u2192                             Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::conn_handler> {\n   172\u2192  ActiveTcpConnection(ActiveConnections& active_connections,\n   173\u2192                      Network::ConnectionPtr&& new_connection, TimeSource& time_system,\n   174\u2192                      std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo>&& stream_info);\n   175\u2192  ~ActiveTcpConnection() override;\n   176\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n   177\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n   178\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {}\n   179\u2192  void ListenerMethodFn = std::function<void(Network::ConnectionHandler::ActiveListener&)>;\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192    /**\n   109\u2192     * A helper to execute specific method on each PerAddressActiveListenerDetails item.\n   110\u2192     */\n   111\u2192    void invokeListenerMethod(ListenerMethodFn fn) {\n   112\u2192      std::for_each(per_address_details_list_.begin(), per_address_details_list_.end(),\n   113\u2192                    [&fn](std::shared_ptr<PerAddressActiveListenerDetails>& details) {\n   114\u2192                      fn(*details->listener_);\n   115\u2192                    });\n   116\u2192    }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    /**\n   119\u2192     * Add an ActiveListener into the list.\n   120\u2192     */\n   121\u2192    template <class 126\u2192      auto per_address_details = std::make_shared<PerAddressActiveListenerDetails>();\n   127\u2192      per_address_details->typed_listener_ = *listener;\n   128\u2192      per_address_details->listener_ = std::move(listener);\n   129\u2192      per_address_details->address_ = address;\n   130\u2192      if (disable_listeners) {\n   131\u2192        per_address_details->listener_->pauseListening();\n   132\u2192      }\n   133\u2192      if (auto* listener = per_address_details->listener_->listener(); listener != nullptr) {\n   134\u2192        listener->setRejectFraction(listener_reject_fraction);\n   135\u2192        if (overload_manager) {\n   136\u2192          listener->configureLoadShedPoints(overload_manager.value());\n   137\u2192        }\n   138\u2192      }\n   139\u2192      per_address_details->listener_tag_ = config.listenerTag();\n   140\u2192      per_address_details_list_.emplace_back(per_address_details);\n   141\u2192 ActiveStreamListenerBase::emitLogs(Network::ListenerConfig& config,\n    31\u2192                                        StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info) {\n    32\u2192  stream_info.onRequestComplete();\n    33\u2192  for (const auto& access_log : config.accessLogs()) {\n    34\u2192    access_log->log({}, stream_info);\n    35\u2192  }\n    36\u2192}\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192void ActiveStreamListenerBase::newConnection(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n    39\u2192                                             std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo> stream_info) {\n    40\u2192  // Find matching filter chain.\n    41\u2192  const auto filter_chain = config_->filterChainManager().findFilterChain(*socket, *stream_info);\n    42\u2192  if (filter_chain == nullptr) {\n    43\u2192    RELEASE_ASSERT(socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n    44\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"closing connection from {}: no matching filter chain found\",\n    45\u2192              socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n    46\u2192    stats_.no_filter_chain_match_.inc();\n    47\u2192    stream_info->setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoRouteFound);\n    48\u2192    stream_info->setResponseCodeDetails(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterChainNotFound);\n    49\u2192    emitLogs(*config_, *stream_info);\n    50\u2192    socket->close();\n    51\u2192    return;\n    52\u2192  }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  socket->connectionInfoProvider().setListenerInfo(config_->listenerInfo());\n    55\u2192  socket->connectionInfoProvider().setFilterChainInfo(\n    56\u2192      std::make_shared<FilterChainInfoImpl>(filter_chain->name()));\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192  auto transport_socket = filter_chain->transportSocketFactory().createDownstreamTransportSocket();\n    59\u2192  auto server_conn_ptr = dispatcher().createServerConnection(\n    60\u2192      std::move(socket), std::move(transport_socket), *stream_info);\n    61\u2192  if (const auto timeout = filter_chain->transportSocketConnectTimeout();\n    62\u2192      timeout != std::chrono::milliseconds::zero()) {\n    63\u2192    server_conn_ptr->setTransportSocketConnectTimeout(\n    64\u2192        timeout, stats_.downstream_cx_transport_socket_connect_timeout_);\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192  server_conn_ptr->setBufferLimits(config_->perConnectionBufferLimitBytes());\n    67\u2192  RELEASE_ASSERT(server_conn_ptr->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n    68\u2192  const bool empty_filter_chain = !config_->filterChainFactory().createNetworkFilterChain(\n    69\u2192      *server_conn_ptr, filter_chain->networkFilterFactories());\n    70\u2192  if (empty_filter_chain) {\n    71\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"closing connection from {}: no filters\", *server_conn_ptr,\n    72\u2192                   server_conn_ptr->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n    73\u2192    server_conn_ptr->close(Network::ConnectionCloseType::NoFlush, \"no_filters\");\n    74\u2192  }\n    75\u2192  newActiveConnection(*filter_chain, std::move(server_conn_ptr), std::move(stream_info));\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192ActiveConnections::ActiveConnections(OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase& listener,\n    79\u2192                                     const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain)\n    80\u2192    : listener_(listener), filter_chain_(filter_chain) {}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192ActiveConnections::~ActiveConnections() {\n    83\u2192  // connections should be defer deleted already.\n    84\u2192  ASSERT(connections_.empty());\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192ActiveTcpConnection::ActiveTcpConnection(ActiveConnections& active_connections,\n    88\u2192                                         Network::ConnectionPtr&& new_connection,\n    89\u2192                                         TimeSource& time_source,\n    90\u2192                                         std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo>&& stream_info)\n    91\u2192    : stream_info_(std::move(stream_info)), active_connections_(active_connections),\n    92\u2192      connection_(std::move(new_connection)),\n    93\u2192      conn_length_(new Stats::HistogramCompletableTimespanImpl(\n    94\u2192          active_connections_.listener_.stats_.downstream_cx_length_ms_, time_source)) {\n    95\u2192  // We just universally set no delay on connections. Theoretically we might at some point want\n    96\u2192  // to make this configurable.\n    97\u2192  connection_->noDelay(true);\n    98\u2192  auto& listener = active_connections_.listener_;\n    99\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_total_.inc();\n   100\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_active_.inc();\n   101\u2192  listener.per_worker_stats_.downstream_cx_total_.inc();\n   102\u2192  listener.per_worker_stats_.downstream_cx_active_.inc();\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192  // Active connections on the handler (not listener). The per listener connections have already\n   105\u2192  // been incremented at this point either via the connection balancer or in the socket accept\n   106\u2192  // path if there is no configured balancer.\n   107\u2192  listener.parent_.incNumConnections();\n   108\u2192}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192ActiveTcpConnection::~ActiveTcpConnection() {\n   111\u2192  ActiveStreamListenerBase::emitLogs(*active_connections_.listener_.config_, *stream_info_);\n   112\u2192  auto& listener = active_connections_.listener_;\n   113\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_active_.dec();\n   114\u2192  listener.stats_.downstream_cx_destroy_.inc();\n   115\u2192  listener.per_worker_stats_.downstream_cx_active_.dec();\n   116\u2192  conn_length_->complete();\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  // Active listener connections (not handler).\n   119\u2192  listener.decNumConnections();\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  // Active handler connections (not listener).\n   122\u2192  listener.parent_.decNumConnections();\n   123\u2192}\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192void ActiveTcpConnection::onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) {\n   126\u2192  ENVOY_CONN_LOG(trace, \"tcp connection on event {}\", *connection_, static_cast<int>(event));\n   127\u2192  // Any event leads to destruction of the connection.\n   128\u2192  if (event == Network::ConnectionEvent::LocalClose ||\n   129\u2192      event == Network::ConnectionEvent::RemoteClose) {\n   130\u2192    stream_info_->setDownstreamTransportFailureReason(connection_->transportFailureReason());\n   131\u2192    active_connections_.listener_.removeConnection(*this);\n   132\u2192  }\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192void OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase::removeConnection(ActiveTcpConnection& connection) {\n   136\u2192  ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"adding to cleanup list\", *connection.connection_);\n   137\u2192  ActiveConnections& active_connections = connection.active_connections_;\n   138\u2192  ActiveConnectionPtr removed = connection.removeFromList(active_connections.connections_);\n   139\u2192  dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(removed));\n   140\u2192  // Delete map entry if and only if connections_ becomes empty.\n   141\u2192  if (active_connections.connections_.empty()) {\n   142\u2192    auto iter = connections_by_context_.find(&active_connections.filter_chain_);\n   143\u2192    ASSERT(iter != connections_by_context_.end());\n   144\u2192    // To cover the lifetime of every single connection, Connections need to be deferred deleted\n   145\u2192    // because the previously contained connection is deferred deleted.\n   146\u2192    dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(iter->second));\n   147\u2192    // The erase will break the iteration over the connections_by_context_ during the deletion.\n   148\u2192    if (!is_deleting_) {\n   149\u2192      connections_by_context_.erase(iter);\n   150\u2192    }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider \"source/common/network/utility.h\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    13\u2192namespace Server {\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192ActiveTcpListener::ActiveTcpListener(Network::TcpConnectionHandler& parent,\n    16\u2192                                     Network::ListenerConfig& config, Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n    17\u2192                                     Random::RandomGenerator& random,\n    18\u2192                                     Network::SocketSharedPtr&& socket,\n    19\u2192                                     Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& listen_address,\n    20\u2192                                     Network::ConnectionBalancer& connection_balancer,\n    21\u2192                                     ThreadLocalOverloadStateOptRef overload_state)\n    22\u2192    : OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase(\n    23\u2192          parent, parent.dispatcher(),\n    24\u2192          parent.createListener(std::move(socket), *this, runtime, random, config, overload_state),\n    25\u2192          config),\n    26\u2192      tcp_conn_handler_(parent), connection_balancer_(connection_balancer),\n    27\u2192      listen_address_(listen_address) {\n    28\u2192  connection_balancer_.registerHandler(*this);\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192ActiveTcpListener::ActiveTcpListener(Network::TcpConnectionHandler& parent,\n    32\u2192                                     Network::ListenerPtr&& listener,\n    33\u2192                                     Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr& listen_address,\n    34\u2192                                     Network::ListenerConfig& config,\n    35\u2192                                     Network::ConnectionBalancer& connection_balancer,\n    36\u2192                                     Runtime::Loader&)\n    37\u2192    : OwnedActiveStreamListenerBase(parent, parent.dispatcher(), std::move(listener), config),\n    38\u2192      tcp_conn_handler_(parent), connection_balancer_(connection_balancer),\n    39\u2192      listen_address_(listen_address) {\n    40\u2192  connection_balancer_.registerHandler(*this);\n    41\u2192}\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192ActiveTcpListener::~ActiveTcpListener() {\n    44\u2192  is_deleting_ = true;\n    45\u2192  connection_balancer_.unregisterHandler(*this);\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192  // Purge sockets that have not progressed to connections. This should only happen when\n    48\u2192  // a listener filter stops iteration and never resumes.\n    49\u2192  while (!sockets_.empty()) {\n    50\u2192    auto removed = sockets_.front()->removeFromList(sockets_);\n    51\u2192    dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(removed));\n    52\u2192  }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  for (auto& [chain, active_connections] : connections_by_context_) {\n    55\u2192    ASSERT(active_connections != nullptr);\n    56\u2192    auto& connections = active_connections->connections_;\n    57\u2192    while (!connections.empty()) {\n    58\u2192      connections.front()->connection_->close(\n    59\u2192          Network::ConnectionCloseType::NoFlush,\n    60\u2192          \"purging_socket_that_have_not_progressed_to_connections\");\n    61\u2192    }\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  dispatcher().clearDeferredDeleteList();\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  // By the time a listener is destroyed, in the common case, there should be no connections.\n    66\u2192  // However, this is not always true if there is an in flight rebalanced connection that is\n    67\u2192  // being posted. This assert is extremely useful for debugging the common path so we will leave it\n    68\u2192  // for now. If it becomes a problem (developers hitting this assert when using debug builds) we\n    69\u2192  // can revisit. This case, if it happens, should be benign on production builds. This case is\n    70\u2192  // covered in ConnectionHandlerTest::RemoveListenerDuringRebalance.\n    71\u2192  ASSERT(num_listener_connections_ == 0, fmt::format(\"destroyed listener {} has {} connections\",\n    72\u2192                                                     config_->name(), numConnections()));\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::updateListenerConfig(Network::ListenerConfig& config) {\n    76\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"replacing listener \", config_->listenerTag(), \" by \", config.listenerTag());\n    77\u2192  config_ = &config;\n    78\u2192}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::onAccept(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket) {\n    81\u2192  if (listenerConnectionLimitReached()) {\n    82\u2192    RELEASE_ASSERT(socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress() != nullptr, \"\");\n    83\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(trace, \"closing connection from {}: listener connection limit reached for {}\",\n    84\u2192              socket->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString(), config_->name());\n    85\u2192    socket->close();\n    86\u2192    stats_.downstream_cx_overflow_.inc();\n    87\u2192    return;\n    88\u2192  }\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  onAcceptWorker(std::move(socket), config_->handOffRestoredDestinationConnections(), false);\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::onReject(RejectCause cause) {\n    94\u2192  switch (cause) {\n    95\u2192  case RejectCause::GlobalCxLimit:\n    96\u2192    stats_.downstream_global_cx_overflow_.inc();\n    97\u2192    break;\n    98\u2192  case RejectCause::OverloadAction:\n    99\u2192    stats_.downstream_cx_overload_reject_.inc();\n   100\u2192    break;\n   101\u2192  }\n   102\u2192}\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::recordConnectionsAcceptedOnSocketEvent(uint32_t connections_accepted) {\n   105\u2192  stats_.connections_accepted_per_socket_event_.recordValue(connections_accepted);\n   106\u2192}\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::onAcceptWorker(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n   109\u2192                                       bool hand_off_restored_destination_connections,\n   110\u2192                                       bool rebalanced) {\n   111\u2192  // Get Round Trip Time\n   112\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> t = socket->lastRoundTripTime();\n   113\u2192  if (t.has_value()) {\n   114\u2192    socket->connectionInfoProvider().setRoundTripTime(t.value());\n   115\u2192  }\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192  if (!rebalanced) {\n   118\u2192    Network::BalancedConnectionHandler& target_handler =\n   119\u2192        connection_balancer_.pickTargetHandler(*this);\n   120\u2192    if (&target_handler != this) {\n   121\u2192      target_handler.post(std::move(socket));\n   122\u2192      return;\n   123\u2192    }\n   124\u2192  }\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  auto active_socket = std::make_unique<ActiveTcpSocket>(*this, std::move(socket),\n   127\u2192                                                         hand_off_restored_destination_connections);\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192  onSocketAccepted(std::move(active_socket));\n   130\u2192}\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::pauseListening() {\n   133\u2192  if (listener_ != nullptr) {\n   134\u2192    listener_->disable();\n   135\u2192  }\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::resumeListening() {\n   139\u2192  if (listener_ != nullptr) {\n   140\u2192    listener_->enable();\n   141\u2192  }\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192Network::BalancedConnectionHandlerOptRef\n   145\u2192ActiveTcpListener::getBalancedHandlerByAddress(const Network::Address::Instance& address) {\n   146\u2192  return tcp_conn_handler_.getBalancedHandlerByAddress(address);\n   147\u2192}\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::newActiveConnection(const Network::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n   150\u2192                                            Network::ServerConnectionPtr server_conn_ptr,\n   151\u2192                                            std::unique_ptr<StreamInfo::StreamInfo> stream_info) {\n   152\u2192  auto& active_connections = getOrCreateActiveConnections(filter_chain);\n   153\u2192  auto active_connection =\n   154\u2192      std::make_unique<ActiveTcpConnection>(active_connections, std::move(server_conn_ptr),\n   155\u2192                                            dispatcher().timeSource(), std::move(stream_info));\n   156\u2192  // If the connection is already closed, we can just let this connection immediately die.\n   157\u2192  if (active_connection->connection_->state() != Network::Connection::State::Closed) {\n   158\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(\n   159\u2192        debug, \"new connection from {}\", *active_connection->connection_,\n   160\u2192        active_connection->connection_->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString());\n   161\u2192    active_connection->connection_->addConnectionCallbacks(*active_connection);\n   162\u2192    LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(active_connection), active_connections.connections_);\n   163\u2192  }\n   164\u2192}\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192void ActiveTcpListener::post(Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket) {\n   167\u2192  // It is not possible to capture a unique_ptr because the post() API copies the lambda, so we must\n   168\u2192  // bundle the socket inside a shared_ptr that can be captured.\n   169\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): It may be possible to change the post() API such that the lambda is only\n   170\u2192  // moved, but this is non-trivial and needs investigation.\n   171\u2192  RebalancedSocketSharedPtr socket_to_rebalance = std::make_shared<RebalancedSocket>();\n   172\u2192  socket_to_rebalance->socket = std::move(socket);\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192  dispatcher().post([socket_to_rebalance, address = listen_address_, tag = config_->listenerTag(),\n   175\u2192                     &tcp_conn_handler = tcp_conn_handler_,\n   176\u2192                     handoff = config_->handOffRestoredDestinationConnections()]() {\n   177\u2192    auto balanced_handler = tcp_conn_handler.getBalancedHandlerByTag(tag, *address);\n   178\u2192    if (balanced_handler.has_value()) {\n   179\u2192      balanced_handler->get().onAcceptWorker(std::move(socket_to_rebalance->socket), handoff, true);\n   180\u2192      return;\n   181\u2192    }\n   182\u2192  });\n   183\u2192}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192} // namespace Server\n   186\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   187\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

{

Output
"     1\u2192#include \"source/common/listener_manager/active_tcp_socket.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/network/filter.h\"\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192#include \"source/common/listener_manager/active_stream_listener_base.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"source/common/stream_info/stream_info_impl.h\"\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192namespace Envoy {\n     9\u2192namespace Server {\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192ActiveTcpSocket::ActiveTcpSocket(ActiveStreamListenerBase& listener,\n    12\u2192                                 Network::ConnectionSocketPtr&& socket,\n    13\u2192                                 bool hand_off_restored_destination_connections)\n    14\u2192    : listener_(listener), socket_(std::move(socket)),\n    15\u2192      hand_off_restored_destination_connections_(hand_off_restored_destination_connections),\n    16\u2192      iter_(accept_filters_.end()),\n    17\u2192      stream_info_(std::make_unique<StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl>(\n    18\u2192          listener_.dispatcher().timeSource(), socket_->connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr(),\n    19\u2192          StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Connection)) {\n    20\u2192  listener_.stats_.downstream_pre_cx_active_.inc();\n    21\u2192}\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192ActiveTcpSocket::~ActiveTcpSocket() {\n    24\u2192  accept_filters_.clear();\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192  listener_.stats_.downstream_pre_cx_active_.dec();\n    27\u2192  // If the underlying socket is no longer attached, it means that it has been transferred to\n    28\u2192  // an active connection. In this case, the active connection will decrement the number\n    29\u2192  // of listener connections.\n    30\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): In general the way we account for the number of listener connections\n    31\u2192  // is incredibly fragile. Revisit this by potentially merging ActiveTcpSocket and\n    32\u2192  // ActiveTcpConnection, having a shared object which does accounting (but would require\n    33\u2192  // another allocation, etc.).\n    34\u2192  if (socket_ != nullptr) {\n    35\u2192    listener_.decNumConnections();\n    36\u2192  }\n    37\u2192}\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192Event::Dispatcher& ActiveTcpSocket::dispatcher() { return listener_.dispatcher(); }\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::onTimeout() {\n    42\u2192  listener_.stats_.downstream_pre_cx_timeout_.inc();\n    43\u2192  ASSERT(inserted());\n    44\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"listener filter times out after {} ms\",\n    45\u2192            listener_.listener_filters_timeout_.count());\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192  if (listener_.continue_on_listener_filters_timeout_) {\n    48\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"fallback to default listener filter\");\n    49\u2192    newConnection();\n    50\u2192  }\n    51\u2192  unlink();\n    52\u2192}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::startTimer() {\n    55\u2192  if (listener_.listener_filters_timeout_.count() > 0) {\n    56\u2192    timer_ = listener_.dispatcher().createTimer([this]() -> void { onTimeout(); });\n    57\u2192    timer_->enableTimer(listener_.listener_filters_timeout_);\n    58\u2192  }\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::unlink() {\n    62\u2192  auto removed = listener_.removeSocket(std::move(*this));\n    63\u2192  if (removed->timer_ != nullptr) {\n    64\u2192    removed->timer_->disableTimer();\n    65\u2192  }\n    66\u2192  // Emit logs if a connection is not established.\n    67\u2192  if (!connected_ && stream_info_ != nullptr) {\n    68\u2192    ActiveStreamListenerBase::emitLogs(*listener_.config_, *stream_info_);\n    69\u2192  }\n    70\u2192  listener_.dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(removed));\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::createListenerFilterBuffer() {\n    74\u2192  listener_filter_buffer_ = std::make_unique<Network::ListenerFilterBufferImpl>(\n    75\u2192      socket_->ioHandle(), listener_.dispatcher(),\n    76\u2192      [this](bool error) {\n    77\u2192        socket_->ioHandle().close();\n    78\u2192        if (error) {\n    79\u2192          listener_.stats_.downstream_listener_filter_error_.inc();\n    80\u2192        } else {\n    81\u2192          listener_.stats_.downstream_listener_filter_remote_close_.inc();\n    82\u2192        }\n    83\u2192        continueFilterChain(false);\n    84\u2192      },\n    85\u2192      [this](Network::ListenerFilterBufferImpl& filter_buffer) {\n    86\u2192        Network::FilterStatus status = (*iter_)->onData(filter_buffer);\n    87\u2192        if (status == Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration) {\n    88\u2192          if (socket_->ioHandle().isOpen()) {\n    89\u2192            // The listener filter should not wait for more data when it has already received\n    90\u2192            // all the data it requested.\n    91\u2192            ASSERT(filter_buffer.rawSlice().len_ < (*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n    92\u2192            // Check if the maxReadBytes is changed or not. If change,\n    93\u2192            // reset the buffer capacity.\n    94\u2192            if ((*iter_)->maxReadBytes() > filter_buffer.capacity()) {\n    95\u2192              filter_buffer.resetCapacity((*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n    96\u2192              // Activate `Read` event manually in case the data already\n    97\u2192              // available in the socket buffer.\n    98\u2192              filter_buffer.activateFileEvent(Event::FileReadyType::Read);\n    99\u2192            }\n   100\u2192          } else {\n   101\u2192            // The filter closed the socket.\n   102\u2192            continueFilterChain(false);\n   103\u2192          }\n   104\u2192          return;\n   105\u2192        }\n   106\u2192        continueFilterChain(true);\n   107\u2192      },\n   108\u2192      (*iter_)->maxReadBytes() == 0, (*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::continueFilterChain(bool success) {\n   112\u2192  if (success) {\n   113\u2192    bool no_error = true;\n   114\u2192    if (iter_ == accept_filters_.end()) {\n   115\u2192      iter_ = accept_filters_.begin();\n   116\u2192    } else {\n   117\u2192      iter_ = std::next(iter_);\n   118\u2192    }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192    for (; iter_ != accept_filters_.end(); iter_++) {\n   121\u2192      Network::FilterStatus status = (*iter_)->onAccept(*this);\n   122\u2192      if (status == Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration) {\n   123\u2192        // The filter is responsible for calling us again at a later time to continue the filter\n   124\u2192        // chain from the next filter.\n   125\u2192        if (!socket().ioHandle().isOpen()) {\n   126\u2192          // Break the loop but should not create new connection.\n   127\u2192          no_error = false;\n   128\u2192          break;\n   129\u2192        } else {\n   130\u2192          // There are two cases for returning StopIteration. One is listener filter\n   131\u2192          // needs to wait for data. The other is listener filter with 0 maxReadBytes()\n   132\u2192          // doesn't inspect data, but needs to wait for some asynchronous callback and\n   133\u2192          // listener filter buffer needs to listen for socket close event.\n   134\u2192          if (listener_filter_buffer_ == nullptr) {\n   135\u2192            createListenerFilterBuffer();\n   136\u2192          }\n   137\u2192          if ((*iter_)->maxReadBytes() > 0) {\n   138\u2192            listener_filter_buffer_->disableOnDataCallback(false);\n   139\u2192            // If the current filter expect more data than previous filters, then\n   140\u2192            // increase the filter buffer's capacity.\n   141\u2192            if (listener_filter_buffer_->capacity() < (*iter_)->maxReadBytes()) {\n   142\u2192              listener_filter_buffer_->resetCapacity((*iter_)->maxReadBytes());\n   143\u2192            }\n   144\u2192            if (listener_filter_buffer_ != nullptr) {\n   145\u2192              // There are two cases for activate event manually: One is\n   146\u2192              // the data is already available when connect, activate the read event to peek\n   147\u2192              // data from the socket . Another one is the data already\n   148\u2192              // peeked into the buffer when previous filter processing the data, then activate the\n   149\u2192              // read event to trigger the current filter callback to process the data.\n   150\u2192              listener_filter_buffer_->activateFileEvent(Event::FileReadyType::Read);\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

recordConnectionsAcceptedOnSocketEvent(uint32_t connections_accepted) PURE;\n   335\u2192};\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192/**\n   338\u2192 * Utility struct that encapsulates the information from a udp socket's recvmmsg call.\n   339\u2192 */\n   340\u2192struct UdpRecvData {\n   341\u2192  struct LocalPeerAddresses {\n   342\u2192    bool operator==(const LocalPeerAddresses& rhs) const {\n 372\u2192  // The buffer is a reference so that it can be reused by the sender to send different\n   373\u2192  // messages\n   374\u2192  Buffer::Instance& buffer_;\n   375\u2192};\n   376\u2192\n   377\u2192/**\n   378\u2192 * UDP listener callbacks.\n   379\u2192 */\n   380\u2192class UdpListenerCallbacks {\n   381\u2192public:\n   382\u2192  virtual ~UdpListenerCallbacks() = default;\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  /**\n   385\u2192   * Called whenever data is received by the underlying udp socket.\n   386\u2192   * TODO(danzh2010): Consider returning a value to indicate if more work is "    75\u2192class FilterChainUtility {\n    76\u2192public:\n    77\u2192  /**\n    78\u2192   * Given a connection and a list of factories, create a new filter chain. Chain creation will\n    79\u2192   * exit early if any filters immediately close the connection.\n    80\u2192   */\n    81\u2192  static bool buildFilterChain(Network::FilterManager& filter_manager,\n    82\u2192                               const Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList& factories);\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192  /**\n    85\u2192   * Given a ListenerFilterManager and a list of factories, create a new filter chain. Chain\n    86\u2192   * creation will exit early if any filters immediately close the connection.\n    87\u2192   *\n    88\u2192   * TODO(sumukhs): Coalesce with the above as they are very similar\n    89\u2192   */\n    90\u2192  static bool buildFilterChain(Network::ListenerFilterManager& filter_manager,\n    91\u2192                               const Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList& factories);\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  /**\n    94\u2192   * Given a UdpListenerFilterManager and a list of factories, create a new filter chain. Chain\n    95\u2192   * creation will exit early if any filters immediately close the connection.\n    96\u2192   */\n    97\u2192  static void\n    98\u2192  buildUdpFilterChain(Network::UdpListenerFilterManager& filter_manager,\n    99\u2192                      Network::UdpReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks,\n   100\u2192                      const std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>& factories);\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  /**\n   103\u2192   * Given a QuicListenerFilterManager and a list of factories, create a new filter chain. Chain\n   104\u2192   * creation will exit early if any filters don't have a valid config.\n   105\u2192   *\n   106\u2192   * TODO(sumukhs): Coalesce with the above as they are very similar\n   107\u2192   */\n   108\u2192  static bool buildQuicFilterChain(Network::QuicListenerFilterManager& filter_manager,\n   109\u2192                                   const Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList& factories);\n   110\u2192};\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/**\n   113\u2192 * Implementation of Server::Configuration::Main that reads a configuration from\n   114\u2192 * a JSON file.\n   115\u2192 */\n   116\u2192class MainImpl : Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config>, public Main {\n   117\u2192public:\n   118\u2192  /**\n   119\u2192   * MainImpl is created in two phases. In the first phase it is\n   120\u2192   * default-constructed without a configuration as part of the server. The\n   121\u2192   * server won't be fully populated yet. initialize() applies the\n   122\u2192   * configuration in the second phase, as it requires a fully populated server.\n   123\u2192   *\n   124\u2192   * @param bootstrap v2 bootstrap proto.\n   125\u2192   * @param server supplies the owning server.\n   126\u2192   * @param cluster_manager_factory supplies the cluster manager creation factory.\n   127\u2192   * @return a status indicating initialization success or failure.\n   128\u2192   */\n   129\u2192  absl::Status initialize(const envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Bootstrap& bootstrap,\n   130\u2192                          Instance& server,\n   131\u2192                          Upstream::ClusterManagerFactory& cluster_manager_factory);\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192  // Server::Configuration::Main\n   134\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager* clusterManager() override { return cluster_manager_.get(); }\n   135\u2192  const Upstream::ClusterManager* clusterManager() const override { return cluster_manager_.get(); }\n   136\u2192  StatsConfig& statsConfig() override { return *stats_config_; }\n   137\u2192  const Watchdog& mainThreadWatchdogConfig() const override { return *main_thread_watchdog_; }\n   138\u2192  const Watchdog& workerWatchdogConfig() const override { return *worker_watchdog_; }\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192private:\n   141\u2192  /**\n   142\u2192   * Initialize tracers and corresponding sinks.\n   143\u2192   */\n   144\u2192  void initializeTracers(const envoy::config::trace::v3::Tracing& configuration, Instance& server);\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  /**\n   147\u2192   * Initialize stats configuration.\n   148\u2192   */\n   149\u2192  absl::Status initializeStatsConfig(const envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Bootstrap& bootstrap,\n   150\u2192                                     Instance& server);\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192  /**\n   153\u2192   * Initialize watchdog(s). Call before accessing any watchdog configuration.\n   154\u2192   */\n   155\u2192  absl::Status initializeWatchdogs(const envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Bootstrap& bootstrap,\n   156\u2192                                   Instance& server);\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Upstream::ClusterManager> cluster_manager_;\n   159\u2192  std::unique_ptr<StatsConfigImpl> stats_config_;\n   160\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Watchdog> main_thread_watchdog_;\n   161\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Watchdog> worker_watchdog_;\n   162\u2192};\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192class WatchdogImpl : public Watchdog {\n   165\u2192public:\n   166\u2192  WatchdogImpl(const envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Watchdog& watchdog, Instance& server);\n   167\u2192\n   168\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds missTimeout() const override { return miss_timeout_; }\n   169\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds megaMissTimeout() const override { return megamiss_timeout_; }\n   170\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds killTimeout() const override { return kill_timeout_; }\n   171\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds multiKillTimeout() const override { return multikill_timeout_; }\n   172\u2192  double multiKillThreshold() const override { return multikill_threshold_; }\n   173\u2192  Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Watchdog::WatchdogAction>\n   174\u2192  actions() const override {\n   175\u2192    return actions_;\n   176\u2192  }\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192private:\n   179\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds miss_timeout_;\n   180\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds megamiss_timeout_;\n   181\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds kill_timeout_;\n   182\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds multikill_timeout_;\n   183\u2192  double multikill_threshold_;\n   184\u2192  Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Watchdog::WatchdogAction> actions_;\n   185\u2192};\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192/**\n   188\u2192 * Initial configuration that reads from JSON.\n   189\u2192 */\n   190\u2192class InitialImpl : public Initial {\n   191\u2192public:\n   192\u2192  InitialImpl(const envoy::config::bootstrap::v3::Bootstrap& bootstrap,\n   193\u2192              absl::Status& creation_status);\n   194\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. {

Output
"   170\u2192  if (inserted()) {\n   171\u2192    unlink();\n   172\u2192  }\n   173\u2192}\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::setDynamicMetadata(const std::string& name,\n   176\u2192                                         const ProtobufWkt::Struct& value) {\n   177\u2192  stream_info_->setDynamicMetadata(name, value);\n   178\u2192}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::setDynamicTypedMetadata(const std::string& name,\n   181\u2192                                              const ProtobufWkt::Any& value) {\n   182\u2192  stream_info_->setDynamicTypedMetadata(name, value);\n   183\u2192}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192void ActiveTcpSocket::newConnection() {\n   186\u2192  connected_ = true;\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  // Check if the socket may need to be redirected to another listener.\n   189\u2192  Network::BalancedConnectionHandlerOptRef new_listener;\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192  if (hand_off_restored_destination_connections_ &&\n   192\u2192      socket_->connectionInfoProvider().localAddressRestored()) {\n   193\u2192    // Find a listener associated with the original destination address.\n   194\u2192    new_listener =\n   195\u2192        listener_.getBalancedHandlerByAddress(*socket_->connectionInfoProvider().localAddress());\n   196\u2192  }\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  // Reset the file events which are registered by listener filter.\n   199\u2192  // reference https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/8925.\n   200\u2192  if (listener_filter_buffer_ != nullptr) {\n   201\u2192    listener_filter_buffer_->reset();\n   202\u2192  }\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  if (new_listener.has_value()) {\n   205\u2192    // Hands off connections redirected by iptables to the listener associated with the\n   206\u2192    // original destination address. Pass 'hand_off_restored_destination_connections' as false to\n   207\u2192    // prevent further redirection.\n   208\u2192    // Leave the new listener to decide whether to execute re-balance.\n   209\u2192    // Note also that we must account for the number of connections properly across both listeners.\n   210\u2192    // TODO(mattklein123): See note in ~ActiveTcpSocket() related to making this accounting better.\n   211\u2192    listener_.decNumConnections();\n   212\u2192    new_listener.value().get().onAcceptWorker(std::move(socket_), false, false);\n   213\u2192  } else {\n   214\u2192    // Set default transport protocol if none of the listener filters did it.\n   215\u2192    if (socket_->detectedTransportProtocol().empty()) {\n   216\u2192      socket_->setDetectedTransportProtocol(\"raw_buffer\");\n   217\u2192    }\n   218\u2192    accept_filters_.clear();\n   219\u2192    // Create a new connection on this listener.\n   220\u2192    listener_.newConnection(std::move(socket_), std::move(stream_info_));\n   221\u2192  }\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192} // namespace Server\n   224\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   225\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
