59. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore HTTP parsing and filter chain flow",
  "prompt": "Explore the Envoy codebase to understand HTTP parsing and filter chain iteration:\n\n1. Find the HTTP codec implementation (HTTP/1, HTTP/2) and where onData() is called on the codec\n2. Find the ActiveStream class and how it's created when a request arrives\n3. Find the FilterManager class and how it iterates through decoder filters\n4. Find which filter return values stop or continue iteration\n5. Find where the codec parses bytes into HTTP headers/body\n\nSearch for:\n- ServerConnection and ServerConnectionCallbacks \n- HTTP codec classes (Http1ServerConnection, Http2ServerConnection)\n- FilterManager class in source/common/http/\n- ActiveStream creation flow when codec signals new stream\n- How decodeHeaders() and decodeData() flow through filters\n\nReturn specific file paths and class/method names with line numbers.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "ad616b05463f6f4b4",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! Now I have all the critical information. Let me create a comprehensive report:\n\n## Envoy HTTP Parsing and Filter Chain Iteration - Complete Analysis\n\nBased on my exploration of the Envoy codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how HTTP parsing and filter chain iteration works:\n\n### 1. HTTP Codec Implementation and onData() Entry Point\n\n**File: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` (lines 486-542)**\n\nThe entry point for HTTP parsing is:\n- **Method**: `ConnectionManagerImpl::onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool)`\n- **Line**: 486\n\nWhen data arrives on the network connection:\n```cpp\nNetwork::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool) {\n  // Line 503: Calls codec dispatch\n  const Status status = codec_->dispatch(data);\n```\n\nThe codec is created lazily on first data arrival (line 496) and then its `dispatch()` method is called repeatedly (line 503).\n\n### 2. HTTP/1.1 Codec Implementation\n\n**File: `/workspace/source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.h` (lines 465-583)**\n\n**HTTP/1 Server Codec Class**: `ServerConnectionImpl : public ServerConnection, public ConnectionImpl`\n- **Location**: Lines 465-583\n\nKey methods in HTTP/1 parsing flow:\n- **`dispatch(Buffer::Instance& data)`** (line 513) - Parses raw bytes using internal parser\n- **`onMessageBeginBase()`** (line 524) - Called when new HTTP message begins\n- **`onHeadersCompleteBase()`** (line 525) - Called after all headers parsed\n- **`onBody(Buffer::Instance& data)`** (line 528) - Called for request body chunks\n\n**Actual Implementation in .cc**: `/workspace/source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc`\n\n**Critical Flow**:\n1. Line 1266-1277: `onMessageBeginBase()` creates new `ActiveRequest` and calls `callbacks_.newStream()`:\n   ```cpp\n   active_request_->request_decoder_ = &callbacks_.newStream(active_request_->response_encoder_);\n   ```\n\n2. Line 1199-1264: `onHeadersCompleteBase()` calls `decodeHeaders()` on the request_decoder:\n   ```cpp\n   active_request_->request_decoder_->decodeHeaders(std::move(headers), false);\n   ```\n\n3. Line 1290-1296: `onBody()` calls `decodeData()` on request_decoder:\n   ```cpp\n   active_request_->request_decoder_->decodeData(data, false);\n   ```\n\n4. Line 1325-1353: `onMessageCompleteBase()` sends end-of-stream\n\n### 3. HTTP/2 Codec Implementation\n\n**File: `/workspace/source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.h` (lines 815-879)**\n\n**HTTP/2 Server Codec Classes**:\n- **`ServerConnectionImpl : public ServerConnection, public ConnectionImpl`** (lines 842-879)\n- Uses `Http2Visitor` callback interface (lines 191-258) which overrides `Http2VisitorInterface`\n\n**Key Visitor Methods**:\n- `OnBeginHeadersForStream()` (line 215) - Stream header parsing begins\n- `OnHeaderForStream()` (line 216) - Individual header received\n- `OnEndHeadersForStream()` (line 218) - Headers complete\n- `OnDataForStream()` (line 221) - Body data received\n- `OnEndStream()` (line 222) - Stream end signal\n\nThe visitor triggers decoder callbacks just like HTTP/1.\n\n### 4. ActiveStream Creation Flow\n\n**File: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h` (lines 134-532)**\n\n**ActiveStream Definition**: Lines 141-532 (private struct inside ConnectionManagerImpl)\n\n**Creation**: `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` (lines 387-442)\n\nThe `newStream()` method creates and initializes ActiveStream:\n```cpp\nRequestDecoder& ConnectionManagerImpl::newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n                                                 bool is_internally_created) {\n  // Line 407: Create ActiveStream\n  auto new_stream = std::make_unique<ActiveStream>(\n      *this, response_encoder.getStream().bufferLimit(), std::move(downstream_stream_account));\n  \n  // Line 431-434: Set up callbacks and encoder\n  new_stream->response_encoder_ = &response_encoder;\n  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().addCallbacks(*new_stream);\n  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().registerCodecEventCallbacks(new_stream.get());\n  \n  // Line 440-441: Add to active streams list\n  LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(new_stream), streams_);\n  return **streams_.begin();\n}\n```\n\n**ActiveStream Implements**:\n- `Http::RequestDecoder` - receives decoded headers/data/trailers from codec\n- `Http::StreamCallbacks` - stream lifecycle callbacks\n- `Http::CodecEventCallbacks` - codec-level events\n- `FilterManagerCallbacks` - interface to FilterManager\n\n### 5. FilterManager and Filter Chain Iteration\n\n**File: `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` (lines 34-1090)**\n\n**Key Classes**:\n\n1. **`StreamDecoderFilters`** (lines 73-81): Container for decoder filters\n   ```cpp\n   using Element = ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;\n   using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr>::iterator;\n   std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n   ```\n\n2. **`StreamEncoderFilters`** (lines 92-99): Container for encoder filters (reverse iteration)\n   ```cpp\n   using Element = ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;\n   using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator;\n   ```\n\n3. **`FilterManager` Main Class** (lines 600-1090): Orchestrates filter chain iteration\n\n4. **`ActiveStreamDecoderFilter`** (lines 240-330): Wraps each decoder filter\n   ```cpp\n   struct ActiveStreamDecoderFilter : public ActiveStreamFilterBase,\n                                      public StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks {\n     StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr handle_;\n     StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry_{};\n   };\n   ```\n\n5. **`ActiveStreamEncoderFilter`** (lines 335-387): Wraps each encoder filter\n\n6. **`ActiveStreamFilterBase`** (lines 105-237): Base class with iteration state management\n   ```cpp\n   enum class IterationState : uint8_t {\n     Continue,            // Iteration not stopped\n     StopSingleIteration, // Stop for current frame type\n     StopAllBuffer,       // Stop all, buffer data\n     StopAllWatermark,    // Stop all, watermark flow control\n   };\n   ```\n\n### 6. Decoder Filter Chain Iteration\n\n**File**: `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc`\n\n**Method**: `FilterManager::decodeHeaders()` (lines 537-636)\n- **Entry Point**: Called from `ActiveStream::decodeHeaders()` when codec signals new headers\n- **Filter Loop**: Lines 547-628 - iterates through `decoder_filters_`\n- **Key Line 555**: Calls filter's `decodeHeaders()` method\n- **Status Handling**: Line 577 calls `commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback()` to process return status\n\n**Method**: `FilterManager::decodeData()` (lines 638-762)\n- **Entry Point**: Called from `ActiveStream::decodeData()` when codec signals body data\n- **Filter Loop**: Lines 658-750 - iterates through decoder filters\n- **Key Line 710**: Calls filter's `decodeData()` method\n- **Status Handling**: Line 743 calls `commonHandleAfterDataCallback()` to process return status\n\n### 7. Filter Return Values and Iteration Control\n\n**File**: `/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h` (lines 38-165)**\n\n**FilterHeadersStatus Return Values** (lines 38-110):\n1. **`Continue`** - Continue to next filter\n2. **`StopIteration`** - Stop headers iteration for remaining filters (line 49)\n3. **`StopAllIterationAndBuffer`** - Stop all iteration, buffer body data (line 98)\n4. **`StopAllIterationAndWatermark`** - Stop all, apply flow control watermarks (line 109)\n5. **`ContinueAndDontEndStream`** - Continue but don't mark end_stream as true (line 86)\n\n**FilterDataStatus Return Values** (lines 129-165):\n1. **`Continue`** - Continue to next filter\n2. **`StopIterationAndBuffer`** - Stop data iteration, buffer for later (line 144)\n3. **`StopIterationAndWatermark`** - Stop data iteration, apply watermarks (line 155)\n4. **`StopIterationNoBuffer`** - Stop iteration, don't buffer (line 164)\n\n**Iteration Control in FilterManager** (filter_manager.cc lines 130-162):\n\nThe `commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback()` method processes return values:\n```cpp\nbool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status,\n                                                              bool& end_stream) {\n  switch (status) {\n  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n    break;\n  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer:\n    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllBuffer;\n    break;\n  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark:\n    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n    break;\n  case FilterHeadersStatus::Continue:\n    headers_continued_ = true;\n    break;\n  }\n  \n  if (stoppedAll() || status == FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration) {\n    return false;  // Stop iteration\n  } else {\n    return true;   // Continue iteration\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 8. HTTP Header/Body Parsing at Codec Level\n\n**HTTP/1 Parsing**: `/workspace/source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc`\n\n- Lines 738-759: Parser callbacks (`onMessageBegin`, `onHeadersComplete`)\n- Parser is set up in `ConnectionImpl` base class (line 304): `std::unique_ptr<Parser> parser_;`\n- Parser uses either Balsa parser or legacy parser to extract:\n  - Request line (method, URL, version)\n  - Headers (field-value pairs)\n  - Body (chunked or content-length)\n\n**HTTP/2 Parsing**: `/workspace/source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc`\n\n- Uses nghttp2 or oghttp2 library for frame parsing\n- `Http2Visitor::OnHeaderForStream()` (line 216) accumulates headers\n- `Http2Visitor::OnDataForStream()` (line 221) receives body frames\n\n### 9. Code File Summary\n\n**Critical Files and Line Numbers**:\n\n| Component | File | Key Lines |\n|-----------|------|-----------|\n| Entry Point (onData) | `source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` | 486-542 |\n| Codec Dispatch (HTTP/1) | `source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc` | 1266-1294 |\n| Codec Dispatch (HTTP/2) | `source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.h` | 191-258 |\n| ActiveStream Creation | `source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc` | 387-442 |\n| ActiveStream Definition | `source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.h` | 141-532 |\n| FilterManager Header Iteration | `source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` | 537-636 |\n| FilterManager Data Iteration | `source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` | 638-762 |\n| Filter Wrappers | `source/common/http/filter_manager.h` | 240-387 |\n| Filter Status Enums | `envoy/http/filter.h` | 38-165 |\n| Iteration State Control | `source/common/http/filter_manager.h` | 211-223 |\n| ActiveStreamFilterBase | `source/common/http/filter_manager.h` | 105-237 |\n\n### 10. Data Flow Summary\n\n```\nNetwork Data\n    \u2193\nConnectionManagerImpl::onData() [conn_manager_impl.cc:486]\n    \u2193\ncodec_->dispatch(data) [conn_manager_impl.cc:503]\n    \u2193\nHttp1/Http2::onMessageBegin() [http1/codec_impl.cc:1266 or http2 visitor]\n    \u2193\ncallbacks_.newStream() [http1/codec_impl.cc:1270]\n    \u2193\nConnectionManagerImpl::newStream() [conn_manager_impl.cc:387]\n    \u2193\nCreates ActiveStream [conn_manager_impl.cc:407]\n    \u2193\nHttp1/Http2::onHeadersComplete() [http1/codec_impl.cc:1251]\n    \u2193\nrequest_decoder_->decodeHeaders() [http1/codec_impl.cc:1251]\n    \u2193\nActiveStream::decodeHeaders() [conn_manager_impl.h:184]\n    \u2193\nFilterManager::decodeHeaders() [filter_manager.cc:537]\n    \u2193\nIterates through decoder_filters_ [filter_manager.cc:547]\n    \u2193\nCalls filter->decodeHeaders() [filter_manager.cc:555]\n    \u2193\nHandles return status [filter_manager.cc:577]\n    \u2193\n(Same pattern for decodeData and decodeTrailers)\n```\n\nThis comprehensive mapping shows exactly where bytes are parsed into HTTP structures and how they flow through the filter chain with full control over iteration based on filter return values.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Envoy codebase to understand HTTP parsing and filter chain iteration:\n\n1. Find the HTTP codec implementation (HTTP/1, HTTP/2) and where onData() is called on the codec\n2. Find the ActiveStream class and how it's created when a request arrives\n3. Find the FilterManager class and how it iterates through decoder filters\n4. Find which filter return values stop or continue iteration\n5. Find where the codec parses bytes into HTTP headers/body\n\nSearch for:\n- ServerConnection and ServerConnectionCallbacks \n- HTTP codec classes (Http1ServerConnection, Http2ServerConnection)\n- FilterManager class in source/common/http/\n- ActiveStream creation flow when codec signals new stream\n- How decodeHeaders() and decodeData() flow through filters\n\nReturn specific file paths and class/method names with line numbers.",

generateStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope);\n    76\u2192  static ConnectionManagerTracingStats generateTracingStats(const std::string& prefix,\n    77\u2192                        Stats::Scope& scope);\n    82\u2192  static const ResponseHeaderMap& continueHeader();\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192  // Currently the ConnectionManager creates a codec lazily when either:\n    85\u2192  //   a) onConnection for H3.\n    86\u2192  //   b) onData for H1 and H2.\n    87\u2192  // With the introduction of ApiListeners, neither event occurs. This function allows consumer code\n    88\u2192  // to manually create a codec.\n    89\u2192  // TODO(junr03): consider passing a synthetic codec instead of creating once. The codec in the\n    90\u2192  // ApiListener case is solely used to determine the protocol version.\n    91\u2192  void createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data);\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  // Network::ReadFilter\n    94\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    95\u2192  Network::FilterStatus onNewConnection() override;\n    96\u2192  void initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  // Http::ConnectionCallbacks\n    99\u2192  void onGoAway(GoAwayErrorCode error_code) override;\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192  // Http::ServerConnectionCallbacks\n   102\u2192  RequestDecoder& newStream(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   103\u2192                            bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192  RequestDecoderHandlePtr newStreamHandle(ResponseEncoder& response_encoder,\n   106\u2192                                          bool is_internally_created = false) override;\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n   109\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n   110\u2192  // Pass connection watermark events on to all the streams associated with that connection.\n   111\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {\n   112\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark();\n   113\u2192  }\n   114\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {\n   115\u2192    codec_->onUnderlyingConnectionBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark();\n   116\u2192  }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  TimeSource& timeSource() { return time_source_; }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192  void setClearHopByHopResponseHeaders(bool value) { clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_ = value; }\n   121\u2192  bool clearHopByHopResponseHeaders() const { return clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_; }\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192  // This runtime key configures the number of streams which must be closed on a connection before\n   124\u2192  // envoy will potentially drain a connection due to excessive prematurely reset streams.\n   125\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetTotalStreamCountKey;\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  // The minimum lifetime of a stream, in seconds, in order not to be considered\n   128\u2192  // prematurely closed.\n   129\u2192  static const absl::string_view PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey;\n   130\u2192  static const absl::string_view MaxRequestsPerIoCycle;\n   131\u2192  static const absl::string_view OptionallyDelayClose;\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192private:\n   134\u2192  struct ActiveStream;\n   135\u2192  class MobileConnectionManagerImpl;\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  /**\n   138\u2192   * Wraps a single active stream on the connection. These are either full request/response pairs\n   139\u2192   * or pushes.\n   140\u2192   */\n   141\u2192  struct ActiveStream final : LinkedObject<ActiveStream>,\n   142\u2192                              public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n   143\u2192                              public StreamCallbacks,\n   144\u2192                              public CodecEventCallbacks,\n   145\u2192                              public RequestDecoder,\n   146\u2192                              public Tracing::Config,\n   147\u2192                              public ScopeTrackedObject,\n   148\u2192                              public FilterManagerCallbacks,\n   149\u2192                              public DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks,\n   150\u2192                              public RouteCache {\n   151\u2192    ActiveStream(ConnectionManagerImpl& connection_manager, uint32_t buffer_limit,\n   152\u2192                 Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account);\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    // Event::DeferredDeletable\n   155\u2192    void deleteIsPending() override {\n   156\u2192      // The stream should not be accessed once deferred delete has been called.\n   157\u2192      still_alive_.reset();\n   158\u2192    }\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192    void log(AccessLog::AccessLogType type);\n   161\u2192    void completeRequest();\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192    const Network::Connection* connection();\n   164\u2192    uint64_t streamId() { return stream_id_; }\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192    // Http::StreamCallbacks\n   167\u2192    void onResetStream(StreamResetReason reason,\n   168\u2192                       absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   169\u2192    void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   170\u2192    void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    // Http::CodecEventCallbacks\n   173\u2192    void onCodecEncodeComplete() override;\n   174\u2192    void onCodecLowLevelReset() override;\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    // Http::StreamDecoder\n   177\u2192    void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   178\u2192    void decodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&&) override;\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192    // Record the timestamp of last downstream byte is received.\n   181\u2192    void maybeRecordLastByteReceived(bool end_stream);\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    // Http::RequestDecoder\n   184\u2192    void decodeHeaders(RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   185\u2192    void decodeTrailers(RequestTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n   186\u2192    StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() override { return filter_manager_.streamInfo(); }\n   187\u2192    void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   188\u2192                        const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n   189\u2192                        const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   190\u2192                        absl::string_view details) override {\n   191\u2192      return filter_manager_.sendLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, grpc_status, details);\n   192\u2192    }\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192    void sendGoAwayAndClose() override { return connection_manager_.sendGoAwayAndClose(); }\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector accessLogHandlers() override {\n   197\u2192      const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& config_log_handlers =\n   198\u2192          connection_manager_.config_->accessLogs();\n   199\u2192      const AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector& filter_log_handlers =\n   200\u2192          filter_manager_.accessLogHandlers();\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192      AccessLog::InstanceSharedPtrVector combined_log_handlers;\n   203\u2192      combined_log_handlers.reserve(config_log_handlers.size() + filter_log_handlers.size());\n   204\u2192\n   205\u2192      if (!Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   206\u2192              \"envoy.reloadable_features.filter_access_loggers_first\")) {\n   207\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), filter_log_handlers.begin(),\n   208\u2192                                     filter_log_handlers.end());\n   209\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), config_log_handlers.begin(),\n   210\u2192                                     config_log_handlers.end());\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192      } else {\n   213\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), config_log_handlers.begin(),\n   214\u2192                                     config_log_handlers.end());\n   215\u2192        combined_log_handlers.insert(combined_log_handlers.end(), filter_log_handlers.begin(),\n   216\u2192                                     filter_log_handlers.end());\n   217\u2192      }\n   218\u2192      return combined_log_handlers;\n   219\u2192    }\n   220\u2192    // Hand off headers/trailers and stream info to the codec's response encoder, for logging later\n   221\u2192    // (i.e. possibly after this stream has been destroyed).\n   222\u2192    //\n   223\u2192    // TODO(paulsohn): Investigate whether we can move the headers/trailers and stream info required\n   224\u2192    // for logging instead of copying them (as is currently done in the HTTP/3 implementation) or\n   225\u2192    // using a shared pointer. See\n   226\u2192    // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/pull/23648#discussion_r1066095564 for more details.\n   227\u2192    void deferHeadersAndTrailers() {\n   228\u2192      response_encoder_->setDeferredLoggingHeadersAndTrailers(request_headers_, response_headers_,\n   229\u2192                                                              response_trailers_, streamInfo());\n   230\u2192    }\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192    // ScopeTrackedObject\n   233\u2192    OptRef<const StreamInfo::StreamInfo> trackedStream() const override {\n   234\u2192      return filter_manager_.trackedStream();\n   235\u2192    }\n   236\u2192    void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const override {\n   237\u2192      const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);\n   238\u2192      os << spaces << \"ActiveStream \" << this << DUMP_MEMBER(stream_id_);\n   239\u2192\n   240\u2192      DUMP_DETAILS(&filter_manager_);\n   241\u2192    }\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192    // FilterManagerCallbacks\n   244\u2192    void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   245\u2192    void encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) override;\n   246\u2192    void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   247\u2192    void encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) override;\n   248\u2192    void encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata) override;\n   249\u2192    void setRequestTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMapPtr&& request_trailers) override {\n   250\u2192      ASSERT(!request_trailers_);\n   251\u2192      request_trailers_ = std::move(request_trailers);\n   252\u2192    }\n   253\u2192    void setInformationalHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& informational_headers) override {\n   254\u2192      ASSERT(!informational_headers_);\n   255\u2192      informational_headers_ = std::move(informational_headers);\n   256\u2192    }\n   257\u2192    void setResponseHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) override {\n   258\u2192      // We'll overwrite the headers in the case where we fail the stream after upstream headers\n   259\u2192      // have begun filter processing but before they have been sent downstream.\n   260\u2192      response_headers_ = std::move(response_headers);\n   261\u2192    }\n   262\u2192    void setResponseTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& response_trailers) override {\n   263\u2192      response_trailers_ = std::move(response_trailers);\n   264\u2192    }\n   265\u2192    void chargeStats(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers) override;\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192    Http::RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() override {\n   268\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(request_headers_.get());\n   269\u2192    }\n   270\u2192    Http::RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() override {\n   271\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(request_trailers_.get());\n   272\u2192    }\n   273\u2192    Http::ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() override {\n   274\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(informational_headers_.get());\n   275\u2192    }\n   276\u2192    Http::ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() override {\n   277\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(response_headers_.get());\n   278\u2192    }\n   279\u2192    Http::ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() override {\n   280\u2192      return makeOptRefFromPtr(response_trailers_.get());\n   281\u2192    }\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192    void endStream() override {\n   284\u2192      ASSERT(!state_.codec_saw_local_complete_);\n   285\u2192      state_.codec_saw_local_complete_ = true;\n   286\u2192      connection_manager_.doEndStream(*this);\n   287\u2192    }\n   288\u2192    void onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   289\u2192    void onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   290\u2192    void disarmRequestTimeout() override;\n   291\u2192    void resetIdleTimer() override;\n   292\u2192    void recreateStream(StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr filter_state) override;\n   293\u2192    void resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason = Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset,\n   294\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason = \"\") override;\n   295\u2192    const Router::RouteEntry::UpgradeMap* upgradeMap() override;\n   296\u2192    Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() override;\n   297\u2192    Tracing::Span& activeSpan() override;\n   298\u2192    void onResponseDataTooLarge() override;\n   299\u2192    void onRequestDataTooLarge() override;\n   300\u2192    Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() override;\n   301\u2192    void onLocalReply(Code code) override;\n   302\u2192    OptRef<const Tracing::Config> tracingConfig() const override;\n   303\u2192    const ScopeTrackedObject& scope() override;\n   304\u2192    OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstreamCallbacks() override { return *this; }\n   305\u2192    bool isHalfCloseEnabled() override { return connection_manager_.allow_upstream_half_close_; }\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192    // DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks\n   308\u2192    void setRoute(Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route) override;\n   309\u2192    Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route(const Router::RouteCallback& cb) override;\n   310\u2192    void clearRouteCache() override;\n   311\u2192    void requestRouteConfigUpdate(\n   312\u2192        Http::RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr route_config_updated_cb) override;\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192    // Set cached route. This method should never be called directly. This is only called in the\n   315\u2192    // setRoute(), clearRouteCache(), and refreshCachedRoute() methods.\n   316\u2192    void setCachedRoute(absl::optional<Router::RouteConstSharedPtr>&& route);\n   317\u2192    // Block the route cache and clear the snapped route config. By doing this the route cache will\n   318\u2192    // not be updated. And if the route config is updated by the RDS, the snapped route config may\n   319\u2192    // be freed before the stream is destroyed.\n   320\u2192    // This will be called automatically at the end of handle response headers.\n   321\u2192    void blockRouteCache();\n   322\u2192    // Return true if the cached route is blocked.\n   323\u2192    bool routeCacheBlocked() const {\n   324\u2192      ENVOY_BUG(!route_cache_blocked_, \"Should never try to refresh or clear the route cache when \"\n   325\u2192                                       \"it is blocked!\");\n   326\u2192      return route_cache_blocked_;\n   327\u2192    }\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192    absl::optional<Router::ConfigConstSharedPtr> routeConfig();\n   330\u2192    void traceRequest();\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192    // Updates the snapped_route_config_ (by reselecting scoped route configuration), if a scope is\n   333\u2192    // not found, snapped_route_config_ is set to Router::NullConfigImpl.\n   334\u2192    void snapScopedRouteConfig();\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192    void refreshCachedRoute(const Router::RouteCallback& cb);\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192    void refreshCachedTracingCustomTags();\n   339\u2192    void refreshDurationTimeout();\n   340\u2192    void refreshIdleTimeout();\n   341\u2192    void refreshAccessLogFlushTimer();\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192    // All state for the stream. Put here for readability.\n   344\u2192    struct State {\n   345\u2192      State()\n   346\u2192          : codec_saw_local_complete_(false), codec_encode_complete_(false),\n   347\u2192            on_reset_stream_called_(false), is_zombie_stream_(false), successful_upgrade_(false),\n   348\u2192            is_internally_destroyed_(false), is_internally_created_(false), is_tunneling_(false),\n   349\u2192            decorated_propagate_(true), deferred_to_next_io_iteration_(false),\n   350\u2192            deferred_end_stream_(false) {}\n   351\u2192\n   352\u2192      // It's possibly for the codec to see the completed response but not fully\n   353\u2192      // encode it.\n   354\u2192      bool codec_saw_local_complete_ : 1; // This indicates that local is complete as the completed\n   355\u2192                                          // response has made its way to the codec.\n   356\u2192      bool codec_encode_complete_ : 1;    // This indicates that the codec has\n   357\u2192                                          // completed encoding the response.\n   358\u2192      bool on_reset_stream_called_ : 1;   // Whether the stream has been reset.\n   359\u2192      bool is_zombie_stream_ : 1;         // Whether stream is waiting for signal\n   360\u2192                                          // the underlying codec to be destroyed.\n   361\u2192      bool successful_upgrade_ : 1;\n   362\u2192\n   363\u2192      // True if this stream was the original externally created stream, but was\n   364\u2192      // destroyed as part of internal redirect.\n   365\u2192      bool is_internally_destroyed_ : 1;\n   366\u2192      // True if this stream is internally created. Currently only used for\n   367\u2192      // internal redirects or other streams created via recreateStream().\n   368\u2192      bool is_internally_created_ : 1;\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192      // True if the response headers indicate a successful upgrade or connect\n   371\u2192      // response.\n   372\u2192      bool is_tunneling_ : 1;\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192      bool decorated_propagate_ : 1;\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192      // Indicates that sending headers to the filter manager is deferred to the\n   377\u2192      // next I/O cycle. If data or trailers are received when this flag is set\n   378\u2192      // they are deferred too.\n   379\u2192      // TODO(yanavlasov): encapsulate the entire state of deferred streams into a separate\n   380\u2192      // structure, so it can be atomically created and cleared.\n   381\u2192      bool deferred_to_next_io_iteration_ : 1;\n   382\u2192      bool deferred_end_stream_ : 1;\n   383\u2192    };\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192    bool canDestroyStream() const {\n   386\u2192      return state_.on_reset_stream_called_ || state_.codec_encode_complete_ ||\n   387\u2192             state_.is_internally_destroyed_;\n   388\u2192    }\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192    // Per-stream idle timeout callback.\n   391\u2192    void onIdleTimeout();\n   392\u2192    // Per-stream request timeout callback.\n   393\u2192    void onRequestTimeout();\n   394\u2192    // Per-stream request header timeout callback.\n   395\u2192    void onRequestHeaderTimeout();\n   396\u2192    // Per-stream alive duration reached.\n   397\u2192    void onStreamMaxDurationReached();\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192    // RouteCache\n   400\u2192    bool hasCachedRoute() const override {\n   401\u2192      return cached_route_.has_value() && cached_route_.value();\n   402\u2192    }\n   403\u2192    void refreshCachedRoute() override;\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192    // Return local port of the connection.\n   406\u2192    uint32_t localPort();\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192    friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const ActiveStream& s) {\n   409\u2192      s.dumpState(os);\n   410\u2192      return os;\n   411\u2192    }\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192    Tracing::CustomTagMap& getOrMakeTracingCustomTagMap() {\n   414\u2192      if (tracing_custom_tags_ == nullptr) {\n   415\u2192        tracing_custom_tags_ = std::make_unique<Tracing::CustomTagMap>();\n   416\u2192      }\n   417\u2192      return *tracing_custom_tags_;\n   418\u2192    }\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192    // Note: this method is a noop unless ENVOY_ENABLE_UHV is defined\n   421\u2192    // Call header validator extension to validate request header map after it was deserialized.\n   422\u2192    // If header map failed validation, it sends an error response and returns false.\n   423\u2192    bool validateHeaders();\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192    // Note: this method is a noop unless ENVOY_ENABLE_UHV is defined\n   426\u2192    // Call header validator extension to validate the request trailer map after it was\n   427\u2192    // deserialized. If the trailer map failed validation, this method does the following:\n   428\u2192    // 1. For H/1 it sends 400 response and returns false.\n   429\u2192    // 2. For H/2 and H/3 it resets the stream (without error response). Issue #24735 is filed to\n   430\u2192    //    harmonize this behavior with H/1.\n   431\u2192    // 3. If the `stream_error_on_invalid_http_message` is set to `false` (it is by default) in the\n   432\u2192    // HTTP connection manager configuration, then the entire connection is closed.\n   433\u2192    bool validateTrailers(RequestTrailerMap& trailers);\n   434\u2192\n   435\u2192    std::weak_ptr<bool> stillAlive() { return {still_alive_}; }\n   436\u2192\n   437\u2192    // Dispatch deferred headers, body and trailers to the filter manager.\n   438\u2192    // Return true if this stream was deferred and dispatched pending headers, body and trailers (if\n   439\u2192    // present). Return false if this stream was not deferred.\n   440\u2192    bool onDeferredRequestProcessing();\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192    ConnectionManagerImpl& connection_manager_;\n   443\u2192    OptRef<const TracingConnectionManagerConfig> connection_manager_tracing_config_;\n   444\u2192    // TODO(snowp): It might make sense to move this to the FilterManager to avoid storing it in\n   445\u2192    // both locations, then refer to the FM when doing stream logs.\n   446\u2192    const uint64_t stream_id_;\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192    RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr request_headers_;\n   449\u2192    RequestTrailerMapPtr request_trailers_;\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192    ResponseHeaderMapPtr informational_headers_;\n   452\u2192    ResponseHeaderMapSharedPtr response_headers_;\n   453\u2192    ResponseTrailerMapSharedPtr response_trailers_;\n   454\u2192\n   455\u2192    // Note: The FM must outlive the above headers, as they are possibly accessed during filter\n   456\u2192    // destruction.\n   457\u2192    DownstreamFilterManager filter_manager_;\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192    Tracing::SpanPtr active_span_;\n   460\u2192    ResponseEncoder* response_encoder_{};\n   461\u2192    Stats::TimespanPtr request_response_timespan_;\n   462\u2192    // Per-stream idle timeout. This timer gets reset whenever activity occurs on the stream, and,\n   463\u2192    // when triggered, will close the stream.\n   464\u2192    Event::TimerPtr stream_idle_timer_;\n   465\u2192    // Per-stream request timeout. This timer is enabled when the stream is created and disabled\n   466\u2192    // when the stream ends. If triggered, it will close the stream.\n   467\u2192    Event::TimerPtr request_timer_;\n   468\u2192    // Per-stream request header timeout. This timer is enabled when the stream is created and\n   469\u2192    // disabled when the downstream finishes sending headers. If triggered, it will close the\n   470\u2192    // stream.\n   471\u2192    Event::TimerPtr request_header_timer_;\n   472\u2192    // Per-stream alive duration. This timer is enabled once when the stream is created and, if\n   473\u2192    // triggered, will close the stream.\n   474\u2192    Event::TimerPtr max_stream_duration_timer_;\n   475\u2192    // Per-stream access log flush duration. This timer is enabled once when the stream is created\n   476\u2192    // and will log to all access logs once per trigger.\n   477\u2192    Event::TimerPtr access_log_flush_timer_;\n   478\u2192\n   479\u2192    std::chrono::milliseconds idle_timeout_ms_{};\n   480\u2192    State state_;\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192    // Snapshot of the route configuration at the time of request is started. This is used to ensure\n   483\u2192    // that the same route configuration is used throughout the lifetime of the request. This\n   484\u2192    // snapshot will be cleared when the cached route is blocked. Because after that we will not\n   485\u2192    // refresh the cached route and release this snapshot can help to release the memory when the\n   486\u2192    // route configuration is updated frequently and the request is long-lived.\n   487\u2192    Router::ConfigConstSharedPtr snapped_route_config_;\n   488\u2192    Router::ScopedConfigConstSharedPtr snapped_scoped_routes_config_;\n   489\u2192    // This is used to track the route that has been cached in the request. And we will keep this\n   490\u2192    // route alive until the request is finished.\n   491\u2192    absl::optional<Router::RouteConstSharedPtr> cached_route_;\n   492\u2192    // This is used to track whether the route has been blocked. If the route is blocked, we can not\n   493\u2192    // clear it or refresh it.\n   494\u2192    bool route_cache_blocked_{false};\n   495\u2192    // This is used to track routes that have been cleared from the request. By this way, all the\n   496\u2192    // configurations that have been used in the processing of the request will be alive until the\n   497\u2192    // request is finished.\n   498\u2192    // For example, if a filter stored a per-route config in the decoding phase and may try to\n   499\u2192    // use it in the encoding phase, but the route is cleared and refreshed by another decoder\n   500\u2192    // filter, we must keep the per-route config alive to avoid use-after-free.\n   501\u2192    // Note that we assume that the number of routes that have been cleared is small. So we use\n   502\u2192    // inline vector to avoid heap allocation. If this assumption is wrong, we should consider using\n   503\u2192    // a list or other data structures.\n   504\u2192    //\n   505\u2192    // TODO(wbpcode): This is a helpless compromise. To avoid exposing the complexity of the route\n   506\u2192    // lifetime management to every HTTP filter, we do a hack here. But if every filter could manage\n   507\u2192    // the lifetime of the route config by itself easily, we could remove this hack.\n   508\u2192    absl::InlinedVector<Router::RouteConstSharedPtr, 3> cleared_cached_routes_;\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192    absl::optional<Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr> cached_cluster_info_;\n   511\u2192    const std::string* decorated_operation_{nullptr};\n stream_;\n   553\u2192  };\n   554\u2192\n   555\u2192  class HttpStreamIdProviderImpl : public StreamInfo::StreamIdProvider {\n   556\u2192  public:\n   557\u2192 567\u2192   * Check to see if the connection can be closed after gracefully waiting to send pending codec\n   568\u2192   * data.\n   569\u2192   */\n   570\u2192  void checkForDeferredClose(bool Also calls onDestroy() for\n   574\u2192   * each filter.\n   575\u2192   */\n   576\u2192  void doDeferredStreamDestroy(ActiveStream& stream);\n   577\u2192\n   578\u2192  /**\n   579\u2192   * Process a stream that is ending due to upstream response or reset.\n   580\u2192   * If check_for_deferred_close is true, the ConnectionManager will check to\n   581\u2192   * see if the connection was drained and should be closed if no streams remain.\n   582\u2192   */\n   583\u2192  void doEndStream(ActiveStream& stream, bool check_for_deferred_close = true);\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192  void resetAllStreams(absl::optional<StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag> response_flag,\n   586\u2192                       absl::string_view details);\n   587\u2192  void onIdleTimeout();\n   588\u2192  void onConnectionDurationTimeout();\n   589\u2192  void onDrainTimeout();\n   590\u2192  void startDrainSequence();\n   591\u2192  Tracing::Tracer& tracer() { return *config_->tracer(); }\n   592\u2192  void handleCodecErrorImpl(absl::string_view error, absl::string_view details,\n   593\u2192                            StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag response_flag);\n   594\u2192  void handleCodecError(absl::string_view error);\n   595\u2192  void handleCodecOverloadError(absl::string_view error);\n   596\u2192  void doConnectionClose(absl::optional<Network::ConnectionCloseType> close_type,\n   597\u2192                         absl::optional<StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag> response_flag,\n   598\u2192                         absl::string_view details);\n   599\u2192  void sendGoAwayAndClose();\n   600\u2192\n   601\u2192  // Returns true if a RST_STREAM for the given stream is premature. Premature\n   602\u2192  // means the RST_STREAM arrived before response headers were sent and than\n   603\u2192  // the stream was alive for short period of time. This period is specified\n   604\u2192  // by the optional runtime value PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey,\n   605\u2192  // or one second if that is not present.\n   606\u2192  bool isPrematureRstStream(const ActiveStream& stream) const;\n   607\u2192  // Sends a GOAWAY if both sufficient streams have been closed on a connection\n   608\u2192  // and at least half have been prematurely reset?\n   609\u2192  void maybeDrainDueToPrematureResets();\n   610\u2192\n   611\u2192  bool shouldDeferRequestProxyingToNextIoCycle();\n   612\u2192  void onDeferredRequestProcessing();\n   613\u2192\n   614\u2192  enum class DrainState { NotDraining, Draining, Closing };\n   615\u2192\n   616\u2192  ConnectionManagerConfigSharedPtr config_;\n   617\u2192  ConnectionManagerStats& stats_; // We store a reference here to avoid an extra stats() call on\n   618\u2192                                  // the config in the hot path.\n   619\u2192  ServerConnectionPtr codec_;\n   620\u2192  std::list<ActiveStreamPtr> streams_;\n   621\u2192  Stats::TimespanPtr conn_length_;\n   622\u2192  const Network::DrainDecision& drain_close_;\n   623\u2192  DrainState drain_state_{DrainState::NotDraining};\n   624\u2192  UserAgent user_agent_;\n   625\u2192  // An idle timer for the connection. This is only armed when there are no streams on the\n   626\u2192  // connection. When there are active streams it is disarmed in favor of each stream's\n   627\u2192  // stream_idle_timer_.\n   628\u2192  Event::TimerPtr connection_idle_timer_;\n   629\u2192  // A connection duration timer. Armed during handling new connection if enabled in config.\n   630\u2192  Event::TimerPtr connection_duration_timer_;\n   631\u2192  Event::TimerPtr drain_timer_;\n   632\u2192  // When set to true, add Connection:close response header to nudge downstream client to reconnect.\n   633\u2192  bool soft_drain_http1_{false};\n   634\u2192  Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator_;\n   635\u2192  Runtime::Loader& runtime_;\n   636\u2192  const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info_;\n   637\u2192  Upstream::ClusterManager& cluster_manager_;\n   638\u2192  Network::ReadFilterCallbacks* read_callbacks_{};\n   639\u2192  Event::Dispatcher* dispatcher_{};\n   640\u2192  ConnectionManagerListenerStats& listener_stats_;\n   641\u2192  Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager_;\n   642\u2192  Server::ThreadLocalOverloadState& overload_state_;\n   643\u2192  Server::LoadShedPoint* accept_new_http_stream_{nullptr};\n   644\u2192  Server::LoadShedPoint* hcm_ondata_creating_codec_{nullptr};\n   645\u2192  // References into the overload manager thread local state map. Using these lets us avoid a\n   646\u2192  // map lookup in the hot path of processing each request.\n   647\u2192  const Server::OverloadActionState& overload_stop_accepting_requests_ref_;\n   648\u2192  const Server::OverloadActionState& overload_disable_keepalive_ref_;\n   649\u2192  TimeSource& time_source_;\n   650\u2192  bool go_away_sent_{false};\n   651\u2192  bool remote_close_{};\n   652\u2192  // Hop by hop headers should always be cleared for Envoy-as-a-proxy but will\n   653\u2192  // not be for Envoy-mobile.\n   654\u2192  bool clear_hop_by_hop_response_headers_{true};\n   655\u2192  // The number of requests accumulated on the current connection.\n   656\u2192  uint64_t accumulated_requests_{};\n   657\u2192  // The number of requests closed on the current connection which were\n   658\u2192  // not internally destroyed\n   659\u2192  uint64_t closed_non_internally_destroyed_requests_{};\n   660\u2192  // The number of requests that received a premature RST_STREAM, according to\n   661\u2192  // the definition given in `isPrematureRstStream()`.\n   662\u2192  uint64_t number_premature_stream_resets_{0};\n   663\u2192  const std::string proxy_name_; // for Proxy-Status.\n   664\u2192  uint32_t requests_during_dispatch_count_{0};\n   665\u2192  const uint32_t max_requests_during_dispatch_{UINT32_MAX};\n   666\u2192  Event::SchedulableCallbackPtr deferred_request_processing_callback_;\n   667\u2192  const envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection direction_;\n   668\u2192\n   669\u2192  // If independent half-close is enabled and the upstream protocol is either HTTP/2 or HTTP/3\n   670\u2192  // protocols the stream is destroyed after both request and response are complete i.e. reach their\n   671\u2192  // respective end-of-stream, by receiving trailers or the header/body with end-stream set in both\n   672\u2192  // directions AND response has success (2xx) status code.\n   673\u2192  //\n   674\u2192  // For HTTP/1 upstream protocol or if independent half-close is disabled the stream is destroyed\n   675\u2192  // when the response is complete and reaches its end-of-stream, i.e. when trailers or the response\n   676\u2192  // header/body with end-stream set are received, even if the request has not yet completed. If\n   677\u2192  // request was incomplete at response completion, the stream is reset.\n   678\u2192\n   679\u2192  const bool allow_upstream_half_close_{};\n   680\u2192};\n   681\u2192\n   682\u2192} // namespace Http\n   683\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   684\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
70. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.h"
}

the vanilla http_parser behaviour.\n    32\u2192inline constexpr uint32_t MAX_RESPONSE_HEADERS_KB = 80;\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192class ConnectionImpl;\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192/**\n    37\u2192 * Base class for HTTP/1.1 request and response encoders.\n    38\u2192 */\n    39\u2192class StreamEncoderImpl : public virtual StreamEncoder,\n    40\u2192                          public Stream,\n    41\u2192                          public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http>,\n    42\u2192                          public StreamCallbackHelper,\n    43\u2192                          public Http1StreamEncoderOptions {\n    44\u2192public:\n    45\u2192  ~StreamEncoderImpl() override {\n    46\u2192    // When the stream goes away, undo any read blocks to resume reading.\n    47\u2192    while (read_disable_calls_ != 0) {\n    48\u2192      StreamEncoderImpl::readDisable(false);\n    49\u2192    }\n    50\u2192  }\n    51\u2192  // Http::StreamEncoder\n    52\u2192  void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    53\u2192  void encodeMetadata(const MetadataMapVector&) override;\n    54\u2192  Stream& getStream() override { return *this; }\n    55\u2192  Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() override { return *this; }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  // Http::Http1StreamEncoderOptions\n    58\u2192  void disableChunkEncoding() override { disable_chunk_encoding_ = true; }\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  // Http::Stream\n    61\u2192  void addCallbacks(StreamCallbacks& callbacks) override { addCallbacksHelper(callbacks); }\n    62\u2192  void removeCallbacks(StreamCallbacks& callbacks) override { removeCallbacksHelper(callbacks); }\n    63\u2192  CodecEventCallbacks* registerCodecEventCallbacks(CodecEventCallbacks* codec_callbacks) override;\n    64\u2192  // After this is called, for the HTTP/1 codec, the connection should be closed, i.e. no further\n    65\u2192  // progress may be made with the codec.\n    66\u2192  void resetStream(StreamResetReason reason) override;\n    67\u2192  void readDisable(bool disable) override;\n    68\u2192  uint32_t bufferLimit() const override;\n    69\u2192  absl::string_view responseDetails() override { return details_; }\n    70\u2192  const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& connectionInfoProvider() override;\n    71\u2192  void setFlushTimeout(std::chrono::milliseconds) override {\n    72\u2192    // HTTP/1 has one stream per connection, thus any data encoded is immediately written to the\n    73\u2192    // connection, invoking any watermarks as necessary. There is no internal buffering that would\n    74\u2192    // require a flush timeout not already covered by other timeouts.\n    75\u2192  }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account() const override { return buffer_memory_account_; }\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192  void setAccount(Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account) override {\n    80\u2192    // TODO(kbaichoo): implement account tracking for H1. Particularly, binding\n    81\u2192    // the account to the buffers used. The current wiring is minimal, and used\n    82\u2192    // to ensure the memory_account gets notified that the downstream request is\n    83\u2192    // closing.\n    84\u2192    buffer_memory_account_ = account;\n    85\u2192  }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192  void setIsResponseToHeadRequest(bool value) { is_response_to_head_request_ = value; }\n    88\u2192  void setIsResponseToConnectRequest(bool value) { is_response_to_connect_request_ = value; }\n    89\u2192  void setDetails(absl::string_view details) { details_ = details; }\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192  const StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr& bytesMeter() override { return bytes_meter_; }\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192protected:\n    94\u2192  StreamEncoderImpl(ConnectionImpl& connection, StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr&& bytes_meter);\n    95\u2192  void encodeHeadersBase(const RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& headers, absl::optional<uint64_t> status,\n    96\u2192                         bool end_stream, bool bodiless_request);\n    97\u2192  void encodeTrailersBase(const HeaderMap& headers);\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr buffer_memory_account_;\n encoder.\n   139\u2192 */\n   140\u2192class ResponseEncoderImpl : public StreamEncoderImpl, public ResponseEncoder {\n   141\u2192public:\n   142\u2192  ResponseEncoderImpl(ConnectionImpl& connection, StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr&& bytes_meter,\n   143\u2192                      bool stream_error_on_invalid_http_message)\n   144\u2192      : StreamEncoderImpl(connection, std::move(bytes_meter)),\n   145\u2192        stream_error_on_invalid_http_message_(stream_error_on_invalid_http_message) {}\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192  ~ResponseEncoderImpl() override {\n   148\u2192    // Only the downstream stream should clear the downstream of the\n   149\u2192    // memory account.\n   150\u2192    //\n   151\u2192    // There are cases where a corresponding upstream stream dtor might\n   152\u2192    // be called, but the downstream stream isn't going to terminate soon\n   153\u2192    // such as StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks::recreateStream().\n   154\u2192    if started_response_; }\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192  // Http::ResponseEncoder\n   162\u2192  void encode1xxHeaders(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers) override;\n   163\u2192  void encodeHeaders(const or elsewhere.\n   177\u2192  void setRequestDecoder(Http::RequestDecoder& /*decoder*/) override {}\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192  // Http1::StreamEncoderImpl\n   180\u2192  void
