{}\n   194\u2192  bool upgradeRequest() const { return upgrade_request_; }\n   195\u2192  bool headRequest() const { return head_request_; }\n   196\u2192  bool connectRequest() const { return connect_request_; }\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192  // Http::RequestEncoder\n   199\u2192  Status encodeHeaders(const RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   200\u2192  void encodeTrailers(const RequestTrailerMap& trailers) override { encodeTrailersBase(trailers); }\n   201\u2192  void enableTcpTunneling() override { is_tcp_tunneling_ = true; }\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192private:\n   204\u2192  bool upgrade_request_{};\n   205\u2192  bool head_request_{};\n   206\u2192};\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192/**\n   209\u2192 * Base class for HTTP/1.1 client and server connections.\n   210\u2192 * Handles the callbacks of http_parser with its own base routine and then\n   211\u2192 * virtual dispatches to its subclasses.\n   212\u2192 */\n   213\u2192class ConnectionImpl : public virtual Connection,\n   214\u2192                       protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http>,\n   215\u2192                       public ParserCallbacks,\n   216\u2192                       public ScopeTrackedObject {\n   217\u2192public:\n   218\u2192  /**\n   219\u2192   * @return Network::Connection& the backing network connection.\n   220\u2192   */\n   221\u2192  Network::Connection& connection() { return connection_; }\n   222\u2192  const Network::Connection& connection() const { return connection_; }\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192  /**\n   225\u2192   * Called when the active encoder has completed encoding the outbound half of the stream.\n   226\u2192   */\n   227\u2192  virtual void onEncodeComplete() PURE;\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::BytesMeter& getBytesMeter() PURE;\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192  /**\n   232\u2192   * Called when resetStream() has been called on an active stream. In HTTP/1.1 the only\n   233\u2192   * valid operation after this point is for the connection to get blown away, but we will not\n   234\u2192   * fire any more callbacks in case some stack has to unwind.\n   235\u2192   */\n   236\u2192  void onResetStreamBase(StreamResetReason reason);\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192  /**\n   239\u2192   * Flush all pending output from encoding.\n   240\u2192   */\n   241\u2192  uint64_t flushOutput(bool end_encode = false);\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192  Buffer::Instance& buffer() { return *output_buffer_; }\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192  void readDisable(bool disable) {\n   246\u2192    if (connection_.state() == Network::Connection::State::Open) {\n   247\u2192      connection_.readDisable(disable);\n   248\u2192    }\n   249\u2192  }\n   250\u2192  uint32_t bufferLimit() { return connection_.bufferLimit(); }\n   251\u2192  virtual bool supportsHttp10() { return false; }\n   252\u2192  bool maybeDirectDispatch(Buffer::Instance& data);\n   253\u2192  virtual void maybeAddSentinelBufferFragment(Buffer::Instance&) {}\n   254\u2192  CodecStats& stats() { return stats_; }\n   255\u2192  bool enableTrailers() const { return codec_settings_.enable_trailers_; }\n   256\u2192  virtual bool sendFullyQualifiedUrl() const { return codec_settings_.send_fully_qualified_url_; }\n   257\u2192  HeaderKeyFormatterOptConstRef formatter() const {\n   258\u2192    return makeOptRefFromPtr(encode_only_header_key_formatter_.get());\n   259\u2192  }\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192  // Http::Connection\n   262\u2192  Http::Status dispatch(Buffer::Instance& data) override;\n   263\u2192  void goAway() override {} // Called during connection manager drain flow\n   264\u2192  Protocol protocol() override { return protocol_; }\n   265\u2192  void shutdownNotice() override {} // Called during connection manager drain flow\n   266\u2192  bool wantsToWrite() override { return false; }\n   267\u2192  void onUnderlyingConnectionAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override { onAboveHighWatermark(); }\n   268\u2192  void onUnderlyingConnectionBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override { onBelowLowWatermark(); }\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192  // Codec errors found in callbacks are overridden within the http_parser library. This holds those\n   271\u2192  // errors to propagate them through to dispatch() where we can handle the error.\n   272\u2192  Envoy::Http::Status codec_status_;\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192  // ScopeTrackedObject\n   275\u2192  OptRef<const StreamInfo::StreamInfo> trackedStream() const override;\n   276\u2192  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192protected:\n   279\u2192  ConnectionImpl(Network::Connection& connection, CodecStats& stats, const Http1Settings& settings,\n   280\u2192                 MessageType type, uint32_t max_headers_kb, const uint32_t max_headers_count);\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192  bool resetStreamCalled() { return reset_stream_called_; }\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192  // This must be protected because it is called through ServerConnectionImpl::sendProtocolError.\n   285\u2192  Status onMessageBeginImpl();\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192  /**\n   288\u2192   * Get memory used to represent HTTP headers or trailers currently being parsed.\n   289\u2192   * Computed by adding the partial header field and value that is currently being parsed and the\n   290\u2192   * estimated header size for previous header lines provided by HeaderMap::byteSize().\n   291\u2192   */\n   292\u2192  virtual uint32_t getHeadersSize();\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192  /**\n   295\u2192   * Called from onUrl, onHeaderFields and onHeaderValue to verify that the headers do not exceed\n   296\u2192   * the configured max header size limit.\n   297\u2192   * @return A codecProtocolError status if headers exceed the size limit.\n   298\u2192   */\n   299\u2192  Status checkMaxHeadersSize();\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192  Network::Connection& connection_;\n   302\u2192  CodecStats& stats_;\n   303\u2192  const Http1Settings codec_settings_;\n   304\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Parser> parser_;\n   305\u2192  Buffer::Instance* current_dispatching_buffer_{};\n   306\u2192  Buffer::Instance* output_buffer_ = nullptr; // Not owned\n   307\u2192  Http::Code error_code_{Http::Code::BadRequest};\n   308\u2192  const HeaderKeyFormatterConstPtr encode_only_header_key_formatter_;\n   309\u2192  HeaderString current_header_field_;\n   310\u2192  HeaderString current_header_value_;\n   311\u2192  bool processing_trailers_ : 1;\n   312\u2192  bool handling_upgrade_ : 1;\n   313\u2192  bool reset_stream_called_ : 1;\n   314\u2192  // Deferred end stream headers indicate that we are not going to raise headers until the full\n   315\u2192  // HTTP/1 message has been flushed from the parser. This allows raising an HTTP/2 style headers\n   316\u2192  // block with end stream set to true with no further protocol data remaining.\n   317\u2192  bool deferred_end_stream_headers_ : 1;\n   318\u2192  bool dispatching_ : 1;\n   319\u2192  bool dispatching_slice_already_drained_ : 1;\n   320\u2192  StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr bytes_meter_before_stream_;\n   321\u2192  const uint32_t max_headers_kb_;\n   322\u2192  const uint32_t max_headers_count_;\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192private:\n   325\u2192  enum class HeaderParsingState { Field, Value, Done };\n   326\u2192  friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, HeaderParsingState parsing_state) {\n   327\u2192    switch (parsing_state) {\n   328\u2192    case ConnectionImpl::HeaderParsingState::Field:\n   329\u2192      return os << \"Field\";\n   330\u2192    case ConnectionImpl::HeaderParsingState::Value:\n   331\u2192      return os << \"Value\";\n   332\u2192    case ConnectionImpl::HeaderParsingState::Done:\n   333\u2192      return os << \"Done\";\n   334\u2192    }\n   335\u2192    return os;\n   336\u2192  }\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192  virtual HeaderMap& headersOrTrailers() PURE;\n   339\u2192  virtual RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& requestOrResponseHeaders() PURE;\n   340\u2192  virtual void allocHeaders(StatefulHeaderKeyFormatterPtr&& formatter) PURE;\n   341\u2192  virtual void allocTrailers() PURE;\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192  /**\n   344\u2192   * Called for each header in order to complete an in progress header decode.\n   345\u2192   * @return A status representing success.\n   346\u2192   */\n   347\u2192  Status completeCurrentHeader();\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192  /**\n   350\u2192   * Check if header name contains underscore character.\n   351\u2192   * Underscore character is allowed in header names by the RFC-7230 and this check is implemented\n   352\u2192   * as a security measure due to systems that treat '_' and '-' as interchangeable.\n   353\u2192   * The ServerConnectionImpl may drop header or reject request based on the\n   354\u2192   * `common_http_protocol_options.headers_with_underscores_action` configuration option in the\n   355\u2192   * HttpConnectionManager.\n   356\u2192   */\n   357\u2192  virtual bool shouldDropHeaderWithUnderscoresInNames(absl::string_view /* header_name */) const {\n   358\u2192    return false;\n   359\u2192  }\n   360\u2192\n   361\u2192  /**\n   362\u2192   * Dispatch a memory span.\n   363\u2192   * @param slice supplies the start address.\n   364\u2192   * @len supplies the length of the span.\n   365\u2192   */\n   366\u2192  Envoy::StatusOr<size_t> dispatchSlice(const char* slice, size_t len);\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192  void onDispatch(const Buffer::Instance& data);\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192  // ParserCallbacks.\n   371\u2192  CallbackResult onMessageBegin() override;\n   372\u2192  CallbackResult onUrl(const char* data, size_t length) override;\n   373\u2192  CallbackResult onStatus(const char* data, size_t length) override;\n   374\u2192  CallbackResult onHeaderField(const char* data, size_t length) override;\n   375\u2192  CallbackResult onHeaderValue(const char* data, size_t length) override;\n   376\u2192  CallbackResult onHeadersComplete() override;\n   377\u2192  void bufferBody(const char* data, size_t length) override;\n   378\u2192  CallbackResult onMessageComplete() override;\n   379\u2192  void onChunkHeader(bool is_final_chunk) override;\n   380\u2192\n   381\u2192  // Internal implementations of ParserCallbacks methods,\n   382\u2192  // and virtual methods for connection-specific implementations.\n   383\u2192  virtual Status onMessageBeginBase() PURE;\n   384\u2192  virtual Status onUrlBase(const char* data, size_t length) PURE;\n   385\u2192  virtual Status onStatusBase(const char* data, size_t length) PURE;\n   386\u2192  Status onHeaderFieldImpl(const char* data, size_t length);\n   387\u2192  Status onHeaderValueImpl(const char* data, size_t length);\n   388\u2192  StatusOr<CallbackResult> onHeadersCompleteImpl();\n   389\u2192  virtual StatusOr<CallbackResult> onHeadersCompleteBase() PURE;\n   390\u2192  StatusOr<CallbackResult> onMessageCompleteImpl();\n   391\u2192  virtual CallbackResult onMessageCompleteBase() PURE;\n   392\u2192\n   393\u2192  // These helpers wrap *Impl() calls in the overrides of non-void\n   394\u2192  // ParserCallbacks methods.\n   395\u2192  CallbackResult setAndCheckCallbackStatus(Status&& status);\n   396\u2192  CallbackResult setAndCheckCallbackStatusOr(StatusOr<CallbackResult>&& statusor);\n   397\u2192\n   398\u2192  /**\n   399\u2192   * Push the accumulated body through the filter pipeline.\n   400\u2192   */\n   401\u2192  void dispatchBufferedBody();\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192  /**\n   404\u2192   * Called to see if upgrade transition is allowed.\n   405\u2192   */\n   406\u2192  virtual bool upgradeAllowed() const PURE;\n   407\u2192\n   408\u2192  /**\n   409\u2192   * Called with body data is available for processing when either:\n   410\u2192   * - There is an accumulated partial body after the parser is done processing bytes read from the\n   411\u2192   * socket\n   412\u2192   * - The parser encounters the last byte of the body\n   413\u2192   * - The codec does a direct dispatch from the read buffer\n   414\u2192   * For performance reasons there is at most one call to onBody per call to HTTP/1\n   415\u2192   * ConnectionImpl::dispatch call.\n   416\u2192   * @param data supplies the body data\n   417\u2192   */\n   418\u2192  virtual void onBody(Buffer::Instance& data) PURE;\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192  /**\n   421\u2192   * @see onResetStreamBase().\n   422\u2192   */\n   423\u2192  virtual void onResetStream(StreamResetReason reason) PURE;\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192  /**\n   426\u2192   * Send a protocol error response to remote.\n   427\u2192   */\n   428\u2192  virtual Status sendProtocolError(absl::string_view details) PURE;\n   429\u2192\n   430\u2192  /**\n   431\u2192   * Called when output_buffer_ or the underlying connection go from below a low watermark to over\n   432\u2192   * a high watermark.\n   433\u2192   */\n   434\u2192  virtual void onAboveHighWatermark() PURE;\n   435\u2192\n   436\u2192  /**\n   437\u2192   * Called when output_buffer_ or the underlying connection  go from above a high watermark to\n   438\u2192   * below a low watermark.\n   439\u2192   */\n   440\u2192  virtual void onBelowLowWatermark() PURE;\n   441\u2192\n   442\u2192  /**\n   443\u2192   * Check if header name contains underscore character.\n   444\u2192   * The ServerConnectionImpl may drop header or reject request based on configuration.\n   445\u2192   * @return A status representing whether the request is rejected.\n   446\u2192   */\n   447\u2192  virtual Status checkHeaderNameForUnderscores() { return okStatus(); }\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192  /**\n   450\u2192   * Additional state to dump on crash.\n   451\u2192   */\n   452\u2192  virtual void dumpAdditionalState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const PURE;\n   453\u2192\n   454\u2192  HeaderParsingState header_parsing_state_{HeaderParsingState::Field};\n   455\u2192  // Used to accumulate the HTTP message body during the current dispatch call. The accumulated body\n   456\u2192  // is pushed through the filter pipeline either at the end of the current dispatch call, or when\n   457\u2192  // the last byte of the body is processed (whichever happens first).\n   458\u2192  Buffer::OwnedImpl buffered_body_;\n   459\u2192  Protocol protocol_{Protocol::Http11};\n   460\u2192};\n   461\u2192\n   462\u2192/**\n   463\u2192 * Implementation of Http::ServerConnection for HTTP/1.1.\n   464\u2192 */\n   465\u2192class ServerConnectionImpl : public ServerConnection, public ConnectionImpl {\n   466\u2192public:\n   467\u2192  ServerConnectionImpl(Network::Connection& connection, CodecStats& stats,\n   468\u2192                       ServerConnectionCallbacks& callbacks, const Http1Settings& settings,\n   469\u2192                       uint32_t max_request_headers_kb, const uint32_t max_request_headers_count,\n   470\u2192                       envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\n   471\u2192                           headers_with_underscores_action,\n   472\u2192                       Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager);\n   473\u2192  bool supportsHttp10() override { return codec_settings_.accept_http_10_; }\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192protected:\n   476\u2192  /**\n   477\u2192   * An active HTTP/1.1 request.\n   478\u2192   */\n   479\u2192  struct ActiveRequest : public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n   480\u2192    ActiveRequest(ServerConnectionImpl& connection, StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr&& bytes_meter)\n   481\u2192        : response_encoder_(connection, std::move(bytes_meter),\n   482\u2192                            connection.codec_settings_.stream_error_on_invalid_http_message_) {}\n   483\u2192    ~ActiveRequest() override = default;\n   484\u2192\n   485\u2192    void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const;\n   486\u2192    HeaderString request_url_;\n   487\u2192    RequestDecoder* request_decoder_{};\n   488\u2192    ResponseEncoderImpl response_encoder_;\n   489\u2192    bool remote_complete_{};\n   490\u2192  };\n   491\u2192  ActiveRequest* activeRequest() { return active_request_.get(); }\n   492\u2192  // ConnectionImpl\n   493\u2192  CallbackResult onMessageCompleteBase() override;\n   494\u2192  // Add the size of the request_url to the reported header size when processing request headers.\n   495\u2192  uint32_t getHeadersSize() override;\n   496\u2192\n   497\u2192private:\n   498\u2192  /**\n   499\u2192   * Manipulate the request's first line, parsing the url and converting to a relative path if\n   500\u2192   * necessary. Compute Host / :authority headers based on 7230#5.7 and 7230#6\n   501\u2192   *\n   502\u2192   * @param is_connect true if the request has the CONNECT method\n   503\u2192   * @param headers the request's headers\n   504\u2192   * @return Status representing success or failure. This will fail if there is an invalid url in\n   505\u2192   * the request line.\n   506\u2192   */\n   507\u2192  Status handlePath(RequestHeaderMap& headers, absl::string_view method);\n   508\u2192\n   509\u2192  // ParserCallbacks.\n   510\u2192  Status onUrlBase(const char* data, size_t length) override;\n   511\u2192  Status onStatusBase(const char*, size_t) override { return okStatus(); }\n   512\u2192  // ConnectionImpl\n   513\u2192  Http::Status dispatch(Buffer::Instance& data) override;\n   514\u2192  void onEncodeComplete() override;\n   515\u2192  StreamInfo::BytesMeter& getBytesMeter() override {\n   516\u2192    if (active_request_) {\n   517\u2192      return *(active_request_->response_encoder_.getStream().bytesMeter());\n   518\u2192    }\n   519\u2192    if (bytes_meter_before_stream_ == nullptr) {\n   520\u2192      bytes_meter_before_stream_ = std::make_shared<StreamInfo::BytesMeter>();\n   521\u2192    }\n   522\u2192    return *bytes_meter_before_stream_;\n   523\u2192  }\n   524\u2192  Status onMessageBeginBase() override;\n   525\u2192  Envoy::StatusOr<CallbackResult> onHeadersCompleteBase() override;\n   526\u2192  // If upgrade behavior is not allowed, the HCM will have sanitized the headers out.\n   527\u2192  bool upgradeAllowed() const override { return true; }\n   528\u2192  void onBody(Buffer::Instance& data) override;\n   529\u2192  void onResetStream(StreamResetReason reason) override;\n   530\u2192  Status sendProtocolError(absl::string_view details) override;\n   531\u2192  Status sendOverloadError();\n   532\u2192  void onAboveHighWatermark() override;\n   533\u2192  void onBelowLowWatermark() override;\n   534\u2192  HeaderMap& headersOrTrailers() override {\n   535\u2192    if (absl::holds_alternative<RequestHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)) {\n   536\u2192      return *absl::get<RequestHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   537\u2192    } else {\n   538\u2192      return *absl::get<RequestTrailerMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   539\u2192    }\n   540\u2192  }\n   541\u2192  RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& requestOrResponseHeaders() override {\n   542\u2192    return *absl::get<RequestHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   543\u2192  }\n   544\u2192  void allocHeaders(StatefulHeaderKeyFormatterPtr&& formatter) override {\n   545\u2192    ASSERT(!processing_trailers_);\n   546\u2192    auto headers = RequestHeaderMapImpl::create(max_headers_kb_, max_headers_count_);\n   547\u2192    headers->setFormatter(std::move(formatter));\n   548\u2192    headers_or_trailers_.emplace<RequestHeaderMapPtr>(std::move(headers));\n   549\u2192  }\n   550\u2192  void allocTrailers() override {\n   551\u2192    ASSERT(processing_trailers_);\n   552\u2192    if (!absl::holds_alternative<RequestTrailerMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)) {\n   553\u2192      headers_or_trailers_.emplace<RequestTrailerMapPtr>(\n   554\u2192          RequestTrailerMapImpl::create(max_headers_kb_, max_headers_count_));\n   555\u2192    }\n   556\u2192  }\n   557\u2192  void dumpAdditionalState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n   558\u2192\n   559\u2192  void releaseOutboundResponse(const Buffer::OwnedBufferFragmentImpl* fragment);\n   560\u2192  void maybeAddSentinelBufferFragment(Buffer::Instance& output_buffer) override;\n   561\u2192\n   562\u2192  Status doFloodProtectionChecks() const;\n   563\u2192  Status checkHeaderNameForUnderscores() override;\n   564\u2192  Status checkProtocolVersion(RequestHeaderMap& headers);\n   565\u2192\n   566\u2192  ServerConnectionCallbacks& callbacks_;\n   567\u2192  std::unique_ptr<ActiveRequest> active_request_;\n   568\u2192  const Buffer::OwnedBufferFragmentImpl::Releasor response_buffer_releasor_;\n   569\u2192  uint32_t outbound_responses_{};\n   570\u2192  // Buffer used to encode the HTTP message before moving it to the network connection's output\n   571\u2192  // buffer. This buffer is always allocated, never nullptr.\n   572\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr owned_output_buffer_;\n   573\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): This should be a member of ActiveRequest but this change needs dedicated\n   574\u2192  // thought as some of the reset and no header code paths make this difficult. Headers are\n   575\u2192  // populated on message begin. Trailers are populated on the first parsed trailer field (if\n   576\u2192  // trailers are enabled). The variant is reset to null headers on message complete for assertion\n   577\u2192  // purposes.\n   578\u2192  absl::variant<RequestHeaderMapPtr, RequestTrailerMapPtr> headers_or_trailers_;\n   579\u2192  // The action to take when a request header name contains underscore characters.\n   580\u2192  const envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\n   581\u2192      headers_with_underscores_action_;\n   582\u2192  Server::LoadShedPoint* abort_dispatch_{nullptr};\n   583\u2192};\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192/**\n   586\u2192 * Implementation of Http::ClientConnection for HTTP/1.1.\n   587\u2192 */\n   588\u2192class ClientConnectionImpl : public ClientConnection, public ConnectionImpl {\n   589\u2192public:\n   590\u2192  ClientConnectionImpl(Network::Connection& connection, CodecStats& stats,\n   591\u2192                       ConnectionCallbacks& callbacks, const Http1Settings& settings,\n   592\u2192                       absl::optional<uint16_t> max_response_headers_kb,\n   593\u2192                       const uint32_t max_response_headers_count,\n   594\u2192                       bool passing_through_proxy = false);\n   595\u2192  // Http::ClientConnection\n   596\u2192  RequestEncoder& newStream(ResponseDecoder& response_decoder) override;\n   597\u2192\n   598\u2192private:\n   599\u2192  struct PendingResponse {\n   600\u2192    PendingResponse(ConnectionImpl& connection, StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr&& bytes_meter,\n   601\u2192                    ResponseDecoder* decoder)\n   602\u2192        : encoder_(connection, std::move(bytes_meter)), decoder_(decoder) {}\n   603\u2192    RequestEncoderImpl encoder_;\n   604\u2192    ResponseDecoder* decoder_;\n   605\u2192  };\n   606\u2192\n   607\u2192  bool cannotHaveBody();\n   608\u2192\n   609\u2192  bool sendFullyQualifiedUrl() const override {\n   610\u2192    // Send fully qualified URLs either if the parent connection is configured to do so or this\n   611\u2192    // stream is passing through a proxy and the underlying transport is plaintext.\n   612\u2192    return ConnectionImpl::sendFullyQualifiedUrl() ||\n   613\u2192           (passing_through_proxy_ && !connection().ssl());\n   614\u2192  }\n   615\u2192\n   616\u2192  // ParserCallbacks.\n   617\u2192  Status onUrlBase(const char*, size_t) override { return okStatus(); }\n   618\u2192  Status onStatusBase(const char* data, size_t length) override;\n   619\u2192  // ConnectionImpl\n   620\u2192  Http::Status dispatch(Buffer::Instance& data) override;\n   621\u2192  void onEncodeComplete() override { encode_complete_ = true; }\n   622\u2192  StreamInfo::BytesMeter& getBytesMeter() override {\n   623\u2192    if (pending_response_.has_value()) {\n   624\u2192      return *(pending_response_->encoder_.getStream().bytesMeter());\n   625\u2192    }\n   626\u2192    if (bytes_meter_before_stream_ == nullptr) {\n   627\u2192      bytes_meter_before_stream_ = std::make_shared<StreamInfo::BytesMeter>();\n   628\u2192    }\n   629\u2192    return *bytes_meter_before_stream_;\n   630\u2192  }\n   631\u2192  Status onMessageBeginBase() override { return okStatus(); }\n   632\u2192  Envoy::StatusOr<CallbackResult> onHeadersCompleteBase() override;\n   633\u2192  bool upgradeAllowed() const override;\n   634\u2192  void onBody(Buffer::Instance& data) override;\n   635\u2192  CallbackResult onMessageCompleteBase() override;\n   636\u2192  void onResetStream(StreamResetReason reason) override;\n   637\u2192  Status sendProtocolError(absl::string_view details) override;\n   638\u2192  void onAboveHighWatermark() override;\n   639\u2192  void onBelowLowWatermark() override;\n   640\u2192  HeaderMap& headersOrTrailers() override {\n   641\u2192    if (absl::holds_alternative<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)) {\n   642\u2192      return *absl::get<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   643\u2192    } else {\n   644\u2192      return *absl::get<ResponseTrailerMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   645\u2192    }\n   646\u2192  }\n   647\u2192  RequestOrResponseHeaderMap& requestOrResponseHeaders() override {\n   648\u2192    return *absl::get<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   649\u2192  }\n   650\u2192  void allocHeaders(StatefulHeaderKeyFormatterPtr&& formatter) override {\n   651\u2192    ASSERT(nullptr == absl::get<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_));\n   652\u2192    ASSERT(!processing_trailers_);\n   653\u2192    auto headers = ResponseHeaderMapImpl::create(max_headers_kb_, max_headers_count_);\n   654\u2192    headers->setFormatter(std::move(formatter));\n   655\u2192    headers_or_trailers_.emplace<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(std::move(headers));\n   656\u2192  }\n   657\u2192  void allocTrailers() override {\n   658\u2192    ASSERT(processing_trailers_);\n   659\u2192    if (!absl::holds_alternative<ResponseTrailerMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)) {\n   660\u2192      headers_or_trailers_.emplace<ResponseTrailerMapPtr>(\n   661\u2192          ResponseTrailerMapImpl::create(max_headers_kb_, max_headers_count_));\n   662\u2192    }\n   663\u2192  }\n   664\u2192  void dumpAdditionalState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n   665\u2192\n   666\u2192  // Buffer used to encode the HTTP message before moving it to the network connection's output\n   667\u2192  // buffer. This buffer is always allocated, never nullptr.\n   668\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr owned_output_buffer_;\n   669\u2192\n   670\u2192  absl::optional<PendingResponse> pending_response_;\n   671\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): The following bool tracks whether a pending response is complete before\n   672\u2192  // dispatching callbacks. This is needed so that pending_response_ stays valid during callbacks\n   673\u2192  // in order to access the stream, but to avoid invoking callbacks that shouldn't be called once\n   674\u2192  // the response is complete. The existence of this variable is hard to reason about and it should\n   675\u2192  // be combined with pending_response_ somehow in a follow up cleanup.\n   676\u2192  bool pending_response_done_{true};\n   677\u2192  // Set true between receiving non-101 1xx headers and receiving the spurious onMessageComplete.\n   678\u2192  bool ignore_message_complete_for_1xx_{};\n   679\u2192  // TODO(mattklein123): This should be a member of PendingResponse but this change needs dedicated\n   680\u2192  // thought as some of the reset and no header code paths make this difficult. Headers are\n   681\u2192  // populated on message begin. Trailers are populated when the switch to trailer processing is\n   682\u2192  // detected while parsing the first trailer field (if trailers are enabled). The variant is reset\n   683\u2192  // to null headers on message complete for assertion purposes.\n   684\u2192  absl::variant<ResponseHeaderMapPtr, ResponseTrailerMapPtr> headers_or_trailers_;\n   685\u2192\n   686\u2192  // True if the upstream connection is pointed at an HTTP/1.1 proxy, and\n   687\u2192  // plaintext HTTP should be sent with fully qualified URLs.\n   688\u2192  bool passing_through_proxy_ = false;\n   689\u2192\n   690\u2192  const bool force_reset_on_premature_upstream_half_close_{};\n   691\u2192  bool encode_complete_{false};\n   692\u2192};\n   693\u2192\n   694\u2192} // namespace Http1\n   695\u2192} // namespace Http\n   696\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   697\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
71. Read @ -

  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.h"
}

0) {\n   409\u2192        codec_callbacks_->onCodecLowLevelReset();\n   410\u2192      }\n   411\u2192    }\n   412\u2192\n   413\u2192    const StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr& bytesMeter() override { return bytes_meter_; }\n   414\u2192    ConnectionImpl& parent_;\n   415\u2192    int32_t stream_id_{-1};\n   416\u2192    uint32_t unconsumed_bytes_{0};\n   417\u2192    uint32_t read_disable_count_{0};\n   418\u2192    StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr bytes_meter_{std::make_shared<StreamInfo::BytesMeter>()};\n   419\u2192\n   420\u2192    Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr buffer_memory_account_;\n   421\u2192    // This buffer may accumulate data and be drained in scheduleProcessingOfBufferedData.\n   422\u2192    // See source/docs/flow_control.md for more information.\n   423\u2192    Buffer::InstancePtr pending_recv_data_;\n   424\u2192    Buffer::InstancePtr pending_send_data_;\n   425\u2192    HeaderMapPtr pending_trailers_to_encode_;\n   426\u2192    std::unique_ptr<MetadataDecoder> metadata_decoder_;\n   427\u2192    std::unique_ptr<NewMetadataEncoder> metadata_encoder_;\n   428\u2192    absl::optional<StreamResetReason> deferred_reset_;\n   429\u2192    // Holds the reset reason for this stream. Useful if we have buffered data\n   430\u2192    // to determine whether we should continue processing that data.\n   431\u2192    absl::optional<StreamResetReason> reset_reason_;\n   432\u2192    HeaderString cookies_;\n   433\u2192    bool local_end_stream_sent_ : 1;\n   434\u2192    bool remote_end_stream_ : 1;\n   435\u2192    bool remote_rst_ : 1;\n   436\u2192    bool data_deferred_ : 1;\n   437\u2192    bool received_noninformational_headers_ : 1;\n   438\u2192    bool pending_receive_buffer_high_watermark_called_ : 1;\n   439\u2192    bool pending_send_buffer_high_watermark_called_ : 1;\n   440\u2192    bool reset_due_to_messaging_error_ : 1;\n   441\u2192    // Latch whether this stream is operating with this flag.\n   442\u2192    bool extend_stream_lifetime_flag_ : 1;\n   443\u2192    absl::string_view details_;\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192    /**\n   446\u2192     * Tracks buffering that may occur for a stream if it is backed up.\n   447\u2192     */\n   448\u2192    struct BufferedStreamManager {\n   449\u2192      bool body_buffered_{false};\n   450\u2192      bool trailers_buffered_{false};\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192      // We received a call to onStreamClose for the stream, but deferred it\n   453\u2192      // as the stream had pending data to process and the stream was not reset.\n   454\u2192      bool buffered_on_stream_close_{false};\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192      // Segment size for processing body data. Defaults to the value of high\n   457\u2192      // watermark of the *pending_recv_data_* buffer.\n   458\u2192      // If 0, we will process all buffered data.\n   459\u2192      uint32_t defer_processing_segment_size_{0};\n   460\u2192\n   461\u2192      bool decodeAsChunks() const { return defer_processing_segment_size_ > 0; }\n   462\u2192      bool hasBufferedBodyOrTrailers() const { return body_buffered_ || trailers_buffered_; }\n   463\u2192    };\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192    BufferedStreamManager stream_manager_;\n   466\u2192    Event::SchedulableCallbackPtr process_buffered_data_callback_;\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192  protected:\n   469\u2192    // Http::MultiplexedStreamImplBase\n   470\u2192    bool hasPendingData() override {\n   471\u2192      return pending_send_data_->length() > 0 || pending_trailers_to_encode_ != nullptr;\n   472\u2192    }\n   473\u2192    bool continueProcessingBufferedData() const {\n   474\u2192      // We should stop processing buffered data if either\n   475\u2192      // 1) Buffers become overrun\n   476\u2192      // 2) The stream ends up getting reset\n   477\u2192      // Both of these can end up changing as a result of processing buffered data.\n   478\u2192      return !buffersOverrun() && !reset_reason_.has_value();\n   479\u2192    }\n   480\u2192\n   481\u2192    // Avoid inversion in the case where we saw trailers, acquiring the\n   482\u2192    // remote_end_stream_ being set to true, but the trailers ended up being\n   483\u2192    // buffered.\n   484\u2192    // All buffered body must be consumed before we send end stream.\n   485\u2192    bool sendEndStream() const {\n   486\u2192      return remote_end_stream_ && !stream_manager_.trailers_buffered_ &&\n   487\u2192             !stream_manager_.body_buffered_;\n   488\u2192    }\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192    // Schedules a callback either in the current or next iteration to process\n   491\u2192    // buffered data.\n   492\u2192    void scheduleProcessingOfBufferedData(bool schedule_next_iteration);\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192    // Marks data consumed by the stream, granting the peer additional stream\n   495\u2192    // window.\n   496\u2192    void grantPeerAdditionalStreamWindow();\n   497\u2192  };\n   498\u2192\n   499\u2192  using StreamImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<StreamImpl>;\n   500\u2192\n   501\u2192  /**\n   502\u2192   * Client side stream (request).\n   503\u2192   */\n   504\u2192  struct ClientStreamImpl : public StreamImpl, public RequestEncoder {\n   505\u2192    ClientStreamImpl(ConnectionImpl& parent, uint32_t buffer_limit,\n   506\u2192                     ResponseDecoder& response_decoder)\n   507\u2192        : StreamImpl(parent, buffer_limit), response_decoder_(response_decoder),\n   508\u2192          headers_or_trailers_(\n   509\u2192              ResponseHeaderMapImpl::create(parent_.max_headers_kb_, parent_.max_headers_count_)) {}\n   510\u2192\n   511\u2192    // Http::MultiplexedStreamImplBase\n   512\u2192    // Client streams do not need a flush timer because we currently assume that any failure\n   513\u2192    // to flush would be covered by a request/stream/etc. timeout.\n   514\u2192    void setFlushTimeout(std::chrono::milliseconds /*timeout*/) override {}\n   515\u2192    CodecEventCallbacks* registerCodecEventCallbacks(CodecEventCallbacks*) override {\n   516\u2192      ENVOY_BUG(false, \"CodecEventCallbacks for HTTP2 client stream unimplemented.\");\n   517\u2192      return nullptr;\n   518\u2192    }\n   519\u2192    // StreamImpl\n   520\u2192    void submitHeaders(const HeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   521\u2192    Status onBeginHeaders() override;\n   522\u2192    void advanceHeadersState() override;\n   523\u2192    HeadersState headersState() const override { return headers_state_; }\n   524\u2192    // Do not use deferred reset on upstream connections.\n   525\u2192    bool useDeferredReset() const override { return false; }\n   526\u2192    StreamDecoder& decoder() override { return response_decoder_; }\n   527\u2192    void decodeHeaders() override;\n   528\u2192    void decodeTrailers() override;\n   529\u2192    HeaderMap& headers() override {\n   530\u2192      if (absl::holds_alternative<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)) {\n   531\u2192        return *absl::get<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   532\u2192      } else {\n   533\u2192        return *absl::get<ResponseTrailerMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   534\u2192      }\n   535\u2192    }\n   536\u2192    void allocTrailers() override {\n   537\u2192      // If we are waiting for informational headers, make a new response header map, otherwise\n   538\u2192      // we are about to receive trailers. The codec makes sure this is the only valid sequence.\n   539\u2192      if (received_noninformational_headers_) {\n   540\u2192        headers_or_trailers_.emplace<ResponseTrailerMapPtr>(\n   541\u2192            ResponseTrailerMapImpl::create(parent_.max_headers_kb_, parent_.max_headers_count_));\n   542\u2192      } else {\n   543\u2192        headers_or_trailers_.emplace<ResponseHeaderMapPtr>(\n   544\u2192            ResponseHeaderMapImpl::create(parent_.max_headers_kb_, parent_.max_headers_count_));\n   545\u2192      }\n   546\u2192    }\n   547\u2192    HeaderMapPtr cloneTrailers(const HeaderMap& trailers) override {\n   548\u2192      return createHeaderMap<RequestTrailerMapImpl>(trailers);\n   549\u2192    }\n   550\u2192\n   551\u2192    // RequestEncoder\n   552\u2192    Status encodeHeaders(const RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   553\u2192    void encodeTrailers(const RequestTrailerMap& trailers) override {\n   554\u2192      encodeTrailersBase(trailers);\n   555\u2192    }\n   556\u2192    void enableTcpTunneling() override {}\n   557\u2192\n   558\u2192    // ScopeTrackedObject\n   559\u2192    void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n   560\u2192\n   561\u2192    ResponseDecoder& response_decoder_;\n   562\u2192    absl::variant<ResponseHeaderMapPtr, ResponseTrailerMapPtr> headers_or_trailers_;\n   563\u2192    std::string upgrade_type_;\n   564\u2192    HeadersState headers_state_ = HeadersState::Response;\n   565\u2192  };\n   566\u2192\n   567\u2192  using ClientStreamImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<ClientStreamImpl>;\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192  /**\n   570\u2192   * Server side stream (response).\n   571\u2192   */\n   572\u2192  struct ServerStreamImpl : public StreamImpl, public ResponseEncoder {\n   573\u2192    ServerStreamImpl(ConnectionImpl& parent, uint32_t buffer_limit)\n   574\u2192        : StreamImpl(parent, buffer_limit),\n   575\u2192          headers_or_trailers_(\n   576\u2192              RequestHeaderMapImpl::create(parent_.max_headers_kb_, parent_.max_headers_count_)) {}\n   577\u2192\n   578\u2192    // StreamImpl\n   579\u2192    void destroy() override;\n   580\u2192    void submitHeaders(const HeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   581\u2192    Status onBeginHeaders() override;\n   582\u2192    void advanceHeadersState() override;\n   583\u2192    HeadersState headersState() const override { return headers_state_; }\n   584\u2192    // Enable deferred reset on downstream connections so outbound HTTP internal error replies are\n   585\u2192    // written out before force resetting the stream, assuming there is enough H2 connection flow\n   586\u2192    // control window is available.\n   587\u2192    bool useDeferredReset() const override { return true; }\n   588\u2192    StreamDecoder& decoder() override { return *request_decoder_; }\n   589\u2192    void decodeHeaders() override;\n   590\u2192    void decodeTrailers() override;\n   591\u2192    HeaderMap& headers() override {\n   592\u2192      if (absl::holds_alternative<RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)) {\n   593\u2192        return *absl::get<RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   594\u2192      } else {\n   595\u2192        return *absl::get<RequestTrailerMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n   596\u2192      }\n   597\u2192    }\n   598\u2192    void allocTrailers() override {\n   599\u2192      headers_or_trailers_.emplace<RequestTrailerMapPtr>(\n   600\u2192          RequestTrailerMapImpl::create(parent_.max_headers_kb_, parent_.max_headers_count_));\n   601\u2192    }\n   602\u2192    HeaderMapPtr cloneTrailers(const HeaderMap& trailers) override {\n   603\u2192      return createHeaderMap<ResponseTrailerMapImpl>(trailers);\n   604\u2192    }\n   605\u2192    void resetStream(StreamResetReason reason) override;\n   606\u2192\n   607\u2192    // ResponseEncoder\n   608\u2192    void encode1xxHeaders(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers) override;\n   609\u2192    void encodeHeaders(const ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n   610\u2192    void encodeTrailers(const ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) override {\n   611\u2192      encodeTrailersBase(trailers);\n   612\u2192    }\n   613\u2192    void setRequestDecoder(Http::RequestDecoder& decoder) override { request_decoder_ = &decoder; }\n   614\u2192    void setDeferredLoggingHeadersAndTrailers(Http::RequestHeaderMapConstSharedPtr,\n   615\u2192                                              Http::ResponseHeaderMapConstSharedPtr,\n   616\u2192                                              Http::ResponseTrailerMapConstSharedPtr,\n   617\u2192                                              StreamInfo::StreamInfo&) override {}\n   618\u2192\n   619\u2192    // ScopeTrackedObject\n   620\u2192    void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n   621\u2192\n   622\u2192    absl::variant<RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr, RequestTrailerMapPtr> headers_or_trailers_;\n   623\u2192\n   624\u2192    bool streamErrorOnInvalidHttpMessage() const override {\n   625\u2192      return parent_.stream_error_on_invalid_http_messaging_;\n   626\u2192    }\n   627\u2192\n   628\u2192  private:\n   629\u2192    RequestDecoder* request_decoder_{};\n   630\u2192    HeadersState headers_state_ = HeadersState::Request;\n   631\u2192  };\n   632\u2192\n   633\u2192  using ServerStreamImplPtr = std::unique_ptr<ServerStreamImpl>;\n   634\u2192\n   635\u2192  ConnectionImpl* base() { return this; }\n   636\u2192  // NOTE: Always use non debug nullptr checks against the return value of this function. There are\n   637\u2192  // edge cases (such as for METADATA frames) where nghttp2 will issue a callback for a stream_id\n   638\u2192  // that is not associated with an existing stream.\n   639\u2192  const StreamImpl* getStream(int32_t stream_id) const;\n   640\u2192  StreamImpl* getStream(int32_t stream_id);\n   641\u2192  // Same as getStream, but without the ASSERT.\n   642\u2192  const StreamImpl* getStreamUnchecked(int32_t stream_id) const;\n   643\u2192  StreamImpl* getStreamUnchecked(int32_t stream_id);\n   644\u2192  int saveHeader(int32_t stream_id, HeaderString&& name, HeaderString&& value);\n   645\u2192\n   646\u2192  /**\n   647\u2192   * Copies any frames pending internally by nghttp2 into outbound buffer.\n   648\u2192   * The `sendPendingFrames()` can be called in 3 different contexts:\n   649\u2192   * 1. dispatching_ == true, aka the dispatching context. The `sendPendingFrames()` is no-op and\n   650\u2192   *    always returns success to avoid reentering nghttp2 library.\n   651\u2192   * 2. Server codec only. dispatching_ == false.\n   652\u2192   *    The `sendPendingFrames()` returns the status of the protocol constraint checks. Outbound\n   653\u2192   *    frame accounting is performed.\n   654\u2192   * 3. dispatching_ == false. The `sendPendingFrames()` always returns success. No outbound\n   655\u2192   *    frame accounting.\n   656\u2192   *\n   657\u2192   * TODO(yanavlasov): harmonize behavior for cases 2, 3.\n   658\u2192   */\n   659\u2192  Status sendPendingFrames();\n   660\u2192\n   661\u2192  /**\n   662\u2192   * Call the sendPendingFrames() method and schedule disconnect callback when\n   663\u2192   * sendPendingFrames() returns an error.\n   664\u2192   * Return true if the disconnect callback has been scheduled.\n   665\u2192   */\n   666\u2192  bool sendPendingFramesAndHandleError();\n   667\u2192  void sendSettings(const envoy::config::core::v3::Http2ProtocolOptions& http2_options,\n   668\u2192                    bool disable_push);\n   669\u2192  void sendSettingsHelper(const envoy::config::core::v3::Http2ProtocolOptions& http2_options,\n   670\u2192                          bool disable_push);\n   671\u2192  // Callback triggered when the peer's SETTINGS frame is received.\n   672\u2192  virtual void onSettings(absl::Span<const http2::adapter::Http2Setting> settings) {\n   673\u2192    ReceivedSettingsImpl received_settings(settings);\n   674\u2192    callbacks().onSettings(received_settings);\n   675\u2192  }\n   676\u2192\n   677\u2192  /**\n   678\u2192   * Check if header name contains underscore character.\n   679\u2192   * Underscore character is allowed in header names by the RFC-7230 and this check is implemented\n   680\u2192   * as a security measure due to systems that treat '_' and '-' as interchangeable.\n   681\u2192   * The ServerConnectionImpl may drop header or reject request based on the\n   682\u2192   * `common_http_protocol_options.headers_with_underscores_action` configuration option in the\n   683\u2192   * HttpConnectionManager.\n   684\u2192   */\n   685\u2192  virtual absl::optional<int> checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(absl::string_view /* header_name */) {\n   686\u2192    return absl::nullopt;\n   687\u2192  }\n   688\u2192\n   689\u2192  /**\n   690\u2192   * Save `status` into codec_callback_status_.\n   691\u2192   * Return codec callback return code corresponding to `status`.\n   692\u2192   */\n   693\u2192  int setAndCheckCodecCallbackStatus(Status&& status);\n   694\u2192\n   695\u2192  /**\n   696\u2192   * Callback for terminating connection when protocol constrain has been violated\n   697\u2192   * outside of the dispatch context.\n   698\u2192   */\n   699\u2192  void scheduleProtocolConstraintViolationCallback();\n   700\u2192  void onProtocolConstraintViolation();\n   701\u2192\n   702\u2192  // Whether to use the new HTTP/2 library.\n   703\u2192  bool use_oghttp2_library_;\n   704\u2192\n   705\u2192  // If deferred processing, the streams will be in LRU order based on when the\n   706\u2192  // stream encoded to the http2 connection. The LRU property is used when\n   707\u2192  // raising low watermark on the http2 connection to prioritize how streams get\n   708\u2192  // notified, prefering those that haven't recently written.\n   709\u2192  std::list<StreamImplPtr> active_streams_;\n   710\u2192\n   711\u2192  // Tracks the stream id of the current stream we're processing.\n   712\u2192  // This should only be set while we're in the context of dispatching to nghttp2.\n   713\u2192  absl::optional<int32_t> current_stream_id_;\n   714\u2192  std::unique_ptr<http2::adapter::Http2VisitorInterface> visitor_;\n   715\u2192  std::unique_ptr<http2::adapter::Http2Adapter> adapter_;\n   716\u2192\n   717\u2192  CodecStats& stats_;\n   718\u2192  Network::Connection& connection_;\n   719\u2192  const uint32_t max_headers_kb_;\n   720\u2192  const uint32_t max_headers_count_;\n   721\u2192  uint32_t per_stream_buffer_limit_;\n   722\u2192  bool allow_metadata_;\n   723\u2192  uint64_t max_metadata_size_;\n   724\u2192  const bool stream_error_on_invalid_http_messaging_;\n   725\u2192\n   726\u2192  // Status for any errors encountered by the nghttp2 callbacks.\n   727\u2192  // nghttp2 library uses single return code to indicate callback failure and\n   728\u2192  // `codec_callback_status_` is used to save right error information returned by a callback. The\n   729\u2192  // `codec_callback_status_` is valid iff nghttp call returned NGHTTP2_ERR_CALLBACK_FAILURE.\n   730\u2192  Status codec_callback_status_;\n   731\u2192\n   732\u2192  // Set if the type of frame that is about to be sent is PING or SETTINGS with the ACK flag set, or\n   733\u2192  // RST_STREAM.\n   734\u2192  bool is_outbound_flood_monitored_control_frame_ = 0;\n   735\u2192  ProtocolConstraints protocol_constraints_;\n   736\u2192\n   737\u2192  // For the flood mitigation to work the onSend callback must be called once for each outbound\n   738\u2192  // frame. This is what the nghttp2 library is doing, however this is not documented. The\n   739\u2192  // Http2FloodMitigationTest.* tests in test/integration/http2_integration_test.cc will break if\n   740\u2192  // this changes in the future. Also it is important that onSend does not do partial writes, as the\n   741\u2192  // nghttp2 library will keep calling this callback to write the rest of the frame.\n   742\u2192  ssize_t onSend(const uint8_t* data, size_t length);\n   743\u2192\n   744\u2192  // Called when a stream encodes to the http2 connection which enables us to\n   745\u2192  // keep the active_streams list in LRU if deferred processing.\n   746\u2192  void updateActiveStreamsOnEncode(StreamImpl& stream) {\n   747\u2192    LinkedList::moveIntoList(stream.removeFromList(active_streams_), active_streams_);\n   748\u2192  }\n   749\u2192\n   750\u2192  // dumpState helper method.\n   751\u2192  virtual void dumpStreams(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const;\n   752\u2192\n   753\u2192  // Send a keepalive ping, and set the idle timer for ping timeout.\n   754\u2192  void sendKeepalive();\n   755\u2192\n   756\u2192  const MonotonicTime& lastReceivedDataTime() { return last_received_data_time_; }\n   757\u2192\n   758\u2192private:\n   759\u2192  friend class Http2CodecImplTestFixture;\n   760\u2192\n   761\u2192  virtual ConnectionCallbacks& callbacks() PURE;\n   762\u2192  virtual Status onBeginHeaders(int32_t stream_id) PURE;\n   763\u2192  int onData(int32_t stream_id, const uint8_t* data, size_t len);\n   764\u2192  Status onBeforeFrameReceived(int32_t stream_id, size_t length, uint8_t type, uint8_t flags);\n   765\u2192  Status onPing(uint64_t opaque_data, bool is_ack);\n   766\u2192  Status onBeginData(int32_t stream_id, size_t length, uint8_t flags, size_t padding);\n   767\u2192  Status onGoAway(uint32_t error_code);\n   768\u2192  Status onHeaders(int32_t stream_id, size_t length, uint8_t flags);\n   769\u2192  Status onRstStream(int32_t stream_id, uint32_t error_code);\n   770\u2192  int onBeforeFrameSend(int32_t stream_id, size_t length, uint8_t type, uint8_t flags);\n   771\u2192  int onFrameSend(int32_t stream_id, size_t length, uint8_t type, uint8_t flags,\n   772\u2192                  uint32_t error_code);\n   773\u2192  int onError(absl::string_view error);\n   774\u2192  virtual int onHeader(int32_t stream_id, HeaderString&& name, HeaderString&& value) PURE;\n   775\u2192  int onInvalidFrame(int32_t stream_id, int error_code);\n   776\u2192  // Pass through invoking with the actual stream.\n   777\u2192  Status onStreamClose(int32_t stream_id, uint32_t error_code);\n   778\u2192  // Should be invoked directly in buffered onStreamClose scenarios\n   779\u2192  // where nghttp2 might have already forgotten about the stream.\n   780\u2192  Status onStreamClose(StreamImpl* stream, uint32_t error_code);\n   781\u2192  int onMetadataReceived(int32_t stream_id, const uint8_t* data, size_t len);\n   782\u2192  int onMetadataFrameComplete(int32_t stream_id, bool end_metadata);\n   783\u2192\n   784\u2192  // Adds buffer fragment for a new outbound frame to the supplied Buffer::OwnedImpl.\n   785\u2192  void addOutboundFrameFragment(Buffer::OwnedImpl& output, const uint8_t* data, size_t length);\n   786\u2192  Status trackInboundFrames(int32_t stream_id, size_t length, uint8_t type, uint8_t flags,\n   787\u2192                            uint32_t padding_length);\n   788\u2192  void onKeepaliveResponse();\n   789\u2192  void onKeepaliveResponseTimeout();\n   790\u2192  bool slowContainsStreamId(int32_t stream_id) const;\n   791\u2192  virtual StreamResetReason getMessagingErrorResetReason() const PURE;\n   792\u2192\n   793\u2192  // Tracks the current slice we're processing in the dispatch loop.\n   794\u2192  const Buffer::RawSlice* current_slice_ = nullptr;\n   795\u2192  // Streams that are pending deferred reset. Using an ordered map provides determinism in the rare\n   796\u2192  // case where there are multiple streams waiting for deferred reset. The stream id is also used to\n   797\u2192  // remove streams from the map when they are closed in order to avoid calls to resetStreamWorker\n   798\u2192  // after the stream has been removed from the active list.\n   799\u2192  std::map<int32_t, StreamImpl*> pending_deferred_reset_streams_;\n   800\u2192  bool dispatching_ : 1;\n   801\u2192  bool raised_goaway_ : 1;\n   802\u2192  Event::SchedulableCallbackPtr protocol_constraint_violation_callback_;\n   803\u2192  Random::RandomGenerator& random_;\n   804\u2192  MonotonicTime last_received_data_time_{};\n   805\u2192  Event::TimerPtr keepalive_send_timer_;\n   806\u2192  Event::TimerPtr keepalive_timeout_timer_;\n   807\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds keepalive_interval_;\n   808\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds keepalive_timeout_;\n   809\u2192  uint32_t keepalive_interval_jitter_percent_;\n   810\u2192};\n   811\u2192\n   812\u2192/**\n   813\u2192 * HTTP/2 client connection codec.\n   814\u2192 */\n   815\u2192class ClientConnectionImpl : public ClientConnection, public ConnectionImpl {\n   816\u2192public:\n   817\u2192  using SessionFactory = Http2SessionFactory;\n   818\u2192  ClientConnectionImpl(Network::Connection& connection, ConnectionCallbacks& callbacks,\n   819\u2192                       CodecStats& stats, Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator,\n   820\u2192                       const envoy::config::core::v3::Http2ProtocolOptions& http2_options,\n   821\u2192                       const uint32_t max_response_headers_kb,\n   822\u2192                       const uint32_t max_response_headers_count,\n   823\u2192                       SessionFactory& http2_session_factory);\n   824\u2192\n   825\u2192  // Http::ClientConnection\n   826\u2192  RequestEncoder& newStream(ResponseDecoder& response_decoder) override;\n   827\u2192\n   828\u2192private:\n   829\u2192  // ConnectionImpl\n   830\u2192  ConnectionCallbacks& callbacks() override { return callbacks_; }\n   831\u2192  Status onBeginHeaders(int32_t stream_id) override;\n   832\u2192  int onHeader(int32_t stream_id, HeaderString&& name, HeaderString&& value) override;\n   833\u2192  void dumpStreams(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n   834\u2192  StreamResetReason getMessagingErrorResetReason() const override;\n   835\u2192  Http::ConnectionCallbacks& callbacks_;\n   836\u2192  std::chrono::milliseconds idle_session_requires_ping_interval_;\n   837\u2192};\n   838\u2192\n   839\u2192/**\n   840\u2192 * HTTP/2 server connection codec.\n   841\u2192 */\n   842\u2192class ServerConnectionImpl : public ServerConnection, public ConnectionImpl {\n   843\u2192public:\n   844\u2192  ServerConnectionImpl(Network::Connection& connection, ServerConnectionCallbacks& callbacks,\n   845\u2192                       CodecStats& stats, Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator,\n   846\u2192                       const envoy::config::core::v3::Http2ProtocolOptions& http2_options,\n   847\u2192                       const uint32_t max_request_headers_kb,\n   848\u2192                       const uint32_t max_request_headers_count,\n   849\u2192                       envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\n   850\u2192                           headers_with_underscores_action,\n   851\u2192                       Server::OverloadManager& overload_manager);\n   852\u2192\n   853\u2192private:\n   854\u2192  // ConnectionImpl\n   855\u2192  ConnectionCallbacks& callbacks() override { return callbacks_; }\n   856\u2192  Status onBeginHeaders(int32_t stream_id) override;\n   857\u2192  int onHeader(int32_t stream_id, HeaderString&& name, HeaderString&& value) override;\n   858\u2192  absl::optional<int> checkHeaderNameForUnderscores(absl::string_view header_name) override;\n   859\u2192  StreamResetReason getMessagingErrorResetReason() const override {\n   860\u2192    return StreamResetReason::LocalReset;\n   861\u2192  }\n   862\u2192\n   863\u2192  // Http::Connection\n   864\u2192  // The reason for overriding the dispatch method is to do flood mitigation only when\n   865\u2192  // processing data from downstream client. Doing flood mitigation when processing upstream\n   866\u2192  // responses makes clean-up tricky, which needs to be improved (see comments for the\n   867\u2192  // ClientConnectionImpl::checkProtocolConstraintsStatus method). The dispatch method on the\n   868\u2192  // ServerConnectionImpl objects is called only when processing data from the downstream client in\n   869\u2192  // the ConnectionManagerImpl::onData method.\n   870\u2192  Http::Status dispatch(Buffer::Instance& data) override;\n   871\u2192\n   872\u2192  ServerConnectionCallbacks& callbacks_;\n   873\u2192\n   874\u2192  // The action to take when a request header name contains underscore characters.\n   875\u2192  envoy::config::core::v3::HttpProtocolOptions::HeadersWithUnderscoresAction\n   876\u2192      headers_with_underscores_action_;\n   877\u2192  Server::LoadShedPoint* should_send_go_away_on_dispatch_{nullptr};\n   878\u2192  bool sent_go_away_on_dispatch_{false};\n   879\u2192};\n   880\u2192\n   881\u2192} // namespace Http2\n   882\u2192} // namespace Http\n   883\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   884\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. "     1\u2192#include \"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <functional>\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/common/enum_to_int.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/http/codes.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/http/header_map_impl.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"source/common/http/header_utility.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192#include \"matching/data_impl.h\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    19\u2192namespace Http {\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192namespace {\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192// Shared helper for recording the latest filter used.\n    24\u2192template <class Filters>\n    25\u2192void recordLatestDataFilter(typename Filters::Iterator current_filter,\n    26\u2192                            typename Filters::Element*& latest_filter, Filters& filters) {\n    27\u2192  // If this is the first time we're calling onData, just record the current filter.\n    28\u2192  if (latest_filter == nullptr) {\n    29\u2192    latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n    30\u2192    return;\n    31\u2192  }\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192  // We want to keep this pointing at the latest filter in the filter list that has received the\n    34\u2192  // onData callback. To do so, we compare the current latest with the *previous* filter. If they\n    35\u2192  // match, then we must be processing a new filter for the first time. We omit this check if we're\n    36\u2192  // the first filter, since the above check handles that case.\n    37\u2192  //\n    38\u2192  // We compare against the previous filter to avoid multiple filter iterations from resetting the\n    39\u2192  // pointer: If we just set latest to current, then the first onData filter iteration would\n    40\u2192  // correctly iterate over the filters and set latest, but on subsequent onData iterations\n    41\u2192  // we'd start from the beginning again, potentially allowing filter N to modify the buffer even\n    42\u2192  // though filter M > N was the filter that inserted data into the buffer.\n    43\u2192  if (current_filter != filters.begin() && latest_filter == std::prev(current_filter)->get()) {\n    44\u2192    latest_filter = current_filter->get();\n    45\u2192  }\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192} // namespace\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() {\n    51\u2192  if (!canContinue()) {\n    52\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"cannot continue filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n    53\u2192                     static_cast<const void*>(this));\n    54\u2192    return;\n    55\u2192  }\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192  // Set ScopeTrackerScopeState if there's no existing crash context.\n    58\u2192  ScopeTrackedObjectStack encapsulated_object;\n    59\u2192  absl::optional<ScopeTrackerScopeState> state;\n    60\u2192  if (parent_.dispatcher_.trackedObjectStackIsEmpty()) {\n    61\u2192    restoreContextOnContinue(encapsulated_object);\n    62\u2192    state.emplace(&encapsulated_object, parent_.dispatcher_);\n    63\u2192  }\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"continuing filter chain: filter={}\", *this,\n    66\u2192                   static_cast<const void*>(this));\n    67\u2192  ASSERT(!canIterate(),\n    68\u2192         \"Attempting to continue iteration while the IterationState is already Continue\");\n    69\u2192  // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, set iterate_from_current_filter_ to true so the\n    70\u2192  // filter iteration starts with the current filter instead of the next one.\n    71\u2192  if (stoppedAll()) {\n    72\u2192    iterate_from_current_filter_ = true;\n    73\u2192  }\n    74\u2192  allowIteration();\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  // Only resume with do1xxHeaders() if we've actually seen 1xx headers.\n    77\u2192  if (has1xxHeaders()) {\n    78\u2192    continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\n    79\u2192    do1xxHeaders();\n    80\u2192    // If the response headers have not yet come in, don't continue on with\n    81\u2192    // headers and body. doHeaders expects request headers to exist.\n    82\u2192    if (!parent_.filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders()) {\n    83\u2192      return;\n    84\u2192    }\n    85\u2192  }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192  // Make sure that we handle the zero byte data frame case. We make no effort to optimize this\n    88\u2192  // case in terms of merging it into a header only request/response. This could be done in the\n    89\u2192  // future.\n    90\u2192  if (!headers_continued_) {\n    91\u2192    headers_continued_ = true;\n    92\u2192    doHeaders(observedEndStream() && !bufferedData() && !hasTrailers());\n    93\u2192  }\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192  doMetadata();\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  // It is possible for trailers to be added during doData(). doData() itself handles continuation\n    98\u2192  // of trailers for the non-continuation case. Thus, we must keep track of whether we had\n    99\u2192  // trailers prior to calling doData(). If we do, then we continue them here, otherwise we rely\n   100\u2192  // on doData() to do so.\n   101\u2192  const bool had_trailers_before_data = hasTrailers();\n   102\u2192  if (bufferedData()) {\n   103\u2192    doData(observedEndStream() && !had_trailers_before_data);\n   104\u2192  }\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192  if (had_trailers_before_data) {\n   107\u2192    doTrailers();\n   108\u2192  }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192  iterate_from_current_filter_ = false;\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(Filter1xxHeadersStatus status) {\n   114\u2192  ASSERT(parent_.state_.has_1xx_headers_);\n   115\u2192  ASSERT(!continued_1xx_headers_);\n   116\u2192  ASSERT(canIterate());\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  switch (status) {\n   119\u2192  case Filter1xxHeadersStatus::Continue:\n   120\u2192    continued_1xx_headers_ = true;\n   121\u2192    return true;\n   122\u2192  case Filter1xxHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n   123\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n   124\u2192    return false;\n   125\u2192  }\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  PANIC_DUE_TO_CORRUPT_ENUM;\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status,\n   131\u2192                                                              bool& end_stream) {\n   132\u2192  ASSERT(!headers_continued_);\n   133\u2192  ASSERT(canIterate());\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  switch (status) {\n   136\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration:\n   137\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n   138\u2192    break;\n   139\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndBuffer:\n   140\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllBuffer;\n   141\u2192    break;\n   142\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::StopAllIterationAndWatermark:\n   143\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n   144\u2192    break;\n   145\u2192  case FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream:\n   146\u2192    end_stream = false;\n   147\u2192    headers_continued_ = true;\n   148\u2192 FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration) {\n   158\u2192    return false;\n   159\u2192  } else {\n   160\u2192    return true;\n   161\u2192  }\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192void ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleBufferData(Buffer::Instance& provided_data) {\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192  // The way we do buffering is a little complicated which is why we have this common function\n   167\u2192  // which is used for both encoding and decoding. When data first comes into our filter pipeline,\n   168\u2192  // we send it through. Any filter can choose to stop iteration and buffer or not. If we then\n   169\u2192  // continue iteration in the future, we use the buffered data. A future filter can stop and\n   170\u2192  // buffer again. In this case, since we are already operating on buffered data, we don't\n   171\u2192  // rebuffer, because we assume the filter has modified the buffer as it wishes in place.\n   172\u2192  if (bufferedData().get() != &provided_data) {\n   173\u2192    if (!bufferedData()) {\n   174\u2192      bufferedData() = createBuffer();\n   175\u2192    }\n   176\u2192    bufferedData()->move(provided_data);\n   177\u2192  }\n   178\u2192}\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192bool ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonHandleAfterDataCallback(FilterDataStatus status,\n   181\u2192                                                           Buffer::Instance& provided_data,\n   182\u2192                                                           bool& buffer_was_streaming) {\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  if (status == FilterDataStatus::Continue) {\n   185\u2192    if (iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopSingleIteration) {\n   186\u2192      commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\n   187\u2192      commonContinue();\n   188\u2192      return false;\n   189\u2192    } else {\n   190\u2192      ASSERT(headers_continued_);\n   191\u2192    }\n   192\u2192  } else {\n   193\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::StopSingleIteration;\n   194\u2192    if (status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndBuffer ||\n   195\u2192        status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark) {\n   196\u2192      buffer_was_streaming = status == FilterDataStatus::StopIterationAndWatermark;\n   197\u2192      commonHandleBufferData(provided_data);\n   198\u2192    } else if (observedEndStream() && !hasTrailers() && !bufferedData() &&\n   199\u2192               // If the stream is destroyed, no need to handle the data buffer or trailers.\n   200\u2192               // This can occur if the filter calls sendLocalReply.\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. 59\u2192namespace Http {\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192const absl::string_view ConnectionManagerImpl::PrematureResetTotalStreamCountKey =\n    62\u2192    \"overload.premature_reset_total_stream_count\";\n    63\u2192const absl::string_view ConnectionManagerImpl::PrematureResetMinStreamLifetimeSecondsKey =\n    64\u2192    \"overload.premature_reset_min_stream_lifetime_seconds\";\n    65\u2192// Runtime key for maximum number of requests that can be processed from a single connection per\n    66\u2192// I/O cycle. Requests over this limit are deferred until the next I/O cycle.\n    67\u2192const absl::string_view ConnectionManagerImpl::MaxRequestsPerIoCycle =\n    68\u2192    \"http.max_requests_per_io_cycle\";\n    69\u2192// Don't attempt to intelligently delay close: https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/30010\n    70\u2192const absl::string_view ConnectionManagerImpl::OptionallyDelayClose =\n    71\u2192    \"http1.optionally_delay_close\";\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192bool requestWasConnect(const RequestHeaderMapSharedPtr& headers, Protocol protocol) {\n    74\u2192  if (!headers) {\n    75\u2192    return false;\n    76\u2192  }\n    77\u2192  if (protocol <= Protocol::Http11) {\n    78\u2192    return HeaderUtility::isConnect(*headers);\n    79\u2192  }\n    80\u2192  // All HTTP/2 style upgrades were originally connect requests.\n    81\u2192  return HeaderUtility::isConnect(*headers) || Utility::isUpgrade(*headers);\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192ConnectionManagerStats ConnectionManagerImpl::generateStats(const std::string& prefix,\n    85\u2192 92\u2192ConnectionManagerTracingStats ConnectionManagerImpl::generateTracingStats(const std::string& prefix,\n    93\u2192 98\u2192ConnectionManagerImpl::generateListenerStats(const std::string& prefix, Stats::Scope& scope) {\n    99\u2192  return {CONN_MAN_LISTENER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER_PREFIX(scope, prefix))};\n   100\u2192}\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192ConnectionManagerImpl::ConnectionManagerImpl(\n   103\u2192    ConnectionManagerConfigSharedPtr config, const Network::DrainDecision& drain_close,\n   104\u2192 config_(std::move(config)), stats_(config_->stats()),\n   109\u2192      conn_length_(new Stats::HistogramCompletableTimespanImpl(\n   110\u2192          stats_.named_.downstream_cx_length_ms_, 134\u2192      trace, accept_new_http_stream_ == nullptr,\n   135\u2192      \"LoadShedPoint envoy.load_shed_points.http_connection_manager_decode_headers is not \"\n   136\u2192      \"found. Is it configured?\");\n   137\u2192  ENVOY_LOG_ONCE_IF(trace, hcm_ondata_creating_codec_ == nullptr,\n   138\u2192                    \"LoadShedPoint envoy.load_shed_points.hcm_ondata_creating_codec is not found. \"\n   139\u2192                    \"Is it configured?\");\n   140\u2192}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192const ResponseHeaderMap& ConnectionManagerImpl::continueHeader() {\n   143\u2192  static const auto headers = createHeaderMap<ResponseHeaderMapImpl>(\n   144\u2192      {{Http::Headers::get().Status, std::to_string(enumToInt(Code::Continue))}});\n   145\u2192  return *headers;\n   146\u2192}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::initializeReadFilterCallbacks(Network::ReadFilterCallbacks& callbacks) {\n   149\u2192  read_callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n   150\u2192  dispatcher_ = &callbacks.connection().dispatcher();\n   151\u2192  if (max_requests_during_dispatch_ != UINT32_MAX) {\n   152\u2192    deferred_request_processing_callback_ =\n   153\u2192        dispatcher_->createSchedulableCallback([this]() -> void { onDeferredRequestProcessing(); });\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  stats_.named_.downstream_cx_total_.inc();\n   157\u2192  stats_.named_.downstream_cx_active_.inc();\n   158\u2192  if (read_callbacks_->connection().ssl()) {\n   159\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_ssl_total_.inc();\n   160\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_ssl_active_.inc();\n   161\u2192  }\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192  read_callbacks_->connection().addConnectionCallbacks(*this);\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192  if (config_->addProxyProtocolConnectionState() &&\n   166\u2192      !read_callbacks_->connection()\n   167\u2192           .streamInfo()\n   168\u2192           .filterState()\n   169\u2192           ->hasData<Network::ProxyProtocolFilterState>(Network::ProxyProtocolFilterState::key())) {\n   170\u2192    read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().filterState()->setData(\n   171\u2192        Network::ProxyProtocolFilterState::key(),\n   172\u2192        std::make_unique<Network::ProxyProtocolFilterState>(Network::ProxyProtocolData{\n   173\u2192            read_callbacks_->connection().connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress(),\n   174\u2192            read_callbacks_->connection().connectionInfoProvider().localAddress()}),\n   175\u2192        StreamInfo::FilterState::StateType::ReadOnly,\n   176\u2192        StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Connection);\n   177\u2192  }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192  if (config_->idleTimeout()) {\n   180\u2192    connection_idle_timer_ =\n   181\u2192        dispatcher_->createScaledTimer(Event::ScaledTimerType::HttpDownstreamIdleConnectionTimeout,\n   182\u2192                                       [this]() -> void { onIdleTimeout(); });\n   183\u2192    connection_idle_timer_->enableTimer(config_->idleTimeout().value());\n   184\u2192  }\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192  if (config_->maxConnectionDuration()) {\n   187\u2192    connection_duration_timer_ =\n   188\u2192        dispatcher_->createScaledTimer(Event::ScaledTimerType::HttpDownstreamMaxConnectionTimeout,\n   189\u2192                                       [this]() -> void { onConnectionDurationTimeout(); });\n   190\u2192    connection_duration_timer_->enableTimer(config_->maxConnectionDuration().value());\n   191\u2192  }\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192  read_callbacks_->connection().setDelayedCloseTimeout(config_->delayedCloseTimeout());\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192  read_callbacks_->connection().setConnectionStats(\n   196\u2192      {stats_.named_.downstream_cx_rx_bytes_total_, stats_.named_.downstream_cx_rx_bytes_buffered_,\n   197\u2192       stats_.named_.downstream_cx_tx_bytes_total_, stats_.named_.downstream_cx_tx_bytes_buffered_,\n   198\u2192       nullptr, &stats_.named_.downstream_cx_delayed_close_timeout_});\n   199\u2192}\n   200\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
77. Grep @ -
Input
{
  "path": "/workspace/envoy/http",
  "pattern": "FilterStatus|FilterHeadersStatus|FilterDataStatus",
  "type": "h"
}
Output
"Found 1 file\nenvoy/http/filter.h"
78. Read @ -
Input
