  "file_path": "/workspace/envoy/http/filter.h",
  "limit": 300
}
Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     4\u2192#include <functional>\n     5\u2192#include <memory>\n     6\u2192#include <string>\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/access_log/access_log.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/buffer/buffer.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/event/dispatcher.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/grpc/status.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"envoy/http/codec.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/http/filter_factory.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"envoy/router/router.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"envoy/router/scopes.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"envoy/ssl/connection.h\"\n    20\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    21\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/load_balancer.h\"\n    22\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/upstream.h\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192#include \"absl/types/optional.h\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    29\u2192namespace Router {\n    30\u2192class RouteConfigProvider;\n    31\u2192}\n    32\u2192namespace Http {\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192/**\n    35\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode headers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n    36\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n    37\u2192 */\n    38\u2192enum class FilterHeadersStatus {\n    39\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n    40\u2192  Continue,\n    41\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n    42\u2192  //\n    43\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n    44\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    45\u2192  //\n    46\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for headers as well as data and\n    47\u2192  // trailers for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n    48\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n    49\u2192  StopIteration,\n    50\u2192  // Continue headers iteration to remaining filters, but delay ending the stream. This status MUST\n    51\u2192  // NOT be returned when end_stream is already set to false.\n    52\u2192  //\n    53\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to add a body to a headers-only request/response, but this body is not\n    54\u2192  // readily available. Delaying end_stream allows the filter to add the body once it's available\n    55\u2192  // without stopping headers iteration.\n    56\u2192  //\n    57\u2192  // The filter is responsible to continue the stream by providing a body through calling\n    58\u2192  // injectDecodedDataToFilterChain()/injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(), possibly multiple times\n    59\u2192  // if the body needs to be divided into several chunks. The filter may need to handle\n    60\u2192  // watermark events when injecting a body, see:\n    61\u2192  // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/main/source/docs/flow_control.md.\n    62\u2192  //\n    63\u2192  // The last call to inject data MUST have end_stream set to true to conclude the stream.\n    64\u2192  // If the filter cannot provide a body the stream should be reset.\n    65\u2192  //\n    66\u2192  // Adding a body through calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData() then\n    67\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is currently NOT supported and causes an assert failure.\n    68\u2192  //\n    69\u2192  // Adding trailers in this scenario is currently NOT supported.\n    70\u2192  //\n    71\u2192  // The filter MUST NOT attempt to continue the stream without providing a body using\n    72\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding().\n    73\u2192  //\n    74\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding a body in this case by calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData()\n    75\u2192  // then continueDecoding()/continueEncoding(). To support this a new FilterManager::IterationState\n    76\u2192  // needs to be added and set when a filter returns this status in\n    77\u2192  // FilterManager::decodeHeaders/FilterManager::encodeHeaders()\n    78\u2192  // Currently, when a filter returns this, the IterationState is Continue. This causes ASSERTs in\n    79\u2192  // FilterManager::commonContinue() to fail when continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is called;\n    80\u2192  // due to trying to continue iteration when the IterationState is already Continue.\n    81\u2192  // In this case, a different ASSERT will be needed to make sure the filter does not try to\n    82\u2192  // continue without adding a body first.\n    83\u2192  //\n    84\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding trailers in this case by implementing new functions to inject\n    85\u2192  // trailers, similar to the inject data functions.\n    86\u2192  ContinueAndDontEndStream,\n    87\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n    88\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. ContinueDecoding() MUST\n    89\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    90\u2192  //\n    91\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n    92\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n    93\u2192  //\n    94\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n    95\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n    96\u2192  //\n    97\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndBuffer is set.\n    98\u2192  StopAllIterationAndBuffer,\n    99\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n   100\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. continueDecoding() MUST\n   101\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   102\u2192  //\n   103\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n   104\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n   105\u2192  //\n   106\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until continueDecoding() function is called.\n   107\u2192  //\n   108\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndWatermark is set.\n   109\u2192  StopAllIterationAndWatermark,\n   110\u2192};\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/**\n   113\u2192 * Return codes for encode on informative headers filter invocations.\n   114\u2192 */\n   115\u2192enum class Filter1xxHeadersStatus {\n   116\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   117\u2192  Continue,\n   118\u2192  // Do not iterate for informative headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n   119\u2192  //\n   120\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from encodeData() or calling\n   121\u2192  // continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   122\u2192  StopIteration\n   123\u2192};\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192/**\n   126\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode data filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   127\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   128\u2192 */\n   129\u2192enum class FilterDataStatus {\n   130\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   131\u2192  // will be sent first via decodeHeaders()/encodeHeaders(). If data has previously been buffered,\n   132\u2192  // the data in this callback will be added to the buffer before the entirety is sent to the next\n   133\u2192  // filter.\n   134\u2192  Continue,\n   135\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   136\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   137\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   138\u2192  //\n   139\u2192  // This should be called by filters which must parse a larger block of the incoming data before\n   140\u2192  // continuing processing and so can not push back on streaming data via watermarks.\n   141\u2192  //\n   142\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n   143\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n   144\u2192  StopIterationAndBuffer,\n   145\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   146\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   147\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   148\u2192  //\n   149\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until one of the continue.*() functions is\n   150\u2192  // called.\n   151\u2192  //\n   152\u2192  // This should be returned by filters which can nominally stream data but have a transient back-up\n   153\u2192  // such as the configured delay of the fault filter, or if the router filter is still fetching an\n   154\u2192  // upstream connection.\n   155\u2192  StopIterationAndWatermark,\n   156\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, but do not buffer any of the\n   157\u2192  // body data for later dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from\n   158\u2192  // decodeData()/encodeData() or calling continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if\n   159\u2192  // continued filter iteration is desired.\n   160\u2192  //\n   161\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for either data or trailers\n   162\u2192  // for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n   163\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n   164\u2192  StopIterationNoBuffer\n   165\u2192};\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192/**\n   168\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode trailers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   169\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   170\u2192 */\n   171\u2192enum class FilterTrailersStatus {\n   172\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   173\u2192  Continue,\n   174\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain. Calling\n   175\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   176\u2192  StopIteration\n   177\u2192};\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192/**\n   180\u2192 * Return codes for encode metadata filter invocations. Metadata currently can not stop filter\n   181\u2192 * iteration except in the case of local replies.\n   182\u2192 */\n   183\u2192enum class FilterMetadataStatus {\n   184\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration for metadata only. Does not unblock returns of StopIteration*\n   185\u2192  // from (decode|encode)(Headers|Data).\n   186\u2192  Continue,\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   189\u2192  // will be sent first via (decode|encode)Headers().\n   190\u2192  ContinueAll,\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  // Stops iteration of the entire filter chain. Only to be used in the case of sending a local\n   193\u2192  // reply from (decode|encode)Metadata.\n   194\u2192  StopIterationForLocalReply,\n   195\u2192};\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192/**\n   198\u2192 * Return codes for onLocalReply filter invocations.\n   199\u2192 */\n   200\u2192enum class LocalErrorStatus {\n   201\u2192  // Continue sending the local reply after onLocalReply has been sent to all filters.\n   202\u2192  Continue,\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  // Continue sending onLocalReply to all filters, but reset the stream once all filters have been\n   205\u2192  // informed rather than sending the local reply.\n   206\u2192  ContinueAndResetStream,\n   207\u2192};\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192// These are events that upstream HTTP filters can register for, via the addUpstreamCallbacks\n   210\u2192// function.\n   211\u2192class UpstreamCallbacks {\n   212\u2192public:\n   213\u2192  virtual ~UpstreamCallbacks() = default;\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192  // Called when the upstream connection is established and\n   216\u2192  // UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks::upstream should be available.\n   217\u2192  //\n   218\u2192  // This indicates that data may begin flowing upstream.\n   219\u2192  virtual void onUpstreamConnectionEstablished() PURE;\n   220\u2192};\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192// These are filter callbacks specific to upstream HTTP filters, accessible via\n   223\u2192// StreamFilterCallbacks::upstreamCallbacks()\n   224\u2192class UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks {\n   225\u2192public:\n   226\u2192  virtual ~UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks() = default;\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192  // Returns a handle to the upstream stream's stream info.\n   229\u2192  virtual StreamInfo::StreamInfo& upstreamStreamInfo() PURE;\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192  // Returns a handle to the generic upstream.\n   232\u2192  virtual OptRef<Router::GenericUpstream> upstream() PURE;\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192  // Dumps state for the upstream request.\n   235\u2192  virtual void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const PURE;\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192  // Setters and getters to determine if sending body payload is paused on\n   238\u2192  // confirmation of a CONNECT upgrade. These should only be used by the upstream codec filter.\n   239\u2192  // TODO(alyssawilk) after deprecating the classic path, move this logic to the\n   240\u2192  // upstream codec filter and remove these APIs\n   241\u2192  virtual bool pausedForConnect() const PURE;\n   242\u2192  virtual void setPausedForConnect(bool value) PURE;\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  // Setters and getters to determine if sending body payload is paused on\n   245\u2192  // confirmation of a WebSocket upgrade. These should only be used by the upstream codec filter.\n   246\u2192  virtual bool pausedForWebsocketUpgrade() const PURE;\n   247\u2192  virtual void setPausedForWebsocketUpgrade(bool value) PURE;\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192  // Return the upstreamStreamOptions for this stream.\n   250\u2192  virtual const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& upstreamStreamOptions() const PURE;\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192  // Adds the supplied UpstreamCallbacks to the list of callbacks to be called\n   253\u2192  // as various upstream events occur. Callbacks should persist for the lifetime\n   254\u2192  // of the upstream stream.\n   255\u2192  virtual void addUpstreamCallbacks(UpstreamCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192  // This should only be called by the UpstreamCodecFilter, and is used to let the\n   258\u2192  // UpstreamCodecFilter supply the interface used by the GenericUpstream to receive\n   259\u2192  // response data from the upstream stream once it is established.\n   260\u2192  virtual void\n   261\u2192  setUpstreamToDownstream(Router::UpstreamToDownstream& upstream_to_downstream_interface) PURE;\n   262\u2192};\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192/**\n   265\u2192 * RouteConfigUpdatedCallback is used to notify an OnDemandRouteUpdate filter about completion of a\n   266\u2192 * RouteConfig update. The filter (and the associated ActiveStream) where the original on-demand\n   267\u2192 * request was originated can be destroyed before a response to an on-demand update request is\n   268\u2192 * received and updates are propagated. To handle this:\n   269\u2192 *\n   270\u2192 * OnDemandRouteUpdate filter instance holds a RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr to a callback.\n   271\u2192 * Envoy::Router::RdsRouteConfigProviderImpl holds a weak pointer to the RouteConfigUpdatedCallback\n   272\u2192 * above in an Envoy::Router::UpdateOnDemandCallback struct\n   273\u2192 *\n   274\u2192 * In RdsRouteConfigProviderImpl::onConfigUpdate(), before invoking the callback, a check is made to\n   275\u2192 * verify if the callback is still available.\n   276\u2192 */\n   277\u2192using RouteConfigUpdatedCallback = std::function<void(bool)>;\n   278\u2192using RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr = std::shared_ptr<RouteConfigUpdatedCallback>;\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192// This class allows entities caching a route (e.g.) ActiveStream to delegate route clearing to xDS\n   281\u2192// components.\n   282\u2192class RouteCache {\n   283\u2192public:\n   284\u2192  virtual ~RouteCache() = default;\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192  // Returns true if the route cache currently has a cached route.\n   287\u2192  virtual bool hasCachedRoute() const PURE;\n   288\u2192  // Forces the route cache to refresh the cached route.\n   289\u2192  virtual void refreshCachedRoute() PURE;\n   290\u2192};\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192class RouteConfigUpdateRequester {\n   293\u2192public:\n   294\u2192  virtual ~RouteConfigUpdateRequester() = default;\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192  virtual void\n   297\u2192  requestRouteConfigUpdate(RouteCache& route_cache,\n   298\u2192                           Http::RouteConfigUpdatedCallbackSharedPtr route_config_updated_cb,\n   299\u2192                           absl::optional<Router::ConfigConstSharedPtr> route_config,\n   300\u2192                           Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) PURE;\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

391\u2192 */\n   392\u2192class FilterManagerCallbacks {\n   393\u2192public:\n   394\u2192  virtual ~FilterManagerCallbacks() = default;\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192  /**\n   397\u2192   * Called when the provided headers have been encoded by all the filters in the chain.\n   398\u2192   * @param response_headers the encoded headers.\n   399\u2192   * @param end_stream whether this is a header only response.\n   400\u2192   */\n   401\u2192  virtual void encodeHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192  /**\n   404\u2192   * Called when the provided 100 Continue headers have been encoded by all the filters in the\n   405\u2192   * chain.\n   406\u2192   * @param response_headers the encoded headers.\n   407\u2192   */\n   408\u2192  virtual void encode1xxHeaders(ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers) PURE;\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192  /**\n   411\u2192   * Called when the provided data has been encoded by all filters in the chain.\n   412\u2192   * @param data the encoded data.\n   413\u2192   * @param end_stream whether this is the end of the response.\n   414\u2192   */\n   415\u2192  virtual void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192  /**\n   418\u2192   * Called when the provided trailers have been encoded by all filters in the chain.\n   419\u2192   * @param trailers the encoded trailers.\n   420\u2192   */\n   421\u2192  virtual void encodeTrailers(ResponseTrailerMap& trailers) PURE;\n   422\u2192\n   423\u2192  /**\n   424\u2192   * Called when the provided metadata has been encoded by all filters in the chain.\n   425\u2192   * @param trailers the encoded trailers.\n   426\u2192   */\n   427\u2192  virtual void encodeMetadata(MetadataMapPtr&& metadata) PURE;\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192  /**\n   430\u2192   * Injects request trailers into a stream that originally did not have request trailers.\n   431\u2192   */\n   432\u2192  virtual void setRequestTrailers(RequestTrailerMapPtr&& request_trailers) PURE;\n   433\u2192\n   434\u2192  /**\n   435\u2192   * Passes ownership of received informational headers to the parent. This may be called multiple\n   436\u2192   * times in the case of multiple upstream calls.\n   437\u2192   */\n   438\u2192  virtual void setInformationalHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) PURE;\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  /**\n   441\u2192   * Passes ownership of received response headers to the parent. This may be called multiple times\n   442\u2192   * in the case of multiple upstream calls.\n   443\u2192   */\n   444\u2192  virtual void setResponseHeaders(ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) PURE;\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192  /**\n   447\u2192   * Passes ownership of received response trailers to the parent. This may be called multiple times\n   448\u2192   * in the case of multiple upstream calls.\n   449\u2192   */\n   450\u2192  virtual void setResponseTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& response_trailers) PURE;\n   451\u2192\n   452\u2192  /**\n   453\u2192   * Optionally updates response code stats based on the details in the headers.\n   454\u2192   */\n   455\u2192  virtual void chargeStats(const ResponseHeaderMap& /*headers*/) {}\n   456\u2192\n   457\u2192  // TODO(snowp): We should consider moving filter access to headers/trailers to happen via the\n   458\u2192  // callbacks instead of via the encode/decode callbacks on the filters.\n   459\u2192\n   460\u2192  /**\n   461\u2192   * The downstream request headers if set.\n   462\u2192   */\n   463\u2192  virtual RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() PURE;\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192  /**\n   466\u2192   * The downstream request trailers if present.\n   467\u2192   */\n   468\u2192  virtual RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() PURE;\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192  /**\n   471\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the continue headers set via the call to setInformationalHeaders.\n   472\u2192   */\n   473\u2192  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() PURE;\n   474\u2192\n   475\u2192  /**\n   476\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the response headers set via the last call to setResponseHeaders.\n   477\u2192   * Note that response headers might be set multiple times (e.g. if a local reply is issued after\n   478\u2192   * headers have been received but before headers have been encoded), so it is not safe in general\n   479\u2192   * to assume that any set of headers will be valid for the duration of a stream.\n   480\u2192   */\n   481\u2192  virtual ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() PURE;\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192  /**\n   484\u2192   * Retrieves a pointer to the last response trailers set via setResponseTrailers.\n   485\u2192   * Note that response trailers might be set multiple times, so it is not safe in general to assume\n   486\u2192   * that any set of trailers will be valid for the duration of the stream.\n   487\u2192   */\n   488\u2192  virtual ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() PURE;\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192  /**\n   491\u2192   * Called after encoding has completed.\n   492\u2192   */\n   493\u2192  virtual void endStream() PURE;\n   494\u2192\n   495\u2192  /**\n   496\u2192   * Attempt to send GOAWAY and close the connection.\n   497\u2192   */\n   498\u2192  virtual void sendGoAwayAndClose() PURE;\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192  /**\n   501\u2192   * Called when the stream write buffer is no longer above the low watermark.\n   502\u2192   */\n   503\u2192  virtual void onDecoderFilterBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() PURE;\n   504\u2192\n   505\u2192  /**\n   506\u2192   * Called when the stream write buffer is above above the high watermark.\n   507\u2192   */\n   508\u2192  virtual void onDecoderFilterAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() PURE;\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192  /**\n   511\u2192   * Called when request activity indicates that the request timeout should be disarmed.\n   512\u2192   */\n   513\u2192  virtual void disarmRequestTimeout() PURE;\n   514\u2192\n   515\u2192  /**\n   516\u2192   * Called when stream activity indicates that the stream idle timeout should be reset.\n   517\u2192   */\n   518\u2192  virtual void resetIdleTimer() PURE;\n   519\u2192\n   520\u2192  /**\n   521\u2192   * Called when the stream should be re-created, e.g. for an internal redirect.\n   522\u2192   */\n   523\u2192  virtual void recreateStream(StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr filter_state) PURE;\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192  /**\n   526\u2192   * Called when the stream should be reset.\n   527\u2192   */\n   528\u2192  virtual void\n   529\u2192  resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason = Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset,\n   530\u2192              absl::string_view transport_failure_reason = \"\") PURE;\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192  /**\n   533\u2192   * Returns the upgrade map for the current route entry.\n   534\u2192   */\n   535\u2192  virtual const Router::RouteEntry::UpgradeMap* upgradeMap() PURE;\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192  /**\n   538\u2192   * Returns the cluster info for the current route entry.\n   539\u2192   */\n   540\u2192  virtual Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() PURE;\n   541\u2192\n   542\u2192  /**\n   543\u2192   * Returns the current active span.\n   544\u2192   */\n   545\u2192  virtual Tracing::Span& activeSpan() PURE;\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192  // TODO(snowp): It might make more sense to pass (optional?) counters to the FM instead of\n   548\u2192  // calling back out to the AS to record them.\n   549\u2192  /**\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. read_callbacks_->connection());\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192  Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr downstream_stream_account =\n   397\u2192      response_encoder.getStream().account();\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192  if (downstream_stream_account == nullptr) {\n   400\u2192    // Create account, wiring the stream to use it for tracking bytes.\n   401\u2192    // If tracking is disabled, the wiring becomes a NOP.\n   402\u2192    auto& buffer_factory = dispatcher_->getWatermarkFactory();\n   403\u2192    downstream_stream_account = buffer_factory.createAccount(response_encoder.getStream());\n   404\u2192    response_encoder.getStream().setAccount(downstream_stream_account);\n   405\u2192  }\n   406\u2192\n   407\u2192  auto new_stream = std::make_unique<ActiveStream>(\n   408\u2192      *this, response_encoder.getStream().bufferLimit(), std::move(downstream_stream_account));\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192  accumulated_requests_++;\n   411\u2192  if (config_->maxRequestsPerConnection() > 0 &&\n   412\u2192      accumulated_requests_ >= config_->maxRequestsPerConnection()) {\n   413\u2192    if (codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2) {\n   414\u2192      new_stream->filter_manager_.streamInfo().setShouldDrainConnectionUponCompletion(true);\n   415\u2192      // Prevent erroneous debug log of closing due to incoming connection close header.\n   416\u2192      drain_state_ = DrainState::Closing;\n   417\u2192    } else if (drain_state_ == DrainState::NotDraining) {\n   418\u2192      startDrainSequence();\n   419\u2192    }\n   420\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"max requests per connection reached\", read_callbacks_->connection());\n   421\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_max_requests_reached_.inc();\n   422\u2192  }\n   423\u2192\n   424\u2192  if (soft_drain_http1_) {\n   425\u2192    new_stream->filter_manager_.streamInfo().setShouldDrainConnectionUponCompletion(true);\n   426\u2192    // Prevent erroneous debug log of closing due to incoming connection close header.\n   427\u2192    drain_state_ = DrainState::Closing;\n   428\u2192  }\n   429\u2192\n   430\u2192  new_stream->state_.is_internally_created_ = is_internally_created;\n   431\u2192  new_stream->response_encoder_ = &response_encoder;\n   432\u2192  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().addCallbacks(*new_stream);\n   433\u2192  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().registerCodecEventCallbacks(new_stream.get());\n   434\u2192  new_stream->response_encoder_->getStream().setFlushTimeout(new_stream->idle_timeout_ms_);\n   435\u2192  new_stream->streamInfo().setDownstreamBytesMeter(response_encoder.getStream().bytesMeter());\n   436\u2192  // If the network connection is backed up, the stream should be made aware of it on creation.\n   437\u2192  // Both HTTP/1.x and HTTP/2 codecs handle this in StreamCallbackHelper::addCallbacksHelper.\n   438\u2192  ASSERT(read_callbacks_->connection().aboveHighWatermark() == false ||\n   439\u2192         new_stream->filter_manager_.aboveHighWatermark());\n   440\u2192  LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(new_stream), streams_);\n   441\u2192  return **streams_.begin();\n   442\u2192}\n   443\u2192\n   444\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::handleCodecErrorImpl(absl::string_view error, absl::string_view details,\n   445\u2192                                                 StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag response_flag) {\n   446\u2192  ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"dispatch error: {}\", read_callbacks_->connection(), error);\n   447\u2192  read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().setResponseFlag(response_flag);\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192  // HTTP/1.1 codec has already sent a 400 response if possible. HTTP/2 codec has already sent\n   450\u2192  // GOAWAY.\n   451\u2192  doConnectionClose(Network::ConnectionCloseType::FlushWriteAndDelay, response_flag, details);\n   452\u2192}\n   453\u2192\n   454\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::handleCodecError(absl::string_view error) {\n   455\u2192  handleCodecErrorImpl(error, absl::StrCat(\"codec_error:\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(error)),\n   456\u2192                       StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::DownstreamProtocolError);\n   457\u2192}\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::handleCodecOverloadError(absl::string_view error) {\n   460\u2192  handleCodecErrorImpl(error,\n   461\u2192                       absl::StrCat(\"overload_error:\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(error)),\n   462\u2192                       StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::OverloadManager);\n   463\u2192}\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::createCodec(Buffer::Instance& data) {\n   466\u2192  ASSERT(!codec_);\n   467\u2192  codec_ = config_->createCodec(read_callbacks_->connection(), data, *this, overload_manager_);\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192  switch (codec_->protocol()) {\n   470\u2192  case Protocol::Http3:\n   471\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http3_total_.inc();\n   472\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http3_active_.inc();\n   473\u2192    break;\n   474\u2192  case Protocol::Http2:\n   475\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http2_total_.inc();\n   476\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http2_active_.inc();\n   477\u2192    break;\n   478\u2192  case Protocol::Http11:\n   479\u2192  case Protocol::Http10:\n   480\u2192    stats_.named_.downstream_cx_http1_total_.inc();\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "   537\u2192void FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   538\u2192                                  bool end_stream) {\n   539\u2192  // Headers filter iteration should always start with the next filter if available.\n   540\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n   541\u2192      commonDecodePrefix(filter, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n   542\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator continue_data_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n   543\u2192  bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n   544\u2192  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n   545\u2192         (*entry)->end_stream_);\n   546\u2192\n   547\u2192  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n   548\u2192    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n   549\u2192    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders));\n   550\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n   551\u2192    (*entry)->end_stream_ = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end());\n   552\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   553\u2192      state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n   554\u2192    }\n   555\u2192    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n   556\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders;\n   557\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   558\u2192      state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n   559\u2192    }\n   560\u2192    if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n   561\u2192      executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n   562\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace,\n   563\u2192                       \"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n   564\u2192                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n   565\u2192      status = FilterHeadersStatus::StopIteration;\n   566\u2192    }\n   567\u2192\n   568\u2192    ASSERT(!(status == FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream && !(*entry)->end_stream_),\n   569\u2192           \"Filters should not return FilterHeadersStatus::ContinueAndDontEndStream from \"\n   570\u2192           \"decodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\");\n   571\u2192\n   572\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode headers called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n   573\u2192                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192    (*entry)->processed_headers_ = true;\n   576\u2192\n   577\u2192    const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n   578\u2192    ENVOY_BUG(!continue_iteration || !stopDecoderFilterChain(),\n   579\u2192              fmt::format(\n   580\u2192                  \"filter={} did not return StopAll or StopIteration after sending a local reply.\",\n   581\u2192                  (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name));\n   582\u2192\n   583\u2192    // If this filter ended the stream, decodeComplete() should be called for it.\n   584\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   585\u2192      (*entry)->handle_->decodeComplete();\n   586\u2192    }\n   587\u2192\n   588\u2192    // Skip processing metadata after sending local reply\n   589\u2192    if (stopDecoderFilterChain() && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   590\u2192      maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n   591\u2192      return;\n   592\u2192    }\n   593\u2192\n   594\u2192    const bool new_metadata_added = processNewlyAddedMetadata();\n   595\u2192    // If end_stream is set in headers, and a filter adds new metadata, we need to delay end_stream\n   596\u2192    // in headers by inserting an empty data frame with end_stream set. The empty data frame is sent\n   597\u2192    // after the new metadata.\n   598\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_ && new_metadata_added && !buffered_request_data_) {\n   599\u2192      Buffer::OwnedImpl empty_data(\"\");\n   600\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(\n   601\u2192          trace, \"inserting an empty data frame for end_stream due metadata being added.\", *this);\n   602\u2192      // Metadata frame doesn't carry end of stream bit. We need an empty data frame to end the\n   603\u2192      // stream.\n   604\u2192      addDecodedData(*(*entry), empty_data, true);\n   605\u2192    }\n   606\u2192\n   607\u2192    if (!continue_iteration && std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   608\u2192      // Stop iteration IFF this is not the last filter. If it is the last filter, continue with\n   609\u2192      // processing since we need to handle the case where a terminal filter wants to buffer, but\n   610\u2192      // a previous filter has added body.\n   611\u2192      maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n   612\u2192      return;\n   613\u2192    }\n   614\u2192\n   615\u2192    // Here we handle the case where we have a header only request, but a filter adds a body\n   616\u2192    // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n   617\u2192    if (end_stream && buffered_request_data_ && continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   618\u2192      continue_data_entry = entry;\n   619\u2192    }\n   620\u2192    // The decoder filter chain is finished here if the following is true:\n   621\u2192    // 1. the last filter has observed the end_stream AND\n   622\u2192    // 2. no filter, including the last filter, has injected body during header iteration.\n   623\u2192    // If body was injected the end_stream will be processed in the `decodeData()` method below.\n   624\u2192    const bool no_body_was_injected = continue_data_entry == decoder_filters_.end();\n   625\u2192    terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream =\n   626\u2192        (std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end() && (*entry)->end_stream_) &&\n   627\u2192        no_body_was_injected;\n   628\u2192  }\n   629\u2192\n   630\u2192  maybeContinueDecoding(continue_data_entry);\n   631\u2192\n   632\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n   633\u2192    disarmRequestTimeout();\n   634\u2192  }\n   635\u2192  maybeEndDecode(terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream);\n   636\u2192}\n   637\u2192\n   638\u2192void FilterManager::decodeData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n   639\u2192                               bool end_stream,\n   640\u2192                               FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n   641\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(this, dispatcher_);\n   642\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n   643\u2192\n   644\u2192  // If a response is complete or a reset has been sent, filters do not care about further body\n   645\u2192  // data. Just drop it.\n   646\u2192  if (stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\n   647\u2192    return;\n   648\u2192  }\n   649\u2192\n   650\u2192  auto trailers_added_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n   651\u2192  const bool trailers_exists_at_start = filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers().has_value();\n   652\u2192  // Filter iteration may start at the current filter.\n   653\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry = commonDecodePrefix(filter, filter_iteration_start_state);\n   654\u2192  bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n   655\u2192  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n   656\u2192         (*entry)->end_stream_);\n   657\u2192\n   658\u2192  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n   659\u2192    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n   660\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame types, return now.\n   661\u2192    if (handleDataIfStopAll(**entry, data, state_.decoder_filters_streaming_)) {\n   662\u2192      return;\n   663\u2192    }\n   664\u2192    // If end_stream_ is marked for a filter, the data is not for this filter and filters after.\n   665\u2192    //\n   666\u2192    // In following case, ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() could be called recursively and\n   667\u2192    // its doData() is called with wrong data.\n   668\u2192    //\n   669\u2192    //  There are 3 decode filters and \"wrapper\" refers to ActiveStreamFilter object.\n   670\u2192    //\n   671\u2192    //  filter0->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n   672\u2192    //    return STOP\n   673\u2192    //  filter0->continueDecoding()\n   674\u2192    //    wrapper0->commonContinue()\n   675\u2192    //      wrapper0->decodeHeaders(_, _, true)\n   676\u2192    //        filter1->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n   677\u2192    //          filter1->addDecodeData()\n   678\u2192    //          return CONTINUE\n   679\u2192    //        filter2->decodeHeaders(_, false)\n   680\u2192    //          return CONTINUE\n   681\u2192    //        wrapper1->commonContinue() // Detects data is added.\n   682\u2192    //          wrapper1->doData()\n   683\u2192    //            wrapper1->decodeData()\n   684\u2192    //              filter2->decodeData(_, true)\n   685\u2192    //                 return CONTINUE\n   686\u2192    //      wrapper0->doData() // This should not be called\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   638\u2192void FilterManager::decodeData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n   639\u2192                               bool end_stream,\n   640\u2192                               FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n   641\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(this, dispatcher_);\n   642\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n   643\u2192\n   644\u2192  // If a response is complete or a reset has been sent, filters do not care about further body\n   645\u2192  // data. Just drop it.\n   646\u2192  if (stopDecoderFilterChain()) {\n   647\u2192    return;\n   648\u2192  }\n   649\u2192\n   650\u2192  auto trailers_added_entry = decoder_filters_.end();\n   651\u2192  const bool trailers_exists_at_start = filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers().has_value();\n   652\u2192  // Filter iteration may start at the current filter.\n   653\u2192  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry = commonDecodePrefix(filter, filter_iteration_start_state);\n   654\u2192  bool terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = false;\n   655\u2192  ASSERT(!state_.decoder_filter_chain_complete_ || entry == decoder_filters_.end() ||\n   656\u2192         (*entry)->end_stream_);\n   657\u2192\n   658\u2192  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n   659\u2192    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n   660\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame types, return now.\n   661\u2192    if (handleDataIfStopAll(**entry, data, state_.decoder_filters_streaming_)) {\n   662\u2192      return;\n   663\u2192    }\n   664\u2192    // If end_stream_ is marked for a filter, the data is not for this filter and filters after.\n   665\u2192    //\n   666\u2192    // In following case, ActiveStreamFilterBase::commonContinue() could be called recursively and\n   667\u2192    // its doData() is called with wrong data.\n   668\u2192    //\n   669\u2192    //  There are 3 decode filters and \"wrapper\" refers to ActiveStreamFilter object.\n   670\u2192    //\n   671\u2192    //  filter0->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n   672\u2192    //    return STOP\n   673\u2192    //  filter0->continueDecoding()\n   674\u2192    //    wrapper0->commonContinue()\n   675\u2192    //      wrapper0->decodeHeaders(_, _, true)\n   676\u2192    //        filter1->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n   677\u2192    //          filter1->addDecodeData()\n   678\u2192    //          return CONTINUE\n   679\u2192    //        filter2->decodeHeaders(_, false)\n   680\u2192    //          return CONTINUE\n   681\u2192    //        wrapper1->commonContinue() // Detects data is added.\n   682\u2192    //          wrapper1->doData()\n   683\u2192    //            wrapper1->decodeData()\n   684\u2192    //              filter2->decodeData(_, true)\n   685\u2192    //                 return CONTINUE\n   686\u2192    //      wrapper0->doData() // This should not be called\n   687\u2192    //        wrapper0->decodeData()\n   688\u2192    //          filter1->decodeData(_, true)  // It will cause assertions.\n   689\u2192    //\n   690\u2192    // One way to solve this problem is to mark end_stream_ for each filter.\n   691\u2192    // If a filter is already marked as end_stream_ when decodeData() is called, bails out the\n   692\u2192    // whole function. If just skip the filter, the codes after the loop will be called with\n   693\u2192    // wrong data. For encodeData, the response_encoder->encode() will be called.\n   694\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   695\u2192      return;\n   696\u2192    }\n   697\u2192    ASSERT(!(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeData));\n   698\u2192\n   699\u2192    // We check the request_trailers_ pointer here in case addDecodedTrailers\n   700\u2192    // is called in decodeData during a previous filter invocation, at which point we communicate to\n   701\u2192    // the current and future filters that the stream has not yet ended.\n   702\u2192    if (end_stream) {\n   703\u2192      state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n   704\u2192    }\n   705\u2192\n   706\u2192    recordLatestDataFilter(entry, state_.latest_data_decoding_filter_, decoder_filters_);\n   707\u2192\n   708\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::DecodeData;\n   709\u2192    (*entry)->end_stream_ = end_stream && !filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers();\n   710\u2192    FilterDataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->decodeData(data, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n   711\u2192    if ((*entry)->end_stream_) {\n   712\u2192      (*entry)->handle_->decodeComplete();\n   713\u2192    }\n   714\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::DecodeData;\n   715\u2192    if (end_stream) {\n   716\u2192      state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EndOfStream;\n   717\u2192    }\n   718\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decode data called: filter={} status={}\", *this,\n   719\u2192                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status));\n   720\u2192    if (state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_) {\n   721\u2192      executeLocalReplyIfPrepared();\n   722\u2192      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decodeData filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n   723\u2192                       *this, (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name);\n   724\u2192      return;\n   725\u2192    }\n   726\u2192\n   727\u2192    processNewlyAddedMetadata();\n   728\u2192\n   729\u2192    if (!trailers_exists_at_start && filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers() &&\n   730\u2192        trailers_added_entry == decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   731\u2192      end_stream = false;\n   732\u2192      trailers_added_entry = entry;\n   733\u2192    }\n   734\u2192\n   735\u2192    // The decoder filter chain is finished here if the following is true:\n   736\u2192    // 1. the last filter has observed the end_stream AND\n   737\u2192    // 2. no filter, including the last filter, has injected trailers during header iteration.\n   738\u2192    //    NOTE: end_stream is set to false above if a filter injected trailers.\n   739\u2192    // If trailers were injected the end_stream will be processed in the `decodeTrailers()` method\n   740\u2192    // below.\n   741\u2192    terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream = end_stream && std::next(entry) == decoder_filters_.end();\n   742\u2192\n   743\u2192    if (!(*entry)->commonHandleAfterDataCallback(status, data, state_.decoder_filters_streaming_) &&\n   744\u2192        std::next(entry) != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   745\u2192      // Stop iteration IFF this is not the last filter. If it is the last filter, continue with\n   746\u2192      // processing since we need to handle the case where a terminal filter wants to buffer, but\n   747\u2192      // a previous filter has added trailers.\n   748\u2192      break;\n   749\u2192    }\n   750\u2192  }\n   751\u2192\n   752\u2192  // If trailers were adding during decodeData we need to trigger decodeTrailers in order\n   753\u2192  // to allow filters to process the trailers.\n   754\u2192  if (trailers_added_entry != decoder_filters_.end()) {\n   755\u2192    decodeTrailers(trailers_added_entry->get(), *filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers());\n   756\u2192  }\n   757\u2192\n   758\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n   759\u2192    disarmRequestTimeout();\n   760\u2192  }\n   761\u2192  maybeEndDecode(terminal_filter_decoded_end_stream);\n   762\u2192}\n   763\u2192\n   764\u2192RequestTrailerMap& FilterManager::addDecodedTrailers() {\n   765\u2192  // Trailers can only be added during the last data frame (i.e. end_stream = true).\n   766\u2192  ASSERT(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EndOfStream);\n   767\u2192\n   768\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.setRequestTrailers(RequestTrailerMapImpl::create());\n   769\u2192  return *filter_manager_callbacks_.requestTrailers();\n   770\u2192}\n   771\u2192\n   772\u2192void FilterManager::addDecodedData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n   773\u2192                                   bool streaming) {\n   774\u2192  if (state_.filter_call_state_ == 0 ||\n   775\u2192      (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeHeaders) ||\n   776\u2192      (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeData) ||\n   777\u2192 state_.decoder_filters_streaming_ = streaming;\n   780\u2192    // If no call is happening or we are in the decode headers/data callback, buffer the data.\n   781\u2192    // Inline processing happens in the decodeHeaders() callback if necessary.\n   782\u2192    filter.commonHandleBufferData(data);\n   783\u2192  } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::DecodeTrailers) {\n   784\u2192    // In this case we need to inline dispatch the data to further filters. If those filters\n   785\u2192    // choose to buffer/stop iteration that's fine.\n   786\u2192    decodeData(&filter, data, false, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n   787\u2192  } else {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "   486\u2192Network::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool) {\n   487\u2192  requests_during_dispatch_count_ = 0;\n   488\u2192  if (!codec_) {\n   489\u2192    // Close connections if Envoy is under pressure, typically memory, before creating codec.\n   490\u2192    if (hcm_ondata_creating_codec_ != nullptr && hcm_ondata_creating_codec_->shouldShedLoad()) {\n   491\u2192      stats_.named_.downstream_rq_overload_close_.inc();\n   492\u2192      handleCodecOverloadError(\"onData codec creation overload\");\n   493\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   494\u2192    }\n   495\u2192    // Http3 codec should have been instantiated by now.\n   496\u2192    createCodec(data);\n   497\u2192  }\n   498\u2192\n   499\u2192  bool redispatch;\n   500\u2192  do {\n   501\u2192    redispatch = false;\n   502\u2192\n   503\u2192    const Status status = codec_->dispatch(data);\n   504\u2192\n   505\u2192    if (isBufferFloodError(status) || isInboundFramesWithEmptyPayloadError(status)) {\n   506\u2192      handleCodecError(status.message());\n   507\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   508\u2192    } else if (isCodecProtocolError(status)) {\n   509\u2192      stats_.named_.downstream_cx_protocol_error_.inc();\n   510\u2192      handleCodecError(status.message());\n   511\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   512\u2192    } else if (isEnvoyOverloadError(status)) {\n   513\u2192      // The other codecs aren't wired to send this status.\n   514\u2192      ASSERT(codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2,\n   515\u2192             \"Expected only HTTP1.1 and below to send overload error.\");\n   516\u2192      stats_.named_.downstream_rq_overload_close_.inc();\n   517\u2192      handleCodecOverloadError(status.message());\n   518\u2192      return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   519\u2192    }\n   520\u2192    ASSERT(status.ok());\n   521\u2192\n   522\u2192    // Processing incoming data may release outbound data so check for closure here as well.\n   523\u2192    checkForDeferredClose(false);\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192    // The HTTP/1 codec will pause dispatch after a single message is complete. We want to\n   526\u2192    // either redispatch if there are no streams and we have more data. If we have a single\n   527\u2192    // complete non-WebSocket stream but have not responded yet we will pause socket reads\n   528\u2192    // to apply back pressure.\n   529\u2192    if (codec_->protocol() < Protocol::Http2) {\n   530\u2192      if (read_callbacks_->connection().state() == Network::Connection::State::Open &&\n   531\u2192          data.length() > 0 && streams_.empty()) {\n   532\u2192        redispatch = true;\n   533\u2192      }\n   534\u2192    }\n   535\u2192  } while (redispatch);\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192  if (!read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol()) {\n   538\u2192    read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol(codec_->protocol());\n   539\u2192  }\n   540\u2192\n   541\u2192  return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   542\u2192}\n   543\u2192\n   544\u2192Network::FilterStatus ConnectionManagerImpl::onNewConnection() {\n   545\u2192  if (!read_callbacks_->connection().streamInfo().protocol()) {\n   546\u2192    // For Non-QUIC traffic, continue passing data to filters.\n   547\u2192    return Network::FilterStatus::Continue;\n   548\u2192  }\n   549\u2192  // Only QUIC connection's stream_info_ specifies protocol.\n   550\u2192  Buffer::OwnedImpl dummy;\n   551\u2192  createCodec(dummy);\n   552\u2192  ASSERT(codec_->protocol() == Protocol::Http3);\n   553\u2192  // Stop iterating through network filters for QUIC. Currently QUIC connections bypass the\n   554\u2192  // onData() interface because QUICHE already handles de-multiplexing.\n   555\u2192  return Network::FilterStatus::StopIteration;\n   556\u2192}\n   557\u2192\n   558\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::resetAllStreams(\n   559\u2192    absl::optional<StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag> response_flag, absl::string_view details) {\n   560\u2192  while (!streams_.empty()) {\n   561\u2192    // Mimic a downstream reset in this case. We must also remove callbacks here. Though we are\n   562\u2192    // about to close the connection and will disable further reads, it is possible that flushing\n   563\u2192    // data out can cause stream callbacks to fire (e.g., low watermark callbacks).\n   564\u2192    //\n   565\u2192    // TODO(mattklein123): I tried to actually reset through the codec here, but ran into issues\n   566\u2192    // with nghttp2 state and being unhappy about sending reset frames after the connection had\n   567\u2192    // been terminated via GOAWAY. It might be possible to do something better here inside the h2\n   568\u2192    // codec but there are no easy answers and this seems simpler.\n   569\u2192    auto& stream = *streams_.front();\n   570\u2192    stream.response_encoder_->getStream().removeCallbacks(stream);\n   571\u2192    if (!stream.response_encoder_->getStream().responseDetails().empty()) {\n   572\u2192      stream.filter_manager_.streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(\n   573\u2192          stream.response_encoder_->getStream().responseDetails());\n   574\u2192    } else if (!details.empty()) {\n   575\u2192      stream.filter_manager_.streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n   576\u2192    }\n   577\u2192    if (response_flag.has_value()) {\n   578\u2192      stream.filter_manager_.streamInfo().setResponseFlag(response_flag.value());\n   579\u2192    }\n   580\u2192    stream.onResetStream(StreamResetReason::ConnectionTermination, absl::string_view());\n   581\u2192  }\n   582\u2192}\n   583\u2192\n   584\u2192void ConnectionManagerImpl::onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) {\n   585\u2192  if (event == Network::ConnectionEvent::LocalClose) {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "  1290\u2192void ServerConnectionImpl::onBody(Buffer::Instance& data) {\n  1291\u2192  ASSERT(!deferred_end_stream_headers_);\n  1292\u2192  if (active_request_) {\n  1293\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(trace, \"body size={}\", connection_, data.length());\n  1294\u2192    active_request_->request_decoder_->decodeData(data, false);\n  1295\u2192  }\n  1296\u2192}\n  1297\u2192\n  1298\u2192Http::Status ServerConnectionImpl::dispatch(Buffer::Instance& data) {\n  1299\u2192  if (abort_dispatch_ != nullptr && abort_dispatch_->shouldShedLoad()) {\n  1300\u2192    RETURN_IF_ERROR(sendOverloadError());\n  1301\u2192    return envoyOverloadError(\"Aborting Server Dispatch\");\n  1302\u2192  }\n  1303\u2192\n  1304\u2192  if (active_request_ != nullptr && active_request_->remote_complete_) {\n  1305\u2192    // Eagerly read disable the connection if the downstream is sending pipelined requests as we\n  1306\u2192    // serially process them. Reading from the connection will be re-enabled after the active\n  1307\u2192    // request is completed.\n  1308\u2192    active_request_->response_encoder_.readDisable(true);\n  1309\u2192    return okStatus();\n  1310\u2192  }\n  1311\u2192\n  1312\u2192  Http::Status status = ConnectionImpl::dispatch(data);\n  1313\u2192\n  1314\u2192  if (active_request_ != nullptr && active_request_->remote_complete_) {\n  1315\u2192    // Read disable the connection if the downstream is sending additional data while we are working\n  1316\u2192    // on an existing request. Reading from the connection will be re-enabled after the active\n  1317\u2192    // request is completed.\n  1318\u2192    if (data.length() > 0) {\n  1319\u2192      active_request_->response_encoder_.readDisable(true);\n  1320\u2192    }\n  1321\u2192  }\n  1322\u2192  return status;\n  1323\u2192}\n  1324\u2192\n  1325\u2192CallbackResult ServerConnectionImpl::onMessageCompleteBase() {\n  1326\u2192  ASSERT(!handling_upgrade_);\n  1327\u2192  if (active_request_) {\n  1328\u2192\n  1329\u2192    // The request_decoder should be non-null after we've called the newStream on callbacks.\n  1330\u2192    ASSERT(active_request_->request_decoder_);\n  1331\u2192    active_request_->remote_complete_ = true;\n  1332\u2192\n  1333\u2192    if (deferred_end_stream_headers_) {\n  1334\u2192      active_request_->request_decoder_->decodeHeaders(\n  1335\u2192          std::move(absl::get<RequestHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)), true);\n  1336\u2192      deferred_end_stream_headers_ = false;\n  1337\u2192    } else if (processing_trailers_) {\n  1338\u2192      active_request_->request_decoder_->decodeTrailers(\n  1339\u2192          std::move(absl::get<RequestTrailerMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_)));\n  1340\u2192    } else {\n  1341\u2192      Buffer::OwnedImpl buffer;\n  1342\u2192      active_request_->request_decoder_->decodeData(buffer, true);\n  1343\u2192    }\n  1344\u2192\n  1345\u2192    // Reset to ensure no information from one requests persists to the next.\n  1346\u2192    headers_or_trailers_.emplace<RequestHeaderMapPtr>(nullptr);\n  1347\u2192  }\n  1348\u2192\n  1349\u2192  // Always pause the parser so that the calling code can process 1 request at a time and apply\n  1350\u2192  // back pressure. However this means that the calling code needs to detect if there is more data\n  1351\u2192  // in the buffer and dispatch it again.\n  1352\u2192  return parser_->pause();\n  1353\u2192}\n  1354\u2192\n  1355\u2192void ServerConnectionImpl::onResetStream(StreamResetReason reason) {\n  1356\u2192  if (active_request_) {\n  1357\u2192    active_request_->response_encoder_.runResetCallbacks(reason, absl::string_view());\n  1358\u2192    connection_.dispatcher().deferredDelete(std::move(active_request_));\n  1359\u2192  }\n  1360\u2192}\n  1361\u2192\n  1362\u2192Status ServerConnectionImpl::sendOverloadError() {\n  1363\u2192  const bool latched_dispatching = dispatching_;\n  1364\u2192\n  1365\u2192  // The codec might be in the early stages of server dispatching where this isn't yet\n  1366\u2192  // flipped to true.\n  1367\u2192  dispatching_ = true;\n  1368\u2192  error_code_ = Http::Code::InternalServerError;\n  1369\u2192  auto status = sendProtocolError(Envoy::StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().Overload);\n  1370\u2192  dispatching_ = latched_dispatching;\n  1371\u2192  return status;\n  1372\u2192}\n  1373\u2192\n  1374\u2192Status ServerConnectionImpl::sendProtocolError(absl::string_view details) {\n  1375\u2192  // We do this here because we may get a protocol error before we have a logical stream.\n  1376\u2192  if (active_request_ == nullptr) {\n  1377\u2192    RETURN_IF_ERROR(onMessageBeginImpl());\n  1378\u2192  }\n  1379\u2192\n  1380\u2192  if (resetStreamCalled()) {\n  1381\u2192    return okStatus();\n  1382\u2192  }\n  1383\u2192  ASSERT(active_request_);\n  1384\u2192\n  1385\u2192  active_request_->response_encoder_.setDetails(details);\n  1386\u2192  if (!active_request_->response_encoder_.startedResponse()) {\n  1387\u2192    active_request_->request_decoder_->sendLocalReply(\n  1388\u2192        error_code_, CodeUtility::toString(error_code_), nullptr, absl::nullopt, details);\n  1389\u2192  }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"  1199\u2192Envoy::StatusOr<CallbackResult> ServerConnectionImpl::onHeadersCompleteBase() {\n  1200\u2192  // Handle the case where response happens prior to request complete. It's up to upper layer code\n  1201\u2192  // to disconnect the connection but we shouldn't fire any more events since it doesn't make\n  1202\u2192  // sense.\n  1203\u2192  if (active_request_) {\n  1204\u2192    auto& headers = absl::get<RequestHeaderMapPtr>(headers_or_trailers_);\n  1205\u2192    ENVOY_CONN_LOG(trace, \"Server: onHeadersComplete size={}\", connection_, headers->size());\n  1206\u2192\n  1207\u2192    if (!handling_upgrade_ && headers->Connection()) {\n  1208\u2192      // If we fail to sanitize the request, return a 400 to the client\n  1209\u2192      if (!Utility::sanitizeConnectionHeader(*headers)) {\n  1210\u2192        absl::string_view header_value = headers->getConnectionValue();\n  1211\u2192        ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"Invalid nominated headers in Connection: {}\", connection_,\n  1212\u2192                       header_value);\n  1213\u2192        error_code_ = Http::Code::BadRequest;\n  1214\u2192        RETURN_IF_ERROR(\n  1215\u2192            sendProtocolError(Http1ResponseCodeDetails::get().ConnectionHeaderSanitization));\n  1216\u2192        return codecProtocolError(\"Invalid nominated headers in Connection.\");\n  1217\u2192      }\n  1218\u2192    }\n  1219\u2192\n  1220\u2192    // Inform the response encoder about any HEAD method, so it can set content\n  1221\u2192    // length and transfer encoding headers correctly.\n  1222\u2192    const Http::HeaderValues& header_values = Http::Headers::get();\n  1223\u2192    active_request_->response_encoder_.setIsResponseToHeadRequest(parser_->methodName() ==\n  1224\u2192                                                                  header_values.MethodValues.Head);\n  1225\u2192    active_request_->response_encoder_.setIsResponseToConnectRequest(\n  1226\u2192        parser_->methodName() == header_values.MethodValues.Connect);\n  1227\u2192\n  1228\u2192    RETURN_IF_ERROR(handlePath(*headers, parser_->methodName()));\n  1229\u2192    ASSERT(active_request_->request_url_.empty());\n  1230\u2192\n  1231\u2192    headers->setMethod(parser_->methodName());\n  1232\u2192    RETURN_IF_ERROR(checkProtocolVersion(*headers));\n  1233\u2192\n  1234\u2192    // Make sure the host is valid.\n  1235\u2192    auto details = HeaderUtility::requestHeadersValid(*headers);\n  1236\u2192    if (details.has_value()) {\n  1237\u2192      RETURN_IF_ERROR(sendProtocolError(details.value().get()));\n  1238\u2192      return codecProtocolError(\n  1239\u2192          \"http/1.1 protocol error: request headers failed spec compliance checks\");\n  1240\u2192    }\n  1241\u2192\n  1242\u2192    // Determine here whether we have a body or not. This uses the new RFC semantics where the\n  1243\u2192    // presence of content-length or chunked transfer-encoding indicates a body vs. a particular\n  1244\u2192    // method. If there is no body, we defer raising decodeHeaders() until the parser is flushed\n  1245\u2192    // with message complete. This allows upper layers to behave like HTTP/2 and prevents a proxy\n  1246\u2192    // scenario where the higher layers stream through and implicitly switch to chunked transfer\n  1247\u2192    // encoding because end stream with zero body length has not yet been indicated.\n  1248\u2192    if (parser_->isChunked() ||\n  1249\u2192        (parser_->contentLength().has_value() && parser_->contentLength().value() > 0) ||\n  1250\u2192        handling_upgrade_) {\n  1251\u2192      active_request_->request_decoder_->decodeHeaders(std::move(headers), false);\n  1252\u2192\n  1253\u2192      // If the connection has been closed (or is closing) after decoding headers, pause the parser\n  1254\u2192      // so we return control to the caller.\n  1255\u2192      if (connection_.state() != Network::Connection::State::Open) {\n  1256\u2192        return parser_->pause();\n  1257\u2192      }\n  1258\u2192    } else {\n  1259\u2192      deferred_end_stream_headers_ = true;\n  1260\u2192    }\n  1261\u2192  }\n  1262\u2192\n  1263\u2192  return CallbackResult::Success;\n  1264\u2192}\n  1265\u2192\n  1266\u2192Status ServerConnectionImpl::onMessageBeginBase() {\n  1267\u2192  if (!resetStreamCalled()) {\n  1268\u2192    ASSERT(active_request_ == nullptr);\n  1269\u2192    active_request_ = std::make_unique<ActiveRequest>(*this, std::move(bytes_meter_before_stream_));\n  1270\u2192    active_request_->request_decoder_ = &callbacks_.newStream(active_request_->response_encoder_);\n  1271\u2192\n  1272\u2192    // Check for pipelined request flood as we prepare to accept a new request.\n  1273\u2192    // Parse errors that happen prior to onMessageBegin result in stream termination, it is not\n  1274\u2192    // possible to overflow output buffers with early parse errors.\n  1275\u2192    RETURN_IF_ERROR(doFloodProtectionChecks());\n  1276\u2192  }\n  1277\u2192  return okStatus();\n  1278\u2192}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "112:      : parent_(parent), iteration_state_(IterationState::Continue),\n129:  void commonBufferDataIfStopAll(Buffer::Instance& provided_data, bool& buffer_was_streaming);\n131:  void commonContinue();\n132:  virtual bool canContinue() PURE;\n160:  void restoreContextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) override;\n175:  bool canIterate() { return iteration_state_ == IterationState::Continue; }\n177:    return iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopAllBuffer ||\n178:           iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n181:    ASSERT(iteration_state_ != IterationState::Continue);\n182:    iteration_state_ = IterationState::Continue;\n205:  // either because [de|en]codeHeaders() of the current filter returns StopAllIteration or because\n211:  enum class IterationState : uint8_t {\n212:    Continue,            // Iteration has not stopped for any frame type.\n213:    StopSingleIteration, // Iteration has stopped for headers, 100-continue, or data.\n214:    StopAllBuffer,       // Iteration has stopped for all frame types, and following data should\n216:    StopAllWatermark,    // Iteration has stopped for all frame types, and following data should\n224:  // If the filter resumes iteration from a StopAllBuffer/Watermark state, the current filter\n249:  bool canContinue() override;\n344:  bool canContinue() override;\n404:   * Called when the provided 100 Continue headers have been encoded by all the filters in the\n905:  void contextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack);\n913:    // Stop filter chain iteration by checking encoder or decoder chain.\n947:    // is ever called, this is set to true so commonContinue resumes processing the 1xx.\n953:    // Tracks if headers other than 100-Continue have been encoded to the codec.\n1053:  void maybeContinueDecoding(StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator maybe_continue_data_entry);\n1067:  void maybeContinueEncoding(StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator maybe_continue_data_entry);\n1084:  bool handleDataIfStopAll(ActiveStreamFilterBase& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,"
