Input
{
  "description": "Explore route resolution and upstream selection",
  "prompt": "Explore the Envoy codebase to understand route resolution and upstream selection:\n\n1. Find the Router filter implementation and its decodeHeaders() method\n2. Find how routes are matched against the request\n3. Find how route resolution determines which upstream cluster to use\n4. Find how upstream hosts are selected from a cluster\n5. Find what triggers upstream connection creation\n\nSearch for:\n- Router filter in extensions/filters/http/ - look for \"router\" directory\n- RouteEntry and RouteMatching classes\n- Cluster selection logic\n- ConnectionPool and how it creates upstream connections\n- RouteConfiguration loading\n\nReturn specific file paths, class names, and method names with line numbers.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "af794ade81299d32e",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all the findings:\n\n## Envoy Route Resolution and Upstream Selection - Complete Analysis\n\nBased on my exploration of the Envoy codebase, here is the comprehensive understanding of how route resolution and upstream selection works:\n\n### 1. Router Filter Implementation - decodeHeaders() Method\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.h` (line 347)\n- **Class:** `Filter : public Http::StreamDecoderFilter`\n- **Header file:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.h`\n- **Implementation file:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` (line 445)\n\n**Method Signature:**\n```cpp\nHttp::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::decodeHeaders(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream)\n```\n\n**Key Steps in decodeHeaders() (lines 445-704 in router.cc):**\n\n1. **Route Lookup** (lines 468-477):\n   - Calls `callbacks_->route()` to get the route from the route configuration\n   - If no route found, returns 404 NotFound\n   - Sets `StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoRouteFound`\n\n2. **Direct Response Check** (lines 480-503):\n   - Checks if the route has a direct response entry\n   - If direct response exists, sends local reply instead of forwarding upstream\n\n3. **Cluster Resolution** (lines 505-529):\n   - Extracts route entry via `route_->routeEntry()`\n   - Gets cluster name via `route_entry_->clusterName()`\n   - Looks up cluster from cluster manager: `config_->cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(route_entry_->clusterName())`\n   - If cluster not found, returns 503 Service Unavailable\n\n4. **Host Selection - Synchronous/Asynchronous** (lines 664-704):\n   - Calls `cluster->chooseHost(this)` to select upstream host using load balancer\n   - If asynchronous host selection is enabled, defers to async callback\n   - Otherwise immediately calls `continueDecodeHeaders()`\n\n### 2. Route Matching Logic\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/config_impl.h` (lines 64-77, 647-668)\n\n**Core Matching Interface:**\n```cpp\nclass Matchable {\n  virtual RouteConstSharedPtr matches(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n                                      const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info,\n                                      uint64_t random_value) const PURE;\n};\n```\n\n**Route Entry Base Class:**\n- **Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/config_impl.h` (line 647)\n- **Class:** `RouteEntryImplBase : public Matchable`\n- **Key method:** `bool matchRoute(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, const StreamInfo::StreamInfo& stream_info, uint64_t random_value) const`\n- **Implementation:** `/workspace/source/common/router/config_impl.cc` (line 913)\n\n**Route Entry Implementations:**\n- `UriTemplateMatcherRouteEntryImpl` (line 1266)\n- `PrefixRouteEntryImpl` (line 1300)\n- `PathRouteEntryImpl` (line 1333)\n- `RegexRouteEntryImpl` (line 1365)\n- `ConnectRouteEntryImpl` (line 1397)\n- `PathSeparatedPrefixRouteEntryImpl` (line 1429)\n\n**Route Matching occurs in:**\n- `/workspace/source/common/router/config_impl.cc` lines 1691, 1722, 1754, 1793, 1824, 1855\n\n### 3. Route Resolution - Determining Upstream Cluster\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/config_impl.h` (lines 1569-1694)\n\n**Configuration Classes:**\n- **`CommonConfigImpl`** (line 1569): Shared configuration for routes\n  - Method: `create()` - static factory for creating config from protobuf\n  - Contains `RouteMatcher` for matching routes to virtual hosts\n\n- **`ConfigImpl`** (line 1641): Main route configuration\n  - Method: `RouteConstSharedPtr route()` (lines 1653-1660)\n  - Takes request headers and stream info, returns matched route\n\n**Route Cluster Selection:**\n- **Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` (line 519)\n- Code: `route_entry_->clusterName()` returns the target cluster name\n- **Method name:** `RouteEntryImplBase::clusterName()` (line 965 in config_impl.cc)\n- Returns: `const std::string&` - the cluster name string\n\n**Dynamic Route Entries:**\n- **Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/config_impl.h` (lines 834-983)\n- **Class:** `DynamicRouteEntry : public RouteEntryAndRoute`\n- Allows cluster name to be determined dynamically (not static)\n- Used with cluster specifier plugins\n\n### 4. Upstream Host Selection from Cluster\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` (line 664)\n\n**Method Call:**\n```cpp\nauto host_selection_response = cluster->chooseHost(this);\n```\n\n**Interface Definition:**\n- **Location:** `/workspace/envoy/upstream/thread_local_cluster.h` (line 107)\n- **Class:** `ThreadLocalCluster`\n- **Method:** `HostSelectionResponse chooseHost(LoadBalancerContext* context)`\n- **Returns:** `HostSelectionResponse` with:\n  - `Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host` - selected host\n  - `std::unique_ptr<Upstream::AsyncHostSelectionHandle> cancelable` - for async selection\n  - `absl::string_view details` - selection details\n\n**Load Balancer Integration:**\n- The `Filter` class extends `Upstream::LoadBalancerContextBase` (router.h line 310)\n- Provides LoadBalancer context methods:\n  - `computeHashKey()` (line 363)\n  - `metadataMatchCriteria()` (line 379)\n  - `downstreamConnection()` (line 404)\n  - `downstreamHeaders()` (line 408)\n  - `shouldSelectAnotherHost()` (line 410)\n  - `determinePriorityLoad()` (line 421)\n  - `hostSelectionRetryCount()` (line 433)\n  - `upstreamSocketOptions()` (line 440)\n  - `upstreamTransportSocketOptions()` (line 445)\n  - `overrideHostToSelect()` (line 449)\n\n### 5. Connection Pool Creation - Upstream Connection Trigger\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` (lines 905-948)\n\n**Method:** `std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> Filter::createConnPool(Upstream::ThreadLocalCluster& cluster, Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host)`\n\n**Implementation (lines 905-948):**\n\n1. **Null host check** (line 907): Returns nullptr if no host selected\n2. **Factory lookup** (lines 910-926):\n   - Gets `GenericConnPoolFactory` from cluster config or default\n3. **Upstream protocol determination** (lines 933-944):\n   - Determines if HTTP, TCP, or UDP based on request method\n4. **Pool creation** (line 946):\n   ```cpp\n   return factory->createGenericConnPool(host, cluster, upstream_protocol, \n                                        route_entry_->priority(),\n                                        callbacks_->streamInfo().protocol(), \n                                        this, *message);\n   ```\n\n**Connection Pool Factory Interface:**\n- **Location:** `/workspace/envoy/router/router.h` (lines 1595-1613)\n- **Class:** `GenericConnPoolFactory : public Envoy::Config::TypedFactory`\n- **Method:** `createGenericConnPool()`\n- **Parameters:** Host, cluster, protocol, priority, context\n\n**GenericConnPool Interface:**\n- **Location:** `/workspace/envoy/router/router.h` (lines 1427-1460)\n- **Key method:** `void newStream(GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks* callbacks)`\n- **Callbacks:**\n  - `onPoolReady()` - connection established, stream ready\n  - `onPoolFailure()` - connection/stream creation failed\n\n### 6. Upstream Request Creation\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` (lines 845-849)\n\n**Process:**\n1. Pool is passed to `UpstreamRequest` constructor\n2. **Class:** `UpstreamRequest` defined in `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.h` (line 66)\n3. **Constructor** (line 72):\n   ```cpp\n   UpstreamRequest(RouterFilterInterface& parent, \n                   std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool>&& conn_pool,\n                   bool can_send_early_data, bool can_use_http3, \n                   bool enable_half_close);\n   ```\n4. Added to filter's upstream requests list (line 848)\n5. Headers accepted from router (line 849): `acceptHeadersFromRouter(end_stream)`\n\n**Upstream Pool Callbacks:**\n- **onPoolReady()** (`/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.h` lines 119-122):\n  - Called when connection is established\n  - Receives `GenericUpstream` for encoding data\n\n- **onPoolFailure()** (`/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.h` lines 116-118):\n  - Called on connection failure\n  - Triggers retry logic\n\n### 7. continueDecodeHeaders - Post Host Selection\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/source/common/router/router.cc` (line 714)\n\n**Method Signature:**\n```cpp\nHttp::FilterHeadersStatus Filter::continueDecodeHeaders(\n    Upstream::ThreadLocalCluster* cluster, \n    Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, \n    bool end_stream,\n    std::function<void(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&)> modify_headers, \n    bool* should_continue_decoding,\n    Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr&& selected_host,\n    absl::optional<std::string> host_selection_details)\n```\n\n**Key Steps (lines 714-849):**\n\n1. **Connection pool creation** (line 724):\n   ```cpp\n   std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> generic_conn_pool = \n       createConnPool(*cluster, selected_host);\n   ```\n\n2. **Timeout setup** (line 766):\n   ```cpp\n   timeout_ = FilterUtility::finalTimeout(*route_entry_, headers, ...);\n   ```\n\n3. **Retry state creation** (lines 813-815):\n   ```cpp\n   retry_state_ = createRetryState(\n       route_entry_->retryPolicy(), headers, *cluster_, \n       request_vcluster_, route_stats_context_, ...);\n   ```\n\n4. **Shadow policy setup** (lines 817-827): For traffic shadowing\n\n5. **Upstream request creation** (lines 845-847):\n   ```cpp\n   UpstreamRequestPtr upstream_request = std::make_unique<UpstreamRequest>(\n       *this, std::move(generic_conn_pool), can_send_early_data, \n       can_use_http3, allow_multiplexed_upstream_half_close_);\n   ```\n\n6. **Request forwarding begins** (line 849):\n   ```cpp\n   upstream_requests_.front()->acceptHeadersFromRouter(end_stream);\n   ```\n\n### Summary of Complete Flow\n\n```\ndecodeHeaders() \n  \u2193\n1. Route lookup: callbacks_->route()\n  \u2193\n2. Route matching: RouteEntryImplBase::matchRoute()\n  \u2193\n3. Cluster selection: route_entry_->clusterName()\n  \u2193\n4. Cluster lookup: cm_.getThreadLocalCluster(cluster_name)\n  \u2193\n5. Host selection: cluster->chooseHost(this) [LoadBalancer]\n  \u2193\n6. continueDecodeHeaders()\n  \u2193\n7. Connection pool creation: createConnPool()\n  \u2193\n8. Factory creates GenericConnPool\n  \u2193\n9. UpstreamRequest created with pool\n  \u2193\n10. Pool's newStream() called\n  \u2193\n11. onPoolReady() callback fires when connection established\n  \u2193\n12. Data forwarded upstream via GenericUpstream\n```\n\nAll file paths are absolute and have been verified to exist in the codebase.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Envoy codebase to understand route resolution and upstream selection:\n\n1. Find the Router filter implementation and its decodeHeaders() method\n2. Find how routes are matched against the request\n3. Find how route resolution determines which upstream cluster to use\n4. Find how upstream hosts are selected from a cluster\n5. Find what triggers upstream connection creation\n\nSearch for:\n- Router filter in extensions/filters/http/ - look for \"router\" directory\n- RouteEntry and RouteMatching classes\n- Cluster selection logic\n- ConnectionPool and how it creates upstream connections\n- RouteConfiguration loading\n\nReturn specific file paths, class names, and method names with line numbers.",
  "status": "completed",

  "totalTokens": 75822,

}

* defined for that header.\n    76\u2192     * @param headers supplies the headers from which to get the target header.\n    77\u2192     * @param target_header is the header to be validated.\n    78\u2192     * @return HeaderCheckResult containing the entry for @param target_header\n    79\u2192     *         and valid_ set to FALSE if @param target_header is set to an\n    80\u2192     * If @param target_header doesn't appear in\n    81\u2192     *         @param headers, return a result with valid_ set to TRUE.\n    82\u2192     */\n    83\u2192    static const HeaderCheckResult checkHeader(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n    84\u2192                                               const Http::LowerCaseString& target_header);\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    using ParseRetryFlagsFunc = std::function<std::pair<uint32_t, bool>(absl::string_view)>;\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  private:\n    89\u2192    static HeaderCheckResult hasValidRetryFields(const Http::HeaderEntry* header_entry,\n    90\u2192                                                 const ParseRetryFlagsFunc& parse_fn) {\n    91\u2192      HeaderCheckResult r;\n    92\u2192      if (header_entry) {\n    93\u2192        const auto flags_and_validity = parse_fn(header_entry->value().getStringView());\n    94\u2192        r.valid_ = flags_and_validity.second;\n    95\u2192        r.entry_ = header_entry;\n    96\u2192      }\n    97\u2192      return r;\n    98\u2192    }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    static HeaderCheckResult isInteger(const Http::HeaderEntry* header_entry) {\n   101\u2192      HeaderCheckResult r;\n   102\u2192      if (header_entry) {\n   103\u2192        uint64_t out;\n   104\u2192        r.valid_ = absl::SimpleAtoi(header_entry->value().getStringView(), &out);\n   105\u2192        r.entry_ = header_entry;\n   106\u2192      }\n   107\u2192      return r;\n   108\u2192    }\n   109\u2192  };\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  /**\n   112\u2192   * Returns response_time / timeout, as a  percentage as [0, 100]. Returns 0\n   113\u2192   * if there is no timeout.\n   114\u2192   * @param response_time supplies the response time thus far.\n   115\u2192   * @param timeout supplies  the timeout to get the percentage of.\n   116\u2192   * @return the percentage of timeout [0, 100] for stats use.\n   117\u2192   */\n   118\u2192  static uint64_t percentageOfTimeout(const std::chrono::milliseconds response_time,\n   119\u2192                                      const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout);\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  /**\n   122\u2192   * Set the :scheme header using the best information available. header if valid\n   126\u2192   * - whether the downstream connection is using TLS\n   127\u2192   */\n   128\u2192  static void setUpstreamScheme(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool the request is a gRPC request.\n   149\u2192   * @return TimeoutData for both the global and per try timeouts.\n   150\u2192   */\n   151\u2192  static TimeoutData finalTimeout(const RouteEntry& local reply (e.g. timeout) during host selection, cancel host\n   332\u2192    // selection.\n   333\u2192    if (host_selection_cancelable_) {\n   334\u2192 (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   340\u2192            \"envoy.reloadable_features.router_filter_resetall_on_local_reply\")) {\n   341\u2192      resetAll();\n   342\u2192    }\n   343\u2192  void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192  // Upstream::LoadBalancerContext\n   363\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> computeHashKey() override {\n   364\u2192    if (route_entry_ && downstream_headers_) {\n   365\u2192      auto hash_policy = route_entry_->hashPolicy();\n   366\u2192      if (hash_policy) {\n   367\u2192        return hash_policy->generateHash(\n   368\u2192            callbacks_->streamInfo().downstreamAddressProvider().remoteAddress().get(),\n   369\u2192            *downstream_headers_,\n   370\u2192            [this](const std::string& key, const std::string& path, std::chrono::seconds max_age,\n   371\u2192                   Http::CookieAttributeRefVector attributes) {\n   372\u2192              return addDownstreamSetCookie(key, path, max_age, attributes);\n   373\u2192            },\n   374\u2192            callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState());\n   375\u2192      }\n   376\u2192    }\n   377\u2192    return {};\n   378\u2192  }\n   379\u2192  const Router::MetadataMatchCriteria* metadataMatchCriteria() override {\n   380\u2192    if (route_entry_) {\n   381\u2192      // Have we been called before? If so, there's no need to recompute because\n   382\u2192      // by the time this method is called for the first time, route_entry_ should\n   383\u2192      // not change anymore.\n   384\u2192      if (metadata_match_ != nullptr) {\n   385\u2192        return metadata_match_.get();\n   386\u2192      }\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192      // The request's metadata, if present, takes precedence over the route's.\n   389\u2192      const auto& request_metadata = callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata().filter_metadata();\n   390\u2192      const auto filter_it = request_metadata.find(Envoy::Config::MetadataFilters::get().ENVOY_LB);\n   391\u2192      if (filter_it != request_metadata.end()) {\n   392\u2192        if (route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria() != nullptr) {\n   393\u2192          metadata_match_ =\n   394\u2192              route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria()->mergeMatchCriteria(filter_it->second);\n   395\u2192        } else {\n   396\u2192          metadata_match_ = std::make_unique<Router::MetadataMatchCriteriaImpl>(filter_it->second);\n   397\u2192        }\n   398\u2192        return metadata_match_.get();\n   399\u2192      }\n   400\u2192      return route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria();\n   401\u2192    }\n   402\u2192    return nullptr;\n   403\u2192  }\n   404\u2192  const Network::Connection* downstreamConnection() const override {\n   405\u2192    return callbacks_->connection().ptr();\n   406\u2192  }\n   407\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo* requestStreamInfo() const override { return &callbacks_->streamInfo(); }\n   408\u2192  const Http::RequestHeaderMap* downstreamHeaders() const override { return downstream_headers_; }\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192  bool shouldSelectAnotherHost(const Upstream::Host& host) override {\n   411\u2192    // We only care about host selection when performing a retry, at which point we consult the\n   412\u2192    // RetryState to see if we're configured to avoid certain hosts during retries.\n   413\u2192    if (!is_retry_) {\n   414\u2192      return false;\n   415\u2192    }\n   416\u2192\n   417\u2192    ASSERT(retry_state_);\n   418\u2192    return retry_state_->shouldSelectAnotherHost(host);\n   419\u2192  }\n   420\u2192\n   421\u2192  const Upstream::HealthyAndDegradedLoad& determinePriorityLoad(\n   422\u2192      const Upstream::PrioritySet& priority_set,\n   423\u2192      const Upstream::HealthyAndDegradedLoad& original_priority_load,\n   424\u2192      const Upstream::RetryPriority::PriorityMappingFunc& priority_mapping_func) override {\n   425\u2192    // We only modify the priority load on retries.\n   426\u2192    if (!is_retry_) {\n   427\u2192      return original_priority_load;\n   428\u2192    }\n   429\u2192    return retry_state_->priorityLoadForRetry(priority_set, original_priority_load,\n   430\u2192                                              priority_mapping_func);\n   431\u2192  }\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192  uint32_t hostSelectionRetryCount() const override {\n   434\u2192    if (!is_retry_) {\n   435\u2192      return 1;\n   436\u2192    }\n   437\u2192    return retry_state_->hostSelectionMaxAttempts();\n   438\u2192  }\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr upstreamSocketOptions() const override {\n   441\u2192    return (upstream_options_ != nullptr) ? upstream_options_\n   442\u2192                                          : callbacks_->getUpstreamSocketOptions();\n   443\u2192  }\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192 callbacks_->upstreamOverrideHost();\n   454\u2192  }\n   455\u2192\n   456\u2192  void onAsyncHostSelection(Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr&& host, std::string&& details) override;\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192  /**\n   459\u2192   * Set a computed cookie to be sent with the downstream headers.\n   460\u2192   * @param key supplies the size of the cookie\n   461\u2192   * @param max_age the lifetime of the cookie\n   462\u2192   * @param  path the path of the cookie, or \"\"\n   463\u2192   * @return std::string the value of the new cookie\n   464\u2192   */\n   465\u2192  std::string addDownstreamSetCookie(const std::string& key, const std::string& path,\n   466\u2192                                     std::chrono::seconds max_age,\n   467\u2192                                     Http::CookieAttributeRefVector attributes) {\n   468\u2192    // The cookie value should be the same per connection so that if multiple\n   469\u2192    // streams race on the same path, they all receive the same cookie.\n   470\u2192    // Since the downstream port is part of the hashed value, multiple HTTP1\n   471\u2192    // connections can receive different cookies if they race on requests.\n   472\u2192    std::string value;\n   473\u2192    const Network::Connection* conn = downstreamConnection();\n   474\u2192    // Need to check for null conn if this is ever used by Http::AsyncClient in the future.\n   475\u2192    value = conn->connectionInfoProvider().remoteAddress()->asString() +\n   476\u2192            conn->connectionInfoProvider().localAddress()->asString();\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192    const std::string cookie_value = Hex::uint64ToHex(HashUtil::xxHash64(value));\n   479\u2192    downstream_set_cookies_.emplace_back(\n   480\u2192        Http::Utility::makeSetCookieValue(key, cookie_value, path, max_age, true, attributes));\n   481\u2192    return cookie_value;\n   482\u2192  }\n   483\u2192\n   484\u2192  // RouterFilterInterface\n   485\u2192  void onUpstream1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n   486\u2192                            UpstreamRequest& upstream_request) override;\n   487\u2192  void onUpstreamHeaders(uint64_t response_code, Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers,\n   488\u2192                         UpstreamRequest& upstream_request, 106. Read @ -

\"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n    25\u2192#include \"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\"\n    26\u2192#include \"source/common/stream_info/stream_info_impl.h\"\n    27\u2192#include \"source/common/tracing/null_span_impl.h\"\n    28\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    31\u2192namespace Router {\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192class GenericUpstream;\n    34\u2192class GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks;\n    35\u2192class RouterFilterInterface;\n    36\u2192class UpstreamRequest;\n    37\u2192class UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks;\n    38\u2192class UpstreamFilterManager;\n    39\u2192class UpstreamCodecFilter;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192/* The Upstream request is the base class for forwarding HTTP upstream.\n    42\u2192 *\n    43\u2192 * On the new request path, payload (headers/body/metadata/data) still arrives via\n    44\u2192 * the accept[X]fromRouter functions. Said data is immediately passed off to the\n    45\u2192 * UpstreamFilterManager, which passes each item through the filter chain until\n    46\u2192 * it arrives at the last filter in the chain, the UpstreamCodecFilter. If the upstream\n    47\u2192 * stream is not established, the UpstreamCodecFilter returns StopAllIteration, and the\n    48\u2192 * FilterManager will buffer data, using watermarks to push back to the router\n    49\u2192 * filter if buffers become overrun. When an upstream connection is established,\n    50\u2192 * the UpstreamCodecFilter will send data upstream.\n    51\u2192 *\n    52\u2192 * On the new response path, payload arrives from upstream via the UpstreamCodecFilter's\n    53\u2192 * CodecBridge. It is passed off directly to the FilterManager, traverses the\n    54\u2192 * filter chain, and completion is signaled via the\n    55\u2192 * UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks's encode[X] functions. These somewhat\n    56\u2192 * confusingly pass through the UpstreamRequest's legacy decode[X] functions\n    57\u2192 * (required due to the UpstreamToDownstream interface, but will be renamed once\n    58\u2192 * the classic mode is deprecated), and are finally passed to the router via the\n    59\u2192 * RouterFilterInterface onUpstream[X] functions.\n    60\u2192 *\n    61\u2192 * There is some required communication between the UpstreamRequest and\n    62\u2192 * UpstreamCodecFilter. This is accomplished via the UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks\n    63\u2192 * interface, with the UpstreamFilterManager acting as intermediary.\n    64\u2192 *\n    65\u2192 */\n    66\u2192class UpstreamRequest : public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::router>,\n    67\u2192                        public UpstreamToDownstream,\n    68\u2192                        public LinkedObject<UpstreamRequest>,\n    69\u2192                        public GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks,\n    70\u2192                        public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n    71\u2192public:\n    72\u2192  UpstreamRequest(RouterFilterInterface& parent, std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool>&& conn_pool,\n    73\u2192                  bool can_send_early_data, bool can_use_http3, bool enable_half_close);\n    74\u2192  ~UpstreamRequest() override;\n    75\u2192  void deleteIsPending() override { cleanUp(); }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  // To be called from the destructor, or prior to deferred delete.\n    78\u2192  void cleanUp();\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  virtual void acceptHeadersFromRouter(bool end_stream);\n    81\u2192  virtual void acceptDataFromRouter(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream);\n    82\u2192  void acceptTrailersFromRouter(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers);\n    83\u2192  void acceptMetadataFromRouter(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr);\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  virtual void resetStream();\n    86\u2192  void setupPerTryTimeout();\n    87\u2192  void maybeEndDecode(bool end_stream);\n    88\u2192  void onUpstreamHostSelected(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host, bool pool_success);\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoder\n    91\u2192  void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    92\u2192  void decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) override;\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream (Http::ResponseDecoder)\n    95\u2192  void decode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) override;\n    96\u2192  void decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n    97\u2192  void decodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n    98\u2192  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream (Http::StreamCallbacks)\n   101\u2192  void onResetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reason,\n   102\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   103\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override { disableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl(); }\n   104\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override { enableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl(); }\n   105\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream\n   106\u2192  const Route& route() const override;\n   107\u2192  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() const override;\n   108\u2192  const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& upstreamStreamOptions() const override {\n   109\u2192    return stream_options_;\n   110\u2192  }\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192  void disableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl();\n   113\u2192  void enableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl();\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  // GenericConnPool\n   116\u2192  void onPoolFailure(ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason reason,\n   117\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason,\n   118\u2192                     Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) override;\n   119\u2192  void onPoolReady(std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream>&& upstream,\n   120\u2192                   Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n   121\u2192                   const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& address_provider,\n   122\u2192                   StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info, absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol) override;\n   123\u2192  UpstreamToDownstream& upstreamToDownstream() override;\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  void clearRequestEncoder();\n   126\u2192  void onStreamMaxDurationReached();\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192  // Either disable upstream reading immediately or defer it and keep tracking\n   129\u2192  // of how many read disabling has happened.\n   130\u2192  void readDisableOrDefer(bool disable);\n   131\u2192  // Called upon receiving the first response headers from the upstream. And\n   132\u2192  // applies read disabling to it if there is any pending read disabling.\n   133\u2192  void maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  struct DownstreamWatermarkManager : public Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks {\n   136\u2192    DownstreamWatermarkManager(UpstreamRequest& parent) : parent_(parent) {}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    // Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks\n   139\u2192    void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   140\u2192    void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    UpstreamRequest& parent_;\n   143\u2192  };\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192  void readEnable();\n   146\u2192  void encodeBodyAndTrailers();\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  // Getters and setters\n   149\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionOptConstRef upstreamHost() {\n   150\u2192    return makeOptRefFromPtr(upstream_host_.get());\n   151\u2192  }\n   152\u2192  void outlierDetectionTimeoutRecorded(bool recorded) {\n   153\u2192    outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_ = recorded;\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192  bool outlierDetectionTimeoutRecorded() { return outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_; }\n   156\u2192  void retried(bool value) { retried_ = value; }\n   157\u2192  bool retried() { return retried_; }\n   158\u2192  bool grpcRqSuccessDeferred() { return grpc_rq_success_deferred_; }\n   159\u2192  void grpcRqSuccessDeferred(bool deferred) { grpc_rq_success_deferred_ = deferred; }\n   160\u2192  void upstreamCanary(bool value) { upstream_canary_ = value; }\n   161\u2192  bool upstreamCanary() { return upstream_canary_; }\n   162\u2192  bool awaitingHeaders() { return awaiting_headers_; }\n   163\u2192  void recordTimeoutBudget(bool value) { record_timeout_budget_ = value; }\n   164\u2192  bool createPerTryTimeoutOnRequestComplete() {\n   165\u2192    return create_per_try_timeout_on_request_complete_;\n   166\u2192  }\n   167\u2192  bool encodeComplete() const { return router_sent_end_stream_; }\n   168\u2192  // Exposes streamInfo for the upstream stream.\n   169\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() { return stream_info_; }\n   170\u2192  bool hadUpstream() const { return had_upstream_; }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192private:\n   173\u2192  friend class UpstreamFilterManager;\n   174\u2192  friend class UpstreamCodecFilter;\n   175\u2192  friend class UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks;\n   176\u2192  StreamInfo::UpstreamTiming& upstreamTiming() {\n   177\u2192    return stream_info_.upstreamInfo()->upstreamTiming();\n   178\u2192  }\n   179\u2192  // Records the latency from when the upstream request was first created to\n   180\u2192  // when the pool callback fires. This latency can be useful to track excessive\n   181\u2192  // queuing.\n   182\u2192  void recordConnectionPoolCallbackLatency();\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  void addResponseHeadersStat(uint64_t size, size_t count) {\n   185\u2192    response_headers_size_ = response_headers_size_.value_or(0) + size;\n   186\u2192    response_headers_count_ = response_headers_count_.value_or(0) + count;\n   187\u2192  }\n   188\u2192  void resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   189\u2192  void onPerTryTimeout();\n   190\u2192  void onPerTryIdleTimeout();\n   191\u2192  void upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType access_log_type);\n   192\u2192  void resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192  RouterFilterInterface& parent_;\n   195\u2192  std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> conn_pool_;\n   196\u2192  Event::TimerPtr per_try_timeout_;\n   197\u2192  Event::TimerPtr per_try_idle_timeout_;\n   198\u2192  std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream> upstream_;\n   199\u2192  absl::optional<Http::StreamResetReason> deferred_reset_reason_;\n   200\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host_;\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

                        creation_status);\n   127\u2192  }\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192// Express percentage as [0, TimeoutPrecisionFactor] because stats do not accept floating point\n   131\u2192// values, and getting multiple significant figures on the histogram would be nice.\n   132\u2192uint64_t FilterUtility::percentageOfTimeout(const std::chrono::milliseconds response_time,\n   133\u2192                                            const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout) {\n   134\u2192  // Timeouts of 0 are considered infinite. Any portion of an infinite timeout used is still\n   135\u2192  // none of it.\n   136\u2192  if (timeout.count() == 0) {\n   137\u2192    return 0;\n   138\u2192  }\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  return static_cast<uint64_t>(response_time.count() * TimeoutPrecisionFactor / timeout.count());\n   141\u2192}\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192void FilterUtility::setUpstreamScheme(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool downstream_ssl,\n   144\u2192                                      bool upstream_ssl, bool use_upstream) {\n   145\u2192  if (use_upstream) {\n   146\u2192    if (upstream_ssl) {\n   147\u2192      headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Https);\n   148\u2192    } else {\n   149\u2192      headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Http);\n   150\u2192    }\n   151\u2192    return;\n   152\u2192  }\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192  if (Http::Utility::schemeIsValid(headers.getSchemeValue())) {\n   155\u2192    return;\n   156\u2192  }\n   157\u2192  // After all the changes in https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/14587\n   158\u2192  // this path should only occur if a buggy filter has removed the :scheme\n   159\u2192  // header. In that case best-effort set from X-Forwarded-Proto.\n   160\u2192  absl::string_view xfp = headers.getForwardedProtoValue();\n   161\u2192  if (Http::Utility::schemeIsValid(xfp)) {\n   162\u2192    headers.setScheme(xfp);\n   163\u2192    return;\n   164\u2192  }\n   165\u2192\n   166\u2192  if (downstream_ssl) {\n   167\u2192    headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Https);\n   168\u2192  } else {\n   169\u2192    headers.setReferenceScheme(Http::Headers::get().SchemeValues.Http);\n   170\u2192  }\n   171\u2192}\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192bool FilterUtility::shouldShadow(const ShadowPolicy& policy, Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n   174\u2192                                 uint64_t stable_random) {\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  // The policy's default value is set correctly regardless of whether there is a runtime key\n   177\u2192  // or not, thus this call is sufficient for all cases (100% if no runtime set, otherwise\n   178\u2192  // using the default value within the runtime fractional percent setting).\n   179\u2192  return runtime.snapshot().featureEnabled(policy.runtimeKey(), policy.defaultValue(),\n   180\u2192                                           stable_random);\n   181\u2192}\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192TimeoutData FilterUtility::finalTimeout(const RouteEntry& route,\n   184\u2192                                        Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers,\n   185\u2192                                        bool insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms,\n   186\u2192                                        bool grpc_request, bool per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled,\n   187\u2192                                        bool respect_expected_rq_timeout) {\n   188\u2192  // See if there is a user supplied timeout in a request header. If there is we take that.\n   189\u2192  // Otherwise if the request is gRPC and a maximum gRPC timeout is configured we use the timeout\n   190\u2192  // in the gRPC headers (or infinity when gRPC headers have no timeout), but cap that timeout to\n   191\u2192  // the configured maximum gRPC timeout (which may also be infinity, represented by a 0 value),\n   192\u2192  // or the default from the route config otherwise.\n   193\u2192  TimeoutData timeout;\n   194\u2192  if (!route.usingNewTimeouts()) {\n   195\u2192    if (grpc_request && route.maxGrpcTimeout()) {\n   196\u2192      const std::chrono::milliseconds max_grpc_timeout = route.maxGrpcTimeout().value();\n   197\u2192      auto header_timeout = Grpc::Common::getGrpcTimeout(request_headers);\n   198\u2192      std::chrono::milliseconds grpc_timeout =\n   199\u2192          header_timeout ? header_timeout.value() : std::chrono::milliseconds(0);\n   200\u2192      if (route.grpcTimeoutOffset()) {\n   201\u2192        // We only apply the offset if it won't result in grpc_timeout hitting 0 or below, as\n   202\u2192        // setting it to 0 means infinity and a negative timeout makes no sense.\n   203\u2192        const auto offset = *route.grpcTimeoutOffset();\n   204\u2192        if (offset < grpc_timeout) {\n   205\u2192          grpc_timeout -= offset;\n   206\u2192        }\n   207\u2192      }\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192      // Cap gRPC timeout to the configured maximum considering that 0 means infinity.\n   210\u2192      if (max_grpc_timeout != std::chrono::milliseconds(0) &&\n   211\u2192          (grpc_timeout == std::chrono::milliseconds(0) || grpc_timeout > max_grpc_timeout)) {\n   212\u2192        grpc_timeout = max_grpc_timeout;\n   213\u2192      }\n   214\u2192      timeout.global_timeout_ = grpc_timeout;\n   215\u2192    } else {\n   216\u2192      timeout.global_timeout_ = route.timeout();\n   217\u2192    }\n   218\u2192  }\n   219\u2192  timeout.per_try_timeout_ = route.retryPolicy().perTryTimeout();\n   220\u2192  timeout.per_try_idle_timeout_ = route.retryPolicy().perTryIdleTimeout();\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  uint64_t header_timeout;\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192  if (respect_expected_rq_timeout) {\n   225\u2192    // Check if there is timeout set by egress Envoy.\n   226\u2192    // If present, use that value as route timeout and don't override\n   227\u2192    // *x-envoy-expected-rq-timeout-ms* header. At this point *x-envoy-upstream-rq-timeout-ms*\n   228\u2192    // header should have been sanitized by egress Envoy.\n   229\u2192    const Http::HeaderEntry* header_expected_timeout_entry =\n   230\u2192        request_headers.EnvoyExpectedRequestTimeoutMs();\n   231\u2192    if (header_expected_timeout_entry) {\n   232\u2192      trySetGlobalTimeout(*header_expected_timeout_entry, timeout);\n   233\u2192    } else {\n   234\u2192      const Http::HeaderEntry* header_timeout_entry =\n   235\u2192          request_headers.EnvoyUpstreamRequestTimeoutMs();\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192      if (header_timeout_entry) {\n   238\u2192        trySetGlobalTimeout(*header_timeout_entry, timeout);\n   239\u2192        request_headers.removeEnvoyUpstreamRequestTimeoutMs();\n   240\u2192      }\n   241\u2192    }\n   242\u2192  } else {\n   243\u2192    const Http::HeaderEntry* header_timeout_entry = request_headers.EnvoyUpstreamRequestTimeoutMs();\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192    if (header_timeout_entry) {\n   246\u2192      trySetGlobalTimeout(*header_timeout_entry, timeout);\n   247\u2192      request_headers.removeEnvoyUpstreamRequestTimeoutMs();\n   248\u2192    }\n   249\u2192  }\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192  // See if there is a per try/retry timeout. If it's >= global we just ignore it.\n   252\u2192  const absl::string_view per_try_timeout_entry =\n   253\u2192      request_headers.getEnvoyUpstreamRequestPerTryTimeoutMsValue();\n   254\u2192  if (!per_try_timeout_entry.empty()) {\n   255\u2192    if (absl::SimpleAtoi(per_try_timeout_entry, &header_timeout)) {\n   256\u2192      timeout.per_try_timeout_ = std::chrono::milliseconds(header_timeout);\n   257\u2192    }\n   258\u2192    request_headers.removeEnvoyUpstreamRequestPerTryTimeoutMs();\n   259\u2192  }\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192  if (timeout.per_try_timeout_ >= timeout.global_timeout_ && timeout.global_timeout_.count() != 0) {\n   262\u2192    timeout.per_try_timeout_ = std::chrono::milliseconds(0);\n   263\u2192  }\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192  setTimeoutHeaders(0, timeout, route, request_headers, insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms,\n   266\u2192                    grpc_request, per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled);\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192  return timeout;\n   269\u2192}\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192void FilterUtility::setTimeoutHeaders(uint64_t elapsed_time, const TimeoutData& timeout,\n   272\u2192                                      const RouteEntry& route,\n   273\u2192                                      Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers,\n   274\u2192                                      bool insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms,\n   275\u2192                                      bool grpc_request, bool per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled) {\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  const uint64_t global_timeout = timeout.global_timeout_.count();\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192  // See if there is any timeout to write in the expected timeout header.\n   280\u2192  uint64_t expected_timeout = timeout.per_try_timeout_.count();\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192  // Use the global timeout if no per try timeout was specified or if we're\n   283\u2192  // doing hedging when there are per try timeouts. Either of these scenarios\n   284\u2192  // mean that the upstream server can use the full global timeout.\n   285\u2192  if (per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled || expected_timeout == 0) {\n   286\u2192    expected_timeout = global_timeout;\n   287\u2192  }\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192  // If the expected timeout is 0 set no timeout, as Envoy treats 0 as infinite timeout.\n   290\u2192  if (expected_timeout > 0) {\n   291\u2192\n   292\u2192    if (global_timeout > 0) {\n   293\u2192      if (elapsed_time >= global_timeout) {\n   294\u2192        // We are out of time, but 0 would be an infinite timeout. So instead we send a 1ms timeout\n   295\u2192        // and assume the timers armed by onRequestComplete() will fire very soon.\n   296\u2192        expected_timeout = 1;\n   297\u2192      } else {\n   298\u2192        expected_timeout = std::min(expected_timeout, global_timeout - elapsed_time);\n   299\u2192      }\n   300\u2192    }\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192    if (insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms) {\n   303\u2192      request_headers.setEnvoyExpectedRequestTimeoutMs(expected_timeout);\n   304\u2192    }\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192    // If we've configured max_grpc_timeout, override the grpc-timeout header with\n   307\u2192    // the expected timeout. This ensures that the optional per try timeout is reflected\n   308\u2192    // in grpc-timeout, ensuring that the upstream gRPC server is aware of the actual timeout.\n   309\u2192    if (grpc_request && !route.usingNewTimeouts() && route.maxGrpcTimeout()) {\n   310\u2192      Grpc::Common::toGrpcTimeout(std::chrono::milliseconds(expected_timeout), request_headers);\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192  }\n   313\u2192}\n   314\u2192\n   315\u2192absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>\n   316\u2192FilterUtility::tryParseHeaderTimeout(const Http::HeaderEntry& header_timeout_entry) {\n   317\u2192  uint64_t header_timeout;\n   318\u2192  if (absl::SimpleAtoi(header_timeout_entry.value().getStringView(), &header_timeout)) {\n   319\u2192    return std::chrono::milliseconds(header_timeout);\n   320\u2192  }\n   321\u2192  return absl::nullopt;\n   322\u2192}\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192void FilterUtility::trySetGlobalTimeout(const Http::HeaderEntry& header_timeout_entry,\n   325\u2192                                        TimeoutData& timeout) {\n   326\u2192  const auto timeout_ms = tryParseHeaderTimeout(header_timeout_entry);\n   327\u2192  if (timeout_ms.has_value()) {\n   328\u2192    timeout.global_timeout_ = timeout_ms.value();\n   329\u2192  }\n   330\u2192}\n   331\u2192\n   332\u2192FilterUtility::HedgingParams\n   333\u2192FilterUtility::finalHedgingParams(const RouteEntry& route,\n   334\u2192                                  Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers) {\n   335\u2192  HedgingParams hedging_params;\n   336\u2192  hedging_params.hedge_on_per_try_timeout_ = route.hedgePolicy().hedgeOnPerTryTimeout();\n   337\u2192\n   338\u2192  const Http::HeaderEntry* hedge_on_per_try_timeout_entry =\n   339\u2192      request_headers.EnvoyHedgeOnPerTryTimeout();\n   340\u2192  if (hedge_on_per_try_timeout_entry) {\n   341\u2192    if (hedge_on_per_try_timeout_entry->value() == \"true\") {\n   342\u2192      hedging_params.hedge_on_per_try_timeout_ = true;\n   343\u2192    }\n   344\u2192    if (hedge_on_per_try_timeout_entry->value() == \"false\") {\n   345\u2192      hedging_params.hedge_on_per_try_timeout_ = false;\n   346\u2192    }\n   347\u2192\n   348\u2192    request_headers.removeEnvoyHedgeOnPerTryTimeout();\n   349\u2192  }\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192  return hedging_params;\n   352\u2192}\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192Filter::~Filter() {\n   355\u2192  // Upstream resources should already have been cleaned.\n   356\u2192  ASSERT(upstream_requests_.empty());\n   357\u2192  ASSERT(!retry_state_);\n   358\u2192}\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192const FilterUtility::StrictHeaderChecker::HeaderCheckResult\n   361\u2192FilterUtility::StrictHeaderChecker::checkHeader(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n   362\u2192                                                const Http::LowerCaseString& target_header) {\n   363\u2192  if (target_header == Http::Headers::get().EnvoyUpstreamRequestTimeoutMs) {\n   364\u2192    return isInteger(headers.EnvoyUpstreamRequestTimeoutMs());\n   365\u2192  } else if (target_header == Http::Headers::get().EnvoyUpstreamRequestPerTryTimeoutMs) {\n   366\u2192    return isInteger(headers.EnvoyUpstreamRequestPerTryTimeoutMs());\n   367\u2192  } else if (target_header == Http::Headers::get().EnvoyMaxRetries) {\n   368\u2192    return isInteger(headers.EnvoyMaxRetries());\n   369\u2192  } else if (target_header == Http::Headers::get().EnvoyRetryOn) {\n   370\u2192    return hasValidRetryFields(headers.EnvoyRetryOn(), &Router::RetryStateImpl::parseRetryOn);\n   371\u2192  } else if (target_header == Http::Headers::get().EnvoyRetryGrpcOn) {\n   372\u2192    return hasValidRetryFields(headers.EnvoyRetryGrpcOn(),\n   373\u2192                               &Router::RetryStateImpl::parseRetryGrpcOn);\n   374\u2192  }\n   375\u2192  // Should only validate headers for which we have implemented a validator.\n   376\u2192  PANIC(\"unexpectedly reached\");\n   377\u2192}\n   378\u2192\n   379\u2192Stats::StatName Filter::upstreamZone(Upstream::HostDescriptionOptConstRef upstream_host) {\n   380\u2192  return upstream_host ? upstream_host->localityZoneStatName() : config_->empty_stat_name_;\n   381\u2192}\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192void Filter::chargeUpstreamCode(uint64_t response_status_code,\n   384\u2192                                const Http::ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers,\n   385\u2192                                Upstream::HostDescriptionOptConstRef upstream_host, bool dropped) {\n   386\u2192  // Passing the response_status_code explicitly is an optimization to avoid\n   387\u2192  // multiple calls to slow Http::Utility::getResponseStatus.\n   388\u2192  ASSERT(response_status_code == Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(response_headers));\n   389\u2192  if (config_->emit_dynamic_stats_ && !callbacks_->streamInfo().healthCheck()) {\n   390\u2192    const Http::HeaderEntry* upstream_canary_header = response_headers.EnvoyUpstreamCanary();\n   391\u2192    const bool is_canary = (upstream_canary_header && upstream_canary_header->value() == \"true\") ||\n   392\u2192                           (upstream_host ? upstream_host->canary() : false);\n   393\u2192    const bool internal_request = Http::HeaderUtility::isEnvoyInternalRequest(*downstream_headers_);\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192    Stats::StatName upstream_zone = upstreamZone(upstream_host);\n   396\u2192 response_status_code,\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Envoy::Router::GenericConnPoolFactory::UpstreamProtocol;\n   933\u2192  UpstreamProtocol upstream_protocol = UpstreamProtocol::HTTP;\n   934\u2192  if (route_entry_->connectConfig().has_value()) {\n   935\u2192    auto method = downstream_headers_->getMethodValue();\n   936\u2192    if (Http::HeaderUtility::isConnectUdpRequest(*downstream_headers_)) {\n   937\u2192      upstream_protocol = UpstreamProtocol::UDP;\n   938\u2192    } else if (method == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect ||\n   939\u2192               (route_entry_->connectConfig()->allow_post() &&\n   940\u2192                method == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Post)) {\n   941\u2192      // Allow POST for proxying raw TCP if it is configured.\n   942\u2192      upstream_protocol = UpstreamProtocol::TCP;\n   943\u2192    }\n   944\u2192  }\n   945\u2192\n   946\u2192  return factory->createGenericConnPool(host, cluster, upstream_protocol, route_entry_->priority(),\n   947\u2192                                        callbacks_->streamInfo().protocol(), this, *message);\n   948\u2192}\n   949\u2192\n   950\u2192void Filter::sendNoHealthyUpstreamResponse(absl::optional<std::string> optional_details) {\n   951\u2192  callbacks_->streamInfo().setResponseFlag(StreamInfo::CoreResponseFlag::NoHealthyUpstream);\n   952\u2192  chargeUpstreamCode(Http::Code::ServiceUnavailable, {}, false);\n   953\u2192  absl::string_view details = (optional_details.has_value() && !optional_details->empty())\n   954\u2192                                  ? absl::string_view(*optional_details)\n   955\u2192                                  : StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().NoHealthyUpstream;\n   956\u2192  callbacks_->sendLocalReply(Http::Code::ServiceUnavailable, \"no healthy upstream\", modify_headers_,\n   957\u2192                             absl::nullopt, details);\n   958\u2192}\n   959\u2192\n   960\u2192Http::FilterDataStatus Filter::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   961\u2192  // upstream_requests_.size() cannot be > 1 because that only happens when a per\n   962\u2192  // try timeout occurs with hedge_on_per_try_timeout enabled but the per\n   963\u2192  // try timeout timer is not started until onRequestComplete(). It could be zero\n   964\u2192  // if the first request attempt has already failed and a retry is waiting for\n   965\u2192  // a backoff timer.\n   966\u2192  ASSERT(upstream_requests_.size() <= 1);\n   967\u2192\n   968\u2192  bool buffering = (retry_state_ && retry_state_->enabled()) ||\n   969\u2192                   (!active_shadow_policies_.empty() && !streaming_shadows_) ||\n   970\u2192                   (route_entry_ && route_entry_->internalRedirectPolicy().enabled());\n   971\u2192  if (buffering &&\n   972\u2192      getLength(callbacks_->decodingBuffer()) + data.length() > retry_shadow_buffer_limit_) {\n   973\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug,\n   974\u2192              \"The request payload has at least {} bytes data which exceeds buffer limit {}. Give \"\n   975\u2192              \"up on the retry/shadow.\",\n   976\u2192              getLength(callbacks_->decodingBuffer()) + data.length(), retry_shadow_buffer_limit_);\n   977\u2192    cluster_->trafficStats()->retry_or_shadow_abandoned_.inc();\n   978\u2192    retry_state_.reset();\n   979\u2192    buffering = false;\n   980\u2192    active_shadow_policies_.clear();\n   981\u2192    request_buffer_overflowed_ = true;\n   982\u2192\n   983\u2192    // If we had to abandon buffering and there's no request in progress, abort the request and\n   984\u2192    // clean up. This happens if the initial upstream request failed, and we are currently waiting\n   985\u2192    // for a backoff timer before starting the next upstream attempt.\n   986\u2192    if (upstream_requests_.empty()) {\n   987\u2192      cleanup();\n   988\u2192      callbacks_->sendLocalReply(\n   989\u2192          Http::Code::InsufficientStorage, \"exceeded request buffer limit while retrying upstream\",\n   990\u2192          modify_headers_, absl::nullopt,\n   991\u2192          StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().RequestPayloadExceededRetryBufferLimit);\n   992\u2192      return Http::FilterDataStatus::StopIterationNoBuffer;\n   993\u2192    }\n   994\u2192  }\n   995\u2192\n   996\u2192  for (auto* shadow_stream : shadow_streams_) {\n   997\u2192    if (end_stream) {\n   998\u2192      shadow_stream->removeDestructorCallback();\n   999\u2192      shadow_stream->removeWatermarkCallbacks();\n  1000\u2192    }\n  1001\u2192    Buffer::OwnedImpl copy(data);\n  1002\u2192    shadow_stream->sendData(copy, end_stream);\n  1003\u2192  }\n  1004\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"  1427\u2192class GenericConnPool {\n  1428\u2192public:\n  1429\u2192  virtual ~GenericConnPool() = default;\n  1430\u2192\n  1431\u2192  /**\n  1432\u2192   * Called to create a new HTTP stream, TCP connection for CONNECT streams, or UDP socket for\n  1433\u2192   * CONNECT-UDP streams.\n  1434\u2192   *\n  1435\u2192   * The implementation of the GenericConnPool will either call\n  1436\u2192   * GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks::onPoolReady\n  1437\u2192   * when a stream is available or GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks::onPoolFailure\n  1438\u2192   * if stream creation fails.\n  1439\u2192   *\n  1440\u2192   * The caller is responsible for calling cancelAnyPendingStream() if stream\n  1441\u2192   * creation is no longer desired. newStream may only be called once per\n  1442\u2192   * GenericConnPool.\n  1443\u2192   *\n  1444\u2192   * @param callbacks callbacks to communicate stream failure or creation on.\n  1445\u2192   */\n  1446\u2192  virtual void newStream(GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks* callbacks) PURE;\n  1447\u2192  /**\n  1448\u2192   * Called to cancel any pending newStream request,\n  1449\u2192   */\n  1450\u2192  virtual bool cancelAnyPendingStream() PURE;\n  1451\u2192  /**\n  1452\u2192   * @return optionally returns the host for the connection pool.\n  1453\u2192   */\n  1454\u2192  virtual Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host() const PURE;\n  1455\u2192\n  1456\u2192  /**\n  1457\u2192   * @return returns if the connection pool was initialized successfully.\n  1458\u2192   */\n  1459\u2192  virtual bool valid() const PURE;\n  1460\u2192};\n  1461\u2192\n  1462\u2192/**\n  1463\u2192 * An API for the interactions the upstream stream needs to have with the downstream stream\n  1464\u2192 * and/or router components\n  1465\u2192 */\n  1466\u2192class UpstreamToDownstream : public Http::ResponseDecoder, public Http::StreamCallbacks {\n  1467\u2192public:\n  1468\u2192  /**\n  1469\u2192   * @return return the route for the downstream stream.\n  1470\u2192   */\n  1471\u2192  virtual const Route& route() const PURE;\n  1472\u2192  /**\n  1473\u2192   * @return return the connection for the downstream stream if it exists.\n  1474\u2192   */\n  1475\u2192  virtual OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() const PURE;\n  1476\u2192  /**\n  1477\u2192   * @return returns the options to be consulted with for upstream stream creation.\n  1478\u2192   */\n  1479\u2192  virtual const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& upstreamStreamOptions() const PURE;\n  1480\u2192};\n  1481\u2192\n  1482\u2192/**\n  1483\u2192 * An API for wrapping callbacks from either an HTTP, TCP, or UDP connection pool.\n  1484\u2192 *\n  1485\u2192 * Just like the connection pool callbacks, the GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks\n  1486\u2192 * will either call onPoolReady when a GenericUpstream is ready, or\n  1487\u2192 * onPoolFailure if a connection/stream can not be established.\n  1488\u2192 */\n  1489\u2192class GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks {\n  1490\u2192public:\n  1491\u2192  virtual ~GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks() = default;\n  1492\u2192\n  1493\u2192  /**\n  1494\u2192   * Called to indicate a failure for GenericConnPool::newStream to establish a stream.\n  1495\u2192   *\n  1496\u2192   * @param reason supplies the failure reason.\n  1497\u2192   * @param transport_failure_reason supplies the details of the transport failure reason.\n  1498\u2192   * @param host supplies the description of the host that caused the failure. This may be nullptr\n  1499\u2192   *             if no host was involved in the failure (for example overflow).\n  1500\u2192   */\n  1501\u2192  virtual void onPoolFailure(ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason reason,\n  1502\u2192                             absl::string_view transport_failure_reason,\n  1503\u2192                             Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) PURE;\n  1504\u2192  /**\n  1505\u2192   * Called when GenericConnPool::newStream has established a new stream.\n  1506\u2192   *\n  1507\u2192   * @param upstream supplies the generic upstream for the stream.\n  1508\u2192   * @param host supplies the description of the host that will carry the request. For logical\n  1509\u2192   *             connection pools the description may be different each time this is called.\n  1510\u2192   * @param connection_info_provider, supplies the address provider of the upstream connection.\n  1511\u2192   * @param info supplies the stream info object associated with the upstream connection.\n  1512\u2192   * @param protocol supplies the protocol associated with the upstream connection.\n  1513\u2192   */\n  1514\u2192  virtual void onPoolReady(std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream>&& upstream,\n  1515\u2192                           Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n  1516\u2192                           const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& connection_info_provider,\n  1517\u2192                           StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info,\n  1518\u2192                           absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol) PURE;\n  1519\u2192\n  1520\u2192  // @return the UpstreamToDownstream interface for this stream.\n  1521\u2192  //\n  1522\u2192  // This is the interface for all interactions the upstream stream needs to have with the\n  1523\u2192  // downstream stream. It is in the GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks as the GenericConnectionPool\n  1524\u2192  // creates the GenericUpstream, and the GenericUpstream will need this interface.\n  1525\u2192  virtual UpstreamToDownstream& upstreamToDownstream() PURE;\n  1526\u2192};\n  1527\u2192\n  1528\u2192/**\n  1529\u2192 * An API for sending information to either a TCP, UDP, or HTTP upstream.\n  1530\u2192 *\n  1531\u2192 * It is similar logically to RequestEncoder, only without the getStream interface.\n  1532\u2192 */\n  1533\u2192class GenericUpstream {\n  1534\u2192public:\n  1535\u2192  virtual ~GenericUpstream() = default;\n  1536\u2192  /**\n  1537\u2192   * Encode a data frame.\n  1538\u2192   * @param data supplies the data to encode. The data may be moved by the encoder.\n  1539\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last data frame.\n  1540\u2192   */\n  1541\u2192  virtual void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n  1542\u2192  /**\n  1543\u2192   * Encode metadata.\n  1544\u2192   * @param metadata_map_vector is the vector of metadata maps to encode.\n  1545\u2192   */\n  1546\u2192  virtual void encodeMetadata(const Http::MetadataMapVector& metadata_map_vector) PURE;\n  1547\u2192  /**\n  1548\u2192   * Encode headers, optionally indicating end of stream.\n  1549\u2192   * @param headers supplies the header map to encode.\n  1550\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is a header only request.\n  1551\u2192   * @return status indicating success. Encoding will fail if headers do not have required HTTP\n  1552\u2192   * headers.\n  1553\u2192   */\n  1554\u2192  virtual Http::Status encodeHeaders(const Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) PURE;\n  1555\u2192  /**\n  1556\u2192   * Encode trailers. This implicitly ends the stream.\n  1557\u2192   * @param trailers supplies the trailers to encode.\n  1558\u2192   */\n  1559\u2192  virtual void encodeTrailers(const Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) PURE;\n  1560\u2192\n  1561\u2192  // TODO(vikaschoudhary16): Remove this api.\n  1562\u2192  // This api is only used to enable TCP tunneling semantics in the upstream codec.\n  1563\u2192  // TCP proxy extension uses this API when proxyingn TCP tunnel via HTTP CONNECT or POST.\n  1564\u2192  /**\n  1565\u2192   * Enable TCP tunneling semantics on the upstream codec. Reading a remote half-close\n  1566\u2192   * will not fully close the connection. This is off by default.\n  1567\u2192   */\n  1568\u2192  virtual void enableTcpTunneling() PURE;\n  1569\u2192  /**\n  1570\u2192   * Enable/disable further data from this stream.\n  1571\u2192   */\n  1572\u2192  virtual void readDisable(bool disable) PURE;\n  1573\u2192  /**\n  1574\u2192   * Reset the stream. No events will fire beyond this point.\n  1575\u2192   */\n  1576\u2192  virtual void resetStream() PURE;\n  1577\u2192\n  1578\u2192  /**\n  1579\u2192   * Sets the upstream to use the following account.\n  1580\u2192   * @param the account to assign the generic upstream.\n  1581\u2192   */\n  1582\u2192  virtual void setAccount(Buffer::BufferMemoryAccountSharedPtr account) PURE;\n  1583\u2192\n  1584\u2192  /**\n  1585\u2192   * Get the bytes meter for this stream.\n  1586\u2192   */\n  1587\u2192  virtual const StreamInfo::BytesMeterSharedPtr& bytesMeter() PURE;\n  1588\u2192};\n  1589\u2192\n  1590\u2192using GenericConnPoolPtr = std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool>;\n  1591\u2192\n  1592\u2192/*\n  1593\u2192 * A factory for creating generic connection pools.\n  1594\u2192 */\n  1595\u2192class GenericConnPoolFactory : public Envoy::Config::TypedFactory {\n  1596\u2192public:\n  1597\u2192  /*\n  1598\u2192   * Protocol used by the upstream sockets.\n  1599\u2192   */\n  1600\u2192  enum class UpstreamProtocol { HTTP, TCP, UDP };\n  1601\u2192\n  1602\u2192  ~GenericConnPoolFactory() override = default;\n  1603\u2192\n  1604\u2192  /*\n  1605\u2192 61\u2192   * onClusterAddOrUpdate is called when a new cluster is added or an existing cluster\n    62\u2192   * is updated in the ClusterManager.\n    63\u2192   * @param cluster_name the name of the changed cluster.\n    64\u2192   * @param get_cluster is a callable that will provide the ThreadLocalCluster that represents the\n    65\u2192   * updated cluster. It should be used within the call or discarded.\n    66\u2192   */\n    67\u2192  virtual void onClusterAddOrUpdate(absl::string_view cluster_name,\n    68\u2192                                    ThreadLocalClusterCommand& get_cluster) PURE;\n    69\u2192  /**\n    70\u2192   * onClusterRemoval is called when a cluster is removed; the argument is the cluster name.\n    71\u2192   * @param cluster_name is the name of the removed cluster.\n    72\u2192   */\n    73\u2192  virtual void onClusterRemoval(const std::string& cluster_name) PURE;\n    74\u2192};\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192/**\n    77\u2192 * ClusterUpdateCallbacksHandle is a RAII wrapper for a ClusterUpdateCallbacks. Deleting\n    78\u2192 * the ClusterUpdateCallbacksHandle will remove the callbacks from ClusterManager in O(1).\n    79\u2192 */\n    80\u2192class ClusterUpdateCallbacksHandle {\n    81\u2192public:\n    82\u2192  virtual ~ClusterUpdateCallbacksHandle() = default;\n    83\u2192};\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192using ClusterUpdateCallbacksHandlePtr = std::unique_ptr<ClusterUpdateCallbacksHandle>;\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192/**\n    88\u2192 * Status enum for the result of an attempted cluster discovery.\n    89\u2192 */\n    90\u2192enum class ClusterDiscoveryStatus {\n    91\u2192  /**\n    92\u2192   * The discovery process timed out. This means that we haven't yet received any reply from\n    93\u2192   * on-demand CDS about it.\n    94\u2192   */\n    95\u2192  Timeout,\n    96\u2192  /**\n    97\u2192   * The discovery process has concluded and on-demand CDS has no such cluster.\n    98\u2192   */\n    99\u2192  Missing,\n   100\u2192  /**\n   101\u2192   * Cluster found and currently available through ClusterManager.\n   102\u2192   */\n   103\u2192  Available,\n   104\u2192};\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192/**\n   107\u2192 * ClusterDiscoveryCallback is a callback called at the end of the on-demand cluster discovery\n   108\u2192 * process. The status of the discovery is sent as a parameter.\n   109\u2192 */\n   110\u2192using ClusterDiscoveryCallback = std::function<void(ClusterDiscoveryStatus)>;\n   111\u2192using ClusterDiscoveryCallbackPtr = std::unique_ptr<ClusterDiscoveryCallback>;\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192/**\n   114\u2192 * ClusterDiscoveryCallbackHandle is a RAII wrapper for a ClusterDiscoveryCallback. Deleting the\n   115\u2192 * ClusterDiscoveryCallbackHandle will remove the callbacks from ClusterManager.\n   116\u2192 */\n   117\u2192class ClusterDiscoveryCallbackHandle {\n   118\u2192public:\n   119\u2192  virtual ~ClusterDiscoveryCallbackHandle() = default;\n   120\u2192};\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192using ClusterDiscoveryCallbackHandlePtr = std::unique_ptr<ClusterDiscoveryCallbackHandle>;\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192/**\n   125\u2192 * A handle to an on-demand CDS.\n   126\u2192 */\n   127\u2192class OdCdsApiHandle {\n   128\u2192public:\n   129\u2192  virtual ~OdCdsApiHandle() = default;\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  /**\n   132\u2192   * Request an on-demand discovery of a cluster with a passed name. This ODCDS may be used to\n   133\u2192   * perform the discovery process in the main thread if there is no discovery going on for this\n   134\u2192   * cluster. When the requested cluster is added and warmed up, the passed callback will be invoked\n   135\u2192   * in the same thread that invoked this function.\n   136\u2192   *\n   137\u2192   * The returned handle can be destroyed to prevent the callback from being invoked. Note that the\n   138\u2192   * handle can only be destroyed in the same thread that invoked the function. Destroying the\n   139\u2192   * handle might not stop the discovery process, though. As soon as the callback is invoked,\n   140\u2192   * destroying the handle does nothing. It is a responsibility of the caller to make sure that the\n   141\u2192   * objects captured in the callback outlive the callback.\n   142\u2192   *\n   143\u2192   * This function is thread-safe.\n   144\u2192   *\n   145\u2192   * @param name is the name of the cluster to be discovered.\n   146\u2192   * @param callback will be called when the discovery is finished.\n   147\u2192   * @param timeout describes how long the operation may take before failing.\n   148\u2192   * @return the discovery process handle.\n   149\u2192   */\n   150\u2192  virtual ClusterDiscoveryCallbackHandlePtr\n   151\u2192  requestOnDemandClusterDiscovery(absl::string_view name, ClusterDiscoveryCallbackPtr callback,\n   152\u2192                                  std::chrono::milliseconds timeout) PURE;\n   153\u2192};\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192using OdCdsApiHandlePtr = std::unique_ptr<OdCdsApiHandle>;\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192class ClusterManagerFactory;\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192// These are per-cluster per-thread, so not \"global\" stats.\n   160\u2192struct ClusterConnectivityState {\n   161\u2192  ~ClusterConnectivityState() {\n   162\u2192    ASSERT(pending_streams_ == 0);\n   163\u2192    ASSERT(active_streams_ == 0);\n   164\u2192    ASSERT(connecting_and_connected_stream_capacity_ == 0);\n   165\u2192  }\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192  template <class T> void checkAndDecrement(T& value, uint32_t delta) {\n   168\u2192    ASSERT(std::numeric_limits<T>::min() + delta <= value);\n   169\u2192    value -= delta;\n   170\u2192  }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  template <class T> void checkAndIncrement(T& value, uint32_t delta) {\n   173\u2192    ASSERT(std::numeric_limits<T>::max() - delta >= value);\n   174\u2192    value += delta;\n   175\u2192  }\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192  void incrPendingStreams(uint32_t delta) { checkAndIncrement(pending_streams_, delta); }\n   178\u2192  void decrPendingStreams(uint32_t delta) { checkAndDecrement(pending_streams_, delta); }\n   179\u2192  void incrConnectingAndConnectedStreamCapacity(uint32_t delta) {\n   180\u2192    checkAndIncrement(connecting_and_connected_stream_capacity_, delta);\n   181\u2192  }\n   182\u2192  void decrConnectingAndConnectedStreamCapacity(uint32_t delta) {\n   183\u2192    checkAndDecrement(connecting_and_connected_stream_capacity_, delta);\n   184\u2192  }\n   185\u2192  void incrActiveStreams(uint32_t delta) { checkAndIncrement(active_streams_, delta); }\n   186\u2192  void decrActiveStreams(uint32_t delta) { checkAndDecrement(active_streams_, delta); }\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  // Tracks the number of pending streams for this ClusterManager.\n   189\u2192  uint32_t pending_streams_{};\n   190\u2192  // Tracks the number of active streams for this ClusterManager.\n   191\u2192  uint32_t active_streams_{};\n   192\u2192  // Tracks the available stream capacity if all connecting connections were connected.\n   193\u2192  //\n   194\u2192  // For example, if an H2 connection is started with concurrent stream limit of 100, this\n   195\u2192  // goes up by 100. If the connection is established and 2 streams are in use, it\n   196\u2192  // would be reduced to 98 (as 2 of the 100 are not available).\n   197\u2192  //\n   198\u2192  // Note that if more HTTP/2 streams have been established than are allowed by\n   199\u2192  // a late-received SETTINGS frame, this MAY BE NEGATIVE.\n   200\u2192  // Note this tracks the sum of multiple 32 bit stream capacities so must remain 64 bit.\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
