Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/envoy/upstream/thread_local_cluster.h",
  "limit": 200
}
Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/common/pure.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/http/async_client.h\"\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/tcp/async_tcp_client.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/load_balancer.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/upstream.h\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    10\u2192namespace Upstream {\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192// HttpPoolData returns information about a given pool as well as a function\n    13\u2192// to create streams on that pool.\n    14\u2192class HttpPoolData {\n    15\u2192public:\n    16\u2192  using OnNewStreamFn = std::function<void()>;\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192  HttpPoolData(OnNewStreamFn on_new_stream, Http::ConnectionPool::Instance* pool)\n    19\u2192      : on_new_stream_(on_new_stream), pool_(pool) {}\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192  /**\n    22\u2192   * See documentation of Http::ConnectionPool::Instance.\n    23\u2192   */\n    24\u2192  Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::Cancellable*\n    25\u2192  newStream(Http::ResponseDecoder& response_decoder,\n    26\u2192            Envoy::Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks& callbacks,\n    27\u2192            const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& stream_options) {\n    28\u2192    on_new_stream_();\n    29\u2192    return pool_->newStream(response_decoder, callbacks, stream_options);\n    30\u2192  }\n    31\u2192  bool hasActiveConnections() const { return pool_->hasActiveConnections(); };\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192  /**\n    34\u2192   * See documentation of Envoy::ConnectionPool::Instance.\n    35\u2192   */\n    36\u2192  void addIdleCallback(ConnectionPool::Instance::IdleCb cb) { pool_->addIdleCallback(cb); };\n    37\u2192  void drainConnections(ConnectionPool::DrainBehavior drain_behavior) {\n    38\u2192    pool_->drainConnections(drain_behavior);\n    39\u2192  };\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host() const { return pool_->host(); }\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192private:\n    44\u2192  friend class HttpPoolDataPeer;\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192  OnNewStreamFn on_new_stream_;\n    47\u2192  Http::ConnectionPool::Instance* pool_;\n    48\u2192};\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// Tcp pool returns information about a given pool, as well as a function to\n    51\u2192// create connections on that pool.\n    52\u2192class TcpPoolData {\n    53\u2192public:\n    54\u2192  using OnNewConnectionFn = std::function<void()>;\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192  TcpPoolData(OnNewConnectionFn on_new_connection, Tcp::ConnectionPool::Instance* pool)\n    57\u2192      : on_new_connection_(on_new_connection), pool_(pool) {}\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192  Envoy::Tcp::ConnectionPool::Cancellable*\n    60\u2192  newConnection(Envoy::Tcp::ConnectionPool::Callbacks& callbacks) {\n    61\u2192    on_new_connection_();\n    62\u2192    return pool_->newConnection(callbacks);\n    63\u2192  }\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host() const { return pool_->host(); }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192private:\n    68\u2192  friend class TcpPoolDataPeer;\n    69\u2192  OnNewConnectionFn on_new_connection_;\n    70\u2192  Tcp::ConnectionPool::Instance* pool_;\n    71\u2192};\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192/**\n    74\u2192 * A thread local cluster instance that can be used for direct load balancing and host set\n    75\u2192 * interactions. In general, an instance of ThreadLocalCluster can only be safely used in the\n    76\u2192 * direct call context after it is retrieved from the cluster manager. See ClusterManager::get()\n    77\u2192 * for more information.\n    78\u2192 */\n    79\u2192class ThreadLocalCluster {\n    80\u2192public:\n    81\u2192  virtual ~ThreadLocalCluster() = default;\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192  /**\n    84\u2192   * @return const PrioritySet& the backing priority set.\n    85\u2192   */\n    86\u2192  virtual const PrioritySet& prioritySet() PURE;\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  /**\n    89\u2192   * @return ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr the info for this cluster. The info is safe to store beyond\n    90\u2192   * the lifetime of the ThreadLocalCluster instance itself.\n    91\u2192   */\n    92\u2192  virtual ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr info() PURE;\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  /**\n    95\u2192   * @return LoadBalancer& the backing load balancer.\n    96\u2192   */\n    97\u2192  virtual LoadBalancer& loadBalancer() PURE;\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  /* Choose a host for the next request. If this returns a host, this should immediately be\n   100\u2192   * followed by a call to httpConnPool or tcpConnPool or load balancing may not work as\n   101\u2192   * expected.\n   102\u2192   *\n   103\u2192   * @param context the optional load balancer context.\n   104\u2192   * @return host the next host selected by the load balancer or null if no host\n   105\u2192   * is available.\n   106\u2192   */\n   107\u2192  virtual HostSelectionResponse chooseHost(LoadBalancerContext* context) PURE;\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192  /**\n   110\u2192   * Allocate a load balanced HTTP connection pool for a cluster. This is *per-thread* so that\n   111\u2192   * callers do not need to worry about per thread synchronization. The load balancing policy that\n   112\u2192   * is used is the one defined on the cluster when it was created.\n   113\u2192   *\n   114\u2192   * @param priority the connection pool priority.\n   115\u2192   * @param downstream_protocol the downstream protocol (if one exists) to use in protocol\n   116\u2192   *        selection.\n   117\u2192   * @param context the optional load balancer context. Must continue to be\n   118\u2192   *        valid until newConnection is called on the pool (if it is to be called).\n   119\u2192   * @return the connection pool data or nullopt if there is no host available in the cluster.\n   120\u2192   */\n   121\u2192  virtual absl::optional<HttpPoolData>\n   122\u2192  httpConnPool(HostConstSharedPtr host, ResourcePriority priority,\n   123\u2192               absl::optional<Http::Protocol> downstream_protocol,\n   124\u2192               LoadBalancerContext* context) PURE;\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192  /**\n   127\u2192   * Allocate a load balanced TCP connection pool for a cluster. This is *per-thread* so that\n   128\u2192   * callers do not need to worry about per thread synchronization. The load balancing policy that\n   129\u2192   * is used is the one defined on the cluster when it was created.\n   130\u2192   *\n   131\u2192   * @param priority the connection pool priority.\n   132\u2192   * @param context the optional load balancer context. Must continue to be\n   133\u2192   *        valid until newConnection is called on the pool (if it is to be called).\n   134\u2192   * @return the connection pool data or nullopt if there is no host available in the cluster.\n   135\u2192   */\n   136\u2192  virtual absl::optional<TcpPoolData> tcpConnPool(HostConstSharedPtr host,\n   137\u2192                                                  ResourcePriority priority,\n   138\u2192                                                  LoadBalancerContext* context) PURE;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  /* a legacy API which synchronously chooses a host and creates a conn pool*/\n   141\u2192  virtual absl::optional<TcpPoolData> tcpConnPool(ResourcePriority priority,\n   142\u2192                                                  LoadBalancerContext* context) PURE;\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192  /**\n   145\u2192   * Allocate a load balanced TCP connection for a cluster. The created connection is already\n   146\u2192   * bound to the correct *per-thread* dispatcher, so no further synchronization is needed. The\n   147\u2192   * load balancing policy that is used is the one defined on the cluster when it was created.\n   148\u2192   *\n   149\u2192   * @param context the optional load balancer context.\n   150\u2192   * @return both a connection and the host that backs the connection. Both can be nullptr if there\n   151\u2192   *         is no host available in the cluster.\n   152\u2192   */\n   153\u2192  virtual Host::CreateConnectionData tcpConn(LoadBalancerContext* context) PURE;\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192  /**\n   156\u2192   * @return a client that can be used to make async HTTP calls against the given cluster. The\n   157\u2192   * client may be backed by a connection pool or by a multiplexed connection. The cluster manager\n   158\u2192   * owns the client.\n   159\u2192   */\n   160\u2192  virtual Http::AsyncClient& httpAsyncClient() PURE;\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192  /**\n   163\u2192   * @param context the optional load balancer context.\n   164\u2192   * @param options the tcp client creation config options.\n   165\u2192   * @return a client that can be used to make async Tcp calls against the given cluster.\n   166\u2192   */\n   167\u2192  virtual Tcp::AsyncTcpClientPtr\n   168\u2192  tcpAsyncClient(LoadBalancerContext* context,\n   169\u2192                 Tcp::AsyncTcpClientOptionsConstSharedPtr options) PURE;\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192  /**\n   172\u2192   * @return the thread local cluster drop_overload configuration.\n   173\u2192   */\n   174\u2192  virtual UnitFloat dropOverload() const PURE;\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  /**\n   177\u2192   * @return the thread local cluster drop_category configuration.\n   178\u2192   */\n   179\u2192  virtual const std::string& dropCategory() const PURE;\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192  /**\n   182\u2192   * Set up the drop_overload value for the thread local cluster.\n   183\u2192   */\n   184\u2192  virtual void setDropOverload(UnitFloat drop_overload) PURE;\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192  /**\n   187\u2192   * Set up the drop_category value for the thread local cluster.\n   188\u2192   */\n   189\u2192  virtual void setDropCategory(absl::string_view drop_category) PURE;\n   190\u2192};\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192using ThreadLocalClusterOptRef = absl::optional<std::reference_wrapper<ThreadLocalCluster>>;\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192} // namespace Upstream\n   195\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   196\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

  "command": "grep -n \"matchRoute\" /workspace/source/common/router/config_impl.cc | head -5"
}

137. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore upstream connection and data flow",
  "prompt": "Explore the Envoy codebase to understand upstream connections and data flow:\n\n1. Find how the connection pool creates actual upstream connections\n2. Find the GenericUpstream interface and how it writes HTTP requests\n3. Find how responses flow back from upstream through filters\n4. Find the encoder filter chain invocation\n5. Find where decoder vs encoder filter chains are called\n\nSearch for:\n- GenericConnPool and ConnPool implementation in source/common/router/\n- HttpConnPool, TcpConnPool classes\n- GenericUpstream interface and how HTTP data is written\n- Response decoder callbacks\n- How filter iteration reverses for encoder chain\n- Where response headers trigger encoder filter iteration\n\nReturn specific file paths, class names, and method names with line numbers.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a20f18c30fec2d1b8",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:\n\n## Envoy Upstream Connections and Data Flow Analysis\n\nBased on my exploration of the Envoy codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how upstream connections are created, how data flows through the system, and how encoder/decoder filter chains are invoked:\n\n---\n\n### 1. CONNECTION POOL CREATION AND UPSTREAM CONNECTION ESTABLISHMENT\n\n#### Connection Pool Implementation Files\n\n**Base Classes:**\n- `/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.h` (lines 19-421) - Base connection pool implementation\n- `/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.cc` (lines 145-248) - Connection creation logic\n\n**HTTP Connection Pools:**\n- `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.h` (lines 49-103) - HTTP pool base\n- `/workspace/source/common/http/http1/conn_pool.h` - HTTP/1.1 pool\n- `/workspace/source/common/http/http2/conn_pool.h` - HTTP/2 pool\n\n**TCP Connection Pools:**\n- `/workspace/source/common/tcp/conn_pool.h` (lines 135-177) - TCP pool implementation\n\n#### How Connections are Created\n\n**Key Method:** `ConnPoolImplBase::tryCreateNewConnection()` at `/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.cc` lines 164-201\n\n```\nConnection creation flow:\n1. tryCreateNewConnection() checks if a new connection should be created (line 166)\n2. Calls instantiateActiveClient() (line 182) - PURE VIRTUAL method overridden by HTTP/TCP pools\n3. HTTP pools create CodecClient via ActiveClient constructor at `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.h` lines 107-124\n4. HTTP/1 ActiveClient creates codec via newStreamEncoder() at `/workspace/source/common/http/http1/conn_pool.h` line 24\n5. TCP pool creates direct network connection via /workspace/source/common/tcp/conn_pool.h` lines 89-91\n```\n\n**ActiveClient State Machine:**\n- `/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.h` lines 94-103 define states:\n  - `Connecting` - Connection establishing\n  - `Ready` - Ready to accept streams\n  - `Busy` - At concurrent stream limit\n  - `Draining` - No new streams, closing when empty\n  - `ReadyForEarlyData` - 0-RTT support\n  - `Closed` - Deleted\n\n---\n\n### 2. GENERIC UPSTREAM INTERFACE AND REQUEST WRITING\n\n#### GenericUpstream Interface\n\n**Definition:** `/workspace/envoy/tcp/upstream.h` lines 114-160\n\n```cpp\nclass GenericUpstream : public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n  virtual bool readDisable(bool disable) PURE;\n  virtual void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n  virtual void addBytesSentCallback(Network::Connection::BytesSentCb cb) PURE;\n  // ... additional methods\n```\n\nKey methods:\n- `encodeData()` - Writes HTTP request data upstream (line 132)\n- `readDisable()` - Controls upstream reading (line 125)\n- `onDownstreamEvent()` - Handles downstream connection events (line 146)\n\n#### How HTTP Data is Written\n\n**Connection Pool Callbacks:** `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.cc` lines 84-94\n\n```cpp\nvoid HttpConnPoolImplBase::onPoolReady(...) {\n  ActiveClient* http_client = static_cast<ActiveClient*>(&client);\n  Http::RequestEncoder& new_encoder = http_client->newStreamEncoder(response_decoder);\n  callbacks.onPoolReady(new_encoder, client.real_host_description_, ...);\n}\n```\n\n**Request Writing:**\n1. UpstreamRequest::acceptHeadersFromRouter() at `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` lines 380-434\n   - Calls `conn_pool_->newStream(this)` (line 404) to initiate connection\n   - Passes request through UpstreamFilterManager via `filter_manager_->decodeHeaders()` (line 433)\n\n2. UpstreamRequest::acceptDataFromRouter() at lines 436-441\n   - Sends body data via `filter_manager_->decodeData()` (line 440)\n\n3. HTTP codec client encodes data through RequestEncoder interface\n   - Encoder writes to underlying codec (HTTP/1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3)\n\n---\n\n### 3. RESPONSE FLOW FROM UPSTREAM\n\n#### Response Decoder Callbacks\n\n**Upstream Response Path:** `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` lines 267-340\n\n**decodeHeaders()** - lines 267-307:\n- Receives response headers from upstream codec\n- Calls `parent_.onUpstreamHeaders()` to pass to router filter\n- Router filter then invokes downstream encoder filter chain\n\n**decodeData()** - lines 321-327:\n- Receives response body chunks\n- Calls `parent_.onUpstreamData()` to pass to router filter\n\n**decodeTrailers()** - lines 329-340:\n- Receives response trailers\n- Calls `parent_.onUpstreamTrailers()` to pass to router filter\n\n#### Data Flow Back to Downstream\n\n**Key Components:**\n- UpstreamRequest acts as ResponseDecoder for upstream codec\n- UpstreamRequest's decode* methods receive upstream responses\n- These calls propagate through router filter to downstream filter chain\n\n**Filter Manager Callbacks:** `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.h` lines 265-297\n\n```cpp\nclass UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks : public Http::FilterManagerCallbacks {\n  void encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&, bool end_stream) override {\n    upstream_request_.decodeHeaders(...);  // Maps encode to decode\n  }\n  void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override {\n    upstream_request_.decodeData(data, end_stream);\n  }\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. ENCODER VS DECODER FILTER CHAIN INVOCATION\n\n#### Filter Chain Structure\n\n**Decoder Filters (Request Path):** `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` lines 73-81\n\n```cpp\nstruct StreamDecoderFilters {\n  using Element = ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;\n  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr>::iterator;\n  \n  Iterator begin() { return entries_.begin(); }  // Forward iteration A\u2192B\u2192C\n};\n```\n\n**Encoder Filters (Response Path):** `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` lines 92-100\n\n```cpp\nstruct StreamEncoderFilters {\n  using Element = ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;\n  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator;\n  \n  Iterator begin() { return entries_.rbegin(); }  // REVERSE iteration C\u2192B\u2192A\n};\n```\n\n**Critical Difference:** Encoder filters iterate in REVERSE order! This is documented at lines 89-91.\n\n#### Encoder Filter Chain Invocation\n\n**Main Method:** `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` lines 1225-1314\n\n```cpp\nvoid FilterManager::encodeHeaders(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, \n                                   ResponseHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\n  // Line 1234: Starts from REVERSE iterator\n  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry = \n      commonEncodePrefix(filter, end_stream, FilterIterationStartState::AlwaysStartFromNext);\n  \n  // Line 1238-1286: Iterates through encoder_filters_.end() (which is rend for reverse)\n  for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n    // ... handle status and continue iteration\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Points:**\n- `commonEncodePrefix()` at lines 900-932 returns `encoder_filters_.begin()` which is `rbegin()` (reverse begin)\n- Filter iteration happens in REVERSE order of configuration\n- Configured as [A, B, C] \u2192 executed as C, B, A for encoding\n\n#### Decoder Filter Chain Invocation\n\n**Main Method:** `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` lines 537-623\n\n```cpp\nvoid FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, \n                                   RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool end_stream) {\n  // Line 653: Starts from forward iterator\n  StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator entry = commonDecodePrefix(filter, ...);\n  \n  // Lines 554-600: Iterates normally A\u2192B\u2192C\n  for (; entry != decoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. RESPONSE HEADERS TRIGGERING ENCODER CHAIN\n\n**Where Response Headers Enter Encoder Chain:**\n\n1. **Upstream Response Reception:** \n   - UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders() at line 267\n   - Calls `parent_.onUpstreamHeaders()` \n\n2. **Router Filter Processing:**\n   - Router filter receives `onUpstreamHeaders()` callback\n   - Router calls `callbacks_->encodeHeaders()` to start encoder chain\n\n3. **DownstreamFilterManager::encodeHeaders() Invocation:**\n   - Located at `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` line 1225\n   - Calls `FilterManager::encodeHeaders()` \n\n4. **Encoder Filter Iteration (REVERSE order):**\n   - Starts with last filter in configuration (C)\n   - Each filter can modify headers and call `continueEncoding()`\n   - Proceeds backwards through chain (C\u2192B\u2192A)\n\n---\n\n### 6. KEY FILE LOCATIONS AND LINE NUMBERS SUMMARY\n\n| Component | File | Lines | Description |\n|-----------|------|-------|-------------|\n| **GenericConnPool Interface** | `/workspace/envoy/tcp/upstream.h` | 61-75 | Creates TCP/HTTP connection pools |\n| **GenericUpstream Interface** | `/workspace/envoy/tcp/upstream.h` | 114-160 | Encodes data to upstream |\n| **ActiveClient Base** | `/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.h` | 30-160 | Manages upstream connections |\n| **Connection Creation** | `/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.cc` | 164-201 | Creates new upstream connections |\n| **HTTP Pool onPoolReady** | `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.cc` | 84-94 | Called when connection ready, creates encoder |\n| **HTTP/1 ActiveClient** | `/workspace/source/common/http/http1/conn_pool.h` | 17-69 | HTTP/1.1 connection wrapping |\n| **HTTP/2 ActiveClient** | `/workspace/source/common/http/http2/conn_pool.h` | 18-29 | HTTP/2 connection wrapping |\n| **TCP ActiveClient** | `/workspace/source/common/tcp/conn_pool.h` | 40-133 | TCP connection management |\n| **Request Entry Point** | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` | 380-434 | acceptHeadersFromRouter initiates upstream conn |\n| **Response Headers Received** | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` | 267-307 | decodeHeaders receives upstream response |\n| **Response Data Received** | `/workspace/source/common/router/upstream_request.cc` | 321-327 | decodeData handles response body |\n| **Decoder Filters (A\u2192B\u2192C)** | `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` | 73-81 | Request filter chain structure |\n| **Encoder Filters (C\u2192B\u2192A)** | `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.h` | 92-100 | REVERSE order response filter chain |\n| **Decoder Filter Iteration** | `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` | 537-623 | Forward iteration for request path |\n| **Encoder Filter Iteration** | `/workspace/source/common/http/filter_manager.cc` | 1225-1314 | REVERSE iteration for response path |\n| **Pool Ready Callback** | `/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.cc` | 84-94 | Called when upstream connection established |\n| **Attach Stream to Client** | `/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.cc` | 203-248 | Binds stream to connection and calls onPoolReady |\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL INSIGHT: REVERSE ENCODER FILTER ORDER\n\nThe encoder filter chain iterates in **reverse order** of the configuration:\n- **Configured:** `[RouterFilter, CompressFilter, MetricsFilter]`\n- **Decoder iteration:** RouterFilter \u2192 CompressFilter \u2192 MetricsFilter (forward)\n- **Encoder iteration:** MetricsFilter \u2192 CompressFilter \u2192 RouterFilter (reverse)\n\nThis allows response transformations to flow backwards through the request transformation chain, ensuring symmetrical processing.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Envoy codebase to understand upstream connections and data flow:\n\n1. Find how the connection pool creates actual upstream connections\n2. Find the GenericUpstream interface and how it writes HTTP requests\n3. Find how responses flow back from upstream through filters\n4. Find the encoder filter chain invocation\n5. Find where decoder vs encoder filter chains are called\n\nSearch for:\n- GenericConnPool and ConnPool implementation in source/common/router/\n- HttpConnPool, TcpConnPool classes\n- GenericUpstream interface and how HTTP data is written\n- Response decoder callbacks\n- How filter iteration reverses for encoder chain\n- Where response headers trigger encoder filter iteration\n\nReturn specific file paths, class names, and method names with line numbers.",

         const StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state) const PURE;\n    54\u2192  virtual const Envoy::Router::FilterConfig& routerFilterConfig() const PURE;\n    55\u2192  virtual Server::Configuration::ServerFactoryContext& serverFactoryContext() const PURE;\n    56\u2192};\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192using TunnelingConfigHelperOptConstRef = OptRef<const TunnelingConfigHelper>;\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192// An API for wrapping either a TCP or an HTTP connection pool.\n    61\u2192class GenericConnPool : public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n    62\u2192                        public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::router> {\n    63\u2192public:\n    64\u2192  ~GenericConnPool() override = default;\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192  /**\n    67\u2192   * Called to create a TCP connection or HTTP stream for \"CONNECT\" streams.\n    68\u2192   *\n    69\u2192   * The implementation is then responsible for calling either onGenericPoolReady or\n    70\u2192   * onGenericPoolFailure on the supplied GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks.\n    71\u2192   *\n    72\u2192   * @param callbacks callbacks to communicate stream failure or creation on.\n    73\u2192   */\n    74\u2192  virtual void newStream(GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks& callbacks) PURE;\n    75\u2192};\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192// An API for the UpstreamRequest to get callbacks from either an HTTP or TCP\n    78\u2192// connection pool.\n    79\u2192class GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks {\n    80\u2192public:\n    81\u2192  virtual ~GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks() = default;\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192  /**\n    84\u2192   * Called when GenericConnPool::newStream has established a new stream.\n    85\u2192   *\n    86\u2192   * @param info supplies the stream info object associated with the upstream connection.\n    87\u2192   * @param upstream supplies the generic upstream for the stream.\n    88\u2192   * @param host supplies the description of the host that will carry the request.\n    89\u2192   * @param address_provider supplies the address provider of the upstream connection.\n    90\u2192   * @param ssl_info supplies the ssl information of the upstream connection.\n    91\u2192   */\n    92\u2192  virtual void onGenericPoolReady(StreamInfo::StreamInfo* info,\n    93\u2192                                  std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream>&& upstream,\n    94\u2192                                  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr& host,\n    95\u2192                                  const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& address_provider,\n    96\u2192                                  Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr ssl_info) PURE;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  /**\n    99\u2192   * Called to indicate a failure for GenericConnPool::newStream to establish a stream.\n   100\u2192   *\n   101\u2192   * @param reason supplies the failure reason.\n   102\u2192   * @param failure_reason failure reason string (Note: it is expected that the caller will provide\n   103\u2192   * matching `reason` and `failure_reason`).\n   104\u2192   * @param host supplies the description of the host that caused the failure. This may be nullptr\n   105\u2192   *             if no host was involved in the failure (for example overflow).\n   106\u2192   */\n   107\u2192  virtual void onGenericPoolFailure(ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason reason,\n   108\u2192                                    absl::string_view failure_reason,\n   109\u2192                                    Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) PURE;\n   110\u2192};\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192// Interface for a generic Upstream, which can communicate with a TCP or HTTP\n   113\u2192// upstream.\n   114\u2192class GenericUpstream : public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n   115\u2192public:\n   116\u2192  ~GenericUpstream() override = default;\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192  /**\n   119\u2192   * Enable/disable further data from this stream.\n   120\u2192   *\n   121\u2192   * @param disable true if the stream should be read disabled, false otherwise.\n   122\u2192   * @return returns true if the disable is performed, false otherwise\n   123\u2192   *         (e.g. if the connection is closed)\n   124\u2192   */\n   125\u2192  virtual bool readDisable(bool disable) PURE;\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  /**\n   128\u2192   * Encodes data upstream.\n   129\u2192   * @param data supplies the data to encode. The data may be moved by the encoder.\n   130\u2192   * @param end_stream supplies whether this is the last data to encode.\n   131\u2192   */\n   132\u2192  virtual void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) PURE;\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192  /**\n   135\u2192   * Adds a callback to be called when the data is sent to the kernel.\n   136\u2192   * @param cb supplies the callback to be called\n   137\u2192   */\n   138\u2192  virtual void addBytesSentCallback(Network::Connection::BytesSentCb cb) PURE;\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192  /**\n   141\u2192   * Called when an event is received on the downstream connection\n   142\u2192   * @param event supplies the event which occurred.\n   143\u2192   * @return the underlying ConnectionData if the event is not \"Connected\" and draining\n   144\u2192             is supported for this upstream.\n   145\u2192   */\n   146\u2192  virtual Tcp::ConnectionPool::ConnectionData*\n   147\u2192  onDownstreamEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) PURE;\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192  /* Called to convert underlying transport socket from non-secure mode\n   150\u2192   * to secure mode. Implemented only by start_tls transport socket.\n   151\u2192   */\n   152\u2192  virtual bool startUpstreamSecureTransport() PURE;\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192  /**\n   155\u2192   * Called when upstream starttls socket is converted to tls and upstream ssl info\n   156\u2192   * needs to be set in the connection's stream_info.\n   157\u2192   * @return the const SSL connection data of upstream.\n   158\u2192   */\n   159\u2192  virtual Ssl::ConnectionInfoConstSharedPtr getUpstreamConnectionSslInfo() PURE;\n   160\u2192};\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192using GenericConnPoolPtr = std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool>;\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192/*\n   165\u2192 * A factory for creating generic connection pools.\n   166\u2192 */\n   167\u2192class GenericConnPoolFactory : public Envoy::Config::TypedFactory {\n   168\u2192public:\n   169\u2192  ~GenericConnPoolFactory() override = default;\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192  /*\n   172\u2192   * @param host the host to connect to\n   173\u2192   * @param thread_local_cluster the thread local cluster to use for conn pool creation.\n   174\u2192   * @param config the tunneling config, if doing connect tunneling.\n   175\u2192   * @param context the load balancing context for this connection.\n   176\u2192   * @param upstream_callbacks the callbacks to provide to the connection if successfully created.\n   177\u2192   * @param downstream_info is the downstream connection stream info.\n   178\u2192   * @return may be null if there is no cluster with the given name.\n   179\u2192   */\n   180\u2192  virtual GenericConnPoolPtr createGenericConnPool(\n   181\u2192      Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host, Upstream::ThreadLocalCluster& thread_local_cluster,\n   182\u2192      TunnelingConfigHelperOptConstRef config, Upstream::LoadBalancerContext* context,\n   183\u2192      Tcp::ConnectionPool::UpstreamCallbacks& upstream_callbacks,\n   184\u2192      Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& stream_decoder_callbacks,\n   185\u2192      StreamInfo::StreamInfo& downstream_info) const PURE;\n   186\u2192};\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192using GenericConnPoolFactoryPtr = std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPoolFactory>;\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192} // namespace TcpProxy\n   191\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   192\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <functional>\n     4\u2192#include <memory>\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/common/conn_pool.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/common/pure.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/event/deferred_deletable.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/http/codec.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/upstream.h\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    13\u2192namespace Http {\n    14\u2192namespace ConnectionPool {\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192using PoolFailureReason = ::Envoy::ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason;\n    17\u2192using Cancellable = ::Envoy::ConnectionPool::Cancellable;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192/**\n    20\u2192 * Pool callbacks invoked in the context of a newStream() call, either synchronously or\n    21\u2192 * asynchronously.\n    22\u2192 */\n    23\u2192class Callbacks {\n    24\u2192public:\n    25\u2192  virtual ~Callbacks() = default;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192  /**\n    28\u2192   * Called when a pool error occurred and no connection could be acquired for making the request.\n    29\u2192   * @param reason supplies the failure reason.\n    30\u2192   * @param transport_failure_reason supplies the details of the transport failure reason.\n    31\u2192   * @param host supplies the description of the host that caused the failure. This may be nullptr\n    32\u2192   *             if no host was involved in the failure (for example overflow).\n    33\u2192   */\n    34\u2192  virtual void onPoolFailure(PoolFailureReason reason, absl::string_view transport_failure_reason,\n    35\u2192                             Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) PURE;\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192  /**\n    38\u2192   * Called when a connection is available to process a request/response.\n    39\u2192   * @param encoder supplies the request encoder to use.\n    40\u2192   * @param host supplies the description of the host that will carry the request. For logical\n    41\u2192   *             connection pools the description may be different each time this is called.\n    42\u2192   * @param info supplies the stream info object associated with the upstream L4 connection.\n    43\u2192   * @param protocol supplies the protocol associated with the stream, or absl::nullopt for raw TCP.\n    44\u2192   */\n    45\u2192  virtual void onPoolReady(RequestEncoder& encoder, Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n    46\u2192                           StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info,\n    47\u2192                           absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol) PURE;\n    48\u2192};\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192class Instance;\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192/**\n    53\u2192 * Pool callbacks invoked to track the lifetime of connections in the pool.\n    54\u2192 */\n    55\u2192class ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks {\n    56\u2192public:\n    57\u2192  virtual ~ConnectionLifetimeCallbacks() = default;\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192  /**\n    60\u2192   * Called when a connection is open for requests in a pool.\n    61\u2192   * @param pool which the connection is associated with.\n    62\u2192   * @param hash_key the hash key used for this connection.\n    63\u2192   * @param connection newly open connection.\n    64\u2192   */\n    65\u2192  virtual void onConnectionOpen(Instance& pool, std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key,\n    66\u2192                                const Network::Connection& connection) PURE;\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192  /**\n    69\u2192   * Called when a connection is draining and may no longer be used for requests.\n    70\u2192   * @param pool which the connection is associated with.\n    71\u2192   * @param hash_key the hash key used for this connection.\n    72\u2192   * @param connection newly open connection.\n    73\u2192   */\n    74\u2192  virtual void onConnectionDraining(Instance& pool, std::vector<uint8_t>& hash_key,\n    75\u2192                                    const Network::Connection& connection) PURE;\n    76\u2192};\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192/**\n    79\u2192 * An instance of a generic connection pool.\n    80\u2192 */\n    81\u2192class Instance : public Envoy::ConnectionPool::Instance, public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n    82\u2192public:\n    83\u2192  struct StreamOptions {\n    84\u2192    // True if the request can be sent as early data.\n    85\u2192    bool can_send_early_data_;\n    86\u2192    // True if the request can be sent over HTTP/3.\n    87\u2192    bool can_use_http3_;\n    88\u2192  };\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  ~Instance() override = default;\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192  /**\n    93\u2192   * Determines whether the connection pool is actively processing any requests.\n    94\u2192   * @return true if the connection pool has any pending requests or any active requests.\n    95\u2192   */\n    96\u2192  virtual bool hasActiveConnections() const PURE;\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192  /**\n    99\u2192   * Create a new stream on the pool.\n   100\u2192   * @param response_decoder supplies the decoder events to fire when the response is\n   101\u2192   *                         available.\n   102\u2192   * @param cb supplies the callbacks to invoke when the connection is ready or has failed. The\n   103\u2192   *           callbacks may be invoked immediately within the context of this call if there is a\n   104\u2192   *           ready connection or an immediate failure. In this case, the routine returns nullptr.\n   105\u2192   * @param options specifies how to create the requested stream.\n   106\u2192   * @return Cancellable* If no connection is ready, the callback is not invoked, and a handle\n   107\u2192   *                      is returned that can be used to cancel the request. Otherwise, one of the\n   108\u2192   *                      callbacks is called and the routine returns nullptr. NOTE: Once a callback\n   109\u2192   *                      is called, the handle is no longer valid and any further cancellation\n   110\u2192   *                      should be done by resetting the stream.\n   111\u2192   * @warning Do not call cancel() from the callbacks, as the request is implicitly canceled when\n   112\u2192   *          the callbacks are called.\n   113\u2192   */\n   114\u2192  virtual Cancellable* newStream(Http::ResponseDecoder& response_decoder, Callbacks& callbacks,\n   115\u2192                                 const StreamOptions& options) PURE;\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192  /**\n   118\u2192   * Returns a user-friendly protocol description for logging.\n   119\u2192   * @return absl::string_view a protocol description for logging.\n   120\u2192   */\n   121\u2192  virtual absl::string_view protocolDescription() const PURE;\n   122\u2192};\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192using InstancePtr = std::unique_ptr<Instance>;\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192} // namespace ConnectionPool\n   127\u2192} // namespace Http\n   128\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   129\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
149. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.h"
}
Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/common/conn_pool.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/event/dispatcher.h\"\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/stats/timespan.h\"\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/cluster_manager.h\"\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/common/debug_recursion_checker.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/common/dump_state_utils.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/common/linked_object.h\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192#include \"absl/strings/string_view.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"fmt/ostream.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    17\u2192namespace ConnectionPool {\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192class ConnPoolImplBase;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192// A placeholder struct for whatever data a given connection pool needs to\n    22\u2192// successfully attach an upstream connection to a downstream connection.\n    23\u2192struct AttachContext {\n    24\u2192  // Add a virtual destructor to allow for the dynamic_cast ASSERT in typedContext.\n    25\u2192  virtual ~AttachContext() = default;\n    26\u2192};\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192// ActiveClient provides a base class for connection pool clients that handles connection timings\n    29\u2192// as well as managing the connection timeout.\n    30\u2192class ActiveClient : public LinkedObject<ActiveClient>,\n    31\u2192                     public Network::ConnectionCallbacks,\n    32\u2192                     public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n    33\u2192                     protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::pool> {\n    34\u2192public:\n    35\u2192  ActiveClient(ConnPoolImplBase& parent, uint32_t lifetime_stream_limit,\n    36\u2192               uint32_t effective_concurrent_streams, uint32_t concurrent_stream_limit);\n    37\u2192  ActiveClient(ConnPoolImplBase& parent, uint32_t lifetime_stream_limit,\n    38\u2192               uint32_t concurrent_stream_limit);\n    39\u2192  ~ActiveClient() override;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  virtual void releaseResources() { releaseResourcesBase(); }\n    42\u2192  void releaseResourcesBase();\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n    45\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override {}\n    46\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override {}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192  // Called if the connection does not complete within the cluster's connectTimeout()\n    49\u2192  void onConnectTimeout();\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192  // Called if the maximum connection duration is reached.\n    52\u2192  void onConnectionDurationTimeout();\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192  // Returns the concurrent stream limit, accounting for if the total stream limit\n    55\u2192  // is less than the concurrent stream limit.\n    56\u2192  virtual uint32_t effectiveConcurrentStreamLimit() const {\n    57\u2192    return std::min(remaining_streams_, concurrent_stream_limit_);\n    58\u2192  }\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  // Returns the application protocol, or absl::nullopt for TCP.\n    61\u2192  virtual absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol() const PURE;\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192  virtual int64_t currentUnusedCapacity() const {\n    64\u2192    int64_t remaining_concurrent_streams =\n    65\u2192        static_cast<int64_t>(concurrent_stream_limit_) - numActiveStreams();\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    return std::min<int64_t>(remaining_streams_, remaining_concurrent_streams);\n    68\u2192  }\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  // Initialize upstream read filters. Called when connected.\n    71\u2192  virtual void initializeReadFilters() PURE;\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192  // Closes the underlying connection.\n    74\u2192  virtual void close() PURE;\n    75\u2192  // Returns the ID of the underlying connection.\n    76\u2192  virtual uint64_t id() const PURE;\n    77\u2192  // Returns true if this closed with an incomplete stream, for stats tracking/ purposes.\n    78\u2192  virtual bool closingWithIncompleteStream() const PURE;\n    79\u2192  // Returns the number of active streams on this connection.\n    80\u2192  virtual uint32_t numActiveStreams() const PURE;\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192  // Return true if it is ready to dispatch the next stream.\n    83\u2192  virtual bool readyForStream() const {\n    84\u2192    ASSERT(!supportsEarlyData());\n    85\u2192    return state_ == State::Ready;\n    86\u2192  }\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  // This function is called onStreamClosed to see if there was a negative delta\n    89\u2192  // and (if necessary) update associated bookkeeping.\n    90\u2192  // HTTP/1 and TCP pools can not have negative delta so the default implementation simply returns\n    91\u2192  // false. The HTTP/2 connection pool can have this state, so overrides this function.\n    92\u2192  virtual bool hadNegativeDeltaOnStreamClosed() { return false; }\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  enum class State {\n    95\u2192    Connecting,        // Connection is not yet established.\n    96\u2192    ReadyForEarlyData, // Any additional early data stream can be immediately dispatched to this\n    97\u2192                       // connection.\n    98\u2192    Ready,             // Additional streams may be immediately dispatched to this connection.\n    99\u2192    Busy,              // Connection is at its concurrent stream limit.\n   100\u2192    Draining,          // No more streams can be dispatched to this connection, and it will be\n   101\u2192                       // closed when all streams complete.\n   102\u2192    Closed             // Connection is closed and object is queued for destruction.\n   103\u2192  };\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192  State state() const { return state_; }\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192  void setState(State state) {\n   108\u2192    if (state == State::ReadyForEarlyData && !supportsEarlyData()) {\n   109\u2192      IS_ENVOY_BUG(\"Unable to set state to ReadyForEarlyData in a client which does not support \"\n   110\u2192                   \"early data.\");\n   111\u2192      return;\n   112\u2192    }\n   113\u2192    // If the client is transitioning to draining, update the remaining\n   114\u2192    // streams and pool and cluster capacity.\n   115\u2192    if (state == State::Draining) {\n   116\u2192      drain();\n   117\u2192    }\n   118\u2192    state_ = state;\n   119\u2192  }\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  // Sets the remaining streams to 0, and updates pool and cluster capacity.\n   122\u2192  virtual void drain();\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192  virtual bool hasHandshakeCompleted() const {\n   125\u2192    ASSERT(!supportsEarlyData());\n   126\u2192    return state_ != State::Connecting;\n   127\u2192  }\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192  ConnPoolImplBase& parent_;\n   130\u2192  // The count of remaining streams allowed for this connection.\n   131\u2192  // This will start out as the total number of streams per connection if capped\n   132\u2192  // by configuration, or it will be set to std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max() to be\n   133\u2192  // (functionally) unlimited.\n   134\u2192  // TODO: this could be moved to an optional to make it actually unlimited.\n   135\u2192  uint32_t remaining_streams_;\n   136\u2192  // The will start out as the upper limit of max concurrent streams for this connection\n   137\u2192  // if capped by configuration, or it will be set to std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()\n   138\u2192  // to be (functionally) unlimited.\n   139\u2192  uint32_t configured_stream_limit_;\n   140\u2192  // The max concurrent stream for this connection, it's initialized by `configured_stream_limit_`\n   141\u2192  // and can be adjusted by SETTINGS frame, but the max value of it can't exceed\n   142\u2192  // `configured_stream_limit_`.\n   143\u2192  uint32_t concurrent_stream_limit_;\n   144\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr real_host_description_;\n   145\u2192  Stats::TimespanPtr conn_connect_ms_;\n   146\u2192  Stats::TimespanPtr conn_length_;\n   147\u2192  Event::TimerPtr connect_timer_;\n   148\u2192  Event::TimerPtr connection_duration_timer_;\n   149\u2192  bool resources_released_{false};\n   150\u2192  bool timed_out_{false};\n   151\u2192  // TODO(danzh) remove this once http codec exposes the handshake state for h3.\n   152\u2192  bool has_handshake_completed_{false};\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192protected:\n   155\u2192  // HTTP/3 subclass should override this.\n   156\u2192  virtual bool supportsEarlyData() const { return false; }\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192private:\n   159\u2192  State state_{State::Connecting};\n   160\u2192};\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192// PendingStream is the base class tracking streams for which a connection has been created but not\n   163\u2192// yet established.\n   164\u2192class PendingStream : public LinkedObject<PendingStream>, public ConnectionPool::Cancellable {\n   165\u2192public:\n   166\u2192  PendingStream(ConnPoolImplBase& parent, bool can_send_early_data);\n   167\u2192  ~PendingStream() override;\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  // ConnectionPool::Cancellable\n   170\u2192  void cancel(Envoy::ConnectionPool::CancelPolicy policy) override;\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  // The context here returns a pointer to whatever context is provided with newStream(),\n   173\u2192  // which will be passed back to the parent in onPoolReady or onPoolFailure.\n   174\u2192  virtual AttachContext& context() PURE;\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  ConnPoolImplBase& parent_;\n   177\u2192  // The request can be sent as early data.\n   178\u2192  bool can_send_early_data_;\n   179\u2192};\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192using PendingStreamPtr = std::unique_ptr<PendingStream>;\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192using ActiveClientPtr = std::unique_ptr<ActiveClient>;\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192// Base class that handles stream queueing logic shared between connection pool implementations.\n   186\u2192class ConnPoolImplBase : protected Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::pool> {\n   187\u2192public:\n   188\u2192  ConnPoolImplBase(Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host, Upstream::ResourcePriority priority,\n   189\u2192                   Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher,\n   190\u2192                   const Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n   191\u2192                   const Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr& transport_socket_options,\n   192\u2192                   Upstream::ClusterConnectivityState& state);\n   193\u2192  virtual ~ConnPoolImplBase();\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192  void deleteIsPendingImpl();\n   196\u2192  // By default, the connection pool will track connected and connecting stream\n   197\u2192  // capacity as streams are created and destroyed. QUIC does custom stream\n   198\u2192  // accounting so will override this to false.\n   199\u2192  virtual bool trackStreamCapacity() { return true; }\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192  // A helper function to get the specific context type from the base class context.\n   202\u2192  template <class T> T& typedContext(AttachContext& context) {\n   203\u2192    ASSERT(dynamic_cast<T*>(&context) != nullptr);\n   204\u2192    return *static_cast<T*>(&context);\n   205\u2192  }\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192  // Determines if prefetching is warranted based on the number of streams in\n   208\u2192  // use, pending streams, anticipated and/or currently unused capacity, and\n   209\u2192  // preconnect configuration.\n   210\u2192  //\n   211\u2192  // If anticipate_incoming_stream is true this assumes a call to newStream is\n   212\u2192  // pending, which is true for global preconnect.\n   213\u2192  static bool shouldConnect(size_t pending_streams, size_t active_streams,\n   214\u2192                            int64_t connecting_and_connected_capacity, float preconnect_ratio,\n   215\u2192                            bool anticipate_incoming_stream = false);\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192  // Envoy::ConnectionPool::Instance implementation helpers\n   218\u2192  void addIdleCallbackImpl(Instance::IdleCb cb);\n   219\u2192  // Returns true if the pool is idle.\n   220\u2192  bool isIdleImpl() const;\n   221\u2192  void drainConnectionsImpl(DrainBehavior drain_behavior);\n   222\u2192  const Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr& host() const { return host_; }\n   223\u2192  // Called if this pool is likely to be picked soon, to determine if it's worth preconnecting.\n   224\u2192  bool maybePreconnectImpl(float global_preconnect_ratio);\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192  // Closes and destroys all connections. This must be called in the destructor of\n   227\u2192  // derived classes because the derived ActiveClient will downcast parent_ to a more\n   228\u2192  // specific type of ConnPoolImplBase, but if the more specific part is already destructed\n   229\u2192  // (due to bottom-up destructor ordering in c++) that access will be invalid.\n   230\u2192  void destructAllConnections();\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192  // Returns a new instance of ActiveClient.\n   233\u2192  virtual ActiveClientPtr instantiateActiveClient() PURE;\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192  // Gets a pointer to the list that currently owns this client.\n   236\u2192  std::list<ActiveClientPtr>& owningList(ActiveClient::State state);\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192  // Removes the PendingStream from the list of streams. Called when the PendingStream is\n   239\u2192  // cancelled, e.g. when the stream is reset before a connection has been established.\n   240\u2192  void onPendingStreamCancel(PendingStream& stream, Envoy::ConnectionPool::CancelPolicy policy);\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192  // Fails all pending streams, calling onPoolFailure on the associated callbacks.\n   243\u2192  void purgePendingStreams(const Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr& host_description,\n   244\u2192                           absl::string_view failure_reason,\n   245\u2192                           ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason pool_failure_reason);\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192  // Closes any idle connections as this pool is drained.\n   248\u2192  void closeIdleConnectionsForDrainingPool();\n   249\u2192\n   250\u2192  // Changes the state_ of an ActiveClient and moves to the appropriate list.\n   251\u2192  void transitionActiveClientState(ActiveClient& client, ActiveClient::State new_state);\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192  void onConnectionEvent(ActiveClient& client, absl::string_view failure_reason,\n   254\u2192                         Network::ConnectionEvent event);\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192  // Check if the pool has gone idle and invoke idle notification callbacks.\n   257\u2192  void checkForIdleAndNotify();\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192  // See if the pool has gone idle. If we're draining, this will also close idle connections.\n   260\u2192  void checkForIdleAndCloseIdleConnsIfDraining();\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192  void scheduleOnUpstreamReady();\n   263\u2192  ConnectionPool::Cancellable* newStreamImpl(AttachContext& context, bool can_send_early_data);\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192  virtual ConnectionPool::Cancellable* newPendingStream(AttachContext& context,\n   266\u2192                                                        bool can_send_early_data) PURE;\n   267\u2192\n   268\u2192  virtual void attachStreamToClient(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient& client,\n   269\u2192                                    AttachContext& context);\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192  virtual void onPoolFailure(const Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr& host_description,\n   272\u2192                             absl::string_view failure_reason,\n   273\u2192                             ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason pool_failure_reason,\n   274\u2192                             AttachContext& context) PURE;\n   275\u2192  virtual void onPoolReady(ActiveClient& client, AttachContext& context) PURE;\n   276\u2192  // Called by derived classes any time a stream is completed or destroyed for any reason.\n   277\u2192  void onStreamClosed(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient& client, bool delay_attaching_stream);\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192  Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() { return dispatcher_; }\n   280\u2192  Upstream::ResourcePriority priority() const { return priority_; }\n   281\u2192  const Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr& socketOptions() { return socket_options_; }\n   282\u2192  const Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr& transportSocketOptions() {\n   283\u2192    return transport_socket_options_;\n   284\u2192  }\n   285\u2192  bool hasPendingStreams() const { return !pending_streams_.empty(); }\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192  void decrClusterStreamCapacity(uint32_t delta) {\n   288\u2192    state_.decrConnectingAndConnectedStreamCapacity(delta);\n   289\u2192    connecting_and_connected_stream_capacity_ -= delta;\n   290\u2192  }\n   291\u2192  void incrClusterStreamCapacity(uint32_t delta) {\n   292\u2192    state_.incrConnectingAndConnectedStreamCapacity(delta);\n   293\u2192    connecting_and_connected_stream_capacity_ += delta;\n   294\u2192  }\n   295\u2192  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level = 0) const {\n   296\u2192    const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);\n   297\u2192    os << spaces << \"ConnPoolImplBase \" << this << DUMP_MEMBER(ready_clients_.size())\n   298\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER(busy_clients_.size()) << DUMP_MEMBER(connecting_clients_.size())\n   299\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER(connecting_stream_capacity_) << DUMP_MEMBER(num_active_streams_)\n   300\u2192       << DUMP_MEMBER(pending_streams_.size())\n   301\u2192       << \" per upstream preconnect ratio: \" << perUpstreamPreconnectRatio();\n   302\u2192  }\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192  friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const ConnPoolImplBase& s) {\n   305\u2192    s.dumpState(os);\n   306\u2192    return os;\n   307\u2192  }\n   308\u2192  Upstream::ClusterConnectivityState& state() { return state_; }\n   309\u2192\n   310\u2192  void decrConnectingAndConnectedStreamCapacity(uint32_t delta, ActiveClient& client);\n   311\u2192  void incrConnectingAndConnectedStreamCapacity(uint32_t delta, ActiveClient& client);\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192  // Called when an upstream is ready to serve pending streams.\n   314\u2192  void onUpstreamReady();\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192  // Called when an upstream is ready to serve early data streams.\n   317\u2192  void onUpstreamReadyForEarlyData(ActiveClient& client);\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192protected:\n   320\u2192  virtual void onConnected(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient&) {}\n   321\u2192  virtual void onConnectFailed(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient&) {}\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192  enum class ConnectionResult {\n   324\u2192    FailedToCreateConnection,\n   325\u2192    CreatedNewConnection,\n   326\u2192    ShouldNotConnect,\n   327\u2192    NoConnectionRateLimited,\n   328\u2192    CreatedButRateLimited,\n   329\u2192  };\n   330\u2192  // Creates up to 3 connections, based on the preconnect ratio.\n   331\u2192  // Returns the ConnectionResult of the last attempt.\n   332\u2192  ConnectionResult tryCreateNewConnections();\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192  // Creates a new connection if there is sufficient demand, it is allowed by resourceManager, or\n   335\u2192  // to avoid starving this pool.\n   336\u2192  // Demand is determined either by perUpstreamPreconnectRatio() or global_preconnect_ratio\n   337\u2192  // if this is called by maybePreconnect()\n   338\u2192  ConnectionResult tryCreateNewConnection(float global_preconnect_ratio = 0);\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192  // A helper function which determines if a canceled pending connection should\n   341\u2192  // be closed as excess or not.\n   342\u2192  bool connectingConnectionIsExcess(const ActiveClient& client) const;\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192  // A helper function which determines if a new incoming stream should trigger\n   345\u2192  // connection preconnect.\n   346\u2192  bool shouldCreateNewConnection(float global_preconnect_ratio) const;\n   347\u2192\n   348\u2192  float perUpstreamPreconnectRatio() const;\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192  ConnectionPool::Cancellable*\n   351\u2192  addPendingStream(Envoy::ConnectionPool::PendingStreamPtr&& pending_stream) {\n   352\u2192    LinkedList::moveIntoList(std::move(pending_stream), pending_streams_);\n   353\u2192    state_.incrPendingStreams(1);\n   354\u2192    return pending_streams_.front().get();\n   355\u2192  }\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192  bool hasActiveStreams() const { return num_active_streams_ > 0; }\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192  Upstream::ClusterConnectivityState& state_;\n   360\u2192\n   361\u2192  const Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host_;\n   362\u2192  const Upstream::ResourcePriority priority_;\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192  Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher_;\n   365\u2192  const Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr socket_options_;\n   366\u2192  const Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr transport_socket_options_;\n   367\u2192\n   368\u2192  // True if the max requests circuit breakers apply.\n   369\u2192  // This will be false for the TCP pool, true otherwise.\n   370\u2192  virtual bool enforceMaxRequests() const { return true; }\n   371\u2192\n   372\u2192  std::list<Instance::IdleCb> idle_callbacks_;\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192  // When calling purgePendingStreams, this list will be used to hold the streams we are about\n   375\u2192  // to purge. We need this if one cancelled streams cancels a different pending stream\n   376\u2192  std::list<PendingStreamPtr> pending_streams_to_purge_;\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192  // Clients that are ready to handle additional streams.\n   379\u2192  // All entries are in state Ready.\n   380\u2192  std::list<ActiveClientPtr> ready_clients_;\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192  // Clients that are not ready to handle additional streams due to being Busy or Draining.\n   383\u2192  std::list<ActiveClientPtr> busy_clients_;\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192  // Clients that are not ready to handle additional streams because they are Connecting.\n   386\u2192  std::list<ActiveClientPtr> connecting_clients_;\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192  // Clients that are ready to handle additional early data streams because they have 0-RTT\n   389\u2192  // credentials.\n   390\u2192  std::list<ActiveClientPtr> early_data_clients_;\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192  // The number of streams that can be immediately dispatched\n   393\u2192  // if all Connecting connections become connected.\n   394\u2192  uint32_t connecting_stream_capacity_{0};\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192private:\n   397\u2192  // Drain all the clients in the given list.\n   398\u2192  // Prerequisite: the given clients shouldn't be idle.\n   399\u2192  void drainClients(std::list<ActiveClientPtr>& clients);\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192  void assertCapacityCountsAreCorrect();\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192  std::list<PendingStreamPtr> pending_streams_;\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192  // The number of streams that can be immediately dispatched from the current\n   406\u2192  // `ready_clients_` plus `connecting_stream_capacity_`.\n   407\u2192  int64_t connecting_and_connected_stream_capacity_{0};\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192  // The number of streams currently attached to clients.\n   410\u2192  uint32_t num_active_streams_{0};\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192  // Whether the connection pool is currently in the process of closing\n   413\u2192  // all connections so that it can be gracefully deleted.\n   414\u2192  bool is_draining_for_deletion_{false};\n   415\u2192\n   416\u2192  // True iff this object is in the deferred delete list.\n   417\u2192  bool deferred_deleting_{false};\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192  Event::SchedulableCallbackPtr upstream_ready_cb_;\n   420\u2192  Common::DebugRecursionChecker recursion_checker_;\n   421\u2192};\n   422\u2192\n   423\u2192} // namespace ConnectionPool\n   424\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   425\u2192\n   426\u2192// NOLINT(namespace-envoy)\n   427\u2192namespace fmt {\n   428\u2192template <> struct formatter<Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase> : ostream_formatter {};\n   429\u2192} // namespace fmt\n   430\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
150. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.h"
}
Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"envoy/event/dispatcher.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"envoy/http/conn_pool.h\"\n     5\u2192#include \"envoy/network/connection.h\"\n     6\u2192#include \"envoy/stats/timespan.h\"\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/common/linked_object.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"source/common/conn_pool/conn_pool_base.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"source/common/http/codec_client.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"source/common/http/http_server_properties_cache_impl.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192#include \"absl/strings/string_view.h\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    17\u2192namespace Http {\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192struct HttpAttachContext : public Envoy::ConnectionPool::AttachContext {\n    20\u2192  HttpAttachContext(Http::ResponseDecoder* d, Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks* c)\n    21\u2192      : decoder_(d), callbacks_(c) {}\n    22\u2192  Http::ResponseDecoder* decoder_;\n    23\u2192  Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks* callbacks_;\n    24\u2192};\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192// An implementation of Envoy::ConnectionPool::PendingStream for HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2\n    27\u2192class HttpPendingStream : public Envoy::ConnectionPool::PendingStream {\n    28\u2192public:\n    29\u2192  // OnPoolSuccess for HTTP requires both the decoder and callbacks. OnPoolFailure\n    30\u2192  // requires only the callbacks, but passes both for consistency.\n    31\u2192  HttpPendingStream(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase& parent, Http::ResponseDecoder& decoder,\n    32\u2192                    Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks& callbacks, bool can_send_early_data)\n    33\u2192      : Envoy::ConnectionPool::PendingStream(parent, can_send_early_data),\n    34\u2192        context_(&decoder, &callbacks) {}\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192  Envoy::ConnectionPool::AttachContext& context() override { return context_; }\n    37\u2192  HttpAttachContext context_;\n    38\u2192};\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192class ActiveClient;\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192/* An implementation of Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase for shared code\n    43\u2192 * between HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2\n    44\u2192 *\n    45\u2192 * NOTE: The connection pool does NOT do DNS resolution. It assumes it is being given a numeric IP\n    46\u2192 *       address. Higher layer code should handle resolving DNS on error and creating a new pool\n    47\u2192 *       bound to a different IP address.\n    48\u2192 */\n    49\u2192class HttpConnPoolImplBase : public Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase,\n    50\u2192                             public Http::ConnectionPool::Instance {\n    51\u2192public:\n    52\u2192  HttpConnPoolImplBase(\n    53\u2192      Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host, Upstream::ResourcePriority priority,\n    54\u2192      Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, const Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n    55\u2192      const Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr& transport_socket_options,\n    56\u2192      Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator, Upstream::ClusterConnectivityState& state,\n    57\u2192      std::vector<Http::Protocol> protocols);\n    58\u2192  ~HttpConnPoolImplBase() override;\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192  // Event::DeferredDeletable\n    61\u2192  void deleteIsPending() override { deleteIsPendingImpl(); }\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192  // ConnectionPool::Instance\n    64\u2192  void addIdleCallback(IdleCb cb) override { addIdleCallbackImpl(cb); }\n    65\u2192  bool isIdle() const override { return isIdleImpl(); }\n    66\u2192  void drainConnections(Envoy::ConnectionPool::DrainBehavior drain_behavior) override {\n    67\u2192    drainConnectionsImpl(drain_behavior);\n    68\u2192  }\n    69\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host() const override { return host_; }\n    70\u2192  ConnectionPool::Cancellable* newStream(Http::ResponseDecoder& response_decoder,\n    71\u2192                                         Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks& callbacks,\n    72\u2192                                         const Instance::StreamOptions& options) override;\n    73\u2192  bool maybePreconnect(float ratio) override { return maybePreconnectImpl(ratio); }\n    74\u2192  bool hasActiveConnections() const override;\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192  // Creates a new PendingStream and enqueues it into the queue.\n    77\u2192  ConnectionPool::Cancellable* newPendingStream(Envoy::ConnectionPool::AttachContext& context,\n    78\u2192                                                bool can_send_early_data) override;\n    79\u2192  void onPoolFailure(const Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr& host_description,\n    80\u2192                     absl::string_view failure_reason, ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason reason,\n    81\u2192                     Envoy::ConnectionPool::AttachContext& context) override {\n    82\u2192    auto* callbacks = typedContext<HttpAttachContext>(context).callbacks_;\n    83\u2192    callbacks->onPoolFailure(reason, failure_reason, host_description);\n    84\u2192  }\n    85\u2192  void onPoolReady(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient& client,\n    86\u2192                   Envoy::ConnectionPool::AttachContext& context) override;\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  virtual CodecClientPtr createCodecClient(Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData& data) PURE;\n    89\u2192  Random::RandomGenerator& randomGenerator() { return random_generator_; }\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192  virtual absl::optional<HttpServerPropertiesCache::Origin>& origin() { return origin_; }\n    92\u2192  virtual Http::HttpServerPropertiesCacheSharedPtr cache() { return nullptr; }\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192protected:\n    95\u2192  friend class ActiveClient;\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192  void setOrigin(absl::optional<HttpServerPropertiesCache::Origin> origin) { origin_ = origin; }\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator_;\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192private:\n   102\u2192  absl::optional<HttpServerPropertiesCache::Origin> origin_;\n   103\u2192};\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192// An implementation of Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient for HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2\n   106\u2192class ActiveClient : public Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient {\n   107\u2192public:\n   108\u2192  ActiveClient(HttpConnPoolImplBase& parent, uint64_t lifetime_stream_limit,\n   109\u2192               uint64_t effective_concurrent_stream_limit,\n   110\u2192               uint64_t configured_concurrent_stream_limit,\n   111\u2192               OptRef<Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData> opt_data)\n   112\u2192      : Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient(parent, lifetime_stream_limit,\n   113\u2192                                            effective_concurrent_stream_limit,\n   114\u2192                                            configured_concurrent_stream_limit) {\n   115\u2192    if (opt_data.has_value()) {\n   116\u2192      initialize(opt_data.value(), parent);\n   117\u2192      return;\n   118\u2192    }\n   119\u2192    // The static cast makes sure we call the base class host() and not\n   120\u2192    // HttpConnPoolImplBase::host which is of a different type.\n   121\u2192    Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData data =\n   122\u2192        static_cast<Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase*>(&parent)->host()->createConnection(\n   123\u2192            parent.dispatcher(), parent.socketOptions(), parent.transportSocketOptions());\n   124\u2192    initialize(data, parent);\n   125\u2192  }\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  void initialize(Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData& data, HttpConnPoolImplBase& parent) {\n   128\u2192    real_host_description_ = data.host_description_;\n   129\u2192    codec_client_ = parent.createCodecClient(data);\n   130\u2192    codec_client_->addConnectionCallbacks(*this);\n   131\u2192    Upstream::ClusterTrafficStats& traffic_stats = *parent_.host()->cluster().trafficStats();\n   132\u2192    codec_client_->setConnectionStats(\n   133\u2192        {traffic_stats.upstream_cx_rx_bytes_total_, traffic_stats.upstream_cx_rx_bytes_buffered_,\n   134\u2192         traffic_stats.upstream_cx_tx_bytes_total_, traffic_stats.upstream_cx_tx_bytes_buffered_,\n   135\u2192         &traffic_stats.bind_errors_, nullptr});\n   136\u2192  }\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192  void initializeReadFilters() override { codec_client_->initializeReadFilters(); }\n   139\u2192  absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol() const override { return codec_client_->protocol(); }\n   140\u2192  void close() override { codec_client_->close(); }\n   141\u2192  virtual Http::RequestEncoder& newStreamEncoder(Http::ResponseDecoder& response_decoder) PURE;\n   142\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override {\n   143\u2192    parent_.onConnectionEvent(*this, codec_client_->connectionFailureReason(), event);\n   144\u2192  }\n   145\u2192  uint32_t numActiveStreams() const override { return codec_client_->numActiveRequests(); }\n   146\u2192  uint64_t id() const override { return codec_client_->id(); }\n   147\u2192  HttpConnPoolImplBase& parent() { return *static_cast<HttpConnPoolImplBase*>(&parent_); }\n   148\u2192\n   149\u2192  Http::CodecClientPtr codec_client_;\n   150\u2192};\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192/* An implementation of Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase for HTTP/1 and HTTP/2\n   153\u2192 */\n   154\u2192class FixedHttpConnPoolImpl : public HttpConnPoolImplBase {\n   155\u2192public:\n   156\u2192  using CreateClientFn =\n   157\u2192      std::function<Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClientPtr(HttpConnPoolImplBase* pool)>;\n   158\u2192  using CreateCodecFn = std::function<CodecClientPtr(Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData& data,\n   159\u2192                                                     HttpConnPoolImplBase* pool)>;\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192  FixedHttpConnPoolImpl(\n   162\u2192      Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host, Upstream::ResourcePriority priority,\n   163\u2192      Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, const Network::ConnectionSocket::OptionsSharedPtr& options,\n   164\u2192      const Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr& transport_socket_options,\n   165\u2192      Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator, Upstream::ClusterConnectivityState& state,\n   166\u2192      CreateClientFn client_fn, CreateCodecFn codec_fn, std::vector<Http::Protocol> protocols,\n   167\u2192      absl::optional<Http::HttpServerPropertiesCache::Origin> origin = absl::nullopt,\n   168\u2192      Http::HttpServerPropertiesCacheSharedPtr cache = nullptr)\n   169\u2192      : HttpConnPoolImplBase(host, priority, dispatcher, options, transport_socket_options,\n   170\u2192                             random_generator, state, protocols),\n   171\u2192        codec_fn_(codec_fn), client_fn_(client_fn), protocol_(protocols[0]), cache_(cache) {\n   172\u2192    setOrigin(origin);\n   173\u2192    ASSERT(protocols.size() == 1);\n   174\u2192  }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192  CodecClientPtr createCodecClient(Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData& data) override {\n   177\u2192    return codec_fn_(data, this);\n   178\u2192  }\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192  Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClientPtr instantiateActiveClient() override {\n   181\u2192    return client_fn_(this);\n   182\u2192  }\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  absl::string_view protocolDescription() const override {\n   185\u2192    return Utility::getProtocolString(protocol_);\n   186\u2192  }\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  Http::HttpServerPropertiesCacheSharedPtr cache() override { return cache_; }\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192protected:\n   191\u2192  const CreateCodecFn codec_fn_;\n   192\u2192  const CreateClientFn client_fn_;\n   193\u2192  const Http::Protocol protocol_;\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192  Http::HttpServerPropertiesCacheSharedPtr cache_;\n   196\u2192};\n   197\u2192\n   198\u2192/**\n   199\u2192 * Active client base for HTTP/2 and HTTP/3\n   200\u2192 */\n   201\u2192class MultiplexedActiveClientBase : public CodecClientCallbacks,\n   202\u2192                                    public Http::ConnectionCallbacks,\n   203\u2192 206\u2192                              uint32_t max_configured_concurrent_streams, Stats::Counter& cx_total,\n   207\u2192                              OptRef<Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData> data);\n   208\u2192  ~MultiplexedActiveClientBase() override = default;\n   209\u2192  // Caps max streams per connection below 2^31 to prevent overflow.\n   210\u2192  static uint64_t maxStreamsPerConnection(uint64_t max_streams_config);\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192  // ConnPoolImpl::ActiveClient\n   213\u2192  bool closingWithIncompleteStream() const override;\n   214\u2192 onStreamReset(Http::StreamResetReason reason) override;\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192  // Http::ConnectionCallbacks\n   221\u2192  void onGoAway(Http::GoAwayErrorCode error_code) override;\n   222\u2192 override;\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192  uint32_t numActiveStreams() const override {\n    27\u2192    // Override the parent class using the codec for numActiveStreams.\n    28\u2192    // Unfortunately for the HTTP/1 codec, the stream is destroyed before decode\n    29\u2192    // is complete, and we must make sure the connection pool does not observe available\n    30\u2192    // capacity and assign a new stream before decode is complete.\n    31\u2192    return stream_wrapper_.get() ? 1 : 0;\n    32\u2192  }\n    33\u2192  void releaseResources() override {\n    34\u2192
