Output
"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     4\u2192#include <functional>\n     5\u2192#include <memory>\n     6\u2192#include <string>\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192#include \"envoy/access_log/access_log.h\"\n     9\u2192#include \"envoy/buffer/buffer.h\"\n    10\u2192#include \"envoy/common/scope_tracker.h\"\n    11\u2192#include \"envoy/event/dispatcher.h\"\n    12\u2192#include \"envoy/grpc/status.h\"\n    13\u2192#include \"envoy/http/codec.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/http/filter_factory.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"envoy/http/header_map.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"envoy/matcher/matcher.h\"\n    17\u2192#include \"envoy/router/router.h\"\n    18\u2192#include \"envoy/router/scopes.h\"\n    19\u2192#include \"envoy/ssl/connection.h\"\n    20\u2192#include \"envoy/tracing/tracer.h\"\n    21\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/load_balancer.h\"\n    22\u2192#include \"envoy/upstream/upstream.h\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192#include \"source/common/common/scope_tracked_object_stack.h\"\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192#include \"absl/types/optional.h\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    29\u2192namespace Router {\n    30\u2192class RouteConfigProvider;\n    31\u2192}\n    32\u2192namespace Http {\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192/**\n    35\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode headers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n    36\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n    37\u2192 */\n    38\u2192enum class FilterHeadersStatus {\n    39\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n    40\u2192  Continue,\n    41\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n    42\u2192  //\n    43\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n    44\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    45\u2192  //\n    46\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for headers as well as data and\n    47\u2192  // trailers for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n    48\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n    49\u2192  StopIteration,\n    50\u2192  // Continue headers iteration to remaining filters, but delay ending the stream. This status MUST\n    51\u2192  // NOT be returned when end_stream is already set to false.\n    52\u2192  //\n    53\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to add a body to a headers-only request/response, but this body is not\n    54\u2192  // readily available. Delaying end_stream allows the filter to add the body once it's available\n    55\u2192  // without stopping headers iteration.\n    56\u2192  //\n    57\u2192  // The filter is responsible to continue the stream by providing a body through calling\n    58\u2192  // injectDecodedDataToFilterChain()/injectEncodedDataToFilterChain(), possibly multiple times\n    59\u2192  // if the body needs to be divided into several chunks. The filter may need to handle\n    60\u2192  // watermark events when injecting a body, see:\n    61\u2192  // https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/blob/main/source/docs/flow_control.md.\n    62\u2192  //\n    63\u2192  // The last call to inject data MUST have end_stream set to true to conclude the stream.\n    64\u2192  // If the filter cannot provide a body the stream should be reset.\n    65\u2192  //\n    66\u2192  // Adding a body through calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData() then\n    67\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is currently NOT supported and causes an assert failure.\n    68\u2192  //\n    69\u2192  // Adding trailers in this scenario is currently NOT supported.\n    70\u2192  //\n    71\u2192  // The filter MUST NOT attempt to continue the stream without providing a body using\n    72\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding().\n    73\u2192  //\n    74\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding a body in this case by calling addDecodedData()/addEncodedData()\n    75\u2192  // then continueDecoding()/continueEncoding(). To support this a new FilterManager::IterationState\n    76\u2192  // needs to be added and set when a filter returns this status in\n    77\u2192  // FilterManager::decodeHeaders/FilterManager::encodeHeaders()\n    78\u2192  // Currently, when a filter returns this, the IterationState is Continue. This causes ASSERTs in\n    79\u2192  // FilterManager::commonContinue() to fail when continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() is called;\n    80\u2192  // due to trying to continue iteration when the IterationState is already Continue.\n    81\u2192  // In this case, a different ASSERT will be needed to make sure the filter does not try to\n    82\u2192  // continue without adding a body first.\n    83\u2192  //\n    84\u2192  // TODO(yosrym93): Support adding trailers in this case by implementing new functions to inject\n    85\u2192  // trailers, similar to the inject data functions.\n    86\u2192  ContinueAndDontEndStream,\n    87\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n    88\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. ContinueDecoding() MUST\n    89\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n    90\u2192  //\n    91\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n    92\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n    93\u2192  //\n    94\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n    95\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n    96\u2192  //\n    97\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndBuffer is set.\n    98\u2192  StopAllIterationAndBuffer,\n    99\u2192  // Do not iterate for headers as well as data and trailers for the current filter and the filters\n   100\u2192  // following, and buffer body data for later dispatching. continueDecoding() MUST\n   101\u2192  // be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   102\u2192  //\n   103\u2192  // Used when a filter wants to stop iteration on data and trailers while waiting for headers'\n   104\u2192  // iteration to resume.\n   105\u2192  //\n   106\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until continueDecoding() function is called.\n   107\u2192  //\n   108\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): stop metadata parsing when StopAllIterationAndWatermark is set.\n   109\u2192  StopAllIterationAndWatermark,\n   110\u2192};\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/**\n   113\u2192 * Return codes for encode on informative headers filter invocations.\n   114\u2192 */\n   115\u2192enum class Filter1xxHeadersStatus {\n   116\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   117\u2192  Continue,\n   118\u2192  // Do not iterate for informative headers on any of the remaining filters in the chain.\n   119\u2192  //\n   120\u2192  // Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from encodeData() or calling\n   121\u2192  // continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   122\u2192  StopIteration\n   123\u2192};\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192/**\n   126\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode data filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   127\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   128\u2192 */\n   129\u2192enum class FilterDataStatus {\n   130\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   131\u2192  // will be sent first via decodeHeaders()/encodeHeaders(). If data has previously been buffered,\n   132\u2192  // the data in this callback will be added to the buffer before the entirety is sent to the next\n   133\u2192  // filter.\n   134\u2192  Continue,\n   135\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   136\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   137\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   138\u2192  //\n   139\u2192  // This should be called by filters which must parse a larger block of the incoming data before\n   140\u2192  // continuing processing and so can not push back on streaming data via watermarks.\n   141\u2192  //\n   142\u2192  // If buffering the request causes buffered data to exceed the configured buffer limit, a 413 will\n   143\u2192  // be sent to the user. On the response path exceeding buffer limits will result in a 500.\n   144\u2192  StopIterationAndBuffer,\n   145\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, and buffer body data for later\n   146\u2192  // dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from decodeData()/encodeData() or calling\n   147\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   148\u2192  //\n   149\u2192  // This will cause the flow of incoming data to cease until one of the continue.*() functions is\n   150\u2192  // called.\n   151\u2192  //\n   152\u2192  // This should be returned by filters which can nominally stream data but have a transient back-up\n   153\u2192  // such as the configured delay of the fault filter, or if the router filter is still fetching an\n   154\u2192  // upstream connection.\n   155\u2192  StopIterationAndWatermark,\n   156\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain, but do not buffer any of the\n   157\u2192  // body data for later dispatching. Returning FilterDataStatus::Continue from\n   158\u2192  // decodeData()/encodeData() or calling continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if\n   159\u2192  // continued filter iteration is desired.\n   160\u2192  //\n   161\u2192  // Note that if a local reply was sent, no further iteration for either data or trailers\n   162\u2192  // for the current filter and the filters following will happen. A local reply can be\n   163\u2192  // triggered via sendLocalReply() or encodeHeaders().\n   164\u2192  StopIterationNoBuffer\n   165\u2192};\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192/**\n   168\u2192 * Return codes for encode/decode trailers filter invocations. The connection manager bases further\n   169\u2192 * filter invocations on the return code of the previous filter.\n   170\u2192 */\n   171\u2192enum class FilterTrailersStatus {\n   172\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration.\n   173\u2192  Continue,\n   174\u2192  // Do not iterate to any of the remaining filters in the chain. Calling\n   175\u2192  // continueDecoding()/continueEncoding() MUST be called if continued filter iteration is desired.\n   176\u2192  StopIteration\n   177\u2192};\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192/**\n   180\u2192 * Return codes for encode metadata filter invocations. Metadata currently can not stop filter\n   181\u2192 * iteration except in the case of local replies.\n   182\u2192 */\n   183\u2192enum class FilterMetadataStatus {\n   184\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration for metadata only. Does not unblock returns of StopIteration*\n   185\u2192  // from (decode|encode)(Headers|Data).\n   186\u2192  Continue,\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192  // Continue filter chain iteration. If headers have not yet been sent to the next filter, they\n   189\u2192  // will be sent first via (decode|encode)Headers().\n   190\u2192  ContinueAll,\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192  // Stops iteration of the entire filter chain. Only to be used in the case of sending a local\n   193\u2192  // reply from (decode|encode)Metadata.\n   194\u2192  StopIterationForLocalReply,\n   195\u2192};\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192/**\n   198\u2192 * Return codes for onLocalReply filter invocations.\n   199\u2192 */\n   200\u2192enum class LocalErrorStatus {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

\"source/common/local_reply/local_reply.h\"\n    26\u2192#include \"source/common/matcher/matcher.h\"\n    27\u2192#include \"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\"\n    28\u2192#include \"source/common/runtime/runtime_features.h\"\n    29\u2192#include \"source/common/stream_info/stream_info_impl.h\"\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    32\u2192namespace Http {\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192class FilterManager;\n    35\u2192class DownstreamFilterManager;\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192struct ActiveStreamFilterBase;\n    38\u2192struct ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;\n    39\u2192struct ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;\n    40\u2192using ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr = std::unique_ptr<ActiveStreamDecoderFilter>;\n    41\u2192using ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr = std::unique_ptr<ActiveStreamEncoderFilter>;\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192constexpr absl::string_view LocalReplyFilterStateKey =\n    44\u2192    \"envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager.local_reply_owner\";\n    45\u2192class LocalReplyOwnerObject : public StreamInfo::FilterState::Object {\n    46\u2192public:\n    47\u2192  LocalReplyOwnerObject(const std::string& filter_config_name)\n    48\u2192      : filter_config_name_(filter_config_name) {}\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192  ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr serializeAsProto() const override {\n    51\u2192    auto message = std::make_unique<ProtobufWkt::StringValue>();\n    52\u2192    message->set_value(filter_config_name_);\n    53\u2192    return message;\n    54\u2192  }\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192  absl::optional<std::string> serializeAsString() const override { return filter_config_name_; }\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192private:\n    59\u2192  const std::string filter_config_name_;\n    60\u2192};\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// TODO(wbpcode): Rather than allocating every filter with an unique pointer, we could\n    63\u2192// construct the filter in place in the vector. This should reduce the heap allocation and\n    64\u2192// memory fragmentation.\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192// HTTP decoder filters. If filters are configured in the following order (assume all three\n    67\u2192// filters are both decoder/encoder filters):\n    68\u2192//   http_filters:\n    69\u2192//     - A\n    70\u2192//     - B\n    71\u2192//     - C\n    72\u2192// The decoder filter chain will iterate through filters A, B, C.\n    73\u2192struct StreamDecoderFilters {\n    74\u2192  using Element = ActiveStreamDecoderFilter;\n    75\u2192  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr>::iterator;\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  Iterator begin() { return entries_.begin(); }\n    78\u2192  Iterator end() { return entries_.end(); }\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  std::vector<ActiveStreamDecoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n    81\u2192};\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192// HTTP encoder filters. If filters are configured in the following order (assume all three\n    84\u2192// filters are both decoder/encoder filters):\n    85\u2192//   http_filters:\n    86\u2192//     - A\n    87\u2192//     - B\n    88\u2192//     - C\n    89\u2192// Unlike the decoder filter, the encoder filter chain will iterate with the\n    90\u2192// reverse order of the configured filters, i.e., C, B, A. This is why we use reverse_iterator\n    91\u2192// here.\n    92\u2192struct StreamEncoderFilters {\n    93\u2192  using Element = ActiveStreamEncoderFilter;\n    94\u2192  using Iterator = std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr>::reverse_iterator;\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192  Iterator begin() { return entries_.rbegin(); }\n    97\u2192  Iterator end() { return entries_.rend(); }\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192  std::vector<ActiveStreamEncoderFilterPtr> entries_;\n   100\u2192};\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192/**\n   103\u2192 * Base class wrapper for both stream encoder and decoder filters.\n   104\u2192 *\n   105\u2192 * This class is responsible for performing matching and updating match data when a match tree is\n   106\u2192 * configured for the associated filter. When not using a match tree, only minimal overhead (i.e.\n   107\u2192 * memory overhead of unused fields) should apply.\n   108\u2192 */\n   109\u2192struct ActiveStreamFilterBase : public virtual StreamFilterCallbacks,\n   110\u2192                                Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::http> {\n   111\u2192  ActiveStreamFilterBase(FilterManager& parent, FilterContext filter_context)\n   112\u2192      : parent_(parent), iteration_state_(IterationState::Continue),\n   113\u2192        filter_context_(std::move(filter_context)) {}\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  // Functions in the following block are called after the filter finishes processing\n   116\u2192  // corresponding data. Those functions handle state updates and data storage (if needed)\n   117\u2192  // according to the status returned by filter's callback functions.\n   118\u2192  bool commonHandleAfter1xxHeadersCallback(Filter1xxHeadersStatus status);\n   119\u2192  bool commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(FilterHeadersStatus status, bool& end_stream);\n   120\u2192  bool commonHandleAfterDataCallback(FilterDataStatus status, Buffer::Instance& provided_data,\n   121\u2192                                     bool& buffer_was_streaming);\n   122\u2192  bool commonHandleAfterTrailersCallback(FilterTrailersStatus status);\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192  // Buffers provided_data.\n   125\u2192  void commonHandleBufferData(Buffer::Instance& provided_data);\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192  // If iteration has stopped for all frame types, calls this function to buffer the data before\n   128\u2192  // the filter processes data. The function also updates streaming state.\n   129\u2192  void commonBufferDataIfStopAll(Buffer::Instance& provided_data, bool& buffer_was_streaming);\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  void commonContinue();\n   132\u2192  virtual bool canContinue() PURE;\n   133\u2192  virtual Buffer::InstancePtr createBuffer() PURE;\n   134\u2192  virtual Buffer::InstancePtr& bufferedData() PURE;\n   135\u2192  virtual bool observedEndStream() PURE;\n   136\u2192  virtual bool has1xxHeaders() PURE;\n   137\u2192  virtual void do1xxHeaders() PURE;\n   138\u2192  virtual void doHeaders(bool end_stream) PURE;\n   139\u2192  virtual void doData(bool end_stream) PURE;\n   140\u2192  virtual void doTrailers() PURE;\n   141\u2192  virtual bool hasTrailers() PURE;\n   142\u2192  virtual void doMetadata() PURE;\n   143\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): make this pure when adding impl to encoder filter.\n   144\u2192  virtual void handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() PURE;\n   145\u2192\n   146\u2192  virtual void onMatchCallback(const Matcher::Action& action) PURE;\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  // Http::StreamFilterCallbacks\n   149\u2192  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() override;\n   150\u2192  Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher() override;\n   151\u2192  Router::RouteConstSharedPtr route() override;\n   152\u2192  void resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason,\n   153\u2192                   absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   154\u2192  Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr clusterInfo() override;\n   155\u2192  uint64_t streamId() const override;\n   156\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() override;\n   157\u2192  Tracing::Span& activeSpan() override;\n   158\u2192  OptRef<const Tracing::Config> tracingConfig() const override;\n   159\u2192  const ScopeTrackedObject& scope() override;\n   160\u2192  void restoreContextOnContinue(ScopeTrackedObjectStack& tracked_object_stack) override;\n   161\u2192  void resetIdleTimer() override;\n   162\u2192  const Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfig* mostSpecificPerFilterConfig() const override;\n   163\u2192  Router::RouteSpecificFilterConfigs perFilterConfigs() const override;\n   164\u2192  Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef http1StreamEncoderOptions() override;\n   165\u2192  OptRef<DownstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> downstreamCallbacks() override;\n   166\u2192  OptRef<UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks> upstreamCallbacks() override;\n   167\u2192  absl::string_view filterConfigName() const override { return filter_context_.config_name; }\n   168\u2192  RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() override;\n   169\u2192  RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() override;\n   170\u2192  ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() override;\n   171\u2192  ResponseHeaderMapOptRef responseHeaders() override;\n   172\u2192  ResponseTrailerMapOptRef responseTrailers() override;\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192  // Functions to set or get iteration state.\n   175\u2192  bool canIterate() { return iteration_state_ == IterationState::Continue; }\n   176\u2192  bool stoppedAll() {\n   177\u2192    return iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopAllBuffer ||\n   178\u2192           iteration_state_ == IterationState::StopAllWatermark;\n   179\u2192  }\n   180\u2192  void allowIteration() {\n   181\u2192    ASSERT(iteration_state_ != IterationState::Continue);\n   182\u2192    iteration_state_ = IterationState::Continue;\n   183\u2192  }\n   184\u2192  MetadataMapVector* getSavedRequestMetadata() {\n   185\u2192    if (saved_request_metadata_ == nullptr) {\n   186\u2192      saved_request_metadata_ = std::make_unique<MetadataMapVector>();\n   187\u2192    }\n   188\u2192    return saved_request_metadata_.get();\n   189\u2192  }\n   190\u2192  MetadataMapVector* getSavedResponseMetadata() {\n   191\u2192    if (saved_response_metadata_ == nullptr) {\n   192\u2192      saved_response_metadata_ = std::make_unique<MetadataMapVector>();\n   193\u2192    }\n   194\u2192    return saved_response_metadata_.get();\n   195\u2192  }\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192  Router::RouteConstSharedPtr getRoute() const;\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192  void sendLocalReply(Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   200\u2192                      std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)> modify_headers,\n   201\u2192                      const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status,\n   202\u2192                      absl::string_view details);\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192  // A vector to save metadata when the current filter's [de|en]codeMetadata() can not be called,\n   205\u2192  // either because [de|en]codeHeaders() of the current filter returns StopAllIteration or because\n   206\u2192  // [de|en]codeHeaders() adds new metadata to [de|en]code, but we don't know\n   207\u2192  // [de|en]codeHeaders()'s return value yet. The storage is created on demand.\n   208\u2192  std::unique_ptr<MetadataMapVector> saved_request_metadata_{nullptr};\n   209\u2192  std::unique_ptr<MetadataMapVector> saved_response_metadata_{nullptr};\n   210\u2192  // The state of iteration.\n   211\u2192  enum class IterationState : uint8_t {\n   212\u2192    Continue,            // Iteration has not stopped for any frame type.\n   213\u2192    StopSingleIteration, // Iteration has stopped for headers, 100-continue, or data.\n   214\u2192    StopAllBuffer,       // Iteration has stopped for all frame types, and following data should\n   215\u2192                         // be buffered.\n   216\u2192    StopAllWatermark,    // Iteration has stopped for all frame types, and following data should\n   217\u2192                         // be buffered until high watermark is reached.\n   218\u2192  };\n   219\u2192  FilterManager& parent_;\n   220\u2192  IterationState iteration_state_{};\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  const FilterContext filter_context_;\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192  // If the filter resumes iteration from a StopAllBuffer/Watermark state, the current filter\n   225\u2192  // hasn't parsed data and trailers. As a result, the filter iteration should start with the\n   226\u2192  // current filter instead of the next one. If true, filter iteration starts with the current\n   227\u2192  // filter. Otherwise, starts with the next filter in the chain.\n   228\u2192  bool iterate_from_current_filter_{};\n   229\u2192  bool headers_continued_{};\n   230\u2192  bool continued_1xx_headers_{};\n   231\u2192  // If true, end_stream is called for this filter.\n   232\u2192  bool end_stream_{};\n   233\u2192  // If true, the filter has processed headers.\n   234\u2192  bool processed_headers_{};\n   235\u2192};\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192/**\n   238\u2192 * Wrapper for a stream decoder filter.\n   239\u2192 */\n   240\u2192struct ActiveStreamDecoderFilter : public ActiveStreamFilterBase,\n   241\u2192                                   public StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks {\n   242\u2192  ActiveStreamDecoderFilter(FilterManager& parent, StreamDecoderFilterSharedPtr filter,\n   243\u2192                            FilterContext filter_context)\n   244\u2192      : ActiveStreamFilterBase(parent, std::move(filter_context)), handle_(std::move(filter)) {\n   245\u2192    handle_->setDecoderFilterCallbacks(*this);\n   246\u2192  }\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192  // ActiveStreamFilterBase\n   249\u2192  bool canContinue() override;\n   250\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr createBuffer() override;\n   251\u2192  Buffer::InstancePtr& bufferedData() override;\n   252\u2192  bool observedEndStream() override;\n   253\u2192  bool has1xxHeaders() override { return false; }\n   254\u2192  void do1xxHeaders() override { IS_ENVOY_BUG(\"unexpected 1xx headers\"); }\n   255\u2192  void doHeaders(bool end_stream) override;\n   256\u2192  void doData(bool end_stream) override;\n   257\u2192  void doMetadata() override {\n   258\u2192    if (saved_request_metadata_ != nullptr) {\n   259\u2192      drainSavedRequestMetadata();\n   260\u2192    }\n   261\u2192  }\n   262\u2192  void doTrailers() override;\n   263\u2192  bool hasTrailers() override;\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192  void drainSavedRequestMetadata();\n   266\u2192  // This function is called after the filter calls decodeHeaders() to drain accumulated metadata.\n   267\u2192  void handleMetadataAfterHeadersCallback() override;\n   268\u2192  void onMatchCallback(const Matcher::Action& action) override {\n   269\u2192    handle_->onMatchCallback(std::move(action));\n   270\u2192  }\n   271\u2192\n   272\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks\n   273\u2192  void addDecodedData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool streaming) override;\n   274\u2192  void injectDecodedDataToFilterChain(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n   275\u2192  RequestTrailerMap& addDecodedTrailers() override;\n   276\u2192  MetadataMapVector& addDecodedMetadata() override;\n   277\u2192  void continueDecoding() override;\n   278\u2192  const Buffer::Instance* decodingBuffer() override;\n   279\u2192\n : 1;\n    64\u2192  };\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192  class StrictHeaderChecker {\n    67\u2192  public:\n    68\u2192    struct HeaderCheckResult {\n    69\u2192      bool valid_ = true;\n    70\u2192 78\u2192     * @return HeaderCheckResult containing the entry for @param target_header\n    79\u2192     *         and valid_ set to FALSE if @param target_header is set to an\n    80\u2192     *         invalid value. If @param target_header doesn't appear in\n    81\u2192     *         @param headers, return a result with valid_ set to TRUE.\n    82\u2192     */\n    83\u2192    static const HeaderCheckResult checkHeader(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n    84\u2192                                               const Http::LowerCaseString& target_header);\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    using ParseRetryFlagsFunc = std::function<std::pair<uint32_t, bool>(absl::string_view)>;\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192  private:\n    89\u2192    static HeaderCheckResult hasValidRetryFields(const Http::HeaderEntry* header_entry,\n    90\u2192                                                 const ParseRetryFlagsFunc& parse_fn) {\n    91\u2192      HeaderCheckResult r;\n    92\u2192      if (header_entry) {\n    93\u2192        const auto flags_and_validity = parse_fn(header_entry->value().getStringView());\n    94\u2192        r.valid_ = flags_and_validity.second;\n    95\u2192        r.entry_ = header_entry;\n    96\u2192      }\n    97\u2192      return r;\n    98\u2192    }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    static HeaderCheckResult isInteger(const Http::HeaderEntry* header_entry) {\n   101\u2192      HeaderCheckResult r;\n   102\u2192      if (header_entry) {\n   103\u2192        uint64_t out;\n   104\u2192        r.valid_ = absl::SimpleAtoi(header_entry->value().getStringView(), &out);\n   105\u2192        r.entry_ = header_entry;\n   106\u2192      }\n   107\u2192      return r;\n   108\u2192    }\n   109\u2192  };\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  /**\n   112\u2192   * Returns response_time / timeout, as a  percentage as [0, 100]. Returns 0\n   113\u2192   * if there is no timeout.\n   114\u2192   * @param response_time supplies the response time thus far.\n   115\u2192   * @param timeout supplies  the timeout to get the percentage of.\n   116\u2192   * @return the percentage of timeout [0, 100] for stats use.\n   117\u2192   */\n   118\u2192  static uint64_t percentageOfTimeout(const std::chrono::milliseconds response_time,\n   119\u2192                                      const std::chrono::milliseconds timeout);\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192  /**\n   122\u2192   * Set the :scheme header using the best information available. In order this is\n   123\u2192   * - whether the upstream connection is using TLS if use_upstream is true\n   124\u2192   * - existing scheme header if valid\n   125\u2192   * - x-forwarded-proto header if valid\n   126\u2192   * - whether the downstream connection is using TLS\n   127\u2192   */\n   128\u2192  static void setUpstreamScheme(Http::RequestHeaderMap& headers, bool downstream_ssl,\n   129\u2192                                bool upstream_ssl, bool use_upstream);\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  /**\n   132\u2192   * Determine whether a request should be shadowed.\n   133\u2192   * @param policy supplies the route's shadow policy.\n   134\u2192   * @param runtime supplies the runtime to lookup the shadow key in.\n   135\u2192   * @param stable_random supplies the random number to use when determining whether shadowing\n   136\u2192   *        should take place.\n   137\u2192   * @return TRUE if shadowing should take place.\n   138\u2192   */\n   139\u2192  static bool shouldShadow(const ShadowPolicy& policy, Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n   140\u2192                           uint64_t stable_random);\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192  /**\n   143\u2192   * Determine the final timeout to use based on the route as well as the request headers.\n   144\u2192   * @param route supplies the request route.\n   145\u2192   * @param request_headers supplies the request headers.\n   146\u2192   * @param insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms insert\n   147\u2192   *        x-envoy-expected-request-timeout-ms?\n   148\u2192   * @param grpc_request tells if the request is a gRPC request.\n   149\u2192   * @return TimeoutData for both the global and per try timeouts.\n   150\u2192   */\n   151\u2192  static TimeoutData finalTimeout(const RouteEntry& route, Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers,\n   152\u2192                                  bool insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms, bool grpc_request,\n   153\u2192                                  bool per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled,\n   154\u2192                                  bool respect_expected_rq_timeout);\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  /**\n   157\u2192   * Set the x-envoy-expected-request-timeout-ms and grpc-timeout headers if needed.\n   158\u2192   * @param elapsed_time time elapsed since completion of the downstream request\n   159\u2192   * @param timeout final TimeoutData to use for the request\n   160\u2192   * @param request_headers the request headers to modify\n   161\u2192   * @param insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms insert\n   162\u2192   *        x-envoy-expected-request-timeout-ms?\n   163\u2192   * @param grpc_request tells if the request is a gRPC request.\n   164\u2192   * @param per_try_timeout_headging_enabled is request hedging enabled?\n   165\u2192   */\n   166\u2192  static void setTimeoutHeaders(uint64_t elapsed_time, const TimeoutData& timeout,\n   167\u2192                                const RouteEntry& route, Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers,\n   168\u2192                                bool insert_envoy_expected_request_timeout_ms, bool grpc_request,\n   169\u2192                                bool per_try_timeout_hedging_enabled);\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192  /**\n   172\u2192   * Try to parse a header entry that may have a timeout field\n   173\u2192   *\n   174\u2192   * @param header_timeout_entry header entry which may contain a timeout value.\n   175\u2192   * @return result timeout value from header. It will return nullopt if parse failed.\n   176\u2192   */\n   177\u2192  static absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds>\n   178\u2192  tryParseHeaderTimeout(const Http::HeaderEntry& header_timeout_entry);\n   179\u2192\n   180\u2192  /**\n   181\u2192   * Try to set global timeout.\n   182\u2192   *\n   183\u2192   * @param header_timeout_entry header entry which may contain a timeout value.\n   184\u2192   * @param timeout timeout data to set from header timeout entry.\n   185\u2192   */\n   186\u2192  static void trySetGlobalTimeout(const Http::HeaderEntry& header_timeout_entry,\n   187\u2192                                  TimeoutData& timeout);\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192  /**\n   190\u2192   * Determine the final hedging settings after applying randomized behavior.\n   191\u2192   * @param route supplies the request route.\n   192\u2192   * @param request_headers supplies the request headers.\n   193\u2192   * @return HedgingParams the final parameters to use for request hedging.\n   194\u2192   */\n   195\u2192  static HedgingParams finalHedgingParams(const RouteEntry& route,\n   196\u2192                                          Http::RequestHeaderMap& request_headers);\n   197\u2192};\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192/**\n   200\u2192 * Configuration for the router filter.\n   201\u2192 */\n   202\u2192class FilterConfig : public Http::FilterChainFactory {\n   203\u2192public:\n   204\u2192  FilterConfig(Server::Configuration::CommonFactoryContext& factory_context,\n   205\u2192               Stats::StatName stat_prefix, const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info,\n   206\u2192               Stats::Scope& scope, Upstream::ClusterManager& cm, Runtime::Loader& runtime,\n   207\u2192               Random::RandomGenerator& random, bool suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats,\n   210\u2192               bool flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream,\n   211\u2192               const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<std::string>& strict_check_headers,\n   212\u2192               TimeSource& time_source, Http::Context& http_context,\n   213\u2192               Router::Context& router_context)\n   214\u2192      : factory_context_(factory_context), router_context_(router_context), scope_(scope),\n   215\u2192        local_info_(local_info), cm_(cm), runtime_(runtime),\n   216\u2192        default_stats_(router_context_.statNames(), scope_, stat_prefix),\n   217\u2192        async_stats_(router_context_.statNames(), scope, http_context.asyncClientStatPrefix()),\n   218\u2192        random_(random), emit_dynamic_stats_(emit_dynamic_stats),\n   219\u2192        start_child_span_(start_child_span), suppress_envoy_headers_(suppress_envoy_headers),\n   220\u2192        respect_expected_rq_timeout_(respect_expected_rq_timeout),\n   221\u2192        suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats_(suppress_grpc_request_failure_code_stats),\n   222\u2192        flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream_(flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream),\n   223\u2192        http_context_(http_context), zone_name_(local_info_.zoneStatName()),\n   224\u2192        shadow_writer_(std::move(shadow_writer)), time_source_(time_source) {\n   225\u2192    if (!strict_check_headers.empty()) {\n   226\u2192      strict_check_headers_ = std::make_unique<HeaderVector>();\n   227\u2192      for (const auto& header : strict_check_headers) {\n   228\u2192        strict_check_headers_->emplace_back(Http::LowerCaseString(header));\n   229\u2192      }\n   230\u2192    }\n   231\u2192  }\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192  static absl::StatusOr<std::unique_ptr<FilterConfig>>\n   234\u2192  create(Stats::StatName stat_prefix, // Http::StreamFilterBase\n   328\u2192  void onDestroy() override;\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192  Http::LocalErrorStatus onLocalReply(const LocalReplyData&) override {\n   331\u2192    // If there's a  void setDecoderFilterCallbacks(Http::StreamDecoderFilterCallbacks& callbacks) override;\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192  // Upstream::LoadBalancerContext\n   363\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> computeHashKey() override {\n   364\u2192    if (route_entry_ && downstream_headers_) {\n   365\u2192      auto hash_policy = route_entry_->hashPolicy();\n   366\u2192      if (hash_policy) {\n   367\u2192        return hash_policy->generateHash(\n   368\u2192            callbacks_->streamInfo().downstreamAddressProvider().remoteAddress().get(),\n   369\u2192            *downstream_headers_,\n   370\u2192            [this](const std::string& key, const std::string& path, std::chrono::seconds max_age,\n   371\u2192                   Http::CookieAttributeRefVector attributes) {\n   372\u2192              return addDownstreamSetCookie(key, path, max_age, attributes);\n   373\u2192            },\n   374\u2192            callbacks_->streamInfo().filterState());\n   375\u2192      }\n   376\u2192    }\n   377\u2192    return {};\n   378\u2192  }\n   379\u2192  const Router::MetadataMatchCriteria* metadataMatchCriteria() override {\n   380\u2192    if (route_entry_) {\n   381\u2192      // Have we been called before? If so, there's no need to recompute because\n   382\u2192      // by the time this method is called for the first time, route_entry_ should\n   383\u2192      // not change anymore.\n   384\u2192      if (metadata_match_ != nullptr) {\n   385\u2192        return metadata_match_.get();\n   386\u2192      }\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192      // The request's metadata, if present, takes precedence over the route's.\n   389\u2192      const auto& request_metadata = callbacks_->streamInfo().dynamicMetadata().filter_metadata();\n   390\u2192      const auto filter_it = request_metadata.find(Envoy::Config::MetadataFilters::get().ENVOY_LB);\n   391\u2192      if (filter_it != request_metadata.end()) {\n   392\u2192        if (route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria() != nullptr) {\n   393\u2192          metadata_match_ =\n   394\u2192              route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria()->mergeMatchCriteria(filter_it->second);\n   395\u2192        } else {\n   396\u2192          metadata_match_ = std::make_unique<Router::MetadataMatchCriteriaImpl>(filter_it->second);\n   397\u2192        }\n   398\u2192        return metadata_match_.get();\n   399\u2192      }\n   400\u2192      return route_entry_->metadataMatchCriteria();\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
164. Read @ -

}

"     1\u2192#pragma once\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include <chrono>\n     4\u2192#include <cstdint>\n     5\u2192#include <functional>\n     6\u2192#include <memory>\n     7\u2192#include \"envoy/http/conn_pool.h\"\n    14\u2192#include \"envoy/http/filter.h\"\n    15\u2192#include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n    16\u2192#include \"envoy/tcp/conn_pool.h\"\n    17\u2192\n \"source/common/common/hex.h\"\n    22\u2192#include \"source/common/common/linked_object.h\"\n    23\u2192#include \"source/common/common/logger.h\"\n    24\u2192#include \"source/common/config/well_known_names.h\"\n    25\u2192#include \"source/common/http/filter_manager.h\"\n    26\u2192#include \"source/common/stream_info/stream_info_impl.h\"\n    27\u2192#include \"source/common/tracing/null_span_impl.h\"\n    28\u2192#include \"source/extensions/filters/http/common/factory_base.h\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    31\u2192namespace Router {\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192class GenericUpstream;\n    34\u2192class GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks;\n    35\u2192class RouterFilterInterface;\n    36\u2192class UpstreamRequest;\n    37\u2192class UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks;\n    38\u2192class UpstreamFilterManager;\n    39\u2192class UpstreamCodecFilter;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192/* The Upstream request is the base class for forwarding HTTP upstream.\n    42\u2192 *\n    43\u2192 * On the new request path, payload (headers/body/metadata/data) still arrives via\n    44\u2192 * the accept[X]fromRouter functions. Said data is immediately passed off to the\n    45\u2192 * UpstreamFilterManager, which passes each item through the filter chain until\n    46\u2192 * it arrives at the last filter in the chain, the UpstreamCodecFilter. If the upstream\n    47\u2192 * stream is not established, the UpstreamCodecFilter returns StopAllIteration, and the\n    48\u2192 * FilterManager will buffer data, using watermarks to push back to the router\n    49\u2192 * filter if buffers become overrun. When an upstream connection is established,\n    50\u2192 * the UpstreamCodecFilter will send data upstream.\n    51\u2192 *\n    52\u2192 * On the new response path, payload arrives from upstream via the UpstreamCodecFilter's\n    53\u2192 * CodecBridge. It is passed off directly to the FilterManager, traverses the\n    54\u2192 * filter chain, and completion is signaled via the\n    55\u2192 * UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks's encode[X] functions. These somewhat\n    56\u2192 * confusingly pass through the UpstreamRequest's legacy decode[X] functions\n    57\u2192 * (required due to the UpstreamToDownstream interface, but will be renamed once\n    58\u2192 * the classic mode is deprecated), and are finally passed to the router via the\n    59\u2192 * RouterFilterInterface onUpstream[X] functions.\n    60\u2192 *\n    61\u2192 * There is some required communication between the UpstreamRequest and\n    62\u2192 * UpstreamCodecFilter. This is accomplished via the UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks\n    63\u2192 * interface, with the UpstreamFilterManager acting as intermediary.\n    64\u2192 *\n    65\u2192 */\n    66\u2192class UpstreamRequest : public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::router>,\n    67\u2192                        public UpstreamToDownstream,\n    68\u2192                        public LinkedObject<UpstreamRequest>,\n    69\u2192                        public GenericConnectionPoolCallbacks,\n    70\u2192                        public Event::DeferredDeletable {\n    71\u2192public:\n    72\u2192  UpstreamRequest(RouterFilterInterface& parent, std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool>&& conn_pool,\n    73\u2192                  bool can_send_early_data, bool can_use_http3, bool enable_half_close);\n    74\u2192  ~UpstreamRequest() override;\n    75\u2192  void deleteIsPending() override { cleanUp(); }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192  // To be called from the destructor, or prior to deferred delete.\n    78\u2192  void cleanUp();\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192  virtual void acceptHeadersFromRouter(bool end_stream);\n    81\u2192  virtual void acceptDataFromRouter(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream);\n    82\u2192  void acceptTrailersFromRouter(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers);\n    83\u2192  void acceptMetadataFromRouter(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr);\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  virtual void resetStream();\n    86\u2192  void setupPerTryTimeout();\n    87\u2192  void maybeEndDecode(bool end_stream);\n    88\u2192  void onUpstreamHostSelected(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host, bool pool_success);\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192  // Http::StreamDecoder\n    91\u2192  void decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override;\n    92\u2192  void decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) override;\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream (Http::ResponseDecoder)\n    95\u2192  void decode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers) override;\n    96\u2192  void decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) override;\n    97\u2192  void decodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) override;\n    98\u2192  void dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const override;\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream (Http::StreamCallbacks)\n   101\u2192  void onResetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reason,\n   102\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) override;\n   103\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override { disableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl(); }\n   104\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override { enableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl(); }\n   105\u2192  // UpstreamToDownstream\n   106\u2192  const Route& route() const override;\n   107\u2192  OptRef<const Network::Connection> connection() const override;\n   108\u2192  const Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions& upstreamStreamOptions() const override {\n   109\u2192    return stream_options_;\n   110\u2192  }\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192  void disableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl();\n   113\u2192  void enableDataFromDownstreamForFlowControl();\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192  // GenericConnPool\n   116\u2192  void onPoolFailure(ConnectionPool::PoolFailureReason reason,\n   117\u2192                     absl::string_view transport_failure_reason,\n   118\u2192                     Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host) override;\n   119\u2192  void onPoolReady(std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream>&& upstream,\n   120\u2192                   Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n   121\u2192                   const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& address_provider,\n   122\u2192                   StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info, absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol) override;\n   123\u2192  UpstreamToDownstream& upstreamToDownstream() override;\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  void clearRequestEncoder();\n   126\u2192  void onStreamMaxDurationReached();\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192  // Either disable upstream reading immediately or defer it and keep tracking\n   129\u2192  // of how many read disabling has happened.\n   130\u2192  void readDisableOrDefer(bool disable);\n   131\u2192  // Called upon receiving the first response headers from the upstream. And\n   132\u2192  // applies read disabling to it if there is any pending read disabling.\n   133\u2192  void maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192  struct DownstreamWatermarkManager : public Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks {\n   136\u2192    DownstreamWatermarkManager(UpstreamRequest& parent) : parent_(parent) {}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    // Http::DownstreamWatermarkCallbacks\n   139\u2192    void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override;\n   140\u2192    void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override;\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    UpstreamRequest& parent_;\n   143\u2192  };\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192  void readEnable();\n   146\u2192  void encodeBodyAndTrailers();\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  // Getters and setters\n   149\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionOptConstRef upstreamHost() {\n   150\u2192    return makeOptRefFromPtr(upstream_host_.get());\n   151\u2192  }\n   152\u2192  void outlierDetectionTimeoutRecorded(bool recorded) {\n   153\u2192    outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_ = recorded;\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192  bool outlierDetectionTimeoutRecorded() { return outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_; }\n   156\u2192  void retried(bool value) { retried_ = value; }\n   157\u2192  bool retried() { return retried_; }\n   158\u2192  bool grpcRqSuccessDeferred() { return grpc_rq_success_deferred_; }\n   159\u2192  void grpcRqSuccessDeferred(bool deferred) { grpc_rq_success_deferred_ = deferred; }\n   160\u2192  void upstreamCanary(bool value) { upstream_canary_ = value; }\n   161\u2192  bool upstreamCanary() { return upstream_canary_; }\n   162\u2192  bool awaitingHeaders() { return awaiting_headers_; }\n   163\u2192  void recordTimeoutBudget(bool value) { record_timeout_budget_ = value; }\n   164\u2192  bool createPerTryTimeoutOnRequestComplete() {\n   165\u2192    return create_per_try_timeout_on_request_complete_;\n   166\u2192  }\n   167\u2192  bool encodeComplete() const { return router_sent_end_stream_; }\n   168\u2192  // Exposes streamInfo for the upstream stream.\n   169\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo& streamInfo() { return stream_info_; }\n   170\u2192  bool hadUpstream() const { return had_upstream_; }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192private:\n   173\u2192  friend class UpstreamFilterManager;\n   174\u2192  friend class UpstreamCodecFilter;\n   175\u2192  friend class UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks;\n   176\u2192  StreamInfo::UpstreamTiming& upstreamTiming() {\n   177\u2192    return stream_info_.upstreamInfo()->upstreamTiming();\n   178\u2192  }\n   179\u2192  // Records the latency from when the upstream request was first created to\n   180\u2192  // when the pool callback fires. This latency can be useful to track excessive\n   181\u2192  // queuing.\n   182\u2192  void recordConnectionPoolCallbackLatency();\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192  void addResponseHeadersStat(uint64_t size, size_t count) {\n   185\u2192    response_headers_size_ = response_headers_size_.value_or(0) + size;\n   186\u2192    response_headers_count_ = response_headers_count_.value_or(0) + count;\n   187\u2192  }\n   188\u2192  void resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   189\u2192  void onPerTryTimeout();\n   190\u2192  void onPerTryIdleTimeout();\n   191\u2192  void upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType access_log_type);\n   192\u2192  void resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192  RouterFilterInterface& parent_;\n   195\u2192  std::unique_ptr<GenericConnPool> conn_pool_;\n   196\u2192  Event::TimerPtr per_try_timeout_;\n   197\u2192  Event::TimerPtr per_try_idle_timeout_;\n   198\u2192  std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream> upstream_;\n   199\u2192  absl::optional<Http::StreamResetReason> deferred_reset_reason_;\n   200\u2192  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr upstream_host_;\n   201\u2192  DownstreamWatermarkManager downstream_watermark_manager_{*this};\n   202\u2192  Tracing::SpanPtr span_;\n   203\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfoImpl stream_info_;\n   204\u2192  const MonotonicTime start_time_;\n   205\u2192  // This is wrapped in an optional, since we want to avoid computing zero size headers when in\n   206\u2192  // reality we just didn't get a response back.\n   207\u2192  absl::optional<uint64_t> response_headers_size_;\n   208\u2192  absl::optional<size_t> response_headers_count_;\n   209\u2192  // Copies of upstream headers/trailers. These are only set if upstream\n   210\u2192  // access logging is configured.\n   211\u2192  Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr upstream_headers_;\n   212\u2192  Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr upstream_trailers_;\n   213\u2192  OptRef<UpstreamToDownstream> upstream_interface_;\n   214\u2192  std::list<Http::UpstreamCallbacks*> upstream_callbacks_;\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192  Event::TimerPtr max_stream_duration_timer_;\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192  // Per-stream access log flush duration. This timer is enabled once when the stream is created\n   219\u2192  // and will log to all access logs once per trigger.\n   220\u2192  Event::TimerPtr upstream_log_flush_timer_;\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192  std::unique_ptr<UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks> filter_manager_callbacks_;\n   223\u2192  std::unique_ptr<Http::FilterManager> filter_manager_;\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192  // The number of outstanding readDisable to be called with parameter value true.\n   226\u2192  // When downstream send buffers get above high watermark before response headers arrive, we\n   227\u2192  // increment this counter instead of immediately calling readDisable on upstream stream. This is\n   228\u2192  // to avoid the upstream request from being spuriously retried or reset because of upstream\n   229\u2192  // timeouts while upstream stream is readDisabled by downstream but the response has actually\n   230\u2192  // arrived from upstream. See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/25901. During the\n   231\u2192  // deferring period, if the downstream buffer gets below low watermark, this counter gets\n   232\u2192  // decremented. Once the response headers arrive, call readDisable the number of times as the\n   233\u2192  // remaining value of this counter.\n   234\u2192  size_t deferred_read_disabling_count_{0};\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192  // Keep small members (bools and enums) at the end of class, to reduce alignment overhead.\n   237\u2192  // Tracks the number of times the flow of data from downstream has been disabled.\n   238\u2192  uint32_t downstream_data_disabled_{};\n   239\u2192  bool upstream_canary_ : 1;\n   240\u2192  bool router_sent_end_stream_ : 1;\n   241\u2192  bool encode_trailers_ : 1;\n   242\u2192  bool retried_ : 1;\n   243\u2192  bool awaiting_headers_ : 1;\n   244\u2192  bool outlier_detection_timeout_recorded_ : 1;\n   245\u2192  // Tracks whether we deferred a per try timeout because the downstream request\n   246\u2192  // had not been completed yet.\n   247\u2192  bool create_per_try_timeout_on_request_complete_ : 1;\n   248\u2192  // True if the CONNECT headers have been sent but proxying payload is paused\n   249\u2192  // waiting for response headers.\n   250\u2192  bool paused_for_connect_ : 1;\n   251\u2192  bool paused_for_websocket_ : 1;\n   252\u2192  bool reset_stream_ : 1;\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192  // Sentinel to indicate if timeout budget tracking is configured for the cluster,\n   255\u2192  // and if so, if the per-try histogram should record a value.\n   256\u2192  bool record_timeout_budget_ : 1;\n   257\u2192  // Track if one time clean up has been performed.\n   258\u2192  bool cleaned_up_ : 1;\n   259\u2192  bool had_upstream_ : 1;\n   260\u2192  Http::ConnectionPool::Instance::StreamOptions stream_options_;\n   261\u2192  bool grpc_rq_success_deferred_ : 1;\n   262\u2192  bool enable_half_close_ : 1;\n   263\u2192};\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192class UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks : public Http::FilterManagerCallbacks,\n   266\u2192                                              public Event::DeferredDeletable,\n   267\u2192                                              public Http::UpstreamStreamFilterCallbacks {\n   268\u2192public:\n   269\u2192  UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks(UpstreamRequest& upstream_request)\n   270\u2192      : upstream_request_(upstream_request) {}\n   271\u2192  void encodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&, bool end_stream) override {\n   272\u2192    upstream_request_.decodeHeaders(std::move(response_headers_), end_stream);\n   273\u2192  }\n   274\u2192  void encode1xxHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMap&) override {\n   275\u2192    upstream_request_.decode1xxHeaders(std::move(informational_headers_));\n   276\u2192  }\n   277\u2192  void encodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) override {\n   278\u2192    upstream_request_.decodeData(data, end_stream);\n   279\u2192  }\n   280\u2192  void encodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMap&) override {\n   281\u2192    upstream_request_.decodeTrailers(std::move(response_trailers_));\n   282\u2192  }\n   283\u2192  void encodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata) override {\n   284\u2192    upstream_request_.decodeMetadata(std::move(metadata));\n   285\u2192  }\n   286\u2192  void setRequestTrailers(Http::RequestTrailerMapPtr&& request_trailers) override {\n   287\u2192    trailers_ = std::move(request_trailers);\n   288\u2192  }\n   289\u2192  void setInformationalHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) override {\n   290\u2192    informational_headers_ = std::move(response_headers);\n   291\u2192  }\n   292\u2192  void setResponseHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& response_headers) override {\n   293\u2192    response_headers_ = std::move(response_headers);\n   294\u2192  }\n   295\u2192  void setResponseTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& response_trailers) override {\n   296\u2192    response_trailers_ = std::move(response_trailers);\n   297\u2192  }\n   298\u2192  Http::RequestHeaderMapOptRef requestHeaders() override;\n   299\u2192  Http::RequestTrailerMapOptRef requestTrailers() override;\n   300\u2192  Http::ResponseHeaderMapOptRef informationalHeaders() override {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
165. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "find /workspace/source/common/http -name \"*.cc\" | xargs grep -l \"newStreamEncoder\\|onPoolReady\" | head -5"
}
Output
"/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.cc\n/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_grid.cc\n/workspace/source/common/http/http1/conn_pool.cc"
166. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/source/common/http/conn_pool_base.cc",
  "limit": 200
}

"     1\u2192#include \"source/common/http/conn_pool_base.h\"\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192#include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n     4\u2192#include \"source/common/http/utility.h\"\n \"source/common/stats/timespan_impl.h\"\n     8\u2192#include \"source/common/upstream/upstream_impl.h\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192namespace Envoy {\n    11\u2192namespace Http {\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr\n    14\u2192wrapTransportSocketOptions(Network::TransportSocketOptionsConstSharedPtr transport_socket_options,\n    15\u2192                           std::vector<Protocol> protocols) {\n    16\u2192  std::vector<std::string> fallbacks;\n    17\u2192  for (auto protocol : protocols) {\n    18\u2192    // If configured to do so, we override the ALPN to use for the upstream connection to match the\n    19\u2192    // selected protocol.\n    20\u2192    switch (protocol) {\n    21\u2192    case Http::Protocol::Http10:\n    22\u2192 Http::Protocol::Http2:\n    27\u2192      fallbacks.push_back(Http::Utility::AlpnNames::get().Http2);\n    28\u2192      break;\n    29\u2192    case Http::Protocol::Http3:\n    30\u2192      // HTTP3 ALPN is set in the QUIC stack based on supported versions.\n    31\u2192      break;\n    32\u2192    }\n    33\u2192  }\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  if (transport_socket_options) {\n    36\u2192 std::make_shared<Network::TransportSocketOptionsImpl>(\n    40\u2192        \"\", std::vector<std::string>{}, std::vector<std::string>{}, std::move(fallbacks));\n    41\u2192  }\n    42\u2192}\n Random::RandomGenerator& random_generator, Upstream::ClusterConnectivityState& state,\n    49\u2192    std::vector<Http::Protocol> protocols)\n    50\u2192    : Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase(\n    51\u2192          host, priority, dispatcher, options,\n    52\u2192          wrapTransportSocketOptions(transport_socket_options, protocols), state),\n destructAllConnections(); }\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192ConnectionPool::Cancellable*\n    60\u2192HttpConnPoolImplBase::newStream(Http::ResponseDecoder& response_decoder,\n    61\u2192                                Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks& callbacks,\n    62\u2192                                const Instance::StreamOptions& options) {\n    63\u2192  HttpAttachContext context({&response_decoder, &callbacks});\n    64\u2192  return newStreamImpl(context, options.can_send_early_data_);\n    65\u2192}\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192bool HttpConnPoolImplBase::hasActiveConnections() const {\n    68\u2192  return (hasPendingStreams() || (hasActiveStreams()));\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192ConnectionPool::Cancellable*\n    72\u2192HttpConnPoolImplBase::newPendingStream(Envoy::ConnectionPool::AttachContext& context,\n    73\u2192                                       bool can_send_early_data) {\n    74\u2192  Http::ResponseDecoder& decoder = *typedContext<HttpAttachContext>(context).decoder_;\n    75\u2192  Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks& callbacks = *typedContext<HttpAttachContext>(context).callbacks_;\n    76\u2192  ENVOY_LOG(debug,\n    77\u2192            \"queueing stream due to no available connections (ready={} busy={} connecting={})\",\n    78\u2192            ready_clients_.size(), busy_clients_.size(), connecting_clients_.size());\n    79\u2192  Envoy::ConnectionPool::PendingStreamPtr pending_stream(\n    80\u2192      new HttpPendingStream(*this, decoder, callbacks, can_send_early_data));\n    81\u2192  return addPendingStream(std::move(pending_stream));\n    82\u2192}\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192void HttpConnPoolImplBase::onPoolReady(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient& client,\n    85\u2192                                       Envoy::ConnectionPool::AttachContext& context) {\n    86\u2192  ActiveClient* http_client = static_cast<ActiveClient*>(&client);\n    87\u2192  auto& http_context = typedContext<HttpAttachContext>(context);\n    88\u2192  Http::ResponseDecoder& response_decoder = *http_context.decoder_;\n    89\u2192  Http::ConnectionPool::Callbacks& callbacks = *http_context.callbacks_;\n    90\u2192  Http::RequestEncoder& new_encoder = http_client->newStreamEncoder(response_decoder);\n    91\u2192  callbacks.onPoolReady(new_encoder, client.real_host_description_,\n    92\u2192                        http_client->codec_client_->streamInfo(),\n    93\u2192                        http_client->codec_client_->protocol());\n    94\u2192}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192// All streams are 2^31. Client streams are half that, minus stream 0. Just to be on the safe\n    97\u2192// side we do 2^29.\n    98\u2192static const uint64_t DEFAULT_MAX_STREAMS = (1 << 29);\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192void MultiplexedActiveClientBase::onGoAway(Http::GoAwayErrorCode) {\n   101\u2192  ENVOY_CONN_LOG(debug, \"remote goaway\", *codec_client_);\n   102\u2192  parent_.host()->cluster().trafficStats()->upstream_cx_close_notify_.inc();\n   103\u2192  if (state() != ActiveClient::State::Draining) {\n   104\u2192    if (codec_client_->numActiveRequests() == 0) {\n   105\u2192      codec_client_->close();\n   106\u2192    } else {\n   107\u2192      parent_.transitionActiveClientState(*this, ActiveClient::State::Draining);\n   108\u2192    }\n   109\u2192  }\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192// Adjust the concurrent stream limit if the negotiated concurrent stream limit\n   113\u2192// is lower than the local max configured streams.\n   114\u2192//\n   115\u2192// Note: if multiple streams are assigned to a connection before the settings\n   116\u2192// are received, they may still be reset by the peer. This could be avoided by\n   117\u2192// not considering http/2 connections connected until the SETTINGS frame is\n   118\u2192// received, but that would result in a latency penalty instead.\n   119\u2192void MultiplexedActiveClientBase::onSettings(ReceivedSettings& settings) {\n   120\u2192  if (settings.maxConcurrentStreams().has_value()) {\n   121\u2192    int64_t old_unused_capacity = currentUnusedCapacity();\n   122\u2192    // Given config limits old_unused_capacity should never exceed int32_t.\n   123\u2192    ASSERT(std::numeric_limits<int32_t>::max() >= old_unused_capacity);\n   124\u2192    if (parent().cache() && parent().origin().has_value()) {\n   125\u2192      parent().cache()->setConcurrentStreams(*parent().origin(),\n   126\u2192                                             settings.maxConcurrentStreams().value());\n   127\u2192    }\n   128\u2192    concurrent_stream_limit_ =\n   129\u2192        std::min(settings.maxConcurrentStreams().value(), configured_stream_limit_);\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192    int64_t delta = old_unused_capacity - currentUnusedCapacity();\n   132\u2192    if (state() == ActiveClient::State::Ready && currentUnusedCapacity() <= 0) {\n   133\u2192      parent_.transitionActiveClientState(*this, ActiveClient::State::Busy);\n   134\u2192    } else if (state() == ActiveClient::State::Busy && currentUnusedCapacity() > 0) {\n   135\u2192      parent_.transitionActiveClientState(*this, ActiveClient::State::Ready);\n   136\u2192    }\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    if (delta > 0) {\n   139\u2192      parent_.decrClusterStreamCapacity(delta);\n   140\u2192      ENVOY_CONN_LOG(trace, \"Decreasing stream capacity by {}\", *codec_client_, delta);\n   141\u2192    } else if (delta < 0) {\n   142\u2192      parent_.incrClusterStreamCapacity(-delta);\n   143\u2192      ENVOY_CONN_LOG(trace, \"Increasing stream capacity by {}\", *codec_client_, -delta);\n   144\u2192    }\n   145\u2192  }\n   146\u2192}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192void MultiplexedActiveClientBase::onStreamDestroy() {\n   149\u2192  parent().onStreamClosed(*this, false);\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  // If we are destroying this stream because of a disconnect, do not check for drain here. We will\n   152\u2192  // wait until the connection has been fully drained of streams and then check in the connection\n   153\u2192  // event callback.\n   154\u2192  if (!closed_with_active_rq_) {\n   155\u2192    parent().checkForIdleAndCloseIdleConnsIfDraining();\n   156\u2192  }\n   157\u2192}\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192void MultiplexedActiveClientBase::onStreamReset(Http::StreamResetReason reason) {\n   160\u2192  switch (reason) {\n   161\u2192  case StreamResetReason::ConnectionTermination:\n   162\u2192  case StreamResetReason::LocalConnectionFailure:\n   163\u2192  case StreamResetReason::RemoteConnectionFailure:\n   164\u2192  case StreamResetReason::ConnectionTimeout:\n   165\u2192    parent_.host()->cluster().trafficStats()->upstream_rq_pending_failure_eject_.inc();\n   166\u2192    closed_with_active_rq_ = true;\n   167\u2192    break;\n   168\u2192  case StreamResetReason::LocalReset:\n   169\u2192  case StreamResetReason::ProtocolError:\n   170\u2192  case StreamResetReason::OverloadManager:\n   171\u2192    parent_.host()->cluster().trafficStats()->upstream_rq_tx_reset_.inc();\n   172\u2192    break;\n   173\u2192  case StreamResetReason::RemoteReset:\n   174\u2192    parent_.host()->cluster().trafficStats()->upstream_rq_rx_reset_.inc();\n   175\u2192    break;\n   176\u2192  case StreamResetReason::LocalRefusedStreamReset:\n   177\u2192  case StreamResetReason::RemoteRefusedStreamReset:\n   178\u2192  case StreamResetReason::Overflow:\n   179\u2192  case StreamResetReason::ConnectError:\n   180\u2192  case StreamResetReason::Http1PrematureUpstreamHalfClose:\n   181\u2192    break;\n   182\u2192  }\n   183\u2192}\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192uint64_t MultiplexedActiveClientBase::maxStreamsPerConnection(uint64_t max_streams_config) {\n   186\u2192  return (max_streams_config != 0) ? max_streams_config : DEFAULT_MAX_STREAMS;\n   187\u2192}\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192MultiplexedActiveClientBase::MultiplexedActiveClientBase(\n   190\u2192    HttpConnPoolImplBase& parent, uint32_t effective_concurrent_streams,\n   191\u2192    uint32_t max_configured_concurrent_streams, Stats::Counter& cx_total,\n   192\u2192    OptRef<Upstream::Host::CreateConnectionData> data)\n   193\u2192    : Envoy::Http::ActiveClient(\n   194\u2192          parent, maxStreamsPerConnection(parent.host()->cluster().maxRequestsPerConnection()),\n   195\u2192          effective_concurrent_streams, max_configured_concurrent_streams, data) {\n   196\u2192  codec_client_->setCodecClientCallbacks(*this);\n   197\u2192  codec_client_->setCodecConnectionCallbacks(*this);\n   198\u2192  cx_total.inc();\n   199\u2192}\n   200\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Fake setting StopSingleIteration since the continueDecoding() code\n537:void FilterManager::decodeHeaders(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, RequestHeaderMap& headers,\n555:    FilterHeadersStatus status = (*entry)->decodeHeaders(headers, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n563:                       \"decodeHeaders filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n570:           \"decodeHeaders when end_stream is already false\");\n623:    // If body was injected the end_stream will be processed in the `decodeData()` method below.\n638:void FilterManager::decodeData(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n671:    //  filter0->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n675:    //      wrapper0->decodeHeaders(_, _, true)\n676:    //        filter1->decodeHeaders(_, true)\n679:    //        filter2->decodeHeaders(_, false)\n683:    //            wrapper1->decodeData()\n684:    //              filter2->decodeData(_, true)\n687:    //        wrapper0->decodeData()\n688:    //          filter1->decodeData(_, true)  // It will cause assertions.\n691:    // If a filter is already marked as end_stream_ when decodeData() is called, bails out the\n693:    // wrong data. For encodeData, the response_encoder->encode() will be called.\n700:    // is called in decodeData during a previous filter invocation, at which point we communicate to\n710:    FilterDataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->decodeData(data, (*entry)->end_stream_);\n722:      ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"decodeData filter iteration aborted due to local reply: filter={}\",\n752:  // If trailers were adding during decodeData we need to trigger decodeTrailers in order\n781:    // Inline processing happens in the decodeHeaders() callback if necessary."
