"   900\u2192StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator\n   901\u2192FilterManager::commonEncodePrefix(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter, bool end_stream,\n   902\u2192                                  FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n   903\u2192  // Only do base state setting on the initial call. Subsequent calls for filtering do not touch\n   904\u2192  // the base state.\n   905\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"commonEncodePrefix end_stream: {}, isHalfCloseEnabled: {}\", *this,\n   906\u2192                   end_stream, filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled());\n   907\u2192  if (filter == nullptr) {\n   908\u2192    if (end_stream) {\n   909\u2192      ASSERT(!state_.observed_encode_end_stream_);\n   910\u2192      state_.observed_encode_end_stream_ = true;\n   911\u2192\n   912\u2192      // When half close semantics are disabled, receiving end stream from the upstream causes\n   913\u2192      // decoder filter to stop, as neither filters nor upstream is interested in downstream data.\n   914\u2192      // half close is enabled in case tcp proxying is done with http1 encoder. In this case, we\n   915\u2192      // should not stop decoder filter chain when end_stream is true, as it will cause any data\n   916\u2192      // sent in the upstream direction to be\n   917\u2192      // dropped.\n   918\u2192      if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n   919\u2192        state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   920\u2192      }\n   921\u2192    }\n   922\u2192    return encoder_filters_.begin();\n   923\u2192  }\n   924\u2192\n   925\u2192  if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n   926\u2192      (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n   927\u2192    // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n   928\u2192    // The current filter's encoding callback has not be called. Call it now.\n   929\u2192    return filter->entry();\n   930\u2192  }\n   931\u2192  return std::next(filter->entry());\n   932\u2192}\n   933\u2192\n   934\u2192StreamDecoderFilters::Iterator\n   935\u2192FilterManager::commonDecodePrefix(ActiveStreamDecoderFilter* filter,\n   936\u2192                                  FilterIterationStartState filter_iteration_start_state) {\n   937\u2192  if (!filter) {\n   938\u2192    return decoder_filters_.begin();\n   939\u2192  }\n   940\u2192  if (filter_iteration_start_state == FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent &&\n   941\u2192      (*(filter->entry()))->iterate_from_current_filter_) {\n   942\u2192    // The filter iteration has been stopped for all frame types, and now the iteration continues.\n   943\u2192    // The current filter's callback function has not been called. Call it now.\n   944\u2192    return filter->entry();\n   945\u2192  }\n   946\u2192  return std::next(filter->entry());\n   947\u2192}\n   948\u2192\n   949\u2192void DownstreamFilterManager::onLocalReply(StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData& data) {\n   950\u2192  state_.under_on_local_reply_ = true;\n   951\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.onLocalReply(data.code_);\n   952\u2192\n   953\u2192  for (auto entry : filters_) {\n   954\u2192    if (entry->onLocalReply(data) == LocalErrorStatus::ContinueAndResetStream) {\n   955\u2192      data.reset_imminent_ = true;\n   956\u2192    }\n   957\u2192  }\n   958\u2192  state_.under_on_local_reply_ = false;\n   959\u2192}\n   960\u2192\n   961\u2192void DownstreamFilterManager::sendLocalReply(\n   962\u2192    Code code, absl::string_view body,\n   963\u2192    const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers,\n   964\u2192    const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n   965\u2192  ASSERT(!state_.under_on_local_reply_);\n   966\u2192  const bool is_head_request = state_.is_head_request_;\n   967\u2192  const bool is_grpc_request = state_.is_grpc_request_;\n   968\u2192\n   969\u2192  // Stop filter chain iteration if local reply was sent while filter decoding or encoding callbacks\n   970\u2192  // are running.\n   971\u2192  if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n   972\u2192    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   973\u2192  } else if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask) {\n   974\u2192    state_.encoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   975\u2192  }\n   976\u2192  // When independent half-close is enabled local reply always stops decoder filter chain.\n   977\u2192  if (filter_manager_callbacks_.isHalfCloseEnabled()) {\n   978\u2192    state_.decoder_filter_chain_aborted_ = true;\n   979\u2192  }\n   980\u2192\n   981\u2192  streamInfo().setResponseCodeDetails(details);\n   982\u2192  StreamFilterBase::LocalReplyData data{code, grpc_status, details, false};\n   983\u2192  onLocalReply(data);\n   984\u2192  if (data.reset_imminent_) {\n   985\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. onLocalReply requested reset.\", *this,\n   986\u2192                     details);\n   987\u2192    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream(Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset, \"\");\n   988\u2192    return;\n   989\u2192  }\n   990\u2192\n   991\u2192  if (!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value() &&\n   992\u2192      (!filter_manager_callbacks_.informationalHeaders().has_value() ||\n   993\u2192       (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   994\u2192            \"envoy.reloadable_features.local_reply_traverses_filter_chain_after_1xx\") &&\n   995\u2192        !(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsEncodingMask)))) {\n   996\u2192    // If the response has not started at all, or if the only response so far is an informational\n   997\u2192    // 1xx that has already been fully processed, send the response through the filter chain.\n   998\u2192\n   999\u2192    if (auto cb = filter_manager_callbacks_.downstreamCallbacks(); cb.has_value()) {\n  1000\u2192      // The initial route maybe never be set or the cached route maybe cleared by the filters.\n  1001\u2192      // This will force route refreshment if there is not a cached route to avoid potential\n  1002\u2192      // route refreshment in the response filter chain.\n  1003\u2192      cb->route(nullptr);\n  1004\u2192    }\n  1005\u2192\n  1006\u2192    // We only prepare a local reply to execute later if we're actively\n  1007\u2192    // invoking filters to avoid re-entrant in filters.\n  1008\u2192    if (state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::IsDecodingMask) {\n  1009\u2192      prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n  1010\u2192                                      grpc_status, details);\n  1011\u2192    } else {\n  1012\u2192      sendLocalReplyViaFilterChain(is_grpc_request, code, body, modify_headers, is_head_request,\n  1013\u2192                                   grpc_status, details);\n  1014\u2192    }\n  1015\u2192  } else if (!state_.non_100_response_headers_encoded_) {\n  1016\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Sending local reply with details {} directly to the encoder\", *this,\n  1017\u2192                     details);\n  1018\u2192    // In this case, at least the header and possibly the body has started\n  1019\u2192    // processing through the filter chain, but no non-informational headers\n  1020\u2192    // have been sent downstream. To ensure that filters don't get their\n  1021\u2192    // state machine screwed up, bypass the filter chain and send the local\n  1022\u2192    // reply directly to the codec.\n  1023\u2192    //\n  1024\u2192    sendDirectLocalReply(code, body, modify_headers, state_.is_head_request_, grpc_status);\n  1025\u2192  } else {\n  1026\u2192    // If we land in this branch, response headers have already been sent to the client.\n  1027\u2192    // All we can do at this point is reset the stream.\n  1028\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Resetting stream due to {}. Prior headers have already been sent\",\n  1029\u2192                     *this, details);\n  1030\u2192    // TODO(snowp): This means we increment the tx_reset stat which we weren't doing previously.\n  1031\u2192    // Intended?\n  1032\u2192    filter_manager_callbacks_.resetStream();\n  1033\u2192  }\n  1034\u2192}\n  1035\u2192\n  1036\u2192void DownstreamFilterManager::prepareLocalReplyViaFilterChain(\n  1037\u2192    bool is_grpc_request, Code code, absl::string_view body,\n  1038\u2192    const std::function<void(ResponseHeaderMap& headers)>& modify_headers, bool is_head_request,\n  1039\u2192    const absl::optional<Grpc::Status::GrpcStatus> grpc_status, absl::string_view details) {\n  1040\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"Preparing local reply with details {}\", *this, details);\n  1041\u2192  ASSERT(!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseHeaders().has_value());\n  1042\u2192  // For early error handling, do a best-effort attempt to create a filter chain\n  1043\u2192  // to ensure access logging. If the filter chain already exists this will be\n  1044\u2192  // a no-op.\n  1045\u2192  createDownstreamFilterChain();\n  1046\u2192\n  1047\u2192  if (prepared_local_reply_) {\n  1048\u2192    return;\n  1049\u2192  }\n  1050\u2192\n  1051\u2192  prepared_local_reply_ = Utility::prepareLocalReply(\n  1052\u2192      Utility::EncodeFunctions{\n  1053\u2192          [this, modify_headers](ResponseHeaderMap& headers) -> void {\n  1054\u2192            if (streamInfo().route() && streamInfo().route()->routeEntry()) {\n  1055\u2192              streamInfo().route()->routeEntry()->finalizeResponseHeaders(headers, streamInfo());\n  1056\u2192            }\n  1057\u2192            if (modify_headers) {\n  1058\u2192              modify_headers(headers);\n  1059\u2192            }\n  1060\u2192          },\n  1061\u2192          [this](ResponseHeaderMap& response_headers, Code& code, std::string& body,\n  1062\u2192                 absl::string_view& content_type) -> void {\n  1063\u2192            local_reply_.rewrite(filter_manager_callbacks_.requestHeaders().ptr(), response_headers,\n  1064\u2192 },\n  1070\u2192          [this](Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) -> void {\n  1071\u2192            encodeData(nullptr, data, end_stream,\n  1072\u2192 const auto continue_iteration = (*entry)->commonHandleAfterHeadersCallback(status, end_stream);\n  1268\u2192\n  1269\u2192    // If this filter ended the stream, encodeComplete() should be called for Here we handle the case where we have a header only response, but a filter adds a body\n  1282\u2192    // to it. We need to not raise end_stream = true to further filters during inline iteration.\n  1283\u2192    if (end_stream && buffered_response_data_ && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end()) {\n  1284\u2192      continue_data_entry = entry;\n  1285\u2192    }\n  1286\u2192  }\n  1287\u2192\n  1288\u2192  // Check if the filter chain above did not remove critical headers or set malformed header values.\n  1289\u2192  // We could do this at the codec in order to prevent other places than the filter chain from\n  1290\u2192  // removing critical headers, but it will come with the implementation complexity.\n  1291\u2192  // See the previous attempt (#15658) for detail, and for now we choose to protect only against\n  1292\u2192  // filter chains.\n  1293\u2192  const auto status = HeaderUtility::checkRequiredResponseHeaders(headers);\n  1294\u2192  if (!status.ok()) {\n  1295\u2192    // If the check failed, then we reply with BadGateway, and stop the further processing.\n  1296\u2192    sendLocalReply(\n  1297\u2192        Http::Code::BadGateway, status.message(), nullptr, absl::nullopt,\n  1298\u2192        absl::StrCat(StreamInfo::ResponseCodeDetails::get().FilterRemovedRequiredResponseHeaders,\n  1299\u2192                     \"{\", StringUtil::replaceAllEmptySpace(status.message()), \"}\"));\n  1300\u2192    return;\n  1301\u2192  }\n  1302\u2192\n  1303\u2192  const bool modified_end_stream = (end_stream && continue_data_entry == encoder_filters_.end());\n  1304\u2192 1313\u2192  }\n  1314\u2192}\n  1315\u2192\n  1316\u2192void FilterManager::encodeMetadata(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter* filter,\n  1317\u2192                                   MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) {\n  1318\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.resetIdleTimer();\n  1319\u2192\n  1320\u2192  StreamEncoderFilters::Iterator entry =\n  1321\u2192      commonEncodePrefix(filter, false, FilterIterationStartState::CanStartFromCurrent);\n  1322\u2192\n  1323\u2192  for (; entry != encoder_filters_.end(); entry++) {\n  1324\u2192    ENVOY_EXECUTION_SCOPE(trackedStream(), &(*entry)->filter_context_);\n  1325\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry has stopped for all frame type, stores metadata and returns.\n  1326\u2192    // If the filter pointed by entry hasn't returned from encodeHeaders, stores newly added\n  1327\u2192    // metadata in case encodeHeaders returns StopAllIteration. The latter can happen when headers\n  1328\u2192    // callbacks generate new metadata.\n  1329\u2192    if (!(*entry)->processed_headers_ || (*entry)->stoppedAll()) {\n  1330\u2192      (*entry)->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n  1331\u2192      return;\n  1332\u2192    }\n  1333\u2192\n  1334\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ |= FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\n  1335\u2192\n  1336\u2192    FilterMetadataStatus status = (*entry)->handle_->encodeMetadata(*metadata_map_ptr);\n  1337\u2192\n  1338\u2192    state_.filter_call_state_ &= ~FilterCallState::EncodeMetadata;\n  1339\u2192\n  1340\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"encode metadata called: filter={} status={} metadata={}\", *this,\n  1341\u2192                     (*entry)->filter_context_.config_name, static_cast<uint64_t>(status),\n  1342\u2192                     *metadata_map_ptr);\n  1343\u2192\n  1344\u2192    if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::StopIterationForLocalReply) {\n  1345\u2192      // In case of a local reply, we do not continue encoding metadata. Metadata is not sent on to\n  1346\u2192      // the client.\n  1347\u2192      return;\n  1348\u2192    }\n  1349\u2192\n  1350\u2192    if (status == FilterMetadataStatus::ContinueAll && !(*entry)->canIterate()) {\n  1351\u2192      if (std::next(entry) != encoder_filters_.end()) {\n  1352\u2192        (*std::next(entry))->getSavedResponseMetadata()->emplace_back(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n  1353\u2192      } else {\n  1354\u2192        filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n  1355\u2192      }\n  1356\u2192      (*entry)->commonContinue();\n  1357\u2192      return;\n  1358\u2192    }\n  1359\u2192  }\n  1360\u2192  // TODO(soya3129): update stats with metadata.\n  1361\u2192\n  1362\u2192  // Now encode metadata via the codec.\n  1363\u2192  if (!metadata_map_ptr->empty()) {\n  1364\u2192    filter_manager_callbacks_.encodeMetadata(std::move(metadata_map_ptr));\n  1365\u2192  }\n  1366\u2192}\n  1367\u2192\n  1368\u2192ResponseTrailerMap& FilterManager::addEncodedTrailers() {\n  1369\u2192  // Trailers can only be added during the last data frame (i.e. end_stream = true).\n  1370\u2192  ASSERT(state_.filter_call_state_ & FilterCallState::EndOfStream);\n  1371\u2192\n  1372\u2192  // Trailers can only be added once.\n  1373\u2192  ASSERT(!filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers());\n  1374\u2192\n  1375\u2192  filter_manager_callbacks_.setResponseTrailers(ResponseTrailerMapImpl::create());\n  1376\u2192  return *filter_manager_callbacks_.responseTrailers();\n  1377\u2192}\n  1378\u2192\n  1379\u2192void FilterManager::addEncodedData(ActiveStreamEncoderFilter& filter, Buffer::Instance& data,\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. Because ordering on the deferred delete list is not guaranteed in the\n    63\u2192      // case of a disconnect, make sure parent_ is valid before doing clean-up.\n    64\u2192      if (parent_) {\n    65\u2192        parent_->clearCallbacks();\n    66\u2192      }\n    67\u2192    }\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    Network::ClientConnection& connection() override { return connection_; }\n 74\u2192    void addUpstreamCallbacks(ConnectionPool::UpstreamCallbacks& callbacks) override {\n    75\u2192      parent_->callbacks_ = &callbacks;\n    76\u2192    }\n    77\u2192    void release() { parent_ = nullptr; }\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192  protected:\n    80\u2192    ConnectionPool::ConnectionState* connectionState() override {\n    81\u2192      return parent_->connection_state_.get();\n    82\u2192    }\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192  private:\n    85\u2192    ActiveTcpClient* parent_;\n    86\u2192    Network::ClientConnection& connection_;\n    87\u2192  };\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192  ActiveTcpClient(Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase& parent,\n    90\u2192                  const Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr& host, uint64_t concurrent_stream_limit,\n    91\u2192                  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> idle_timeout);\n    92\u2192  ~ActiveTcpClient() override;\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192  // Override the default's of Envoy::ConnectionPool::ActiveClient for class-specific functions.\n    95\u2192  // Network::ConnectionCallbacks\n    96\u2192  void onEvent(Network::ConnectionEvent event) override;\n    97\u2192  void onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark() override { callbacks_->onAboveWriteBufferHighWatermark(); }\n    98\u2192  void onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark() override { callbacks_->onBelowWriteBufferLowWatermark(); }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192  // Undos the readDisable done in onEvent(Connected)\n   101\u2192  void readEnableIfNew();\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192  void initializeReadFilters() override { connection_->initializeReadFilters(); }\n   104\u2192  absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol() const override { return {}; }\n   105\u2192  void close() override;\n   106\u2192  uint32_t numActiveStreams() const override { return callbacks_ ? 1 : 0; }\n   107\u2192  bool closingWithIncompleteStream() const override { return false; }\n   108\u2192  uint64_t id() const override { return connection_->id(); }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192  void onUpstreamData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   111\u2192    if (callbacks_) {\n   112\u2192      callbacks_->onUpstreamData(data, end_stream);\n   113\u2192    } else {\n   114\u2192      close();\n   115\u2192    }\n   116\u2192  }\n   117\u2192  virtual void clearCallbacks();\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192  // Called if the underlying connection is idle over the cluster's tcpPoolIdleTimeout()\n   120\u2192  void onIdleTimeout();\n   121\u2192  void disableIdleTimer();\n idle_timer_;\n   133\u2192};\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192class ConnPoolImpl : public Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase,\n   136\u2192                     public Tcp::ConnectionPool::Instance {\n   137\u2192public:\n   138\u2192  ConnPoolImpl(Event::Dispatcher& dispatcher, Upstream::HostConstSharedPtr host,\n   139\u2192               Upstream::ResourcePriority priority,\n   140\u2192 Upstream::ClusterConnectivityState& state,\n   143\u2192               absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> idle_timeout)\n   144\u2192      : Envoy::ConnectionPool::ConnPoolImplBase(host, priority, dispatcher, options,\n   145\u2192                                                transport_socket_options, state),\n   146\u2192        idle_timeout_(idle_timeout) {}\n   147\u2192 151\u2192\n   152\u2192  void addIdleCallback(IdleCb cb) override { addIdleCallbackImpl(cb); }\n   153\u2192  bool isIdle() const override { return isIdleImpl(); }\n   154\u2192  void drainConnections(Envoy::ConnectionPool::DrainBehavior drain_behavior) override;\n   155\u2192  void closeConnections() override;\n   156\u2192  ConnectionPool::Cancellable* "   380\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptHeadersFromRouter(bool end_stream) {\n   381\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   382\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  // Make sure that when we are forwarding CONNECT payload we do not do so until\n   385\u2192  // the upstream has accepted the CONNECT request.\n   386\u2192  // This must be done before conn_pool->newStream, as onPoolReady un-pauses for CONNECT\n   387\u2192  // termination.\n   388\u2192  auto* headers = parent_.downstreamHeaders();\n   389\u2192  if (headers->getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect) {\n   390\u2192    paused_for_connect_ = true;\n   391\u2192    // If this is a websocket upgrade request, pause the request until the upstream sends\n   392\u2192    // the 101 Switching Protocols response code. Using the else logic here to obey CONNECT\n   393\u2192    // method which is expecting 2xx response.\n   394\u2192  } else if ((Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   395\u2192                 \"envoy.reloadable_features.check_switch_protocol_websocket_handshake\")) &&\n   396\u2192             Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(*headers)) {\n   397\u2192    paused_for_websocket_ = true;\n   398\u2192  }\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192  // Kick off creation of the upstream connection immediately upon receiving headers.\n   401\u2192  // In future it may be possible for upstream HTTP filters to delay this, or influence connection\n   402\u2192  // creation but for now optimize for minimal latency and fetch the connection\n   403\u2192  // as soon as possible.\n   404\u2192  conn_pool_->newStream(this);\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192  if (parent_.config().upstream_log_flush_interval_.has_value()) {\n   407\u2192    upstream_log_flush_timer_ = parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().createTimer([this]() -> void {\n   408\u2192      // If the request is complete, we've already done the stream-end upstream log, and shouldn't\n   409\u2192      // do the periodic log.\n   410\u2192      if (!streamInfo().requestComplete().has_value()) {\n   411\u2192        upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType::UpstreamPeriodic);\n   412\u2192        resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   413\u2192      }\n   414\u2192      // Both downstream and upstream bytes meters may not be initialized when\n   415\u2192      // the timer goes off, e.g. if it takes longer than the interval for a\n   416\u2192      // connection to be initialized; check for nullptr.\n   417\u2192      auto& downstream_bytes_meter = stream_info_.getDownstreamBytesMeter();\n 424\u2192        upstream_bytes_meter->takeUpstreamPeriodicLoggingSnapshot(now);\n   425\u2192      }\n   426\u2192    });\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192    resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   429\u2192  }\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192  filter_manager_->requestHeadersInitialized();\n   432\u2192  filter_manager_->streamInfo().setRequestHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders());\n   433\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders(), end_stream);\n   434\u2192}\n   435\u2192\n   436\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptDataFromRouter(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   437\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   438\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeData(data, end_stream);\n   441\u2192}\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptTrailersFromRouter(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) {\n   444\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   445\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = UpstreamRequest::acceptMetadataFromRouter(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) {\n   452\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeMetadata(*metadata_map_ptr);\n   453\u2192}\n   454\u2192\n   455\u2192void UpstreamRequest::onResetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reason,\n   456\u2192                                    absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) {\n   457\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n   460\u2192    // Add tags about reset.\n   461\u2192    span_->setTag(Tracing::Tags::get().Error, Tracing::Tags::get().True);\n   462\u2192    span_->setTag(Tracing::Tags::get().ErrorReason, Http::Utility::resetReasonToString(reason));\n   463\u2192  }\n   464\u2192  clearRequestEncoder();\n   465\u2192  awaiting_headers_ = false;\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192  stream_info_.setResponseFlag(Filter::streamResetReasonToResponseFlag(reason));\n   468\u2192 (conn_pool_->cancelAnyPendingStream()) {\n   473\u2192    ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"canceled pool request\", *parent_.callbacks());\n   474\u2192    ASSERT(!upstream_);\n   475\u2192  }\n 503\u2192  if (upstream_log_flush_timer_ != nullptr) {\n   504\u2192    upstream_log_flush_timer_->enableTimer(parent_.config().upstream_log_flush_interval_.value());\n   505\u2192  }\n   506\u2192}\n   507\u2192\n   508\u2192void UpstreamRequest::setupPerTryTimeout() {\n   509\u2192  ASSERT(!per_try_timeout_);\n   510\u2192  if (parent_.timeout().per_try_timeout_.count() > 0) {\n   511\u2192 per_try_timeout_->enableTimer(parent_.timeout().per_try_timeout_);\n   514\u2192  }\n   515\u2192\n   516\u2192  ASSERT(!per_try_idle_timeout_);\n   517\u2192  if (parent_.timeout().per_try_idle_timeout_.count() > 0) {\n   518\u2192    per_try_idle_timeout_ =\n   519\u2192        parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().createTimer([this]() -> void { onPerTryIdleTimeout(); });\n   520\u2192    resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   521\u2192  }\n   522\u2192}\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192void UpstreamRequest::onPerTryIdleTimeout() {\n   525\u2192 "   584\u2192void UpstreamRequest::onPoolReady(std::unique_ptr<GenericUpstream>&& upstream,\n   585\u2192                                  Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n   586\u2192                                  const Network::ConnectionInfoProvider& address_provider,\n   587\u2192                                  StreamInfo::StreamInfo& info,\n   588\u2192                                  absl::optional<Http::Protocol> protocol) {\n   589\u2192  // This may be called under an existing ScopeTrackerScopeState but it will unwind correctly.\n   590\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   591\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(debug, \"pool ready\", *parent_.callbacks());\n   592\u2192  recordConnectionPoolCallbackLatency();\n   593\u2192  upstream_ = std::move(upstream);\n   594\u2192  had_upstream_ = true;\n   595\u2192  // Have the upstream use the account of the downstream.\n   596\u2192  upstream_->setAccount(parent_.callbacks()->account());\n   597\u2192\n   598\u2192  host->outlierDetector().putResult(Upstream::Outlier::Result::LocalOriginConnectSuccess);\n   599\u2192\n   600\u2192  if (protocol) {\n   601\u2192    stream_info_.protocol(protocol.value());\n   602\u2192  } else {\n   603\u2192    // We only pause for CONNECT and WebSocket for HTTP upstreams. If this is a TCP upstream,\n   604\u2192    // unpause.\n   605\u2192    paused_for_connect_ = false;\n   606\u2192    paused_for_websocket_ = false;\n   607\u2192  }\n   608\u2192\n   609\u2192 612\u2192    upstreamTiming().upstream_connect_start_ = upstream_timing.upstream_connect_start_;\n   613\u2192    upstreamTiming().upstream_connect_complete_ = upstream_timing.upstream_connect_complete_;\n   614\u2192    upstreamTiming().upstream_handshake_complete_ = upstream_timing.upstream_handshake_complete_;\n   615\u2192    upstream_info.setUpstreamNumStreams(info.upstreamInfo()->upstreamNumStreams());\n   616\u2192  }\n   617\u2192\n   618\u2192  // Upstream HTTP filters might have already created/set a filter state.\n   619\u2192  const StreamInfo::FilterStateSharedPtr& filter_state = info.filterState();\n   620\u2192  if (!filter_state) {\n   621\u2192    upstream_info.setUpstreamFilterState(\n   622\u2192        std::make_shared<StreamInfo::FilterStateImpl>(StreamInfo::FilterState::LifeSpan::Request));\n   623\u2192  } else {\n   624\u2192    upstream_info.setUpstreamFilterState(filter_state);\n   625\u2192  }\n   626\u2192  upstream_info.setUpstreamLocalAddress(address_provider.localAddress());\n   627\u2192  upstream_info.setUpstreamRemoteAddress(address_provider.remoteAddress());\n   628\u2192  upstream_info.setUpstreamSslConnection(info.downstreamAddressProvider().sslConnection());\n   629\u2192\n   630\u2192  // Invoke the onUpstreamHostSelected after setting ssl_connection_info_ in upstream_info.\n   631\u2192  // This is because the onUpstreamHostSelected callback may need to access the ssl_connection_info\n   632\u2192  // to determine the scheme of the upstream connection.\n   633\u2192  onUpstreamHostSelected(host, true);\n   634\u2192\n   635\u2192  if (info.downstreamAddressProvider().connectionID().has_value()) {\n   636\u2192    upstream_info.setUpstreamConnectionId(info.downstreamAddressProvider().connectionID().value());\n   637\u2192  }\n   638\u2192\n   639\u2192  if (info.downstreamAddressProvider().interfaceName().has_value()) {\n   640\u2192    upstream_info.setUpstreamInterfaceName(\n   641\u2192        info.downstreamAddressProvider().interfaceName().value());\n   642\u2192  }\n   643\u2192\n   644\u2192  stream_info_.setUpstreamBytesMeter(upstream_->bytesMeter());\n   645\u2192  StreamInfo::StreamInfo::syncUpstreamAndDownstreamBytesMeter(parent_.callbacks()->streamInfo(),\n   646\u2192                                                              stream_info_);\n   647\u2192  if (protocol) {\n   648\u2192    upstream_info.setUpstreamProtocol(protocol.value());\n   649\u2192  }\n   650\u2192\n   651\u2192  if (parent_.downstreamEndStream()) {\n   652\u2192    setupPerTryTimeout();\n   653\u2192  } else {\n   654\u2192    create_per_try_timeout_on_request_complete_ = true;\n   655\u2192  }\n   656\u2192\n   657\u2192  // Make sure the connection manager will inform the downstream watermark manager when the\n   658\u2192  // downstream buffers are overrun. This may result in immediate watermark callbacks referencing\n   659\u2192  // the encoder.\n   660\u2192  parent_.callbacks()->addDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(downstream_watermark_manager_);\n   661\u2192\n   662\u2192  absl::optional<std::chrono::milliseconds> max_stream_duration;\n   663\u2192  if (parent_.dynamicMaxStreamDuration().has_value()) {\n   664\u2192    max_stream_duration = parent_.dynamicMaxStreamDuration().value();\n   665\u2192  } else if (upstream_host_->cluster().commonHttpProtocolOptions().has_max_stream_duration()) {\n   666\u2192    max_stream_duration = std::chrono::milliseconds(DurationUtil::durationToMilliseconds(\n   667\u2192        upstream_host_->cluster().commonHttpProtocolOptions().max_stream_duration()));\n   668\u2192  }\n   669\u2192  if (max_stream_duration.has_value() && max_stream_duration->count()) {\n   670\u2192    max_stream_duration_timer_ = parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().createTimer(\n   671\u2192        [this]() -> void { onStreamMaxDurationReached(); });\n   672\u2192    max_stream_duration_timer_->enableTimer(*max_stream_duration);\n   673\u2192  }\n   674\u2192\n   675\u2192  const auto* route_entry = route().routeEntry();\n   676\u2192  if (route_entry->autoHostRewrite() && !host->hostname().empty()) {\n   677\u2192    Http::Utility::updateAuthority(*parent_.downstreamHeaders(), host->hostname(),\n   678\u2192                                   route_entry->appendXfh());\n   679\u2192  }\n   680\u2192\n   681\u2192  stream_info_.setRequestHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders());\n   682\u2192\n   683\u2192  if (parent_.config().flush_upstream_log_on_upstream_stream_) {\n   684\u2192    upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType::UpstreamPoolReady);\n   685\u2192  }\n   686\u2192\n   687\u2192  if (address_provider.connectionID() && stream_info_.downstreamAddressProvider().connectionID()) {\n   688\u2192    ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"Attached upstream connection [C{}] to downstream connection [C{}]\",\n   689\u2192              address_provider.connectionID().value(),\n   690\u2192              stream_info_.downstreamAddressProvider().connectionID().value());\n   691\u2192  }\n   692\u2192\n   693\u2192  for (auto* callback : upstream_callbacks_) {\n   694\u2192    callback->onUpstreamConnectionEstablished();\n   695\u2192  }\n   696\u2192}\n   697\u2192\n   698\u2192UpstreamToDownstream& UpstreamRequest::upstreamToDownstream() { return *upstream_interface_; }\n   699\u2192\n   700\u2192void UpstreamRequest::onStreamMaxDurationReached() {\n   701\u2192  upstream_host_->cluster().trafficStats()->upstream_rq_max_duration_reached_.inc();\n   702\u2192\n   703\u2192  // The upstream had closed then try to retry along with retry policy.\n   704\u2192  parent_.onStreamMaxDurationReached(*this);\n   705\u2192}\n   706\u2192\n   707\u2192void UpstreamRequest::clearRequestEncoder() {\n   708\u2192  // Before clearing the encoder, unsubscribe from callbacks.\n   709\u2192  if (upstream_) {\n   710\u2192    parent_.callbacks()->removeDownstreamWatermarkCallbacks(downstream_watermark_manager_);\n   711\u2192  }\n   712\u2192  upstream_.reset();\n   713\u2192}\n   714\u2192\n   715\u2192void UpstreamRequest::readDisableOrDefer(bool disable) {\n   716\u2192  if (disable) {\n   717\u2192    // See comments on deferred_read_disabling_count_ for when we do and don't defer.\n   718\u2192    if (parent_.downstreamResponseStarted()) {\n   719\u2192      // The downstream connection is overrun. 790\u2192\n   791\u2192Tracing::Span& UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks::activeSpan() {\n   792\u2192  return upstream_request_.parent_.callbacks()->activeSpan();\n   793\u2192}\n   794\u2192\n   795\u2192void UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks::resetStream(\n   796\u2192    Http::StreamResetReason reset_reason, absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) {\n   797\u2192  // The filter manager needs to disambiguate between a filter-driven reset,\n   798\u2192  // which should force reset the stream, and a codec driven reset, which should\n   799\u2192  // tell the router the stream reset, and let the router make the decision to\n   800\u2192  // send a local reply, or retry the stream.\n   801\u2192  bool is_codec_error;\n   802\u2192  if (Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\"envoy.reloadable_features.report_stream_reset_error_code\")) {\n   803\u2192    is_codec_error = absl::StrContains(transport_failure_reason, \"codec_error\");\n   804\u2192  } else {\n   805\u2192    is_codec_error = transport_failure_reason == \"codec_error\";\n   806\u2192  }\n   807\u2192  if (reset_reason == Http::StreamResetReason::LocalReset && !is_codec_error) {\n   808\u2192    upstream_request_.parent_.callbacks()->resetStream();\n   809\u2192    return;\n   810\u2192  }\n   811\u2192  return upstream_request_.onResetStream(reset_reason, transport_failure_reason);\n   812\u2192}\n   813\u2192\n   814\u2192Upstream::ClusterInfoConstSharedPtr UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks::clusterInfo() {\n   815\u2192  return upstream_request_.parent_.callbacks()->clusterInfo();\n   816\u2192}\n   817\u2192\n   818\u2192Http::Http1StreamEncoderOptionsOptRef\n   819\u2192UpstreamRequestFilterManagerCallbacks::http1StreamEncoderOptions() {\n   820\u2192  return upstream_request_.parent_.callbacks()->http1StreamEncoderOptions();\n   821\u2192}\n   822\u2192\n   823\u2192} // namespace Router\n   824\u2192} // namespace Envoy\n   825\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   267\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decodeHeaders(Http::ResponseHeaderMapPtr&& headers, bool end_stream) {\n   268\u2192  ASSERT(headers.get());\n   269\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"end_stream: {}, upstream response headers:\\n{}\", *parent_.callbacks(),\n   270\u2192                   end_stream, *headers);\n   271\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192  resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192  addResponseHeadersStat(headers->byteSize(), headers->size());\n   276\u2192\n   277\u2192  // We drop unsupported 1xx on the floor here. 101 upgrade headers need to be passed to the client\n   278\u2192  // as part of the final response. Most 1xx headers are handled in onUpstream1xxHeaders.\n   279\u2192  //\n   280\u2192  // We could in principle handle other headers here, but this might result in the double invocation\n   281\u2192  // of decodeHeaders() (once for informational, again for non-informational), which is likely an\n   282\u2192  // easy to miss corner case in the filter and HCM contract.\n   283\u2192  //\n   284\u2192  // This filtering is done early in upstream request, unlike 100 coalescing which is performed in\n   285\u2192  // the router filter, since the filtering only depends on the state of a single upstream, and we\n   286\u2192  // don't want to confuse accounting such as onFirstUpstreamRxByteReceived() with informational\n   287\u2192  // headers.\n   288\u2192  const uint64_t response_code = Http::Utility::getResponseStatus(*headers);\n   289\u2192  if (Http::CodeUtility::is1xx(response_code) &&\n   290\u2192      response_code != enumToInt(Http::Code::SwitchingProtocols)) {\n   291\u2192    return;\n   292\u2192  }\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192  awaiting_headers_ = false;\n   295\u2192  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n   296\u2192    Tracing::HttpTracerUtility::onUpstreamResponseHeaders(*span_, headers.get());\n   297\u2192  }\n   298\u2192  if (!parent_.config().upstream_logs_.empty()) {\n   299\u2192    upstream_headers_ = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::ResponseHeaderMapImpl>(*headers);\n   300\u2192  }\n   301\u2192  stream_info_.setResponseCode(static_cast<uint32_t>(response_code));\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192  maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable();\n   304\u2192  ASSERT(headers.get());\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamHeaders(response_code, std::move(headers), *this, end_stream);\n   307\u2192}\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192void UpstreamRequest::maybeHandleDeferredReadDisable() {\n   310\u2192  for (; deferred_read_disabling_count_ > 0; --deferred_read_disabling_count_) {\n   311\u2192    // If the deferred read disabling count hasn't been cancelled out by read\n   312\u2192    // enabling count so far, stop the upstream from reading the rest response.\n   313\u2192    // Because readDisable keeps track of how many time it is called with\n   314\u2192    // \"true\" or \"false\", here it has to be called with \"true\" the same number\n   315\u2192    // of times as it would be called with \"false\" in the future.\n   316\u2192    parent_.cluster()->trafficStats()->upstream_flow_control_paused_reading_total_.inc();\n   317\u2192    upstream_->readDisable(true);\n   318\u2192  }\n   319\u2192}\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decodeData(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   322\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192  resetPerTryIdleTimer();\n   325\u2192  stream_info_.addBytesReceived(data.length());\n   326\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamData(data, *this, end_stream);\n   327\u2192}\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decodeTrailers(Http::ResponseTrailerMapPtr&& trailers) {\n   330\u2192  ENVOY_STREAM_LOG(trace, \"upstream response trailers:\\n{}\", *parent_.callbacks(), *trailers);\n   331\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   332\u2192\n   333\u2192  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n   334\u2192    Tracing::HttpTracerUtility::onUpstreamResponseTrailers(*span_, trailers.get());\n   335\u2192  }\n   336\u2192  if (!parent_.config().upstream_logs_.empty()) {\n   337\u2192    upstream_trailers_ = Http::createHeaderMap<Http::ResponseTrailerMapImpl>(*trailers);\n   338\u2192  }\n   339\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamTrailers(std::move(trailers), *this);\n   340\u2192}\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192void UpstreamRequest::dumpState(std::ostream& os, int indent_level) const {\n   343\u2192  const char* spaces = spacesForLevel(indent_level);\n   344\u2192  os << spaces << \"UpstreamRequest \" << this << \"\\n\";\n   345\u2192  if (connection()) {\n   346\u2192    const auto addressProvider = connection()->connectionInfoProviderSharedPtr();\n   347\u2192    DUMP_DETAILS(addressProvider);\n   348\u2192  }\n   349\u2192  const Http::RequestHeaderMap* request_headers = parent_.downstreamHeaders();\n   350\u2192  DUMP_DETAILS(request_headers);\n   351\u2192  if (filter_manager_) {\n   352\u2192    filter_manager_->dumpState(os, indent_level);\n   353\u2192  }\n   354\u2192}\n   355\u2192\n   356\u2192const Route& UpstreamRequest::route() const { return *parent_.callbacks()->route(); }\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192OptRef<const Network::Connection> UpstreamRequest::connection() const {\n   359\u2192  return parent_.callbacks()->connection();\n   360\u2192}\n   361\u2192\n   362\u2192void UpstreamRequest::decodeMetadata(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map) {\n   363\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamMetadata(std::move(metadata_map));\n   364\u2192}\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192void UpstreamRequest::maybeEndDecode(bool end_stream) {\n   367\u2192  if (end_stream) {\n   368\u2192    upstreamTiming().onLastUpstreamRxByteReceived(parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().timeSource());\n   369\u2192  }\n   370\u2192}\n   371\u2192\n   372\u2192void UpstreamRequest::onUpstreamHostSelected(Upstream::HostDescriptionConstSharedPtr host,\n   373\u2192                                             bool pool_success) {\n   374\u2192  StreamInfo::UpstreamInfo& upstream_info = *streamInfo().upstreamInfo();\n   375\u2192  upstream_info.setUpstreamHost(host);\n   376\u2192  upstream_host_ = host;\n   377\u2192  parent_.onUpstreamHostSelected(host, pool_success);\n   378\u2192}\n   379\u2192\n   380\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptHeadersFromRouter(bool end_stream) {\n   381\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   382\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   383\u2192\n   384\u2192  // Make sure that when we are forwarding CONNECT payload we do not do so until\n   385\u2192  // the upstream has accepted the CONNECT request.\n   386\u2192  // This must be done before conn_pool->newStream, as onPoolReady un-pauses for CONNECT\n   387\u2192  // termination.\n   388\u2192  auto* headers = parent_.downstreamHeaders();\n   389\u2192  if (headers->getMethodValue() == Http::Headers::get().MethodValues.Connect) {\n   390\u2192    paused_for_connect_ = true;\n   391\u2192    // If this is a websocket upgrade request, pause the request until the upstream sends\n   392\u2192    // the 101 Switching Protocols response code. Using the else logic here to obey CONNECT\n   393\u2192    // method which is expecting 2xx response.\n   394\u2192  } else if ((Runtime::runtimeFeatureEnabled(\n   395\u2192                 \"envoy.reloadable_features.check_switch_protocol_websocket_handshake\")) &&\n   396\u2192             Http::Utility::isWebSocketUpgradeRequest(*headers)) {\n   397\u2192    paused_for_websocket_ = true;\n   398\u2192  }\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192  // Kick off creation of the upstream connection immediately upon receiving headers.\n   401\u2192  // In future it may be possible for upstream HTTP filters to delay this, or influence connection\n   402\u2192  // creation but for now optimize for minimal latency and fetch the connection\n   403\u2192  // as soon as possible.\n   404\u2192  conn_pool_->newStream(this);\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192  if (parent_.config().upstream_log_flush_interval_.has_value()) {\n   407\u2192    upstream_log_flush_timer_ = parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().createTimer([this]() -> void {\n   408\u2192      // If the request is complete, we've already done the stream-end upstream log, and shouldn't\n   409\u2192      // do the periodic log.\n   410\u2192      if (!streamInfo().requestComplete().has_value()) {\n   411\u2192        upstreamLog(AccessLog::AccessLogType::UpstreamPeriodic);\n   412\u2192        resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   413\u2192      }\n   414\u2192      // Both downstream and upstream bytes meters may not be initialized when\n   415\u2192      // the timer goes off, e.g. if it takes longer than the interval for a\n   416\u2192      // connection to be initialized; check for nullptr.\n   417\u2192      auto& downstream_bytes_meter = stream_info_.getDownstreamBytesMeter();\n   418\u2192      auto& upstream_bytes_meter = stream_info_.getUpstreamBytesMeter();\n   419\u2192      const SystemTime now = parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher().timeSource().systemTime();\n   420\u2192      if (downstream_bytes_meter) {\n   421\u2192        downstream_bytes_meter->takeUpstreamPeriodicLoggingSnapshot(now);\n   422\u2192      }\n   423\u2192      if (upstream_bytes_meter) {\n   424\u2192        upstream_bytes_meter->takeUpstreamPeriodicLoggingSnapshot(now);\n   425\u2192      }\n   426\u2192    });\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192    resetUpstreamLogFlushTimer();\n   429\u2192  }\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192  filter_manager_->requestHeadersInitialized();\n   432\u2192  filter_manager_->streamInfo().setRequestHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders());\n   433\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeHeaders(*parent_.downstreamHeaders(), end_stream);\n   434\u2192}\n   435\u2192\n   436\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptDataFromRouter(Buffer::Instance& data, bool end_stream) {\n   437\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   438\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = end_stream;\n   439\u2192\n   440\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeData(data, end_stream);\n   441\u2192}\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptTrailersFromRouter(Http::RequestTrailerMap& trailers) {\n   444\u2192  ASSERT(!router_sent_end_stream_);\n   445\u2192  router_sent_end_stream_ = true;\n   446\u2192  encode_trailers_ = true;\n   447\u2192\n   448\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeTrailers(trailers);\n   449\u2192}\n   450\u2192\n   451\u2192void UpstreamRequest::acceptMetadataFromRouter(Http::MetadataMapPtr&& metadata_map_ptr) {\n   452\u2192  filter_manager_->decodeMetadata(*metadata_map_ptr);\n   453\u2192}\n   454\u2192\n   455\u2192void UpstreamRequest::onResetStream(Http::StreamResetReason reason,\n   456\u2192                                    absl::string_view transport_failure_reason) {\n   457\u2192  ScopeTrackerScopeState scope(&parent_.callbacks()->scope(), parent_.callbacks()->dispatcher());\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192  if (span_ != nullptr) {\n   460\u2192    // Add tags about reset.\n   461\u2192    span_->setTag(Tracing::Tags::get().Error, Tracing::Tags::get().True);\n   462\u2192    span_->setTag(Tracing::Tags::get().ErrorReason, Http::Utility::resetReasonToString(reason));\n   463\u2192  }\n   464\u2192  clearRequestEncoder();\n   465\u2192  awaiting_headers_ = false;\n   466\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
